======================================================================
PAGE: Barbara Reen
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Barbara_Reen
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
,
USS
Voyager
Status:
Active
(
2376
)
Barbara Reen
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. Her
serial number
was AR-058-1436 CDE.
In
2371
, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In an
alternate timeline
, her name was listed on the
crew manifest
read by
Harry Kim
in early
2372
.
(
VOY
: "
Non Sequitur
",
display graphic
)
In
2376
, her name was listed on a crew manifest. (
VOY
: "
11:59
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Barredo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Barredo
======================================================================

Barredo
was a
scientist
.
Due to his work, along with that of
Dante
and
Bishop
, scientists were able to calculate "n-dimensional negation of internal quantum numbers, normalizing for space time inversion". This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Ana Barredo
, manager of DVD production at
CBS Home Entertainment
.

of
Hodos
was a
merchant
in the
Delta Quadrant
. His species were a green-skinned
humanoid
with a spiny ridge down the center of their faces and no hair on their heads.
In
2376
, Bathar, after having met four members of the
USS
Voyager
crew
, claimed to have a formula that stopped the
aging
process.
Commander
Chakotay
remarked that it was only a
tripolymer enzyme
, but it made a great
shoe polish
.
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
commented that he was "very photogenic." (
VOY
: "
Memorial
")
Bathar was played by an
unknown actor
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bathkin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bathkin
======================================================================

Doctor
Bathkin
was a
physician
on
Andros III
who developed the "answer" to solving the puzzle of
death
: keep the
cells
of the body energized, and keep them in the game by teaching them new
mitochondrial
tricks. Unfortunately, Dr. Bathkin died in a
shuttlecraft
accident in
2359
before he could ever finish his work.
Eventually, his work was furthered by Dr.
Elias Giger
, who built his
cellular regeneration and entertainment chamber
aboard
Deep Space 9
in
2373
. (
DS9
: "
In the Cards
")
This doctor was only mentioned in dialogue.

was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Bazminti, when he pulled back the veil
", which referred to an undercover operation.
On the
USS
Cerritos
in
2381
, when
Rumdar
, a
Pakled
spy, asked to see the ship's
crimson force field
,
Lt.
Kayshon
remarked to
Cmdr.
Jack Ransom
, "
You getting 'Bazminti, when he pulled back the veil' vibes from this guy?
" (
LD
: "
The Spy Humongous
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Belle Sisko
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Belle_Sisko
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Belle Sisko
was a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
spouse
of
Tyrell Sisko
, and
parent
of
Reggie Sisko
.
In the
32nd century
, Belle's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The family tree does not clearly indicate Belle's relationship to Tyrell's offspring
Nichelle
, or Tyrell's relationship to Reggie.

to the

PAGE: Beverly C. Kurts

Ensign

Ensign
Beverly C. Kurts
was a
Starfleet
officer
during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
and
2375
, files from
starships
USS
Akagi
,
USS
Victory
and
USS
Tripoli
, as well as
Allied
Romulan
vessel
PWB
Tomal
, all reported Kurts
missing in action
. Files from the

Zapata
and
USS
Tecumseh
reported her
killed
. (
DS9
: "

")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Kurts' name appeared on the
casualty report
display seen on a
computer readout
, on both a
PADD
and a
viewscreen
. It is unclear which assignment was her home station, if any.

Beverly C. Kurts
at

, the wiki for licensed

======================================================================
PAGE: Bevvox
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bevvox
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Bioplasmic organism
Affiliation:
Think Tank
Occupation:
Founder of the "Think Tank"
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Bevvox
was a
bioplasmic organism
, who wandered the
galaxy
alone for a few millennia before founding the "
Think Tank
", a group of intellects who solve problems for a price, in the
23rd century
. His interests varied over time. As of
2375
, they were
exosociology
and
quantum mechanics
. He was somewhat sensitive about his age and often acted "grouchy" in the mornings.
In 2375, Bevvox and the other
Think Tank members
attempted to compel
Seven of Nine
of the
USS
Voyager
to join their group. They contracted
Hazari
bounty hunters
to attack the
Voyager
, creating a problem that seemingly only they could solve, in exchange for Seven of Nine. (
VOY
: "
Think Tank
")
External links
Bevvox
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Bezos
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bezos
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Jeff Bezos
Bezos
was an
alien
Starfleet
officer
who was stationed at
Yorktown
starbase
in the mid-
23rd century
.
After
Kalara
arrived at Yorktown in
2263
, he
scanned
her while she made her plea for assistance to
Commodore
Paris
and
Captain
James T. Kirk
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
The name of this character was not given onscreen, but was revealed in
Star Trek Beyond - The Makeup Artistry of Joel Harlow
(pp. 184–185).
Bezos was appropriately played by
Jeff Bezos
in a cameo role. In the words of
Star Trek Beyond
Director
Justin Lin
, Bezos "dropped in" to play the role.
[1]
He greatly enjoyed the experience, although the extensive make-up he wore for the part required him to drink through a straw.
[2]
[3]

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Crewman
Status:
Active
(
2378
)
For the 20th century Human, please see
Biddle Coleridge
.
Crewman
Biddle
was a
24th century
Starfleet
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
.
Biddle became stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
along with the rest of the crew of
Voyager
in
2371
. Biddle was once welcomed to the
mess hall
by
Neelix
in
2378
; the
Talaxian
asked where Biddle had been, as he had not encountered the crewman for days. (
VOY
: "
Q2
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Big Wave Tommy
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Big_Wave_Tommy
======================================================================

"
Big Wave Tommy
" was a
surfer
who lived during the
24th century
.
To encourage his
ensigns
to work together in
2382
,
Commander
Jack Ransom
told them a long rambling story, one part of which was
Kimolu
and
Matt
saying he could not beat Big Wave Tommy in a surfing competition because the latter "
was
king
of the
beach
.
" (
LD
: "
Upper Decks
")
This surfer was only mentioned in dialogue.

.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bishop
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bishop
======================================================================

Bishop
was a
scientist
.
Due to his work, along with that of
Dante
and
Barredo
, scientists were able to calculate "n-dimensional negation of internal quantum numbers, normalizing for space time inversion".  This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Phil Bishop
, an executive producer at
CBS Home Entertainment
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bjayzl
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bjayzl
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Self-affiliated
Fenris Rangers
(formerly)
Status:
Deceased
(
2399
)
Died:
2399,
Freecloud
Played by
:
Necar Zadegan
"
She butchers ex-Borg for parts.
"
–
Seven of Nine
,
2399
("
Stardust City Rag
")
Bjayzl
was a
criminal
businesswoman who was based on
Freecloud
in the late
24th century
. Throughout the late
2380s
and
2390s
, Bjayzl became known for brutalizing
former Borg
drones
, extracting their implants, and selling them on the
black market
. She was wanted by the
Fenris Rangers
for these practices.
Around
2386
, Bjayzl infiltrated the Fenris Rangers in an attempt to get close to
Seven of Nine
. It was from Seven that Bjayzl learned of
Icheb
and his whereabouts, and set up an ambush to capture him while he was investigating a falsified distress signal. At a lab within the
Seven Domes
on
Vergessen
, she employed surgeons who removed Icheb's
Borg
implants – without killing him beforehand, or even providing
anesthetics
to make the process less painful. By the time Seven found Icheb, he was mortally wounded and in such horrific pain that she was forced to
euthanize
him.
Bjayzl loaned
cyberneticist
Bruce Maddox
a substantial sum for a new
laboratory
. In
2399
,
Dr.
Maddox returned to Bjayzl empty-handed after his lab was destroyed by the
Tal Shiar
. Bjayzl at first ordered his death, but then reconsidered and subdued him with
drugged
tranya
, intending to sell him to the Tal Shiar.
Seven disintegrates Bjayzl
Two weeks later,
Jean-Luc Picard
disguised himself as a trader and, with the help of
Cristóbal Rios
,
Elnor
, and Seven of Nine, attempted a ruse to trade Seven for Maddox. The plan went awry as Seven revealed her true intentions: she wanted to kill Bjayzl in retribution for Icheb's death, whom Seven described as "the closest thing I will ever have to kin." Picard and Rios seemingly managed to talk Seven out of murdering Bjayzl, allowing the team to beam back aboard
La Sirena
. Moments later, however, Seven beamed back with two
type 3 phaser
rifles, and, after a short conversation,
vaporized
Bjayzl anyway. (
PIC
: "
Stardust City Rag
")
Bjayzl was played by
Necar Zadegan
.
In the original script for "
Stardust City Rag
", the character was going to be a
Caitian
. She would have lived inside an
aviary
filled with
birds
, which she would periodically eat.
[1]
In an even earlier outline, Bjayzl was intended to be a member of
Balok's species
.
[2]
According to the video game
Star Trek Timelines
, Bjayzl is Human.

Starfleet
officer
and
commanding officer
of the
Federation
starship
USS
Tombaugh
.
According to
Ensign
Stone
's
personal log
of
stardate
52188.7, Captain Blackwood ran a tight ship and took special pleasure in tormenting new
recruits
. The
first officer
felt his attacks were nothing personal.
According to
Seven of Nine
, he and his ship were
assimilated
by the
Borg
in
2362
. (
VOY
: "
Infinite Regress
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bosaal
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bosaal
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Rank:
Commander
Occupation:
Captain
of a starship
Status:
Active
(
2377
)
Played by
:
Michael Shamus Wiles
Commander
Bosaal
was the
captain
of a ship caught in
the Void
. He was from a
Delta Quadrant
species that possessed a bilateral ridge running vertically along the front of their heads to their chest. (
VOY
: "
The Void
")
He was encountered by the
USS
Voyager
when that
Federation
starship
was trapped within the Void in
2377
. Bosaal had technology that could help both ships escape the Void.
However, when he saw
Fantome
aboard
Voyager
, he described him as vermin. Members of
Fantome's species
, who were native to the Void, had infested Bosaal's ship, causing
engineering
problems. After
Voyager
beamed
Fantome's people off Bosaal's ship, Bosaal became an ally of
Captain
Janeway
's and offered to help their ships escape the Void.
Voyager
chief engineer
B'Elanna Torres
said she needed to build a
polaron modulator
to generate the energy to escape the Void. Bosaal produced one, but he was soon found to have stolen it from another Void-trapped ship after he killed its crew. In Janeway's belief that using the device would make her and the crew of
Voyager
accessories to
murder
, she threw Bosaal and his ill-gotten polaron modulator out of the
alliance
.
Bosaal and his ship later reappeared with
Valen
's and sought to destroy
Voyager
. However, they were both left behind in the Void when
Voyager
and its allies escaped. (
VOY
: "
The Void
")
Bosaal was played by
Michael Shamus Wiles
. The final draft of the script describes this species as "a formidable-looking new alien."

PAGE: Bosus

Species:
Unknown
Borg
Affiliation:
Lore
's rogue Borg faction
Formerly
Borg Collective
Status:

, killed by
Worf
(
2369
)
Died:
2369,
Ohniaka III
Played by
:
Richard Gilbert-Hill
Bosus
was a member of the group of rogue
Borg
led by
Lore
.
He was part of a Borg group which attacked a
Federation
outpost
on
Ohniaka III
in
2369
. During a following battle with an
away team
from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, a friend of Bosus named
Torsus
was killed. For this he swore revenge and killed
Enterprise
-D
security officer
Corelki
, before
Lieutenant
Worf
could kill him. (
TNG
: "
Descent
")
Bosus was played by
Richard Gilbert-Hill
.
This character was only named in the source's end credits.

. His

were tested by

genetic
program. The results came back negative. (

: "

",
display graphic

, he had been transferred to the

Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the

was forcefully brought there by the

.

, as a

. (

2374
, he was listed on a

. (

in the years
2369
-
2372
and a
commander
in
2374
.
In 2368, he was a
crew
member aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. In the
week
of
stardate
45852.1, on
Monday
, he had an appointment for an annual career counseling session with
Counselor
Deanna Troi
at 10:30. This information was displayed on a
personal appointment log
in Troi's
quarters
. (
TNG
: "
Imaginary Friend
"
display graphic
) He had the same appointment later with
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
. (
TNG
: "
The Next Phase
"
display graphic
)
The following year, he was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
In
2371
, he was a crew member aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In 2372, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
"
okudagram
)
In 2374, he was listed on a
crew manifest
, holding the rank of
commander
. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game
"
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This officer is named after
Brannon Braga
, one of the producers and writers of
Star Trek
between 1990 and 2005.

Breil
was a
female
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
. She served aboard the
USS
Enterprise
under
Captain
James T. Kirk
, in
2269
as the ship's
mess officer
.
That year, Breil called
security
, following the
panic
caused by the
shrinking
of the ship's crew in the
mess hall
. She reported the apparent increases in the size of
tables
,
chairs
, and
silverware
, as well as the sudden disappearance of smaller objects like
rings
and
hairpins
, which had been noted by
women
crewmembers. (
TAS
: "
The Terratin Incident
")
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
The voice for Breil was provided by
Nichelle Nichols
.
Breil was originally scripted to appear on-screen, where they were described as a "disturbed looking young [male] officer."

======================================================================
PAGE: Brettner
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Brettner
======================================================================

Brettner in Riker's service record
Admiral
Brettner
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in a fictional future created for
William T. Riker
.
In
2367
, while exploring a
cavern
on
Alpha Onias III
,
Commander
Riker was rendered unconscious by
gases
. While unconscious,
neural scanners
scanned Riker's
brain
. The scanners used elements of Riker's reality and constructed a
holodeck simulation
with those elements interspersed throughout, so that it felt real to him. What Riker wanted, the scanners made possible.
In one of these simulations, Starfleet Admiral Brettner awarded a commendation for exceptional leadership to
Captain
Riker, of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, on
stardate
57398, during the joint
Excalbian Campaign
. In
2383
, this admiral was mentioned in Riker's
service record
that was on display in his
quarters
on the
Enterprise
-D. (
TNG-R
: "
Future Imperfect
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered episode of "Future Imperfect", the original text of Riker's service record was replaced.

and

of

, and

and

of

======================================================================
PAGE: Brink
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Brink
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commander
Occupation:
First officer
,
USS
Brattain
Status:
Deceased
(
2367
)
Died:
2367
,
USS
Brattain
Commander
Brink
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. In the
2360s
, he served as the
first officer
of the
USS
Brattain
until his
death
in
2367
.
When the
Brattain
was trapped in a
Tyken's Rift
in 2367, he was unable to
dream
like the rest of the crew. The ship's engines had also become inoperative.
Madness
spread, and
Captain
Zaheva
suspected Brink and several other crewmen were plotting against her,
sabotaging
the engines. Though Zaheva stated that Brink and his men would be dealt with in some fashion, it is unknown if she killed him, or if he was one of the bodies later retrieved by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. In the end, all of the crew save one perished through
homicide
or
suicide
. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Brinner Finok

Ensign
Brinner Finok
was a
Starfleet
officer that served aboard the
USS
Destiny
under the command of
Captain
Raymer
in
2374
-
75
.
Brinner was an individual
Ezri Tigan
had taken interest in when she was still serving aboard the
Destiny
. After being joined with the
Dax symbiont
, Ezri did talk to Brinner once but decided that they were not right for each other anymore. He reminded her too much of
Gran
, the son of her former
host
,
Audrid Dax
, which made it a little uncomfortable for her to be around him. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")

While Brinner Finok's species is not known, it may be assumed that he is not a
Trill
because Ezri was the only Trill on board, as established in "
Shadows and Symbols
". In the non-
canon
stories written by
Judith
and
Garfield Reeves-Stevens
in
The Lives of Dax
anthology, Finok is a Trill and was prevented from being joined with Dax after being rendered unconscious by a
Changeling
who had infiltrated the
Destiny
.

Brinner Finok
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Unknown
Occupation:

Status:
Active
(
2381
)
Played by
:
Eric Bauza
"
Mister
Broker
", as addressed by
Carol Freeman
, was a diminutive
alien
information broker
who operated out of
Mudds
on
New Axton
, where he sat upon a small high-backed
chair
placed atop a
table
.
He was visited at Mudds in
2381
by
Captain
Freeman, who initially believed Broker to be an
alien
puppet
, and physically assaulted him. However,
Lieutenant
Sam Rutherford
discovered Broker to be a real person, causing Freeman to instantly regret her actions. Broker was so offended by Freeman's attitude that he sold the information she needed to someone else instead, unaware that this person was actually
Lieutenant Commander
Andy Billups
. (
LD
: "
The Inner Fight
")
"Mr. Broker" was voiced by
Eric Bauza
.

PAGE: Brooks (Admiral)

was a high-ranking
Starfleet
officer
in the
2360s
.
Following the incident involving the
Borg drone
Hugh
in
2368
,
Captain
Picard
submitted a full report to Admiral Brooks. This report was mentioned one year later in a conversation between Picard and Admiral
Alynna Nechayev
. (
TNG
: "
Descent
")
When
Doctor
Crusher
was faced with a
board of inquiry
in
2369
, she told
Guinan
that she could already hear Admiral Brooks telling Beverly how much she had disgraced
Starfleet Medical
. (
TNG
: "
Suspicions
")

, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 107.

======================================================================
PAGE: Brown (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Brown_(24th_century)
======================================================================

"
Doc
"
Brown
was the
Paris
family
doctor
during the
2350s
.
He always had
lollipops
in the
waiting room
, no
holocomics
more than six
months
old, and even made
house calls
. When
Tom
was nine
years
old, he caught a bad
cold
; the doc whipped up a
pot
of
garlic soup
and brought it over himself.
In
2371
, while undergoing
scans
by
The Doctor
, Paris waxed
nostalgic
about "
good old Doc Brown
", implying he'd rather be under Brown's care than The Doctor's. The Doctor, for his part, found Paris's reminiscences "irritating". (
VOY
: "
Cathexis
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 108 identified Doc Brown as a
pediatrician
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Bruce Lucier
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bruce_Lucier
======================================================================

Bruce Lucier
was an acquaintance of
Julian Bashir
.
In
2367
, he held a
New Year's Eve
party
at his
home
which Bashir and his
Andorian
friend
Erit
attended.
Elizabeth Lense
was also present at the gathering. During the party, another guest pointed out Erit to Lense and told her that the Andorian was Bashir. Lense went on believing Bashir to be an Andorian for the next four
years
. (
DS9
: "
Explorers
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The nature of his acquaintance with Bashir and Lense is unknown, although it seems likely he was another
medical
student. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 491 identifed him as a medical student at
Starfleet Medical School
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Buck Bokai (alien)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Buck_Bokai_(alien)
======================================================================

Species:
Unidentified non-corporeal species
Occupation:
Explorer
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Keone Young
"
You're not simply a figment of my imagination, are you.
"
–
Benjamin Sisko
,
2369
("
If Wishes Were Horses
")
Buck Bokai
was the persona adopted by a
non-corporeal species
who traveled from the
Gamma Quadrant
through the
Bajoran wormhole
and had taken a position in the
plasma fields
near the
Denorios belt
, where they read as elevated
thoron emissions
in
2369
.
That year, this Bokai persona was taken from the
imagination
of
Jake Sisko
, whom he followed home from the
holosuite
, after he had helped Jake work on his fielding techniques.
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
then brought Bokai to the
operations center
and introduced him to the
senior staff
. When they tried to explain his existence, he simply said, "
Don't look at me. I can't figure it out either.
" After recalling the events that lead him to the Sisko's quarters, he explained he and Jake were "getting
hungry
". This surprised
Kira Nerys
, who had never heard of "
a
hologram
with an
appetite
.
" Bokai, had no idea what she meant by "hologram", but after a series of
tricorder
scans
,
Julian Bashir
assured Kira that "he's quite real."
As the alien continued to feign believing that he was the real Bokai, the crew began to compare notes, and realized that the three individuals they were interacting with, Bokai,
Rumpelstiltskin
, and
Jadzia Dax
, were all products of someone's imagination. Bokai continued to pursue Sisko and discuss the topic of
baseball
only to be a bit discouraged by Sisko's desire for him to "disappear for good."
The three aliens later converged in the
replimat
to discuss their findings, only to be confused by the irony of their results. Bokai, however, disagreed with the other two's desire to "
abandon the whole thing,
" explaining that he believed he made a connection with Sisko, as he sensed a curious feeling regarding Sisko's affection for Bokai.
Following the crew's realization that their own imaginations were responsible for the events that transpired on the station, the crew was finally able to return things back to normal. After returning to his
office
, Bokai revealed himself a final time to justify their actions, firstly explaining that "
this imagination of yours, it's a tough concept for us to get a handle on,
" before explaining that "
we're on an
extended mission
exploring
the galaxy
. Awhile back, we followed one of your ships through that "hole in space" out there. We've been watching you, trying to figure out the rules of your game.
" When asked why they didn't just make direct contact like he now was, Bokai explained that, "
we've learned you never can tell how someone will treat the visiting team,
"  and that "
that's what this has been all about. Learning to understand.
"
At the conclusion of their conversation, Bokai explained that they had to go, but when Sisko protested that "
you haven't told us anything about your species,
" Buck simply replied, "
I'd like to. Maybe next year.
" (
DS9
: "
If Wishes Were Horses
")
This alien portrayal of Bokai was played by
Keone Young
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Buckley
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Buckley
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Deceased
(
2259
)
Died:
2259
,
Valeo Beta V
Played by
:
Carlos Albornoz
"
Buckley
" was the
nickname
given by
Oriana
to an
alien
of unknown
species
who lived in the
23rd century
. Their spoken
language
was not recognized by the
universal translator
.
A
refugee
from a
Gorn
breeding planet
, in
2259
Buckley was rescued, alongside Oriana and
Pasko
, from a
class M
planet
by the
USS
Peregrine
. After the
Peregrine
was forced down to the surface of
Valeo Beta V
by Gorn that hatched from Pasko, Buckley remained aboard to protect Oriana while the remaining
Starfleet
crew lured the
hatchlings
outside the ship.
Buckley later confronted a
landing party
from the
USS
Enterprise
sent to investigate the crash site. Unbeknownst to all, Buckley had been infected by Gorn
eggs
as well, which
Doctor
Joseph M'Benga
and
Nurse
Christine Chapel
had no way of detecting because they were unfamiliar with Buckley's
physiology
and the eggs were invisible to
sensors
.
Lieutenant
La'An Noonien-Singh
– the landing party member most familiar with the Gorn – was elsewhere when Buckley began to display visible symptoms, and Buckley was unable to communicate directly due to the language barrier. The violent emergence of the hatchlings from his body blindsided the
Enterprise
personnel, and he died seconds before one of them killed cadet
Chia
. (
SNW
: "
All Those Who Wander
")
Buckley was played by
Carlos Albornoz
, who wore a remote-controlled animatronic head to realize the character.
(
TRR
: "
All Those Who Wander
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Budrow
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Budrow
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer, Commanding officer of
Starbase 29
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Admiral
Budrow
was the
commander
of
Starbase 29
in
2369
.
When
Will Riker
was captured by a warring faction on
Tilonus IV
, he was told in a
delusion
created by
Syrus
that the
Tilonians
had contacted Budrow on Starbase 29 and that he had told them he knew nothing about a
Starfleet
officer
named William T. Riker. (
TNG
: "
Frame of Mind
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Burnett
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Burnett
======================================================================

Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Active
(
Stardate 41039
)
Burnett
, along with
Lay
, was a
scientist
who expressed the relationship between the negation of internal quantum numbers and spacetime inversion as "applying quantum vertex transformation to antiquark phenomena".
As well, in the works of Burnet and
Meyer
, described "consistency with Sweeny field equations requiring Gabl representation to be replaced with Bruno matrices".
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

PAGE: C. Alapag

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
CIC
officer
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
C. Alapag
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. This individual had served under
Leyton
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Combat Information Center
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49260. The officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cabot
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cabot
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Ensign
Cabot
was an
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in the
quantum mechanics
department
.
In
2369
,
Nella Daren
and Cabot had a conversation in which she offered him a transfer to the
stellar sciences department
, her section. She approached
Commander
William T. Riker
about the move, but was told that quantum mechanics didn't want to give Cabot up. When she pressed Riker, saying Cabot desired the move, he asked her how she knew that, and she revealed that she'd made him the offer.
He then berated Daren for having done so, as transfers were supposed to be approved by him before offers could be extended. She apologized, saying they had just been conversing (when the subject came up), and he accepted her apology, but told her that Cabot would remain where he was. When she told Riker that Cabot would be perfect for the job and reminded him that quantum mechanics was overstaffed, he finally agreed to look into the matter.
Daren later discussed the incident with
Jean-Luc Picard
, who had been approached by Riker after this latest request of hers, and assured him that she was trying to improve her department,
not asking for a favor because of her
romantic
relationship with the
captain
. (
TNG
: "
Lessons
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Callahan

crewmember

, over the

======================================================================
PAGE: Callimas
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Callimas
======================================================================

Callimas
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Callimas at
Bahar
".
In
2368
, after an outburst at
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, Captain
Dathon
spoke this phrase, implying his intention to make up for his outburst. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Callimas was pronounced as "kah-lee-MAHS".

Starship

Status:

(
2365
)
Cari L. Thomas
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2353
, she was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. Her office was located in room #45244. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2364
, she was the
commanding officer
of the
USS
Puget Sound
. In that year, the ship was on a survey mission of
Sector 450/32450
. During the mission, she received an order that modified the survey mission from an
admiral
at
Starfleet Mission Operations
. The captain was requested to send acknowledgement of her receipt of the order through
subspace com net
security
mode 045, code 100 alpha. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
In 2365, she was listed as the commanding officer of the
USS
Farouk El-Baz
on a
Starship Deploy Status
chart in the
Starbase 173
courtroom
. At the time, this ship was patrolling the
Neutral Zone
. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
",
okudagram
)
In
2368
, she was listed as an
Engineering Group
officer on the second version of the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after assistant scenic artist
Cari Thomas
.
External link
Cari Thomas
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Carl
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Carl
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Status:
Active
(
3189
)
Played by
:
Bartell La Rue
James Doohan
Paul Guilfoyle
(as "Carl")
"
Are you
machine
or
being
?"
"I am both... and neither. I am my own beginning, my own ending.
"
–
James T. Kirk
questioning the
Guardian of Forever
,
2267
("
The City on the Edge of Forever
")
The
Guardian of Forever
was a mysterious construct of an unknown, ancient alien race, which functioned as a
time portal
: a gateway to the
time vortex
that allowed access to other
times
, locations and
dimensions
.
It was located on an
ancient planet
where the focus of all
timelines
throughout at least the
Milky Way Galaxy
converged. It was positioned among the eventual ruins of a
large, forgotten city
that stretched beyond the
horizon
in all directions around it. Based on initial observations, the ruins appeared to be at least
one million years old
. The Guardian said that it was far older than that, even exceeding the
Earth
's
sun
's age of 4.6 billion years. (
TOS
: "
The City on the Edge of Forever
")
After the factions of the
Temporal Wars
attempted to use it for their own purposes, the Guardian of Forever went into hiding far removed from its original planet. It was eventually located by the
Sphere
data on
Dannus V
where, taking on the form of a man named Carl, it tested
Philippa Georgiou
before it helped her to survive her deteriorating condition from being displaced from her own time and the
mirror universe
. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
", "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
Quick Answers
What is the purpose of the Guardian of Forever?
The Guardian of Forever, an asymmetrical circular gateway, functions as a time portal. It's made of a quasi-metallic substance that exhibits the progression of time and creates significant time ripples. Located on a planet in the Alpha or Beta Quadrant, amidst a vast ruined city, it can transport individuals to survivable times, as demonstrated with Philippa Georgiou's cure.
Provided by: Fandom
Who created the Guardian of Forever and why?
The Guardian of Forever, a sentient time portal, was formed on the time planet before the Sol solar system's inception. Its origin and purpose remain unknown. It manifests as an inert, circular gateway, composed of a quasi-metallic substance, capable of displaying the flow of time.
Provided by: Fandom
What is the significance of the location of the Guardian of Forever?
The Guardian of Forever, an ancient construct of an unknown alien race, resides on a planet in the Alpha or Beta Quadrant. This 'gateway guardian' functions as a time portal, situated amidst the ruins of an ancient city. After the Temporal Wars, it relocated and was found on Dannus V.
Provided by: Fandom
How does the Guardian of Forever function as a time portal?
The Guardian of Forever serves as a sentient time portal, enabling travel across different times and dimensions. It provides access to the time vortex, the intersection of all timelines, allowing observation and travel through various points in time and space. Physically, the Guardian is an inert, circular gateway made of a quasi-metallic substance that resists sensor analysis and displays the flow of time.
Provided by: Fandom
What is the connection between the Guardian of Forever and the ancient city it is located in?
The Guardian of Forever, an entity older than Earth's sun, resides in the ruins of an ancient city on its home planet. This city, stretching beyond the horizon, is at least a million years old. The Guardian can project historical events, as demonstrated with Earth's history. Following the Temporal Wars, it hid and was later discovered on Dannus V.
Provided by: Fandom
Design
The Guardian of Forever appeared physically as an inert, crudely asymmetrical, circular gateway--a rough-hewn torus--comprised of a quasi-metallic substance that defied sensor analysis and was able to display the flow of time itself, even though it could not alter the "speed at which it displayed the past."
It generated immense ripples in time that manifested themselves as spatial disturbances in the region around the planet where it was located. Apparently
sentient
, it was capable of
speech
and interacted with anyone it encountered, responding to external stimuli such as
questions
and actions.
Spock
was unconvinced that the Guardian could be a machine as Federation technology understood mechanics, and it, itself, when
James Kirk
asked it directly whether it was a machine or a being, called itself "both... and neither," implying that it was neither quite a machine as Federation technology understood mechanics nor quite a being as Federation biology understood life, and yet
both
a machine as Federation technology did
not
understand mechanics
and
a being as Federation biology did
not
understand
life
. The object would flash whenever it spoke and emitted a gaseous fog within its center whenever it generated a portal. (
TOS
: "
The City on the Edge of Forever
")
In the
32nd century
, the Guardian assumed Human form as a man named Carl to test subjects that wished to use the Guardian's portal. The Guardian showed the ability to manifest as both its original portal form and Carl simultaneously, and also to take on the form of a simple wooden door. When
Philippa Georgiou
passed through the Guardian's portal into an
alternate timeline
of the
mirror universe
, her physical form appeared to pass out for a minute rather than vanishing. However, Georgiou's
bio-scanner
still showed data points from the three months that she had spent in the alternate timeline before the Guardian pulled her back. This showed that the Guardian of Forever was capable of transporting subjects across both time and universes if it so wished. When the Guardian agreed to help Georgiou, as she did not wish to return to the mirror universe, it was able to set the portal for a time period when the prime and mirror universes were still aligned which would be a place that Georgiou could survive in. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
", "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
History
Contact with the Federation
Captain Kirk, Spock, and
Uhura
view images of the
American Revolution
through the Guardian
The Guardian was first discovered by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
in
2267
. Encountering powerful waves of space displacement, which
Spock
described as "ripples in time", the
Enterprise
tracked the waves back to their point of origin on a previously-uncharted planet.
Doctor
Leonard McCoy
, suffering from paranoid delusions as a result of an accidental overdose of
cordrazine
,
beamed
down to the surface in an attempt to escape the ship.
Searching for McCoy,
Captain
James T. Kirk
and Spock encountered and made contact with the Guardian, who offered them the chance to explore the past. As the Guardian was displaying images from Earth history, McCoy emerged from hiding and leapt through the time portal, arriving on Earth in the year
1930
.
The
landing party
soon discovered that they had lost all contact with the
Enterprise
, and the Guardian informed them that McCoy had effected a change in history, wiping out their civilization. Realizing that they would have to correct the damage to history, Kirk and Spock had the Guardian replay Earth history, and traveled through the portal to a point in time prior to McCoy's arrival. Eventually successful in their effort to restore the timeline, the Guardian returned all three of them to their proper place and time only a moment after they initially departed. (
TOS
: "
The City on the Edge of Forever
")
The Guardian in 2269
In
2269
, a team of
historians
, accompanied by Captain Kirk and Spock, used the Guardian to investigate
Federation
history. Their investigation included firsthand accounts of the formation of the
Orion
civilization and the monitoring of
Vulcan
history of the
2230s
and
2240s
. While Kirk and Spock were visiting Orion, their support team was monitoring Vulcan's past, which, in doing so, inadvertently removed Spock from the proper timeline.
Spock, however, was protected from the change while he was in Orion's past, and the change to the timeline went unnoticed until he and Kirk returned through the Guardian. Once the cause was determined, Spock, using the name
Selek
, was able to use the Guardian to return to his own childhood on Vulcan, and prevent his death during a personal ordeal to determine the course his own life would take before he was scheduled to go through the
kahs-wan
ritual. (
TAS
: "
Yesteryear
")
A model of the Guardian
In
2381
, what appeared to be a
model
of the Guardian of Forever was located behind a
bar
located at
Starbase 25
. Both
Kirk
and
Spock
had previously visited this
bar
sometime during the
2260s
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
That same
year
, there was another model of the Guardian of Forever that was located at
Quark's Federation Experience Bar & Grill
on
Ferenginar
.
It was located near what appeared to be a
taxidermized
Mugatu
. (
LD
: "
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
")
Temporal Wars
During the
Temporal Wars
, many attempted to use the Guardian as a means of
traveling through time
for the
conflict
. Because of this, the Guardian moved itself far away from its original location and went into hiding. By the
32nd century
, it was located on the
planet
Dannus V
. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
", "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
"Carl"
"
I'm, uh... I'm Carl.
"
–
The Guardian of Forever
introducing himself to
Michael Burnham
and
Philippa Georgiou
,
3189
("
Terra Firma, Part 1
")
Carl in 3189
By the
32nd century
, the Guardian appeared in the form of
Carl
, a visual representation in the form of a
Human
male
, who was
guarding
a
trans-dimensional door
on Dannus V.
In
3189
, while searching for a cure for
Philippa Georgiou
's
time/dimensional displacement illness
,
Michael Burnham
used the
Sphere
's
data
in conjunction with Federation
databases
of the 32nd century to locate a potential cure on Dannus V.
When initially encountered, Carl was alone reading a
newspaper
detailing the future
death
of Georgiou. He advised both Georgiou and Burnham that the door was specifically for Georgiou to pass through. He also advised that, while her current condition would stabilize, she could still die on the
other side of the door
. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 1
")
When Georgiou returned from an
alternate timeline
that she had created in the mirror universe, Carl revealed that it was a test to evaluate (to "weigh") how she would affect another universe/timeline. Although Georgiou had failed to save the
Mirror Burnham
, she had tried for peace and saved
Saru
who would go on to save a lot of other people, thus passing Carl's test. Burnham demanded to know who Carl was, and the Guardian, deeming Georgiou and Burnham trustworthy, revealed his true nature as the Guardian of Forever and explained how he had ended up Dannus V. The Guardian opened a portal to a time in which the
prime universe
and the mirror universe were still aligned where Georgiou could survive. Carl encouraged Georgiou to consider herself lucky as she was getting a second chance which was pretty unique. However, Carl warned Georgiou that it wouldn't be easy and will be
"bumpy and painful. Lots of rainstorms, heartaches. But that's life. Or so I'm told."
Georgiou asked if Burnham could come with her, but Carl told the two women that Burnham was right where she was supposed to be and vanished, leaving behind the Guardian's portal. After Georgiou entered it, the Guardian of Forever vanished. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
The Guardian of Forever sent Georgiou to the early
24th century
. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
The Guardian of Forever as part of course material
By
3195
, the existence of The Guardian of Forever was common knowledge in Starfleet, being taught as a subject in the
Starfleet Academy
seminar
Confronting the Unexplainable
. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")

Appendices
See also
The City on the Edge of Forever
,
the original story outline and screenplay which was published by
Harlan Ellison
, who "never forgave [the writing team of
TOS
] for rewriting" his work.
Background information
In the original teleplay for "The City on the Edge of Forever," the Guardians of the Time Vortex were 9-foot (2.7 meter) tall, humanoid statue-like beings.
The original Guardian was designed by
Desilu
Supervising Art Director
Rolland M. Brooks
, because his colleague, regular Set Designer
Matt Jefferies
, was sick with the flu that week. (
Star Trek: The Animated Series
DVDs
special feature ""
Yesteryear
"
text commentary
") When Jefferies returned to his duties and saw the lopsided donut-shaped set piece for the first time, he reportedly exclaimed, "
What the hell is this?!
" according to Story Editor
D.C. Fontana
. (
These Are the Voyages: TOS Season One
, 1st ed, p. 517) Special Effects Artist
Jim Rugg
was responsible for the light effects for the Guardian. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 17, No. 2, p. 24)
The character voice of the Guardian was provided by
Bartell La Rue
in "The City on the Edge of Forever".
Scene from the RKO film
The Spanish Main
, lifted for use in the
Star Trek
episode
The footage seen in "The City on the Edge of Forever" through the time portal is, for the most part, lifted from old
Paramount Pictures
and
RKO Pictures
films, such as the 1945 swashbuckling film
The Spanish Main
in the latter case. It should be noted though, that Desilu had not yet been acquired by Paramount's holding company
Gulf+Western
at the time, and that, while much of RKO's assets were acquired by Desilu Studios after the former went defunct in 1957, its backlog catalog of film productions was not part of it, it being divided over several other major Hollywood studios. The use by Desilu of these clippings in the episode therefore, regardless of their origins, essentially constituted copyright infringements in current understanding.
The Guardian's last words in "The City on the Edge of Forever" were: "
Many such journeys are possible. Let me be your gateway.
" This suggested that further stories could have been written involving the Guardian of Forever appearing in
The Original Series,
but the Guardian's only other
canonical
appearances were in the
animated
episode "
Yesteryear
", and, decades later, the
Star Trek: Discovery
episodes "
Terra Firma, Part 1
" and "
Part 2
". In "Yesteryear," the Guardian's voice was performed by
James Doohan
; in the
Discovery
episodes, a Human-appearing personification of the Guardian, "Carl", was played by
Paul Guilfoyle
, with archive footage of Bartell LaRue's voice used when Carl's true identity was revealed.
An original draft of the episode that eventually became
TNG
: "
Yesterday's Enterprise
" featured a Vulcan science team researching history through the Guardian of Forever. In that story, the team accidentally caused the death of
Surak
, the father of modern Vulcan philosophy – as a result, the
Time of Awakening
never occurred, and the Vulcan race had essentially
evolved
as the
Romulans
. The timeline was reset when
Sarek
, aboard the
Enterprise
-D to greet the scientists, used the Guardian to go back in time and take Surak's place in history. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 117)
Using the Guardian on
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
was discussed at one point but decided against. (
AOL chat
,
1997
) However, one of the
Wadi
chula
game pieces from "
Move Along Home
" was modeled after the Guardian. (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 2, Issue 5
, p. 112)
During production on the
film
Star Trek
, it was briefly rumored that the Guardian would be used by the Romulans to go back in time.
[1]
Apocrypha
In
The Worlds Of The Federation
,
as in
Alan Dean Foster
's prose conversion of "Yesteryear", the Guardian's planet is designated as "The Time Planet." In
The Worlds Of The Federation
, moreover, instead of its coordinates being provided, it is listed as "Stellar Location CLASSIFIED". and it is described as being quarantined against visitations under "General Prohibition P-119", presumably in consequence of the events in "Yesteryear".
The Guardian appears in the game
Star Trek Online
in a mission involving the players character breaking the quarantine of the Guardian's planet. The player must travel through the portal in order to follow a group of Klingons and stop them from destroying the Enterprise from the Original series and rescue
Miral Paris
, who they kidnapped to create and distribute a cure to the
Klingon augment virus
. Also, if the player questions the Guardian about why it speaks in riddles, it gives the same response that it did to Spock: that it answers as simply as their limited understanding allows.
In the novel
Yesterday's Son
, Kirk and Spock use the Guardian to rescue Spock's son Zar from the ancient, doomed world of
Sarpeidon
. They also discover that Zar is able to communicate telepathically with the Guardian. In the novel's sequel,
Time for Yesterday
, the Guardian is summoned away from its duties of regulating time in the Milky Way by its capricious Creators. Zar uses his telepathic powers to return the consciousness of the Guardian to the gateway and banish the Creators to a universe where they cannot harm our space-time.
In the novel
Engines of Destiny
, the Guardian appears in an alternate timeline where the
Borg
have conquered Earth; having learnt of the change from her 'prime' counterpart, an alternate version of
Guinan
visits the Guardian's planet to ask for help, the Guardian revealing how the timeline can be restored to normal.
In the novel
The Devil's Heart
, the titular object, an ancient stone rumored to have vast powers, is revealed to be a "seed" created by the same civilization who created the Guardian and meant to create another Guardian on the world it was sent to, before it went astray.
In the novel
First Frontier
, members of the Clan Ru, a species whose pre-sentient ancestors - Earth
dinosaurs
- had been rescued by the
Preservers
before the
mass extinction
, used the Guardian to destroy the asteroid which would hit Earth, allowing their species to remain and evolve there. The timeline would be reset by Kirk, with the assent of the Clan Ru, when they discovered that in this altered history, their species destroyed itself in a series of nuclear wars before it could reach the stars.
In the
Star Trek: The Next Generation - Q Continuum
series, during a flashback to several thousand years ago, the Guardian is used by a younger
Q
when attempting to find something new, allowing him to make contact with the being known as
0
(although the Guardian briefly tries to deny 0 access to this universe), who subsequently contacts
(*)
,
Gorgan
, and
The One
via the Guardian. It is hinted here that the Guardian was built by the race that would eventually evolve into the Q - when looking at the Guardian, the young Q comments "
At least our ancestors
made
things.
" This reflects his dejection at the stagnant nature of the Q Continuum.
In the alternate future seen in the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
book trilogy
Millennium
, the Guardian of Forever was key to Admiral
Kathryn Janeway
's Project Forever. Janeway, along with a combined Federation/Borg armada, hoped to use the Guardian to go back in time and wipe out
Bajor
(the Federation was at war with the Bajoran Ascendancy in this timeline and were facing the end of the universe). However, at the very moment that Janeway and a team arrived at the Guardian, the Grigari set off a singularity bomb, creating a
black hole
that destroyed the entire Federation/Borg fleet, the Grigari fleet, Janeway, and the Guardian. The timeline was later reset by
Benjamin Sisko
and the crew of
Deep Space 9
.
In the alternate future presented in
Imzadi
, set in 2406, Admiral
Riker
used the Guardian to travel back in time to save
Deanna Troi
's life from an attempt to kill her and prevent her participation in a conference in 2366. In the timeline where she died, the species involved went on to secretly rebuild and become a major military power, but with her survival, Troi's empathic powers revealed that they were lying to gain time, and the conference was abandoned until the species was in desperate need of assistance and were subsequently permitted to rebuild under strict observation.
Although the future
Data
also attempts to travel back in time to maintain continuity (Riker decides to travel back after a new autopsy reveals that Troi was killed by a poison that was only developed after her death, suggesting that she was killed by a time-traveler, but Data feels that Riker is clutching at straws as the poison in her system could have been a natural fluke), it is revealed at the conclusion of the novel that history
had
already been changed, and Riker's actions actually set it back on the right path, with the travelers incredulous that the Guardian never told them that detail simply because they never asked.
In
Spock Vs. Q
, Spock mentions having used the Guardian to travel back to the 20th century. Q describes it as "that lopsided
donut
thing" before Spock corrects him and asks him if he knows it. Q responds, "
Been there, done that, got the T-shirt.
"
In the novel
Provenance of Shadows
, the
Enterprise
returns to the Guardian for a third time, just before the end of its original five-year mission. They respond to a distress call from the science station erected in orbit of the Guardian's planet. Three
Klingon
ships attack the station and the
Enterprise
. They are successful in boarding the
Enterprise
's bridge and manage to take over the ship, forcing the crew to abandon ship, but Captain Kirk manages to escape, obtains several phasers, and transports down to the planet. There, he steps through the Guardian and ends up back on the
Enterprise
's bridge moments before the Klingons boarded. As the crew evacuates the bridge, Kirk sets the phasers on overload and sacrifices himself to kill the Klingons, but his now-alternate timeline counterpart survives. As a last resort, the Klingons crash their ship into the Guardian, apparently destroying it.
The image of the sailing ship firing its cannons seen in the Guardian's portal is also later seen in the intro to the
mirror universe
episodes of
Star Trek: Enterprise
.
A 1978 story in an issue of the
Gold Key
Star Trek
comic, titled "
No Time Like the Past
", features the Guardian.
In the short story "Guardians" from the anthology book
Strange New Worlds VII
, the
Horta
are assigned by the Federation to serve as the protectors of the Guardian after a failed attack/attempt to alter the timeline by the
Romulans
. Since their lifespan extends for the next forty thousand years, they are witnesses to vast changes in the Federation, the ascendancy of Humanity to incorporeal beings and the eventual exploration of the known universe and beyond. The Guardian undergoes periodic attacks and attempted usage from the
Borg
, the Romulans, and the
Crystalline Entity
, each time defeated by the Horta.
In the short story "Orphans" from the anthology book
Strange New Worlds 9
, the
Dominion
attempted to seize the Guardian in
2374
during the
Dominion War
, but the
Jem'Hadar
were unable to activate it as it did not recognize their altered
DNA
.
Section 31
sent
Erik Pressman
and
Julian Bashir
to
Angosia III
to recruit super-soldiers such as
Roga Danar
for a mission to aid them in recapturing the Guardian's planet. After successfully completing the mission, Danar asked the Guardian what it desired. When it said it wanted freedom, Danar granted it permission to leave the planet, which it then did. The story also states that the
Andorians
had their own name for the Guardian: the
T'Sh'Iar
, which meant "God's Window".
The Guardian is also featured in the novel
The Star to Every Wandering
. in which it plays a sizeable role. In this novel, the events of the film
Star Trek Generations
play out differently, with Kirk's emergence from the
Nexus
resulting in a temporal loop with catastrophic effects. This Kirk, returned to the Nexus by these events, then recruits another version of himself from within the Nexus, who then rescues yet another Kirk from the
USS
Enterprise
-B
by employing the Guardian of Forever to travel through time. This third Kirk then travels to
Veridian III
and dies helping Picard save the
Veridian system
while the second Kirk ends up contemplating using the Guardian to travel back in time and save
Edith Keeler
's life without damaging history.

at
StarTrek.com
Guardian of Forever
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Cadet
Status:
Active
(
3196
)
Carl
was a
cadet
at
Starfleet Academy
in the late
32nd century
.
In
3196
,
Tarima Sadal
told
Caleb Mir
that she had just shared an awkward
turbolift
ride with Carl from
Biochemistry
. Mir replied that he was going to guess
Glork
from
Botany
. (
SA
: "
300th Night
")

Carl Banks
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Planet Q
to
Tarsas IV
. His special accommodation was a transfer via
Astral Queen
.
His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named for
stand-in
Debbie David
, formerly known by that name.

but not by

Doctor
Channing
was a
Federation
warp
physicist
. This
scientist
proposed
a
theory
that
suggested
it was
possible
to
force
dilithium
into even more useful
crystals
, which in turn could lead to better control over
matter
/
antimatter
reactions.
Wesley Crusher
was studying this theory in his
physical sciences
class in
2364
. (
TNG
: "
Lonely Among Us
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Chapman
was a
24th century
Starfleet
flag officer
.
In
2374
, while attempting to decode the jumbled data transmission from
Earth
,
Kathryn Janeway
joked
that the data clusters could simply be Chapman's
recipe
for the perfect
pound cake
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
It is unclear if Admiral Chapman is the same individual as
Professor
Chapman
, both of whom are individuals known by the members of
USS
Voyager
's crew.
In an early draft of the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
episode "
Phantasms
", Admiral
Nakamura
was named Admiral Chapman.
Apocrypha
Chapman in the
2380s
In the
first issue
of
IDW Publishing
's
Star Trek: Lower Decks
miniseries, Chapman contacted Captain
Carol Freeman
about the
USS
Cerritos
' run in with a
Catullan
renegade.

Chapman (Admiral)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Chapman (Professor)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Chapman_(Professor)
======================================================================

"
I fought with him almost every single day. I was always questioning his... his methods, his assumptions, and he was always slapping me down like some upstart kid. I was surprised he didn't help me pack my bags."
"Some professors like students who challenge their assumptions, B'Elanna.
"
–
B'Elanna Torres
and
Kathryn Janeway
,
2371
("
Parallax
")
Professor
Chapman
was an
instructor
at
Starfleet Academy
during the
2360s
.
One of his
students
was
B'Elanna Torres
. Though Chapman and Torres fought constantly, he thought she was one of the finest students he ever taught and was sorry when she dropped out of the Academy. He placed a note in her
permanent file
saying he would support her re-entry if she ever re-applied. (
VOY
: "
Parallax
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Since no Starfleet rank was given, this could be the same person as
Admiral
Chapman
.
The
Starfleet Academy Handbook
gives him the first name Paul.

PAGE: Charles Young
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Charles_Young
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Serial number:
TP 500 5388
Status:
Deceased
(
2370s
)
Died:
2370s
Charles Young
was a
Starfleet
officer
assigned to the
starship
USS
Voyager
, an
ensign
who was
killed
in the line of duty sometime before
2377
. Young's name was read out on a
casualty report
of all who died during that
starship
's journey through the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Imperfection
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This name was devised for the list; it was not mentioned as a casualty in any other episode. It is possible this name refers to one of the officers killed en route when
Voyager
was dragged to the Delta Quadrant.
As with many names on the "Imperfection" list, Charlie Young is an
in-joke
referring to the character on
The West Wing
.

language

phrase, "

. The court of silence.

In
2368
, after
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
had been transported from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to the surface of
El-Adrel IV
,
Commander
William T. Riker
contacted a Tamarian
deep space cruiser
. He demanded from the
first officer
the return of the
Enterprise
-D captain and that holding him might be considered an act of war. To this, the Tamarian
officer
uttered this phrase in anger. At an impasse, Riker ordered the channel closed. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Cing'ta
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cing'ta

Cing'ta
was a member of the
Maquis
, who also worked as an
informant
for
Starfleet
.
In
2373
, he was supposed to meet with
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
on
Marva IV
with information he obtained on
Michael Eddington
. Prior to the meeting, the Maquis learned that he was an informant, and they marooned him on an
inhospitable
planet
in the
Badlands
. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
His species is never revealed. However, he may have been
Bolian
, as his name shares naming conventions with other Bolian characters such as
Boq'ta
.

PAGE: Claudia Craig
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Claudia_Craig
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Serial number:
SJ 583 2619
Status:
Deceased
(
2370s
)
Died:
2370s
Ensign
Claudia J. Craig
was a
Starfleet
officer assigned to the
starship
USS
Voyager
.
Craig was killed in the line of duty sometime before
2377
. Her name was visible on a
casualty report
of all who died during that vessel's journey through the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Imperfection
")

Voyager
was dragged to the Delta Quadrant.
As with many names on the "Imperfection" list, C.J. Craig was an in-joke (albeit misspelled) referring to the character on
The West Wing
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Clem
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Clem
======================================================================

Species:
Anaphasic lifeform
Status:
Active
(
2382
)
Played by
:
Jerry O'Connell
Clem
was an
anaphasic lifeform
.
In a bid to show
its superiors
that he was capable of communicating with
corporeal beings
, in
2382
Clem possessed
Lieutenant
Matt
whilst the latter was on an
away mission
to the
oceans
of
Piskes IX
. Once Matt returned to the
USS
Cerritos
, Clem's presence caused the
navigation processor
to malfunction, forcing the ship to divert to
Starbase 80
for a replacement.
While the
Cerritos
was at the
starbase
, Clem began possessing additional crew members through the
combadge
network; starbase personnel remained unaffected as they still used old
wall comms
. However, Clem quickly became overwhelmed by merging with so many minds and lost control. Those it inhabited became cognitively debilitated, leaving them in a
zombie
-like state with a compulsion to lick nearby surfaces.
Commander
Kassia Nox
,
Lieutenant jg
Beckett Mariner
, and
Doctor
Harrison Horseberry
eventually traced the source of the affliction to
Cetacean Ops
. After Clem seized Nox and Mariner, Horseberry made Matt
vomit
it up by hitting him in the head with a
pool skimmer
. He then covered Clem with a
bucket
, ending its control over the
Cerritos
crew and incidentally also restoring the navigation processor to normal function.
Afterwards, Horseberry constructed a
stable vessel
in which Clem could reside. Now able to speak directly to people, Clem apologized for its actions, having never intended any harm. Nox welcomed Clem to stay on Starbase 80 as long as it promised not to possess anyone again. Clem accepted, hoping also its
superiors
would be satisfied by the outcome, as "
they [could] be real
dicks
.
" (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")
Clem was voiced by
Jerry O'Connell
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cleveland Booker (namesake)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cleveland_Booker_(namesake)
======================================================================

Species:
Human
Occupation:
Courier
Status:
Deceased
(
3188
)
Died:
Before
3188
Other relative(s):
L'ak
(son-
in-law
)
Children:
Moll
Cleveland Booker IV
was an alias used by a
Human
courier
who served as
mentor
to
Tareckx
in the
32nd century
.
This Cleveland Booker was the fourth courier to operate under that
alias
. He (and the Cleveland Bookers who preceded him) developed a reputation for trustworthiness. (
DIS
: "
Species Ten-C
")
He had a daughter, Malinne, who later became a courier herself and changed her name to
Moll
. (
DIS
: "
Under the Twin Moons
")
When this Cleveland Booker
retired
, Tareckx adopted his name as an inspiration and motivation for himself to live up to it. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
", "
Species Ten-C
")
He died sometime before
3188
. (
DIS
: "
Mirrors
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.. Given his daughter's
surname
of Ravel, it is likely his true surname was Ravel, as well.

======================================================================
PAGE: Coffin (engineer)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Coffin_(engineer)
======================================================================

For the container, please see
coffin
.
Coffin
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2367
, he was a
crew
member assigned to the
Engineering
Department of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. When it was discovered that a preburner was producing a higher
deuterium
flow,
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
consulted with Coffin and
Ensign
Stepan
. Coffin suggested that this situation might be related to a
pastore pulse
overpressure, while Stepan thought it was an issue of thermal conductivity. The chief engineer agreed with Coffin. (
TNG-R
: "
Galaxy's Child
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered edition of "Galaxy's Child", the original
Okudagram
was replaced by a new graphic. Mr. Coffin was mentioned in that graphic.

PAGE: Colt

was a
female
Olatunde

during the mid-

: "

", "

")

2250s
she was assigned to the
sciences division
aboard the

under

.

Appearances
DIS
:
"
Such Sweet Sorrow
"
"
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
"
Background information

Nicole Dickinson
.

Her name and rank were identified in the episode's end credits, as an apparent homage to
J.M. Colt
, Captain Pike's Human yeoman in "
The Cage
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Companion
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Companion
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Non-corporeal
(pre-merge)
Human
(post-merge)
Status:
Active
(
2267
)
Played by
:
Lisabeth Hush
(voice)
Elinor Donahue
For the reference books, please see
Companions
.
The
Companion
was a
non-corporeal
lifeform
native to the
planetoid
Gamma Canaris N
in the
Gamma Canaris region
. Composed chiefly of
ionized
hydrogen
and
electricity
, it appeared as a
gaseous
, vaguely sparkling
cloud
of yellow, orange, and red.
Encountered first by
Zefram Cochrane
, who gave it the name Companion, shortly after
2119
, it discovered him, old and dying, and brought him to its home,
rejuvenating
him and sustaining him for the next 150 years. The next
Humans
it encountered were a party of four: three
USS
Enterprise
officers and a
Federation
commissioner
,
Nancy Hedford
, in
2267
. Responding to Cochrane's loneliness, it drew their
shuttlecraft
to its home planetoid so that they might be companions for Cochrane. This was a problem for Hedford, who was then suffering from
Sakuro's Disease
.
Zefram Cochrane and the Companion
Zefram Cochrane could communicate with the Companion, in a limited and non-verbal way. According to his account, he "just sort of cleared" his mind, and it came. Despite the non-verbal nature of the communication, Cochrane claimed he was usually able to get his idea across. The Companion was able to answer at least simple questions in the same fashion – when Cochrane asked whether it could cure Nancy Hedford's illness, it was able to tell him that it could not.
Kirk
tried a number of strategems in an attempt to regain his freedom, without much success. He first tried to disable it with an
electronic scrambler
. This device evidently caused the entity some discomfort or pain, as it reacted immediately, attacking and destroying the device, and nearly killing Kirk and
Spock
. (During this attack, the Companion changed from a soft golden color to an angry reddish hue, suggestion that its external appearance might offer some clue to its emotional state.)
Kirk next instructed
Spock
to modify the
universal translator
so that communication might be possible. Although it could now understand the Humans, attempts to persuade it to release them met with resistance. It wanted Cochrane to remain alive, and it believed he needed other Humans for that.
Finally, after realizing that it was in love with Cochrane, Kirk played on its inability to ever really experience true love with Cochrane, because they would always be too different. To overcome this, the Companion merged with Nancy Hedford, who was moments from death, creating a joined entity. In this merged form, it gave up its immortality and its powers, choosing to live out the remainder of a Human lifespan with Cochrane. (
TOS
: "
Metamorphosis
")
The Companion demonstrated a panoply of abilities, including:
Traveling through space at warp speed
Overloading energy producing and using systems
Gross manipulation of objects, perhaps by some process analogous to
telekinesis
Produce material objects, including food and water
Disperse or teleport itself
Attack people and objects with electricity
Rejuvenate humanoid life, and inhibit the aging process
Merging with a humanoid, creating a joined entity. In doing this, the creature sacrificed its immortality and other powers.
The Companion's real name, if it had one, was never revealed.
Appendices
Background information
The Companion was voiced by actress
Lisabeth Hush
, who remained uncredited on-screen despite having dialogue. For nearly a half a century it was incorrectly attributed to
Elizabeth Rogers
.
[1]
The Companion was designed by future
Star Wars
Oscar-winner
Richard Edlund
who created the photographic effects at the
Westheimer Company
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 156) It was a simple animation, placed on live-action footage using an optical matte. The production staff told director
Ralph Senensky
to try avoiding shots which would require the usage of a traveling matte.
[2]

======================================================================
PAGE: Cos
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cos
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Trader
Status:
Deceased
(
2370
)
Died:
2370
,
Deep Space 9
Played by
:
Albert Henderson
For the similarly named Vulcan, please see
Koss
.
Cos
was a
humanoid
trader
who acquired a
gambling device
that altered the
laws of probability
, so that those who won would experience good
luck
, and those who lost would experience bad luck.
In his youth, according to him, he had vigor, fame, and wealth. However, this changed when he acquired the gambling device. His trade deals, alliances, and every other endeavor he made all failed.
In
2370
, he was being held in
Deep Space 9
's
holding cells
, when
Odo
placed
Martus Mazur
in the same cell as Cos. Soon after, Cos finally won a game in the gambling device, and immediately died, a contented man. (
DS9
: "
Rivals
")
Cos was played by
Albert Henderson
.
The name Cos originated from the source's
script
. The script refers to him at different times as "Cos" and "the Cos," making it unclear as to whether Cos is his name or his species.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cosimo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cosimo
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Cosimo
Appearance:
Human
Occupation:
Owner of
a coffee shop
Status:
Exposed
(
2372
)
Identity:
Unknown
Species:
Alien
from a
temporal inversion fold
Played by
:
Louis Giambalvo
Cosimo
was the
Human
identity assumed by
a member of an alien species
from outside the normal
space-time continuum
. Cosimo posed as the owner of
his namesake
coffee shop
in
San Francisco
, from where he interacted with
Ensign
Harry Kim
, who had inadvertently created an
alternate timeline
in
2372
.
Explaining to Harry that history had changed when Harry's
shuttlecraft
intersected one of his species'
timestreams
, he instructed Harry on how to locate the timestream again, informing him that, if he were to fly into it and recreate the same set of circumstances as happened during the initial accident, he might be able to repair the damage to history, but counseled Harry that he could not guarantee that he wouldn't make things worse by doing so. Deciding that he needed to try, Harry used a stolen
runabout
and, with the help of the alternate timeline-counterpart of
Tom Paris
, was able to recreate the accident and undo the alterations to the timeline. (
VOY
: "
Non Sequitur
")
Cosimo was played by
Louis Giambalvo
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Crosis
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Crosis
======================================================================

Species:
Borg
Affiliation:
Lore
's rogue Borg faction
Formerly
Borg Collective
Status:
Deceased
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Brian Cousins
Crosis
was one of the rogue
Borg drones
loyal to
Lore
, acting as the
android
's second-in-command.
In
2369
, Crosis
beamed
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
from
his vessel
and allowed himself to be captured so he could convince
Data
to join the group under the leadership of Lore. Previously, Data had experienced
emotions
after killing a Borg drone. Unknown to him at the time the emotion was not his own, but had been fed to him by the Borg, who were linked to Lore and the
emotion chip
he had previously stolen from Data. Crosis used the memory of the fleeting emotion to convince Data to follow him and defect to the rogue
Borg Collective
.
During an attempt to rescue
Geordi La Forge
and
Jean-Luc Picard
from Lore, a fight broke out among the rogue Borg. Crosis appeared to be about to triumph in a struggle with the leader of a rival faction,
Hugh
, when
William T. Riker
and
Worf
arrived on the scene and Crosis was shot and killed. (
TNG
: "
Descent
", "
Descent, Part II
")
Crosis was played by actor
Brian J. Cousins
.
The
Descent
first draft script calls him Cresis.

male
Starfleet

in the

-D
under

Jean-Luc Picard
in the
2360s
.

, D'Amato was serving in

when he became one of several
crewmembers
affected by

's
Bendii Syndrome
. With Sarek projecting his loss of
emotional
control to others, D'Amato challenged the authority of his
commanding officer
,
Lieutenant

, so Worf put him on report for
insubordination
