======================================================================
PAGE: D'Marr
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/D'Marr
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Trader
Status:
Active
(
2151
)
Played by
:
Tom Bergeron
For the Cardassian, please see
Damar
. For the Nyrian, please see
Dammar
.
"
I prefer to deal in more exotic goods.
"
–
D'Marr
,
2151
("
Oasis
")
D'Marr
was an
alien
trader
,
captain
of his own
starship
.
In
2151
, he discovered an apparently abandoned
Kantare vessel
on an alien
planet
. D'Marr believed the ship was
haunted
.
Later that
year
, D'Marr
dined
aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
with Captain
Jonathan Archer
,
Sub-Commander
T'Pol
, and
Commander
Trip Tucker
, during which time he learned Archer was interested in obtaining raw materials to fix
Enterprise
. D'Marr was quite fond of
coffee
, and offered T'Pol some
Triaxian silk
. In exchange for coffee, he gave the
Enterprise
crew
the
coordinates
of
the planet
where he'd found the seemingly deserted
Kantare
vessel. He told them that no one had salvaged the ship because it was haunted. (
ENT
: "
Oasis
")
Appendices
Background information
D'Marr was played by actor
Tom Bergeron
.
The character's
species
and origin were not identified, the final draft script of "Oasis" describing him simply as "an exotic-looking alien trader" while the call sheet listed him simply as "Exotic Alien". According to this call sheet, Bergeron filmed his scenes on Thursday
24 January
2002
on
Paramount Stage 8
with a makeup call at 8:30 a.m. and a set call at 12:30 p.m.
The prospect of Tom Bergeron, a
Trekkie
, appearing in
Star Trek: Enterprise
came about while he was visiting
Paramount Pictures
' studio lot and was being given, by
Enterprise
co-creator and Executive Producer
Rick Berman
, a personal tour of the show's sets with
Whoopi Goldberg
, a friend and colleague of Bergeron's who was meanwhile taking a break from filming
Guinan
scenes in
Star Trek Nemesis
. "
As we were walking through the various sound stages, I made a comment which showed my knowledge of the
Star Trek
universe. It was at that moment I was asked the question I've always dreamed of: Rick asked me if I'd ever considered doing a guest appearance on the show, and he was serious,
" Bergeron recalled. Berman then asked Goldberg if Bergeron could act. Despite never having seen him do so, Goldberg vouched for Bergeron as an amazing actor. He was invited to be a guest star a short while later. "
I made two requests: I wanted to have the full
Star Trek
experience and be transformed into an alien, plus I wanted to be killed at the end of the episode,
" Bergeron explained. (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 139
, p. 38)
The role of D'Marr didn't disappoint Tom Bergeron. "
When I went into the costume fitting,
" he said, "
I learned that I had many lines and was featured in the entire opening scene of the 'Oasis' episode.
" Bergeron's alien makeup took four hours, so he was given an 8 a.m. makeup call. Having watched many behind-the-scenes documentaries about
Star Trek
makeup, he knew what to expect. Following a dress rehearsal, Bergeron started filming the footage of D'Marr that day, which went smoothly. "
In the afternoon, however, I started getting what I call 'lingo lock' and began having trouble reciting all of the technical lingo that was required of my character,
" he admitted. "
I kept getting alien names and planet names mixed up, or I was mispronouncing them!
" Bergeron's day as a guest star came to an end by 5 p.m. He was convinced he would always fondly remember the experience. However, the part he found most gratifying came at the end of that day. Despite being finished for the day, all the other actors had stayed behind to watch the close-ups of D'Marr be filmed. "
When my shots were totally finished, the entire cast and crew give me a round of applause. I was on cloud nine after that,
" he reminisced. "
It was a magnanimous gesture for them to do that.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 139
, p. 38)
In the course of the day, someone mentioned to Tom Bergeron that the name of his character was also the name of
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
recurring character
Damar
, to which Bergeron clarified that the two names are spelt differently. He later joked that D'Marr was the great-great-grandfather of
Dammar
. (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 139
, p. 39)
Since D'Marr does not die in "Oasis", Tom Bergeron hoped to reprise the part later in the series run of
Star Trek: Enterprise
. He even pitched, in person, a story idea to Rick Berman which would have facilitated this happening. "
Whether or not I get to do it again, I am very content to have had the experience [of playing the character],
" he concluded. (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 139
, p. 39)  Ultimately, D'Marr was not seen or mentioned again, though Bergeron did later appear in the episode "
Demons
" as a
Coridanite ambassador
.
External link
D'Marr
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Federation

Occupation:
Engineering Operations
officer
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
D. Santiago
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
that served under
Admiral
Leyton
.
In
2372
, Santiago was assigned to
Engineering Operations
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49263. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. The officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

-A
crewmember

was a
23rd century
Starfleet
commander
.
They were assigned to the
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
. In
2285
, Wise was listed on a directory placard found on the 2nd level of Starfleet Training Command. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
Wise later served in the
planetary science division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-A
in
2293
. Wise's name appeared among the messages pending display in the
quarters
of the
executive officer
,
Captain
Spock
. The message read "Need meeting on experiment schedule." (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This name was seen on an
okudagram
on Spock's desk
computer
. (
The Art of Star Trek
, p. 262) It was an
in-joke
reference to
Star Trek VI
first assistant director
Douglas E. Wise
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Dala
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dala
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Con artist
Status:
Arrested
(
2376
)
Father:
Dala's father
Played by
:
Kaitlin Hopkins
Dala
was an
alien
con artist
of an
alien species
, who
impersonated
Starfleet
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
in
2376
.
Using this deception, she and her partners
Mobar
and
Zar
were able to move from
star system
to star system offering services for different goods from the various
races
, but not delivering what they promised. Since
USS
Voyager
and Janeway were being blamed, Janeway believed that in order to make things right she and the
crew
of
Voyager
had to stop Dala.
It was eventually discovered that Dala and Mobar had been posing as
monks
, when they met
Neelix
and
Tom Paris
while they were on an
away mission
. They downloaded the
database
from the
Delta Flyer
, which provided them with information about
Voyager
and its crew so they could pose as Janeway and
Tuvok
. They then stole ten
kilotons
of
bolomite
from the
Telsian
miners
and committed other
scams
.
After Dala was captured by
Voyager
, she refused to reveal the location of the stolen property. Janeway allowed Dala to escape, and she led them to her partners after she stole the
Flyer
. Having hidden on board the ship,
The Doctor
assumed her identity and tricked them into revealing the location of the stolen property. Dala was handed over to the authorities. (
VOY
: "
Live Fast and Prosper
")
Dala was played by
Kaitlin Hopkins
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Dan Curry (Lieutenant Commander)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dan_Curry_(Lieutenant_Commander)
======================================================================

For other people also named Dan Curry, please see
Dan Curry
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant commander
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
Planetary Geosciences Division
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Born:
Sigma Draconis star system
Dan Curry
was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was in the
Sigma Draconis star system
.
In
2369
, Curry served as a
lieutenant commander
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named for visual effects supervisor
Dan Curry
.

was a notable
humanoid

.

. The program list code for this comedian was

. (

",
okudagram
)

Dan Curry
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

======================================================================
PAGE: Daneeka
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Daneeka
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Facilities
officer
,
Starfleet Command
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Daneeka
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In the
2360s
, this individual served under
Captain
Leyton
aboard the
USS
Okinawa
when
Benjamin Sisko
was the
ship
's
executive officer
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Facilities
,
Starfleet Command
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49264. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
The unauthorized reference book
The Nitpicker's Guide for Deep Space Nine Trekkers
(p. 339) notes that the crew of the
Okinawa
(apart from Sisko) were named after characters in
Joseph Heller
's novel
Catch-22
.
Doc Daneeka
was a character in the novel.
The
Star Trek Customizable Card Game
also gives him the name of Daneeka, however did not mention his background with Leyton, but suggested that Daneeka was an
enlisted technician
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Dante (physicist)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dante_(physicist)
======================================================================

Dante
was a
scientist
.
Due to his work, along with that of
Bishop
and
Barredo
, scientists were able to calculate "n-dimensional negation of internal quantum numbers, normalizing for space time inversion".  This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Angelo Dante
, the DVD projects manager at
CBS Home Entertainment
.

survey mission revisions from an

======================================================================
PAGE: Darson
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Darson
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commander
,
USS
Adelphi
Status:
Deceased
(
24th century
)
Captain
Darson
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
Darson was the
commanding officer
of the
starship
USS
Adelphi
when that ship made
first contact
at
Ghorusda
.
Forty-seven
crewpeople, including the captain,
died
there in what came to be known at the
Ghorusda Disaster
. A
board of inquiry
officially found Darson responsible for carelessness about
Ghorusdan
cultural taboos, though many Starfleet personnel, (including
William T. Riker
, who lost two
academy
classmates in the incident) blamed
Betazoid
contact specialist
Tam Elbrun
for failing to warn Darson of the severity of Ghorusdan hostility.
Elbrun, for his part, later argued that Darson had failed to heed his warning, though he later privately conceded that he may have been overly caught up in the Ghorusdan point of view and therefore may not have warned Darson as forcefully as he could have. (
TNG
: "
Tin Man
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Darson
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

He was listed as a member of
Science Ops
at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: David Amantes
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/David_Amantes
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Starfleet
Rank:
Crewman
Status:
Deceased
(
2375
)
Died:
2375
Crewman
David Amantes
was a
Starfleet
enlisted
crewmember serving aboard the
USS
Equinox
under the command of
Captain
Rudolph Ransom
in
2371
. In that year, the
Equinox
was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
by the alien entity known as the
Caretaker
.
Amantes was killed in
2375
as the result of retaliatory attacks on the
Equinox
crew by a series of
nucleogenic lifeforms
, whom the crew had been
murdering
for the purpose of converting the remains into a source of fuel. After the surviving
Equinox
crewmembers were rescued by the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, Amantes's name was mentioned during a memorial service aboard that ship. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

David Anderson
was a passenger aboard the
SS
Wisconsin
in
2328
. He boarded the
transport ship
at the
Midos V Satellite City Spaceport
and disembarked at

.

commercial transport database
that was reviewed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Anderson was named after regular background actor and
LeVar Burton
's
stand-in
David Anderson
.

Listed in the
Enterprise
-D's database
David Grant
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to located a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Grant was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
RB-43832
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named for
David S. Grant
, Vice President of Multimedia at
CBS Television Distribution
.
For the remastered edition of the episode, this character replaced
Gene Roddenberry
.

during the construction of the

.

",

David Livingston

.

PAGE: David Sisko

======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
David Sisko
was a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the offspring of
Sandy Sisko
,
spouse
of
Dikembe Sisko
, and
parent
to
Pauline
and
Shevon
.
In the
32nd century
, David's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
An additional dotted line off of Dikembe on the tree suggests they had a second spouse.

, he was listed as a
Tactical Ops
officer on the
dedication plaque
of
Defiant
-class
starships
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
(
2370
)
USS
Defiant
(
2372
)
USS
Valiant
(
2375
)
USS
Sao Paulo
In 2372, Takemura was named as one of the
yard engineers
at the
San Francisco Yards
who were listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after visual effects supervisor
David Takemura
.

======================================================================
PAGE: David Takemura (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/David_Takemura_(comedian)
======================================================================

For the physician, please see
Dr. David Takemura
.
For the Starfleet officer, please see
David Takemura
.
Species:
Humanoid
Occupation:
Comedian
David Takemura
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to located a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Takemura was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
NN-42458
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named after visual effects associate
David Takemura
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

======================================================================
PAGE: Davison
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Davison
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Officer
Occupation:
Security officer
Status:
Active
(
2269
)
Played by
:
Nichelle Nichols
(voice)
Davison
was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
. She served in the
operations division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
under
Captain
James T. Kirk
.  In
2269
, Davison served as the ranking female
security officer
.
That year, she was ordered by
Uhura
to place a female security party on every
transporter
, when the men of the
Enterprise
became incapacitated by
hypnotic
suggestions emanating from the
second planet
of the
Taurean system
. (
TAS
: "
The Lorelei Signal
")
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
Nichelle Nichols
provided the voice for Davison.
The character was not seen, but it seems possible that Davison was one of
several unnamed security lieutenants
who transported down with Uhura and
Christine Chapel
's
landing party
to rescue Kirk,
Spock
,
McCoy
and
Carver
.
Despite the fact that Davison is not physically shown in the episode itself, the
novelization
of "The Lorelei Signal" does feature her physical appearance, describing the character as "a middle-aged, efficient-looking woman." Her name is spelled "Davidson" in the novelization.

PAGE: Dawn Velazquez (Voyager)

,

was a

in the
24th century
.
In
2371
, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the

, when

was forcefully brought there by the

. In that year, her name was listed on a

. (

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: Dawn Velazquez (civilian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dawn_Velazquez_(civilian)
======================================================================

Dawn Velazquez
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Wisconsin
ferried her from
New Vegas colony
to
Beta III
,
C-111
system
. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This is very likely the same individual as either
Dawn Velazquez (Voyager)
or
Dawn Velazquez (Lieutenant)
, but it is impossible to determine which one, given the provided information.
This character was named after post-production coordinator
Dawn Velazquez
.

======================================================================
PAGE: DeLong
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/DeLong
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
Engineer
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
Status:
Active
(
2367
)
Lieutenant
DeLong
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2367
, DeLong was assigned to the
Engineering
Department of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. When it became apparent that the entire system of
power transfer conduits
would be back online before the
starship
finished performing scheduled
impulse
maneuvers, DeLong ordered a
Pre-Cervantes calibration
of the
antimatter injector modules
by the
third shift
personnel. This action would help the department with a scheduled
avitable
alignment. (
TNG-R
: "
Galaxy's Child
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered edition of "Galaxy's Child", the original
Okudagram
was replaced by a new graphic. DeLong was mentioned in that graphic.

======================================================================
PAGE: Debra (entity)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Debra_(entity)
======================================================================

Debra inhabiting Hemmer
"
She was lonely. Like me.
"
–
Rukiya
,
2259
("
The Elysian Kingdom
")
Debra
was the name given to an alien
consciousness
that resided in the
Jonisian Nebula
in the
23rd century
, theorized to be a
Boltzmann brain
.
In
2259
, as the
USS
Enterprise
concluded a routine
survey mission
of the Jonisian Nebula, the entity (that would later be called Debra) brought a version of the
fairy tale
The Kingdom of Elysian
to life from the mind of
Doctor
Joseph M'Benga
's daughter
Rukiya
. Rukiya was later offered the opportunity to stay with the entity, and
named
her "Debra" after
her own mother
. (
SNW
: "
The Elysian Kingdom
")
The
individual
Vezda
that replaced Ensign Gamble would later attempt to intimidate Doctor M'Benga by falsely suggesting that Debra also replaced his daughter. (
SNW
: "
Through the Lens of Time
")
Debra was played by
Bruce Horak
when she inhabited
Lieutenant
Hemmer
's body, and was voiced by an
unknown actress
.

PAGE: Decker (Cadet)

crewmember

was a

.

2256
, Decker was ordered to report to the
ready room
of the
USS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Into the Forest I Go
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The article "Star Trek Discovery's Coolest, Deepest-Cut Easter Eggs" by
StarTrek.com
suggests that it is likely that this cadet may actually be
Will Decker
.
[1]

was a

engineer
aboard
Deep Space 9
in
2373
serving under

Benjamin Sisko
.
Around
stardate 50930
, Decker called in sick, forcing
Chief
Miles O'Brien
to have to
recalibrate
all the station's
EPS regulators
so they wouldn't interfere with the station's
artificial gravity grid
. (
DS9
: "
In the Cards
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Dekora Assan

======================================================================

For the Imhotep, please see
Assan
.
Dekora Assan
was an individual on
Japori II
who was
murdered
sometime before
2371
.
The
Flaxian
assassin
Retaya
was a
suspect
in his
death
, but was cleared on the basis of "mistaken identity." (
DS9

")

The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 198 located the murder of Dekora Assan to the year 2371.

Delaplane
was the leader of the
planet
Pacifica
when the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was scheduled to stop there on a scientific mission in
2364
. He contacted
Starfleet
when
Captain
Picard
canceled the visit due to events occurring on

. (

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Della Santina
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Della_Santina
======================================================================

Della Santina
was an associate of the
Bajoran
criminal
Ibudan
.
According to a personal
calendar
file, Ibudan had a
lunch
meeting with Della Santina on a
Bajoran transport
on
stardate
46383
. This file, along with others, was reviewed by
Odo
when he was conducting an investigation into the
murder
of
Ibudan
. (
DS9
: "
A Man Alone
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Della Santina was named after
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
unit production manager
Robert della Santina
. (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 20)

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Denny Sisko
was a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
spouse
of
Troy Sisko
, and
parent
of
Tyrell Sisko
.
In the
32nd century
, Denny's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

PAGE: Dent Arthur Dent

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Sirius Cybernetics Corporation
Occupation:
Employee at SCC
Status:
Active
(
2364
)
For another in-universe character, please see
Arthur Dent
.
Dent Arthur Dent
was an employee at
Sirius Cybernetics Corporation
in the
24th century
.
In
2364
, he sent an order about the
Sector 500/32450
survey to
Captain
Douglas Adams
of the
USS
Heart of Gold
. His name and employer were mentioned in the order's heading. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Like other elements associated with this order, Dent was a reference to
The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
, particulary to a scene where a character is mistaken to have that name after introducing himself as "Dent. Arthur Dent."

)

======================================================================
PAGE: Dimitris
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dimitris
======================================================================

Dimitris
was the
name
of a member of the
Maquis
called out by
Michael Eddington
during his last stand against the
Jem'Hadar
on the
planet
Athos IV
in
2373
.
Eddington gave orders to the names
Crenshaw
,
Yuen
,
Stiles
, and Dimitris in an attempt to make it appear that more than he was fighting against the Jem'Hadar. Specifically, Eddington ordered Stiles and Dimitris to watch the team's right
flank
. (
DS9
: "
Blaze of Glory
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Given the nature of the situation, this name may have been either made up or an actual Maquis he knew.
According to the script, the pronunciation for Dimitris was "dih-MEE-tris".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Dirgo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dirgo
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Captain
of the
Nenebek
Status:
Deceased
(
2367
)
Died:
2367
,
Lambda Paz
Played by
:
Nick Tate
Dirgo
was a
humanoid
male
– of a species possessing a heavy brow, and a slightly wrinkled nose with a slightly bifurcated nasal tip – who served as the
captain
of the
mining shuttle
Nenebek
during the
2360s
.
In
2367
, he was sent to transport
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
to
Pentarus V
to arbitrate a miners' dispute.
Wesley Crusher
accompanied them on the mission.
His ship, which was in poor condition,
crashed
on
Lambda Paz
, a
moon
of
Pentarus III
. The moon was a
desert
, and his ship had no emergency food or
water
. They found shelter in a
cave
that contained a
fountain
of
water
, but were prevented from getting to it by a
force field
. Dirgo tried to shoot through it, but an
energy
sentry
knocked him down and caused a
rockslide
, injuring Picard. Dirgo tried to use a
phaser
again, and he was enveloped in electrically-deposited
selenium
fibers by the energy sentry, killing him. (
TNG
: "
Final Mission
")
Dirgo was played by actor
Nick Tate
.
According to the script notes, "Dirgo is a tough-looking humanoid who is weathered and worn enough to suggest middle-age. Though his appearance is feral, he will emerge as an intelligent, if inflexible, being. Ultimately, he is self-serving; Dirgo looks out for Dirgo, because he's always had to."
[1]
StarTrek.com
called him a "Pentaran shuttle pilot".
[2]
(X)
It's unclear if "Pentaran" is the name of his species or just refers to him being associated with the
Pentarus system
mining operation, especially since there was no hint of a native population in the system.

======================================================================
PAGE: Donald Kaplan
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Donald_Kaplan
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commander
Occupation:
Chief engineer
,
USS
Intrepid
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Commander
Donald Kaplan
was the
chief engineer
of the
USS
Intrepid
in
2370
.
Kaplan attended
Starfleet Academy
with
Geordi La Forge
; since that time, the two engineers had a friendly rivalry, regularly engaging in contests to see whose vessel had more efficient power conversion systems. When the
Intrepid
achieved a
power conversion level
of 97.1
%
, higher than was achieved on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, he sent a message to La Forge that, in part, read "Maybe you should try cleaning your
plasma grid
once in a while." (
TNG
: "
Force of Nature
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Donas

Affiliation:
Federation

Rank:

Starship
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2293
)
Played by
:
Unknown performer
Petty Officer, 1st Class
Donas
was a
male
Starfleet
crewman
who served in
2293
. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
Donas' name comes from the name tag that was included along with a Starfleet uniform sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
, as well as an individually sold name tag.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
He was played by one of several
unknown actors
who served aboard either the
USS
Enterprise
-A
or
USS
Excelsior
.

Occupation:

Status:

(

)
(
2374
)
Donna W. Nemzek
,
RN
, was a
Starfleet
nurse
who served during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, her name appeared twice on a
casualty list
, where she was reported by the
USS
Akagi
as
MIA
, but was also reported by the
USS
Zapata
as
KIA
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

PAGE: Dorit J. Oberman

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Uncertain
(
2374
)
Ensign
Dorit J. Oberman
was a
Starfleet
officer
during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, a file from the
starship
USS
Akagi
reported Oberman
missing in action
. A file from the
USS
Zapata
reported Oberman
wounded
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Oberman's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode. She was named after art. asst.
Dorit Oberman
.

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Dorothy Chang
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dorothy_Chang
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Deceased
(
KIA
)
(
2375
)
Died:
2375
,
USS
Equinox
Ensign
Dorothy Chang
was a
Starfleet
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Equinox
under the command of
Captain
Rudolph Ransom
in
2371
. In that year, the
Equinox
was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
by the alien entity known as the
Caretaker
.
Chang was killed in
2375
as the result of retaliatory attacks on the
Equinox
crew by a series of
nucleogenic lifeforms
, whom the crew had been
murdering
for the purpose of converting the remains into a source of fuel. After the surviving
Equinox
crewmembers were rescued by the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, Chang's name was mentioned during a
memorial service
aboard that ship. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Dorothy R. Duder
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dorothy_R._Duder
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Status:
Uncertain
(
2374
)
Lieutenant Commander
Dorothy R. Duder
was a
Starfleet
officer
during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
,
USS
Cochrane
,
Starbase 129
and
Starbase 153
reported Duder killed in action; however, the
USS
Repulse
and
USS
Wyoming
reported Duder wounded in action. Her name was listed in a
casualty list
that was displayed in the wardroom of
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after production staff member
Dorothy Duder
.
External link
Dorothy Duder
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================

Doug Aarniokoski
was a
Starfleet
officer
who was listed as a member of
Starfleet Command
on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS
Stargazer
,
USS
Titan
-A
, and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
It can be presumed that this individual was named after second unit director
Douglas Aarniokoski
.
External link
Doug Aarniokoski
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Doug Dean (Captain)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Doug_Dean_(Captain)
======================================================================

For the Voyager crewmember, please see
Doug Dean
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Active
(
2363
)
Doug Dean
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on the directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was located in room #35951. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2363
, as a
captain
, he was listed as an
Orbital Operations
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
production staff
associate
Doug Dean
.
Though illegible on screen, the Phoenix plaque prop was shown, legible, in
Star Trek: The Next Generation - The Continuing Mission
(p. 130).
External link
Doug Dean
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

USS
Voyager

Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Doug Dean
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In
2372
, as a
lieutenant commander
, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Doug Drexler (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Doug_Drexler_(comedian)
======================================================================

For the Titan-A crewmember, please see
Doug Drexler
.
For the Starfleet Flag officer, please see
Douglas Drexler
.
Doug Drexler (comedian)
Species:
Hologram
Holoprogram:
EQ-45932
Occupation:
Comedian
Doug Drexler
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Drexler was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
EQ-45932
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was named for longtime
Trek
staff member
Doug Drexler
, a consultant to the
TNG remastering
team.
In the remastered version of the episode, this character replaced
Heidi Julian
.

Occupation:

)
Lieutenant commander
Doug Durbrow
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. His
serial number
was FSN 580-22-8563.
In
2364
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Robert Louis Stevenson
. On or about
stardate
41044.2, according to an order sent by
Commodore
Bill Marafe
of
Starbase 55
, he was scheduled for a transfer to the
USS
Discovery
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Douglas (Lieutenant)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Douglas_(Lieutenant)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
Supervisor
,
Starfleet Medical
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Lieutenant
Douglas
was a
female
who served as a
Starfleet
officer
during the late-
24th century
.
In
2375
, Douglas was assigned to
Starfleet Medical
as a
supervisor
. Her
commanding officer
was
Commander
Hilliard
and one of her subordinates was
Ensign
Weldon
.
That year,
Doctor
Julian Bashir
spoke to her when trying to obtain
Odo
's
medical
file
but she couldn't help him. (
DS9
: "
When It Rains...
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Douglas's entry on
StarTrek.com
states she was a supervisor in the "records department" of Starfleet Medical.

PAGE: Douglas Adams
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Douglas_Adams
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commanding officer
,
USS
Heart of Gold
Status:
Active
(
2364
)
Captain
Douglas Adams
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2364
, he was the
commander
of the
USS
Heart of Gold
. Sometime in that
year
, he received an order about modifications to the
Sector 500/32450
survey from
Dent Arthur Dent
of the
Sirius Cybernetics Corporation
. The captain was requested to send acknowledgement of the receipt of this order through
subspace com net
security code
045, code 100 alpha. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after writer
Douglas Adams
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Douglas Drexler (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Douglas_Drexler_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

For the comedian, please see
Doug Drexler
.
For the Titan-A crewmember, please see
Doug Drexler
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Flag officer
Occupation:
Member of the
Admiralty Board
Status:
Active
(
2401
)
Douglas "Doug" Drexler
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Gamma Trianguli VI
to
Gothos Space Center
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
He was a
captain
in the
2360s
. He was the
commander
of the
USS
Mare Tranquillitatis
. He was mentioned in the
Personnel Status Report
of
Lieutenant
Keith G. Rocha
. (
TNG
: "
Aquiel
",
okudagram
)
In
2372
, he was listed as a
Systems Management
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
,
dedication plaque
)
In the
2370s
, he was among the members of the
Science Ops
team listed on the dedication plaques of
Defiant
-class
starships
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
(
2372
)
USS
Valiant
(
2375
)
USS
Sao Paulo
In
2401
, he was listed as a member of the
Admiralty Board
on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS
Stargazer
,
USS
Titan
-A
, and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
production staff
associate
Doug Drexler
. This name did not originate in the dialogue or script of a
canon
production, but was featured on set artwork including the
personnel file
of Lieutenant
Keith Rocha
. Coincidentally, Drexler also played an
unnamed Starfleet crewperson
of the same era, as an extra in a cameo role.
He was listed as a member of
Fleet Ops
at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Douglas E. Graves (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Douglas_E._Graves_(comedian)
======================================================================

Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's database
Douglas E. Graves
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to located a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Graves was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program code list for this comedian was
YL-45934
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named for
Douglas E. Graves
, a digital artist who contributed to
Trek
productions including the
2012
Ships of the Line
calendar
.
For the remastered version of the episode, this character replaced
Michael Schoenbrun
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Draim
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Draim
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Orion Syndicate
Occupation:
Extortionist
Draim
was a powerful member of the
Orion Syndicate
who managed his criminal activities from a headquarters on
Finnea Prime
. He specialized in
blackmail
and
extortion
, and employed
net-girls
to obtain illicit and protected information about his enemies.
Draim was considered to be a very dangerous individual. In
2373
, a net-girl in his employment named
Arissa
escaped from his care and intended to defect from the Syndicate. He had her contact with the
Idanian
government
,
Tauvid Rem
, killed by the
assassins
Sorm
and
Traidy
. They were then instructed to terminate Arissa, before they were
arrested
through the intervention of
Chief of Security
Constable Odo
.
The information provided by Arissa was intended to punish Draim for his crimes, although the fate of these efforts was not discussed. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Drazman
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Drazman
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Admiral
Drazman
–
nicknamed
"
Droner
" for his dull personality – was the
commander
of the
Proxima Maintenance Yards
.
In
2371
, the
senior staff
of
Deep Space 9
was asked to attend an annual
Starfleet
symposium
on the
Gamma Quadrant
, which was being held on
Earth
. Drazman, who was on Earth, invited the senior staff to a formal
dinner
.
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
,
Lieutenants
Julian Bashir
and
Jadzia Dax
accepted the invitation. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Drew Dieghan
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Drew_Dieghan
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commander
,
USS
Intrepid
Status:
Active
(
2346
)
Captain
Drew Dieghan
or
Drew Deighan
was a
Starfleet
commanding officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2346
, he was the commander of the
USS
Intrepid
. His ship was the first to respond to
Klingon
distress signals
when the
Khitomer
outpost
was destroyed. (
TNG
: "
Sins of The Father
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Dieghan's name appeared on the monitor display that contained the logs of the
Intrepid
that
Data
was researching, once spelled
Dieghan
, and once
Deighan
. He was named after episode writer,
Drew Deighan
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 120)

======================================================================
PAGE: Duarte
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Duarte
======================================================================

Duarte
was an
engineer
who served aboard
Deep Space 9
in the
2370s
.
In early
2373
, Duarte was as "sick as a
vole
" (in
Whatley
's words) and
Rom
was asked to take the engineer's place on the
swing shift
. Rom then questioned whether a particular seat was where Duarte usually sat. (
DS9
: "
The Assignment
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Duarte's name was pronounced as "DWART-ay".
[1]
Duarte's gender was unknown, as the individual was never referred to by a pronoun.

PAGE: Duke Willer
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Duke_Willer
======================================================================

Duke Willer
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Manark IV City
to
Marcos XII
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

assigned to the

starship

Kaplan
were ordered by

ordered
Worf
off the

. (

)

PAGE: E. Phillips
CATEGORIES: Individuals

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Temporal Integrity Commission
Status:
Active
(
29th century
)
E. Phillips
,
OCL
, was a
29th century
doctor
associated with the
University of Copernicus
.
Phillips who was a member of the
Temporal Integrity Commission
. His work with the Commission included the development of the
USS
Relativity
, which was noted on the
dedication plaque
of that ship. (
VOY
: "
Relativity
",
dedication plaque
)

Voyager
actor
Ethan Phillips
.

======================================================================
PAGE: E.M. Rauch
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/E.M._Rauch
======================================================================

E.M. Rauch
was an individual who was associated with the quote:
"
The only reason for
time
is so that everything doesn't happen all at once.
"
This quote later appeared on the
dedication plaque
of the
29th century
timeship
Relativity
. (
VOY
: "
Relativity
")
This may be a reference to
Earl Mac Rauch
, writer of the film
The Adventures of Buckaroo Banzai Across the Eighth Dimension!
. In actuality,
the quote
is considered to have come from
Albert Einstein
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ed Charnock, Jr. (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ed_Charnock,_Jr._(Starfleet)

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
,
USS
Voyager
Status:

(

)

was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
24th century
.
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was located in room #45197. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
",
crew manifest
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was listed as an
Advanced Technologies
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after art department painter
Ed Charnock, Jr.

======================================================================
PAGE: Eddie Williams (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Eddie_Williams_(24th_century)
======================================================================

Eddie Williams' name on a suspect list
Eddie Williams
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Wisconsin
ferried him from
New Paris Colony Starbase
to
Alpha Braga VII
. His special accomodations were a
Class N
environment suite. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
When
Kira Nerys
was investigating the murder of
Shakaar resistance
members in
2373
, Williams' name appeared on a suspect list. His identity number, name, and location were listed on this list. He was located in the
Galloway sector
. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
External link
Williams Eddie
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Edgar Willoughby
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Edgar_Willoughby
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Chief petty officer
Occupation:
Sector
quartermaster
for the
Bajor sector
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Marital status:
Married
Spouse(s):
Cynthia
Children:
Melissa
(
daughter
) and
Edgar Jr.
(
son
)
Chief
Edgar "Eddie" Willoughby
was the sector
quartermaster
for
Bajor sector
in
2375
. The name of his
wife
was
Cynthia
, and together, they had two
children
: an eight-year-old
daughter
named
Melissa
, and a five-year-old
son
,
Edgar Jr.
When
Miles O'Brien
needed a new
graviton stabilizer
for the
USS
Defiant
in
2375
, he gave permission for
Nog
to place discrete inquiries regarding the availability of such a device.
Nog
gave Willoughby a
bottle
of
Gamzian wine
as a gift, and learned that it would take a week to acquire the replacement, which was too long.
However, Willoughby also informed
Nog
that the
USS
Sentinel
had an extra stabilizer that they were willing to trade. Nog used this information to obtain something the
Sentinel
required and eventually acquired the stabilizer. (
DS9
: "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 489 theorizes that Willoughby's surname may have been a homage to
The Twilight Zone
episode "
A Stop at Willoughby
".
James Daly
, who played
Flint
in "
Requiem for Methuselah
", starred in this episode.

======================================================================
PAGE: Edgar Willoughby, Jr.
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Edgar_Willoughby,_Jr.
======================================================================

Edgar Willoughby, Jr.
was the
son
of
Starfleet
officer
Edgar Willoughby
and his
wife
Cynthia
.
He was
born
in
2370
and had an older
sister
,
Melissa
. (
DS9
: "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Edward Regis
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Edward_Regis
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Deceased
(
2375
)
Died:
2375
Ensign
Edward Regis
was a
Starfleet
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Equinox
under the command of
Captain
Rudolph Ransom
in
2371
. In that year, the
Equinox
was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
by the alien entity known as the
Caretaker
.
Regis was killed in
2375
as the result of retaliatory attacks on the
Equinox
crew by a series of
nucleogenic lifeforms
, whom the crew had been
murdering
for the purpose of converting the remains into a source of fuel. After the surviving
Equinox
crewmembers were rescued by the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, Regis' name was mentioned during a memorial service aboard that ship. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

. According to him Edwell was "

. Her name was listed in a
casualty list
that was displayed in the
wardroom
of
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after producer
Elizabeth Braswell
.
External link
Elizabeth S. Braswell
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Elkins Judy
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Elkins_Judy
======================================================================

Elkins' info (top and bottom names)
Elkins Judy
was an individual living in the
24th century
.
After
Odo
called in a few favors from his sources on
Cardassia
of individuals with "
the
computer skills
, the opportunity and the motive to carry out [recent] attacks
" against former
Shakaar resistance
members in
2373
, Elkins' name appeared on his list as one of twenty-five possible suspects. Her identity number, name, and location were listed on this list. She was located somewhere in the
Lauritson Nebula
. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Considering both the source and parties involved, this individual was likely a
Bajoran
or
Cardassian
.
Named after
Judy Elkins
.
External link
Elkins Judy
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ella Sisko
CATEGORIES: Individuals, Starfleet personnel
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ella_Sisko
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Ella Sisko
was a
Starfleet
officer
and a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
offspring
of
Reggie
and
Asha
, and
sibling
of
Sandy
. Ella also served in Starfleet.
In the
32nd century
, Ella's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
A dotted line off of Ella on the tree suggests they had an offspring.

======================================================================
PAGE: Elway
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Elway
======================================================================

Elway
was a
scientist
who lived before the mid-
23rd century
. He developed a theory on
inter-dimensional travel
that came to be known as the
Elway Theorem
.
Even though by the
2260s
Elway Theorem had proved to be entirely inaccurate, he was still taught to
Starfleet Academy
entrants in the
24th century
.
Wesley Crusher
spent two
hours
learning about him in
astral physics
in
2365
. (
TNG
: "
The High Ground
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Entebe
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Entebe
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commander
,
USS
Ulysses
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Captain
Entebe
was a
female
Starfleet
officer
who served during the late
24th century
.
In
2371
, she was the
commander
of the
USS
Ulysses
. Towards the end of that year, her
starship
was studying
protoplanetary masses
in the
Helaspont Nebula
, near the
Federation-Tzenkethi border
.
After the
USS
Defiant
received a
distress call
from the
Federation
colony
on
Barisa Prime
that was abruptly cut-off, he was left to assume that a war had broken out. En route to Barisa Prime, Sisko had
Kira Nerys
locate any other starships in the
sector
, when she located the
Ulysses
. Sisko then had
Lieutenant
Jadzia Dax
attempt to contact Entebe for assistance, only to discover that the
Defiant
's
communications system
had been compromised. (
DS9
: "
The Adversary
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the script, Entebe was pronounced as "en-TEB-ee".
[1]
External link
Entebe
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Enwright
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Enwright
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commodore
Occupation:
Flag officer
Commanding officer
,
Starbase 6
Status:
Active
(
2260s
)
Played by
:
James Doohan
(voice)
Commodore
Enwright
was a
23rd century
Starfleet
flag officer
who served as the
commander
of
Starbase 6
during the late
2260s
.
In
2268
, Commodore Enwright ordered the
USS
Enterprise
to his station. He contacted the
Enterprise
before they could initiate contact. When asked by
Captain
James T. Kirk
what the reason for the diversion was, Enwright told him that the explanation was
beaming
aboard the
Enterprise
as they spoke and might have already materialized.
The explanation in question,
Bob Wesley
, explained that the
Enterprise
had been chosen to test the tactical abilities of
Dr.
Richard Daystrom
's experimental
M-5 multitronic unit
against an
attack force
commanded by Wesley. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
Background information
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover. The voice of the unseen commodore was provided by
James Doohan
.
According to the first draft of the script, dated
30 November
1967
, Enwright beamed aboard the
Enterprise
along with Wesley to brief
Spock
and Kirk in the
transporter room
.
In this draft he was described as follows:
Enwright is fiftyish, perhaps visibly too heavy for his uniform. He is an
administrative officer
, and a good one, but he's never been a
command pilot
.
The character who appeared in the transporter room was ultimately cut by the final draft, and his lines were given to Wesley.

Eric Alba
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In

, the
SS
Wisconsin
ferried him from

to

. His

was accessed by

aboard the

-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after visual effects artist
Eric Alba
.

PAGE: Ertel

Ertel
was a
courier
who lived in the late
32nd century
.
Cleveland Booker
once stole a shipment of
trance worms
from Ertel, after warning him that it was dangerous business. Ertel spent six months trying to recoup his losses, costing
Haz Mazaro
his patronage in the meantime. Mazaro held the loss of his best customer against Booker, which he collected in
3190
from the
latinum
Booker had intended to use for buying
isolynium
. (
DIS
: "
All In
")

PAGE: Etheria
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Etheria
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Quark's
(employee)
Occupation:
Dabo girl
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Played by
:
Unknown actress
Etheria
was a
dabo girl
in
Quark's
in
2372
.
She attended the
auction
at Quark's where
Rom
and
Nog
sold off Nog's childhood possessions before his start at the
Starfleet Academy
. (
DS9
: "
Little Green Men
")
She was among the employees of Quark's who went on strike because of a pay cut and lack of sick pay, joining the
Guild of Restaurant and Casino Employees
that the disgruntled workers had founded. Together with
Rom
,
Leeta
,
Broik
,
Frool
, and
Grimp
she tried to pay the customers on the
Promenade
for not visiting Quark's. (
DS9
: "
Bar Association
")
When
Quark
was questioned by
Ch'Pok
, a
Klingon
prosecutor during the trial of
Worf
on
Deep Space 9
, about what the
Ferengi
overheard about the attack conducted by Worf on a Klingon ship, Quark said that it was when
Dr.
Julian Bashir
was talking to Etheria. However, Quark then believed it was another dabo girl, either
Glidia
or
Midia
, before realizing it was actually
Ralidia
, and she was actually with
Morn
, not Bashir. (
DS9
: "
Rules of Engagement
")
Etheria was played by an
unknown actress
.

PAGE: Everett

"
My uncle Everett plays in a Beatles cover band; it hardly makes him John Lennon.
"
–

,
2256
("
Context Is for Kings
")

was a
male
individual who lived in the
23rd century
. He was the
uncle
of
Lieutenant Commander
Paul Stamets
.
In
2256
on the

Discovery
, when Paul Stamets was expecting to receive a
Vulcan
in
engineering
,

(a
Human
), told him that while she wasn't Vulcan, she had been raised on
Vulcan
. Stamets responded that Everett was in a
Beatles
cover band
, but that didn't make him
John Lennon
. (
DIS
: "
Context Is for Kings
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: F. Gray
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/F._Gray
======================================================================

F. Gray
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
23rd century
.
Gray was named as one of the
yard engineers
at the
San Francisco Yards
who were listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This officer was named after first assistant director
Felix Gray
.

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: F. Recio

======================================================================

Occupation:
Captain
of the
FMS
Geldonero
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
F. Recio
was a
starship
captain
in the
24th century
. Recio was the
commander
of the
FMS
Geldonero
.
In
2370
, Recio was listed on the
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The graphic was also populated by a number of other in-jokes. He is probably named after the same person as the
GCS
Recio
.

.

Starfleet Command
officer on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS
Shenzhou
, the

Discovery
, the

Enterprise
, and the
USS
Farragut
. (

",

;

: "

",

;
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
";
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
", "
A Quality of Mercy
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This officer was named after co-executive producer
Frank Siracusa
.

)
Lieutenant junior grade
Faith LeVesconte
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, files from the
starships
USS
Akagi
,
USS
Zapata
, and
USS
Tripoli
reported LeVesconte
missing in action
. A file from the
USS
Sarajevo
reported her
killed in action
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
LeVesconte's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Fallit Kot
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fallit_Kot
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Criminal
Status:
Arrested
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Peter Crombie
Fallit Kot
was a former
business partner
of
Quark
's who aided him in a failed scheme to capture a transport of
Romulan ale
in
2362
. Quark gave up Kot to the
Romulans
to avoid serving
prison
time.
Kot finished serving his Romulan prison sentence in
2370
. Soon after, he traveled to
Deep Space 9
and attempted to kill Quark. However, the two entered into partnership again. He shot a
Yridian
partner of Quark's,
Ashrock
, and took the
Rings of Paltriss
that Quark had just given to him.
He later took
Jadzia Dax
and
Melora Pazlar
hostage
and forced them to take him through the
Bajoran wormhole
and into the
Gamma Quadrant
. He shot Melora with a
phaser
, but she was later able to disable the
runabouts
artificial gravity
. Using her
Elaysian
ability to function in low gravity, she was able to launch herself into him, stopping his escape attempt. (
DS9
: "
Melora
")
Appendices
Background information
Fallit Kot was played by actor
Peter Crombie
. The name of his species was never revealed on-screen.
In
Evan Carlos Somers
' original draft of "Melora", Fallit Kot was named Megzie Del. The first draft
script
of "Melora" initially described the character as "
gruff-looking middle-age humanoid alien male.
" By comparison, in the revised final draft of the "Melora" script, Kot was initially described as "a compact, intense humanoid".
[1]
The scene in Quark's quarters was originally intended to be longer, showing him psychologically toying with Quark. As Somers explained, "
Megsy
[sic]
was going to kill Quark by using the
Ceti eels
introduced in
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
, but this variety didn't cause mental vulnerability for mind control […] Megsy
[sic]
had Quark pinned and he was going to insert the creatures in Quark's ears. I thought that would be a fitting, torturous end for someone who had caused eight years of suffering. But in the aired version, Fallit Kot just strangles Quark. It's not quite titanic enough.
" (
The Official Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Magazine
issue 62
)
Of Kot's appearance,
Michael Piller
commented, "
We were doing a story where an alien has to eat, which becomes a pivotal scene where he's feeding, and makeup designed this big kind of handle across his mouth so he couldn't eat. We had to kind of shove the food down his mouth. It was hilarious. We cracked up during dailies.
" (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
, pp. 64-65)
The make-up department created Kot's mouth ridge using durable rubber that looked and moved like a piece of flesh. It is considered one of the more unique designs seen in
Deep Space Nine
. (
Star Trek: Aliens & Artifacts
, p. 152)
Michael Westmore
further discussed the design in the
DS9 Season 2 DVD
special feature "
Michael Westmore's Aliens
: Season Two". He explained that designing Fallit Kot's make-up was the first time he had an opportunity to aesthetically bridge an alien's face. Kot's makeup consisted of two pieces which were later glued together, giving this alien a unique look when he talks.
Apocrypha
In the
Worlds of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
novella
Satisfaction is Not Guaranteed
,
Lieutenant
Ro Laren
reviews Kot's file, along with those of other individuals considered threats to Quark, after becoming chief of security on Deep Space 9.
External link
Fallit Kot
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

sciences division
in

. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
Fambi's uniform, including a name tag, was auctioned off at the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
.
[1]
(X)
He was played by one of several
unknown actors
who served aboard either the
USS
Enterprise
-A
or
USS
Excelsior
.

PAGE: Fang-lee
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fang-lee
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Deceased
(
2368
)
Fang-lee
was a
deceased
crewmember
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
killed in action
sometime prior to
2368
.
Will Riker
reminded
Worf
of Fang-lee when he contemplated
suicide
following a
spinal cord
injury in
2368
. Fang-lee was an example of an honorable
officer
who fought for life even when hope was lost. (
TNG
: "
Ethics
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Farley
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Farley
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2376
)
Ensign
Farley
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
aboard the
USS
Voyager
under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
. Farley became
stranded
in the
Delta Quadrant
along with the rest of the crew of
Voyager
in
2371
.
In
2376
, during a
lecture
by
The Doctor
on
insects
indigenous to the
Delta Quadrant
, Farley fell a
sleep
and began to
snore
, much to the amusement of the other listeners and The Doctor's disdain.
B'Elanna Torres
gossiped about this to
Tom Paris
after he arrived back from a particularly long
away mission
. Paris was too busy watching
hockey
to listen to her, though. (
VOY
: "
Memorial
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Farrakk
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Farrakk
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Arms dealer
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Hagath
(
2373
)
Died:
2373
,
Farrakk's ship
Played by
:
Tim Halligan
Farrakk
was a
humanoid
arms dealer
who worked under
Hagath
and
Gaila
.
In
2373
, he was given the task of making a deal with the
Verillians
. However, Farrakk went vacationing on
Risa
instead, and the
Metron Consortium
obtained the contract with the Verillians.
Hagath had Farrakk killed for failing him by triggering a
warp core breach
aboard
his ship
after he left
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")
Farrakk was played by
Tim Halligan
.
According to the script, Farrakk was described as an alien of
the same race
, and roughly the same age, as Hagath, but was to Hagath, "a "junior" league player". Furthermore, his name was pronounced as "fah-ROCK".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Fats B'Zirtak
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fats_B'Zirtak
======================================================================

Fats B'Zirtak
was a noted
musician
in the
genre
of
Tellarite slop jazz
during the
24th century
. He
overdosed
on
ketracel-white
.
While discussing slop jazz with
Lieutenant jg
Beckett Mariner
in
2381
,
Lieutenant
Dirk
described B'Zirtak as probably being "
The best of them
". (
LD
: "
In the Cradle of Vexilon
")
This Tellarite was only mentioned in dialogue.

officer
,

)
Felchin
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. This individual had served under
Leyton
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Maintenance
,
Utopia Planitia
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49297. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Felix (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Felix_(24th_century)
======================================================================

"
That Felix... when he designs a holosuite program, he doesn't fool around.
"
–
Miles O'Brien
,
2375
("
Badda-Bing, Badda-Bang
")
Felix
was a
holoprogrammer
, the creator of several
holosuite
programs
enjoyed by his good friend
Julian Bashir
aboard
Deep Space 9
. Known for programs based on
Earth
's
1960s
, Felix's creations included
Julian Bashir, Secret Agent
and
Vic Fontaine
's program
Bashir 62
. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
", "
His Way
")
In his later, more elaborate program, Felix made Vic Fontaine completely
self-aware
such that Vic knew (and was comfortable knowing) that he was merely a
hologram
. This helped Vic fit the attitude of a 1960s lounge singer. The sophistication of Vic's program allowed him to access Deep Space 9's station
logs
,
comm system
, and other holoprograms, as well as the ability to turn himself off if he wanted. (
DS9
: "
His Way
")
Felix secretly embedded a
jack-in-the-box
subroutine
in
Bashir 62
, which sprang to life in
2375
. Designed to "stir things up a bit", the jack-in-the-box introduced mobsters
Frankie Eyes
,
Tony Cicci
, and big man
Carl Zeemo
who threatened to end Vic's very existence within the program's universe if not stopped. (
DS9
: "
Badda-Bing, Badda-Bang
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Felix appears in the novel
The Long Mirage
, which gives his full name as Felix Knightly, and describes him as a person of color.
The short story
So a Horse Walks into a Bar...
gives his name as Fritz.
External link
Felix Knightly
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Fennim
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fennim
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Think Tank
Occupation:
Member of the "Think Tank"
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Steven Dennis
Fennim
was a
member
of the "
Think Tank
", one of two
humanoid
members along with
Kurros
. Fennim's speech could not be interpreted by the
universal translator
;
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
speculated that his speech patterns might be too complex for the translator to process.
Some of his duties were to greet those interested in seeking help from the "Think Tank" and introduce them to Kurros. He assisted Kurros in the plot to retrieve
Seven of Nine
and enlist her in the "Think Tank".
When the
USS
Voyager
sent a
carrier wave
to disrupt the
telepathic
communication of the "Think Tank", Fennim was unable to communicate with Kurros. (
VOY
: "
Think Tank
")
Appendices
Background information
Fennim's costume
Fennim was played by actor
Steven Dennis
, who received no credit for this appearance.
He filmed his scenes on Thursday
14 January
1999
and Friday
15 January
1999
on
Paramount Stage 9
.
The costume worn by Dennis was sold off in week 25 of the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$382.77.
[1]
(X)
The costume consists of a large number of ornaments placed in square sections. One of the sections contains a
rear admiral
's triangular braid insignia from the early seasons of
Star Trek: The Next Generation
.
External links
Fennim
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

: "

"
set decoration
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Fernando Sepulveda (Lieutenant)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fernando_Sepulveda_(Lieutenant)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Fernando Sepulveda
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard in
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His room number was #75274. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2367
, as a
lieutenant
, he was the ops engineer for the
Third Shift Antimatter Operations Unit
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was listed as being an
Ops Engineer
on duty in the
Engineering Division
duty roster
. This roster was on display via the
desktop monitor
in the
ready room
. (
TNG
: "
Suddenly Human
",
okudagram
)
In
2372
, as a lieutenant, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster aboard the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after swing gang member
Fernando Sepulveda
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ferris (dictator)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ferris_(dictator)
======================================================================

Ferris
was a
dictator
who became a noted historical figure by the
23rd century
.
In
2266
,
Roger Korby
believed that by transferring
consciousness
into an
android
body, a
Human
would have practical
immortality
.
James T. Kirk
responded that this was programming. He continued further by saying that others had made the same promises, using different words, and that these others were
Genghis Khan
,
Julius Caesar
,
Adolf Hitler
, Ferris, and
Maltuvis
. (
TOS
: "
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the reference book
Star Trek: Federation - The First 150 Years
, Asahf Ferris was an Augment who ruled the American Empire during the 1990s.
According to the
Star Trek: Khan
comic series, Asahf Ferris was an Augment who ruled
South America
as part of the Ferris Dominion. According to
Khan Noonien Singh
, Ferris bled her people dry to amass her own personal wealth and sent armies to battle the New American Empire in the area formerly known as
Mexico
.
External link
Asahf Ferris
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Fi'Ja
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fi'Ja
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Krall
Status:
Unknown
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Danny Pudi
Fi'Ja
was an
alien
male
who was stranded on the
planet
Altamid
by
Krall
.
Along with
Zavanko
and
Hider
, he approached a
crashed
Kelvin pod
and tried to attack
Montgomery Scott
, though they were stopped by
Jaylah
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Background info
Fi'Ja was portrayed by actor
Danny Pudi
.
Concept art of Fi'Ja can be seen at
TrekCore
.
This character was only named in the source's end credits. The character was referred to as "Reptilicus" by the makeup artists.
[1]
The mobile game
Star Trek: Fleet Command
includes an officer named Tiza, who is the same species as Fi'Ja. The species is called the Mirani.

PAGE: Fiscal Vertebrate
