======================================================================

The assay office list
Fiscal Vertebrate
was an
individual
who lived in the late
24th century
.
In
2373
, Vertebrate stored valuables in the
assay office
on
Deep Space 9
. When
Arissa
accessed the office's
computer
, she pulled up a list of
names
, upon which Vertebrate's name appeared. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
",
display graphic
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Flaherty
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Flaherty
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commander
Occupation:
Executive officer
,
USS
Aries
Status:
Active
(
2365
)
Commander
Flaherty
was a
male
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
Flaherty was a noted
linguist
, who had a knack for instantly interpreting and extrapolating any
spoken language
. He was fluent in more than forty
languages
, including
Giamon
,
Klingon
,
Romulan
, and
Stroyerian
.
In
2365
, he was the
first officer
aboard the
USS
Aries
. (
TNG
: "
The Icarus Factor
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Fraddock
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fraddock
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Transport
Captain
Status:
Active
(
2151
)
Played by
:
Michael O'Hagan
Captain
Fraddock
was the
commanding officer
of
a transport vessel
encountered by
Enterprise
in
September
2151
.
Fraddock was transporting a group of
Borothan
pilgrims to the
Great Plume of Agosoria
, which was a protostar that emitted an epic burst of energy every eleven years and was hailed by the Borothans as a
religious
event.
Jonathan Archer
invited Fraddock and his passengers to a social dinner aboard
Enterprise
, not realizing that
Silik
was one of the passengers. The passengers accepted the invitation, but Fraddock opted to remain aboard his ship. (
ENT
: "
Cold Front
")
Fraddock was played by
Michael O'Hagan
.
In the script of "Cold Front", Fraddock was described as "
an alien transport captain sitting in his chair. He's late 60's, gruff, and looks as weather-worn as his ship.
"
External link
Fraddock
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Fred S.

Fred S.
was an individual who was killed by the
Romulan supernova
of
2387
.
In
2399
, on the
anniversary
of this event, someone who knew Fred wrote a brief message to him on the
floor
of a
room
. This message was seen in a
newscast
broadcast by the
Federation News Network
of mourning ceremonies for those lost to the supernova. (
PIC
: "
Remembrance
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Freddy Bristow
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Freddy_Bristow
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Ensign
Freddy Bristow
was an
officer
aboard the
USS
Voyager
during its voyage through the
Delta Quadrant
in the
2370s
.
He was described by
Tom Paris
as "
tall [and] good looking.
" He also allegedly was a good
Parrises squares
player, but had been soundly defeated by
B'Elanna Torres
once. (
VOY
: "
The Swarm
")
Bristow was serving under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
aboard the USS
Voyager
, when the ship and its
crew
were pulled into the Delta Quadrant in
2371
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
In
2373
, Freddy had a
crush
on Torres and made excuses to hang around
USS
Voyager
's
engineering section
in order to be near her. When Paris confronted her about it, she called Bristow a child and assured Paris she could deal with it herself. Shortly after, Paris suggested he and Torres take part in a
holoprogram
of
Sailing on Lake Como
sometime when she was free. Torres replied that she'd rather take her chances with Bristow. (
VOY
: "
The Swarm
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

was a member of

's civilian population who worked in the
assay office
in

.

to ask her out, but the
Starfleet
officer thought she was in
love
with

. (

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Fuchida
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fuchida
======================================================================

Species:
Fuchida's species
Affiliation:
New Sydney Police
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
Police officer
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Clayton Landey
Lieutenant
Fuchida
was an
alien
police officer
in the
New Sydney Police
, on the non-
Federation
colony
world
New Sydney
, in the
Sappora system
.
In
2375
, Fuchida visited
Yanas Tigan
's
pergium mine
on
Sappora VII
to deliver
Miles O'Brien
, who his team had found beaten up, as
Ezri Dax
had been searching for him. He later came to the
Tigan
residence to arrest
Norvo Tigan
for the
murder
of
Morica Bilby
. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
Lieutenant Fuchida was played by actor
Clayton Landey
.
The name Fuchida originated from the source's
script
.
The mirror universe Fuchida is given the first name "Yeroff" in
Saturn's Children
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Fujisaki
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fujisaki
======================================================================

Vice Admiral
Fujisaki
was the
Deputy Chief of Starfleet Intelligence
, who
died
of "
food poisoning
" in
2374
.
When he was recruiting Bashir for an elaborate
conspiracy
involving the
Romulan
Continuing Committee
in
2375
, Sloan informed Bashir that there were suspicions that
Koval
, head of the
Romulan
Tal Shiar
, was involved in the death of Fujisaki, but no proof of this existed.
Later, Koval told another version of the conspiracy to the
Continuing Committee
, that Sloan in fact was the former protégé of the late Fujisaki. Sloan believed that Fujisaki was
murdered
by the Tal Shiar and he felt that the
assassination
of a
Starfleet
admiral
was "stepping over the line." As a result, Sloan created Section 31 so that he could exact revenge against Koval without implicating Starfleet Intelligence. (
DS9
: "
Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The veracity of either account was extremely questionable, and it was known that Section 31 actually existed centuries prior to Fujisaki's death, and the circumstances of Fujisaki's death remain unclear.

.

2372
, this individual was assigned to
Fleet Supply Office
. The effective
date

's temporary office in

. (

",

. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49302. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Galt
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Galt
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Master thrall
on
Triskelion
Status:
Active
(
2268
)
Played by
:
Joseph Ruskin
For the planet of the same name, please see
Galt (planet)
.
"
I am Galt, Master Thrall.  This place is the planet Triskelion.  You are to be trained and spend the rest of your lives here!
"
–
Galt
,
2268
("
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
Galt
was the
master thrall
on
Triskelion
in
2268
. He was in charge of
humanoids
that were brought to the
planet
to fight as
gladiators
in combat games in order to entertain and provide gambling for the disembodied entities called the
Providers
.
His position let him control the levels of pain the
drill thralls
felt from their
collars
. He controlled this without any
gesture
, only his eyes glowed as the pain was inflicted. He also had a limited means of individual
teleportation
and he could appear at any place to summon and direct the thralls. He used these means to pacify and control the thralls, such as stopping an escape attempt by
James T. Kirk
and his
landing party
.
After Kirk had won a wager from the Providers by defeating three thralls that won the landing party their freedom, Galt was left on the planet with the other former thralls to begin a new life of freedom. (
TOS
: "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
In
2381
, a framed
photograph
of Galt,
Tamoon
, and
Shahna
was among several others displayed on the
walls
of a
bar
on
Starbase 25
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Galt was played by
Joseph Ruskin
.
The
Star Trek Concordance
speculates that Galt may be an android due to his ability to trigger the collars of obedience just by making his eyes glow.

======================================================================
PAGE: Garnav
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Garnav
======================================================================

Garnav
was an
actor
, considered to be a master of his craft by the
2360s
, and a proponent of
method acting
.
In
2366
,
Data
had studied virtually every renowned acting master in order to improve his acting skills, and found himself "attracted" to Garnav,
Constantin Stanislavsky
and
Stella Adler
, describing them as his
mentors
. (
TNG
: "
Devil's Due
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Gem (humanoid)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gem_(humanoid)
======================================================================

Species:
Minarian star system native
Status:
Active
(
2268
)
Played by
:
Kathryn Hays
"
Gem
" was the
name
chosen by
Dr.
Leonard McCoy
for a
mute
humanoid
empath
from a
planet
in the
Minarian star system
, as he thought it would be better than calling her "You".
In
2268
, Gem was tested by a
species
called the
Vians
to see if her people were worthy to be saved from a
supernova
that was threatening her and other planets. The Vians had the power to save only one species.
Spock
,
McCoy
, and
Kirk
were
kidnapped
by the Vians and subjected to various forms of
torture
to test Gem's reaction and to determine if her species was worthy of being saved. They wanted to know if Gem would help them. The Vians wanted to be certain that she and her people could learn the principles of self-sacrifice, compassion and the love of life from her contact with the Humans. These qualities would make her civilization worthy to survive.
The Vians tested Gem's capacity for compassion and how far she would go to save another being. After Kirk was tortured, Gem healed his wounds. When Dr. McCoy was tortured and near death, Gem was at first frightened by the extent of his injuries and did not help him. Then, Gem approached McCoy and moved her hands on McCoy's face, transferring his injuries to herself. Gem then collapsed, sobbing, and recoiled in fear because she knew that to heal him, she would have to die. She tried to help him more, but moved away in fear.
However, Gem overcame her fears and offered her life to save McCoy's, but McCoy refused to allow her to die and shoved her away. The Vians healed McCoy after Kirk convinced them that Gem offering her life was sufficiently compassionate and actually going through with it was not necessary.
Taking Gem with them, the Vians teleported from the laboratory. The Vians determined that her people were worth saving, and her species was spared from destruction. (
TOS
: "
The Empath
")
Gem was played by actress
Kathryn Hays
.
Neither Gem's real name nor her species' name was ever identified on-screen.

in

.

PAGE: Gideon S. Turner

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gideon_S._Turner
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Signage honoring Turner
Gideon S. Turner
was a notable individual who became the namesake of the
Gideon S. Turner Wing
at the
Starfleet Academy
War College
facilities in
San Francisco
.
Upon entering the Turner Wing in
3195
,
Cadet
Caleb Mir
and
Tarima Sadal
began to jokingly speculate about what kind of person Turner might have been. Among the possibilities discussed were that he "
high fived
all of his
officers
," "
put
pips
on
his
dog
and left him in charge once,
" and "
made everybody learn
Welsh
, and he didn't even speak Welsh.
" Turner became an in-joke between Mir and Sadal as they continued to bond. (
SA
: "
Beta Test
")
This individual was only mentioned in dialogue and writing.

Gleason
was the
commanding officer
of the
USS
Zhukov
during the mid-
2360s
.
In
2366
, Gleason had heard that the
USS
Enterprise
-D
needed a
systems engineer
, and recommended
Lieutenant
Reginald Barclay
for the position, describing him as a brilliant and talented engineer.
Upon discovering that Barclay was nervous, reclusive, introverted and lacked confidence,
Commander
Riker
suggested to Captain
Picard
that Gleason might have been "
buttering
our
bread
a little
" to get rid of Barclay. However, it transpired that Barclay's abilities were as advertised, when his neuroses weren't getting in the way. (
TNG
: "
Hollow Pursuits
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The novel
Dujonian's Hoard
gives him the first name Neil.

at

, about what the
Ferengi
overheard about the attack Worf conducted on a Klingon ship, Quark said that it was when
Doctor
Julian Bashir
was talking to Glidia. However, Quark then believed it was another dabo girl. (
DS9
: "
Rules of Engagement
")
Glidia was played by background actress
Cenita L. Johnson
who received no credit for her appearances.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Golin Shel-la
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Golin_Shel-la
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Ennis
Occupation:
Leader of the Ennis
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Jonathan Banks
Golin Shel-la
was the leader of the
Ennis
, a faction who fought the
Nol-Ennis
on a small
penal moon
in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
The Ennis and Nol-Ennis were sentenced to eternal warfare on the moon by their planet's leaders to set an example when they could not stop warring. Shel-la could not remember by
2369
what the original war was for. He thought it might have been over water or land.
Artificial
microbes
were deployed on the moon to prevent the Ennis and Nol-Ennis from permanently dying and avoiding the sentence. Shel-la told
Benjamin Sisko
, after the
runabout
USS
Yangtzee Kiang
crash landed
on the moon, that he had died countless times.
The artificial microbes also had the effect of making anyone revived by them permanently dependent on them, and the microbes were designed to work only in the moon's
ecosystem
. This had the effect of preventing anyone who had ever been revived by the microbes from leaving the moon.
When
Dr.
Julian Bashir
thought he should perhaps work on reprogramming the microbes to get them not revive the moon's inhabitants, thus ending their torture, Shel-la's first thought was that Bashir's work could be used as the ultimate weapon against the Nol-Ennis, wiping them out once and for all.
After
Kai
Opaka
died and was revived by the microbes, she remained behind on the moon, intending to negotiate a peace between the warring groups. (
DS9
: "
Battle Lines
")
Golin Shel-la was played by actor
Jonathan Banks
.
In the script, he was described as "battle-scarred and tough-looking, but a great weariness surrounds him." Additionally, from the script, his name was pronounced as "GOH-lin shel-LAH".
[1]
Appendices
Apocrypha
Golin Shel-la appears in the "Victory is Life" expansion of
Star Trek Online
. He and his Nol-Ennis counterpart
Zlangco
now lead a colony near the wreck of the
Yangtzee Kiang
, after Kai Opaka ended the fighting by discovering the
Orb
of Peace. In 2410,
Kira Nerys
- now Kai herself - returns to the moon with Dr. Bashir, with a cure that would remove the microbes from Opaka and the others, and allow them to leave. Within moments of receiving the cure, however, Shel-la is killed - this time permanently - by the
Hur'q
.
External links
Golin Shel-la
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Gomtuu
CATEGORIES: Individuals, Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gomtuu
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Organic creature
Status:
Active
(
2366
)
Gomtuu
was a
sentient
space
borne
organic creature
that functioned as a
space vessel
, and lived for several millennia up through the
24th century
. It survived a near-
extinction
of its race and may have been the last of its kind. It seemed to have been "born" far from
Federation space
, possibly in another
galaxy
. Upon its discovery by the
United Federation of Planets
and the
Romulan Star Empire
, it was dubbed "
Tin Man
" and the "
Star Creature
", respectively.
Quick Answers
What is Gomtuu's connection to the Tin Man episode in Star Trek TNG?
Gomtuu, also known as "Tin Man," is a powerful entity in the "Tin Man" episode of Star Trek: The Next Generation. The Enterprise-D, led by Captain Picard, races against Romulans to contact Gomtuu. An ancient species, Gomtuu connects with Betazoid telepath Tam Elbrun, who offers it an alternative to suicide. Gomtuu provides Elbrun with peace, teleports Data back to the Enterprise, and vanishes with Elbrun.
Provided by: Fandom
How did Gomtuu survive the near-extinction of its race in Star Trek TNG?
Gomtuu, known as "Tin Man" in Star Trek: The Next Generation, survived its race's near-extinction by avoiding suicide in 2366. Discovered by the Federation and Romulans, it was possibly the last of its kind. With no reproduction means and its crew deceased, Gomtuu was in danger. Telepath Tam Elbrun bonded with Gomtuu, offering it an alternative to self-destruction. This bond provided Gomtuu with tranquility, allowing it to continue its existence and vanish with Elbrun to unknown regions.
Provided by: Fandom
What role did Gomtuu play in the Star Trek TNG episode Tin Man?
In the Star Trek TNG episode "Tin Man," Gomtuu, also called "Tin Man," is a powerful entity that the Enterprise and Romulans compete to contact. Gomtuu teleports Data back to the Enterprise and vanishes with Tam Elbrun, providing him with long-sought mental peace.
Provided by: Fandom
Why was Gomtuu called Tin Man in Star Trek The Next Generation?
Gomtuu was named 'Tin Man' in Star Trek: The Next Generation because of its enigmatic nature, similar to the character from The Wizard of Oz. In the episode 'Tin Man,' the Enterprise races against Romulans to contact this powerful entity, highlighting its mysterious qualities.
Provided by: Fandom
How did the Federation and Romulans discover Gomtuu in Star Trek TNG?
In 2366, the Federation and Romulans discovered Gomtuu, known as "Tin Man," near the dying star Beta Stromgren. A Federation probe detected it, prompting the USS Enterprise-D and Romulan warbirds to investigate. Gomtuu, a living ship, intended to die at the star. Tam Elbrun, a Federation contact specialist, communicated with Gomtuu before the supernova. Gomtuu saved both the Enterprise-D and a Romulan ship from the explosion before vanishing.
Provided by: Fandom
Biology
Cross-section scans
Viewed from the exterior, Gomtuu was a massive, dark-colored
starship
shaped like a
sunflower
seed
shell. Its interior cavity consisted of a series of chambers and
corridors
with
gravity
and
atmosphere
to support
carbon-based lifeforms
as a crew, with which Gomtuu lived symbiotically. Its amorphous interior was also capable of growing furniture and equipment from its walls and floors. It could project images from its exterior to the occupants.
Like a traditional
starship
, Gomtuu possessed a
warp drive
,
transporter
capability, and was fully equipped to defend itself with a protective
force field
. However, much of its
technology
was beyond that known to Federation
science
. It was
telepathic
over an extremely long distance.
History
Interior command center
Gomtuu was manned by a
crew
until an explosion in space generated
radiation
that penetrated Gomtuu's "
hull
" and killed the crew. Gomtuu wandered the galaxy for
millennia
after the traumatic experience, vainly searching for more of its kind. Alone and without a purpose, it eventually decided to commit
suicide
by
orbiting
Beta Stromgren
, a
star
for which
supernova
was imminent.
In
2366
, "Tin Man" was detected when Starfleet sent the
Vega Nine probe
to monitor the supernova. At the same time, the Romulans also discovered Gomtuu and a race was on to get to it first. As the
alien
did not respond to traditional
communication
methods, the Federation sent
Tam Elbrun
, a highly
telepathic
Betazoid
, to make
first contact
.
Defending itself from the Romulans
The Federation established contact first through Elbrun, who
telepathically
warned Gomtuu of the Romulans. Gomtuu then demonstrated its defensive capabilities, destroying the
Romulan Warbird
and heavily damaging the
USS
Enterprise
-D
utilizing an
energy
wave. Later, with Elbrun and
Data
on board, Gomtuu came about and fired a second energy wave which propelled the
Enterprise
-D and a
second Warbird
3.8 billion
kilometers
through space.
Gomtuu then teleported Data back onto the
bridge
of the
Enterprise
(through the
Enterprise
's
shields
) and disappeared with Elbrun to parts unknown. Elbrun provided Gomtuu with an alternative to suicide, and Gomtuu provided Elbrun with tranquility. (
TNG
: "
Tin Man
")
Appendices
Background information
According to the script for the episode,
[1]
the pronunciation for Gomtuu was "GOM-too".
According to
Michael Okuda
, in the
TNG Season 3 DVD
special feature "Departmental Briefing Year Three - Creating the "Tin Man"", the design of Gomtuu was based on
Richard D. James
' vision of a vessel with a "very organic look." The
studio model
, built by
Greg Jein
, would be "
partly based on [the look of] a peach pit.
" The
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 123 adds that "
the model was another
Rick Sternbach
creation… It was designed in homage to the thermal pods in
Buckaroo Banzai
.
"
The interior was based on an idea proposed by Richard James, where the set was coated with spray foam, which gave the organic look he was looking for. According to the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 123, "
Rob Legato
created the organic chair that seems to form out of Tin Man's very structure by reversing a time-lapse sequence of a melting wax chair.
"
The sounds from Tin Man's interior were a combination of whale sounds and a recording of sound designer
Jim Wolvington
digesting pizza, recorded through a stethoscope. The explosion (pictured above) was a scene reused from the final
V'ger
scene in
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
.
External link
Gomtuu
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Goopy
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Goopy
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
Owner:
D'Vana Tendi
"
Goopy
" was the
name
given to a small
gelatinous lifeform
that
Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
was studying aboard the
USS
Cerritos
in
2381
. She kept Goopy in a
beaker
of
water
. Its first escape occurred when
Hans Federov
accidentally bumped into her, but she was quickly able to recover it.
Goopy escaped a second time when
Sam Rutherford
bumped her as he jumped down from his
bunk
. Tendi chased it into a
Jefferies tube
, where she overheard a trio of
delta shifters
plotting to rig a
room lottery
for newly available
crew quarters
. (
LD
: "
Room for Growth
")
According to an
Instagram log
recorded after the episode, Goopy escaped at least once more after the events of the episode, leading Rutherford to conclude that it was "getting smarter" and that Tendi needed to keep it in something more secure than the beaker.

======================================================================
PAGE: Grathon Tolar
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Grathon_Tolar
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Forgery
and
con artist
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Elim Garak
(
2374
)
Died:
2374
Played by
:
Howard Shangraw
"
Think of
them both
as tragic victims of war.
"
–
Elim Garak
,
2374
("
In the Pale Moonlight
")
Grathon Tolar
was a skilled
holographic
forgery
artist,
con man
, and general degenerate in the temporary employ of
Starfleet
for the ultimate purposes of bringing the
Romulan Star Empire
into the
Dominion War
on the side of the
Federation
. He belonged to a blue-skinned
humanoid
species
that had a fin-like structure running along the top of the head.
In
2374
, Tolar was awaiting
execution
in a
Klingon
prison
for unspecified
crimes
. It was at this time when
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
, acting under the guidance of
Elim Garak
and with the support of Starfleet, contacted
Chancellor
Gowron
to arrange a pardon.
Garak selected Tolar for the task. Indeed, Tolar knew Garak and was visibly discomforted at knowing Garak was involved.  Under the terms of his
parole
, Tolar was to stay away from the
Klingon Empire
and create a holo-program for Sisko. Specifically, Tolar was enlisted to create a holographic forgery of a high-level
Dominion
meeting in which the supposed plan for the invasion of
Romulus
was discussed.  Initially, Tolar was under the mistaken impression that he was to create a holographic program for the Captain's personal use. The Captain was quick to correct him.
However, before Tolar could complete the recording for Sisko and Garak, he was involved in an altercation in
Quark's
with the
proprietor
and
M'Pella
, one of Quark's
dabo girls
. Consequently, this brought the attention of
station
security
as well as the potential for reports and documentation exposing Tolar's presence on
Deep Space 9
. Ultimately, Quark did not press charges against Tolar, after Captain Sisko negotiated a substantial
bribe
to him in exchange for his silence.
Subsequently restricted to his
quarters
, Tolar finished the ersatz recording and offered it to Sisko and Garak soon afterwards. After Captain Sisko tentatively accepted the recording, he rebuffed Tolar's attempt to excuse himself – he refused to free Tolar until his recording had fooled the
Romulan
official,
Senator
Vreenak
. When Tolar protested, insisting he'd fulfilled the terms of their agreement, Sisko angrily retorted his freedom was contingent on his forgery fooling Vreenak, promising to otherwise hand Tolar back to the Klingons and insist Gowron make his execution a drawn out affair. Deeply rattled, Tolar insisted the forgery would pass.
Ultimately, Tolar's forgery failed to fool Vreenak at all. Shortly after Vreenak left Deep Space 9 to expose the "vile deception" to the entire
Alpha Quadrant
, his shuttle exploded, killing Vreenak and his staff. An investigation by the
Tal Shiar
pointed to Dominion
sabotage
, and the recording was accepted as genuine, with the imperfections in the forgery being attributed to damage from the explosion. Known only to Sisko and Garak at the time, Garak had actually placed a
bomb
aboard
Vreenak's shuttle
, suspecting that Tolar would not be up to the job they needed him to do. At some point, Garak also eliminated Tolar to ensure no other witnesses to the operation would remain. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
Appendices
Background information
Grathon Tolar was played by actor
Howard Shangraw
.
In the first draft script of
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
", the reason that Grathon Tolar was awaiting execution in a Klingon prison, according to Garak, was that he was accused of "the murder of a
High Council
member's
son
."
The final draft script for "In the Pale Moonlight" stated the pronunciation of Tolar's name was "GRAY-thun TOE-lar". Additionally, it described him as "
a small, weasel-faced ALIEN and does not look like someone who'd last very long in a Klingon prison. His clothes are clean and neatly worn, but there's a palpable oiliness to him that makes you want to bath immediately after any visit. He has a low, nervous laugh that only serves to get under Sisko's skin.
"
[1]
Apocrypha
Grathon Tolar is referenced in the
Pocket DS9
novel
Hollow Men
, whereby Enderby of
Starfleet Intelligence
learns of Tolar's involvement in the Romulan plot, and his murder by Garak. Enderby and Jedburgh then blackmail Garak into attempting the assassination of Tomas Roeder by threatening to reveal his involvement. The novel also reveals that Captain Sisko later informs Admirals
William Ross
and
Marta Batanides
of the plot and Tolar's death.
External links
Grathon Tolar
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. At the time it was  featured (April 2005), it was considered one of the best examples of the

's work. This article was  featured more than five years ago though, and its status should be

. You can use the links below to see how the article has changed since it was last featured.

======================================================================
PAGE: Greg Agalsoff (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Greg_Agalsoff_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Yard engineer
,
Beta Antares Ship Yards
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Greg Agalsoff
was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the late
24th century
.
In
2373
, Agalsoff was named as one of the
yard engineers
at the
Beta Antares Ship Yards
who were listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Prometheus
. (
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
"
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was named as a member of the
Fleet Ops
team, at the
Marin County Starfleet Yards
,
Skywalker Division
, listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named for sound technician
Greg Agalsoff
.
External link
Greg Agalsoff
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Greg Berry (Doctor)
CATEGORIES: Individuals

======================================================================

For the 21st century individual, please see
Greg Berry
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Doctor
Occupation:
Science Ops
officer
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Doctor
Greg Berry
was a
Science Ops
officer
who worked on the
USS
Prometheus
.
Berry's name was listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
Prometheus
. (
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
",
set artwork
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
Voyager
set designer
Greg Berry
.
His name on the plaque was unreadable in the episode, but could be read clearly when it went up for auction.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Greg Medina (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Greg_Medina_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

Greg Medina
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the late
24th century
.
In
2372
, Medina was named as one of the
yard engineers
at the
Antares Ship Yards
who were listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after construction foreman
Greg Medina
.
He was listed as a member of
Science Ops
at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. The plaque can be viewed at the Twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

Greg Nelson
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

yard engineers
at the

in

USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
",
dedication plaque
)

2370s
, he was listed as a member of the
Tactical Operations
team on the dedication plaque of several
Federation
starships
, including the
USS
Defiant
at
Antares Ship Yards
in
2370
, the
USS
Voyager
at
Earth Station McKinley
in
2371
, and the
USS
Sao Paulo
at
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
The Search, Part I
",
dedication plaque
;
VOY
: "
Caretaker
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
production staff
associate
Gregory Jein
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Grjördslat
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Grjördslat
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
The Unwanted
Occupation:
Miner
Status:
Active
(
2383
)
Played by
:
Laila Berzins
Grjördslat
was an
alien
Unwanted
who was forced to work as a
chimerium
miner
on
Tars Lamora
for
the Diviner
for several
years
. Their species was distinguished with having four arms and four fingers per hand.
They were pushing a mine cart full of chimerium the day
Dal
attempted his escape in a
class D loader
.
Later, they watched the
USS
Protostar
emerge and escape from the planet. (
PRO
: "
Lost and Found
")
Some time later, following the return of the Dal and the
Protostar
, Grjördslat and their work companion,
Trodo
, were both discovered by Dal, hanging onto a
pipe
after the planet had lost
gravity
. Dal, who was equipped with his
Starfleet
combadge
, a type of
short wave translator
, approached the two and instructed them to get to the
Rev-12
, because the
atmospheric shields
would soon fail.
The two miners, however, where more interested in their new found ability to understand and communicate with each other, and proceeded to introduce themselves. While Trodo confessed their desire to connect with Grjördslat "after so many years," the two miners joined their hands together in an embrace. As Trodo further attempted to express themself, they were cut off by Dal, who apologized for interrupting "this beautiful moment", and pushed them along towards the
Rev-12
. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
")
Grjördslat was voiced by
Laila Berzins
in "A Moral Star, Part 2", where they were credited as "Miner #1".
External link
Grjördslat
at
StarTrek.com

======================================================================
PAGE: Gupta
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gupta
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Admiral
Gupta
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
24th century
.
Gupta visited
Deep Space 9
on
stardate
47552.9
. The purpose of the admiral's visit was to assess
Cardassian
movements along the
DMZ
. Gupta was later satisfied that the Cardassians would not break the
Federation-Cardassian Treaty
.
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
recorded a
log
about Admiral Gupta's visit, which a
replicant
of
Miles O'Brien
later listened to. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: H. Ellison
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/H._Ellison
======================================================================

Occupation:
Captain
of the
GS
12
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
H. Ellison
was a
starship
captain
in the
24th century
. Ellison was the
commander
of the
GS
12
.
In
2370
, Ellison was listed on the
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named after
Harlan Ellison
, the science fiction writer. His name was not mentioned in dialogue, it was simply listed in an
okudagram
graphic of an
arrival roster
on a
viewscreen
. The graphic was also populated by a number of other
in-jokes
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ha'Ryl
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ha'Ryl
======================================================================

Ha'Ryl
was a late
32nd century
individual
.
They were the
partner
of
Commander
Patrick Fickett
, the
first officer
of the
USS
Credence
, and they had two children named
Krenn
and
Na'el
. In
3190
, Ha'Ryl lost their partner, when
Qowat Milat
sister
J'Vini
murdered
Fickett. (
DIS
: "
Choose to Live
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hacopian
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hacopian
======================================================================

Doctor
Hacopian
was a
physician
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
.
That year, Doctor
Beverly Crusher
wanted to consult Hacopian and
Selar
after it appeared as if a
virus
had begun to ravage the
ship
, later determined to be the first case of
Barclay's Protomorphosis Syndrome
. (
TNG
: "
Genesis
")
This doctor was only mentioned in dialogue.
The character was named after writer
Brannon Braga
's chiropractor. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 289)

======================================================================
PAGE: Hagath
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hagath
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Arms dealer
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Played by
:
Steven Berkoff
"
I admire
confidence
in a man. In fact, I demand it of my
salesmen
. But in
business
, one must be careful not to grow too confident.
"
–
Hagath
,
2373
("
Business as Usual
")
Hagath
was a
humanoid
male
who operated as an
arms dealer
during the late-
24th century
. Ruthless and calculating, Hagath hated to be "disappointed" and frequently had subordinates who failed him put to death.
History
During the latter years of the
Bajoran Occupation
, Hagath had the foresight to provide the
Bajoran Resistance
with
weapons
that were ultimately used to defeat their
Cardassian
oppressors. Knowing the Cardassians would eventually lose their hold on
Bajor
, Hagath's actions later allowed him to operate in the
Bajor sector
without interference.
Gaila
was another business partner of his during this time.
In
2373
, Gaila suggested to Hagath that they employ his
cousin
Quark
on
Deep Space 9
, as Quark's
holosuites
were ideal for the showcasing and sale of weaponry without any actual weapons passing through the
station
, circumventing
Starfleet
regulations
. Quark, being heavily in debt at the time, accepted and for a time enjoyed substantial success.
During this time, Hagath invited another of his associates,
Farrakk
to the station, in order to express his disappointment with him for taking a vacation to
Risa
instead of doing his job and opening arms negotiations with the
Verillians
, who had instead signed a contract with
Metron Consortium
. For his failure, Hagath arranged for the
warp core
on
Farrakk's ship
to be blown up, killing Farrakk in the process.
Shortly after this, Hagath took on a new client, the
Regent of Palamar
, who wanted
biogenic weapons
capable of killing 28 million people. Quark, suffering from a crisis of conscience, spoiled the deal by inviting the Regent's arch-enemy,
General
Nassuc
, to Deep Space 9 at the same time. A firefight began when the two sides saw each other, alerting station
security
. Hagath and Gaila barely managed to escape the station alive, both with Nassuc's
Palamarian Freedom Brigade
purification squads
behind them. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")
Memorable quotes
"
Remember we sold a hundred and fifty thousand of these to the
Manchovites
.
"
"
And we sold another hundred thousand to the other side. Now there was a beautiful little war.
"
"
Too bad about the armistice.
"
-
Hagath
and
Gaila
"
If he were a bit shorter and his teeth were a little sharper, he'd make a perfect
Ferengi
.
"
-
Gaila
, of Hagath
"
My dear Quark, not every deal is about making money. Sometimes, you have to look at the big picture, and at times gaining a friend is more important than making profit.
"
-
Hagath
Appendices
Background information
Hagath was played by actor
Steven Berkoff
. He was credited as "Special Guest Star" in "
Business as Usual
".
Executive Producer
Ira Steven Behr
was thrilled with the casting of the character. "
Steven Berkoff is an excellent villain, actor, and all-around interesting dude,
" he said, "
We were glad to get him as Hagath.
" (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 438)
The script for "Business as Usual" gives the pronunciation of his name as "HAG-uth" and goes onto describe the character as, "
a tall, handsome and casually elegant alien
" with "
an air of authority that he wears lightly; he's clearly one of nature's aristocrats and comfortable in his own skin.
" It later describes him as belonging to the same race as his associate Farrakk.
[1]
Apocrypha
According to the
Star Trek: Terok Nor
novel
Dawn of the Eagles
, Hagath began supplying weapons to the Bajorans in
2363
. His Bajoran contact was named
Jas Holza
.
The
Worlds of Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
novella
Satisfaction is Not Guaranteed
reveals that Hagath died sometime prior to
2376
.
Hagath also has a card in the
Star Trek Customizable Card Game
which describes him as "
Ruthless. Treats his business associates like family – until they cross him.
" He is also shown to have qualifications in anthropology and engineering.
External links
Hagath
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

.

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: Hain
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hain
======================================================================

Occupation:
Thief
Status:
Arrested
(
2374
)
Played by
:
Gregory Itzin
Hain
was a
male
thief
in the
24th century
.
In
2365
, Hain and associates
Larell
,
Krit
,
Nahsk
, and
Morn
, planned and executed the
Lissepian Mother's Day Heist
, a notorious
robbery
that occurred on the
planet
Lissepia
, in which one thousand
bricks
of
gold-pressed latinum
was stolen from the planet's
Central Bank
. Though all thieves managed to escape the authorities on Lissepia, Morn double-crossed Hain and the others by keeping the latinum for himself. He let him go for fear of being pursued by authorities.
In
2374
, the
statute of limitations
expired for the
crime
, so Hain decided to go after Morn; however, he learned that the
Lurian
had since died. Discovering that
Quark
was Morn's
heir
, he traveled to
Deep Space 9
. He broke into Quark's
quarters
just as he was about to call for the latinum from the
Bank of Bolias
. Claiming to be a member of
Lurian Security
, he said Morn was the ex-crown
prince
of the
Royal Family of Luria
, and that his riches were their property.
Hain was about to arrest Quark when he learned that the other thieves in their group had also approached Quark to get the latinum, and so he tried to have the others arrested with Quark's help. However, due to interference he met all the others in Quark's quarters and they all agreed to split the money instead. However, when they all found the latinum in a
cargo bay
, they pulled
phasers
on each other.
Hain was wounded and
arrested
with the others by
security
. No one realized Morn had extracted the
latinum
and stored it in his second
stomach
. (
DS9
: "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
Hain was played by Trek regular
Gregory Itzin
.
The script described Hain as:
"an imposing
Human
Alien
male [...] dressed in twenty-fourth century business attire.
"

2374

sensor array
in the

Doctor
Bashir
had to take over
conn
.

Angie Kirby
, the
ensign
found Bashir's claim difficult to swallow. This prompted him to ask
Jadzia Dax
for confirmation, which Dax agreed to supply. (
DS9
: "
Behind the Lines
")

According to the script, the lieutenant's name was pronounced as "hahj".

PAGE: Hargrove
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hargrove
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Lieutenant
Hargrove
was a male
Starfleet
officer
aboard the
USS
Voyager
, serving under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
. Hargrove's
quarters
were on
deck 7
on
Voyager
.
In
2371
, Hargrove was being tested by
Voyager
's
Emergency Medical Hologram
for
Arethian flu
. While
The Doctor
was preparing Hargrove's
culture
, he was accidentally deactivated by
Ensign
Kyoto
. (
VOY
: "
Eye of the Needle
")
When
Kes
began to develop
telepathic
abilities, she once sensed Hargrove's presence in
sickbay
, although he had left hours before. (
VOY
: "
Cathexis
")
When the
Voyager
was affected by an
inversion field
in 2371, Hargrove's quarters suddenly were located on Deck 8 near Kyoto's,
Susan Nicoletti
's, and
Ayala
's quarters, which were also normally on other decks. The fact that Kes remembered exactly the location of Hargrove's quarters aroused
Neelix
's suspicion. (
VOY
: "
Twisted
")
As part of a vision produced by the
Botha
in
2372
, Captain Janeway noticed
cucumber sandwiches
among food Neelix had prepared for her, who claimed Hargrove had asked for them. (
VOY
: "
Persistence of Vision
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Harris (Captain)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Harris_(Captain)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commanding officer
,
USS
Excalibur
Status:
Deceased
(
KIA
)
(
2268
)
Died:
2268
,
USS
Excalibur
Captain
Harris
was a
23rd century
Starfleet
officer
who served as the
commanding officer
of the
Federation
starship
USS
Excalibur
during the late
2260s
.
In
2268
, Harris commanded the
Excalibur
for the
war games problems
of the disastrous
M-5 drills
. When the
M-5
malfunctioned in control of the
USS
Enterprise
, commanded by
James T. Kirk
, it caused the
Enterprise
phasers
to fire full-power on the other ships that participated in the games, severely damaging the unshielded
Excalibur
, Harris and
his first officer
died early in the conflict, and all of the ship's approximately 400 crewmen were eventually lost.
The M-5 recorded, at Kirk's request, that no life remained aboard the hulk of
Excalibur
. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Captain Harris was given the first name Paul in
Who's Who in Star Trek 2
. The novel
After the Fall
calls him Anton Harris.
An
M. Harris
operated in the same century, being listed on the
USS
Enterprise
dedication plaque.

officer
in the

.

, he was assigned to the
Engineering
Department of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. When a
preburner
was turned offline after it was discovered that the
deuterium
flow was 2
%
higher, the unit was turned off.
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
planned for Harris to pull it from the
matter-antimatter reaction assembly
and perform a full
watt
test on the unit a day later. (
TNG-R
: "
Galaxy's Child
")

For the remastered edition of "Galaxy's Child", the original
okudagram
was replaced by a new graphic. Harris was mentioned in that graphic.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hartla
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hartla
======================================================================

Hartla
was a
dabo girl
at
Quark's
on
Terok Nor
during the
Cardassian
Occupation of Bajor
. She was still working there as of
2370
. One of her special clients was a Cardassian
officer
named
Boheeka
.
When
Quark
contacted Boheeka about a clandestine deal he wished to make, Boheeka reminisced about missing Hartla. When Quark suggested the feeling was likely mutual, Boheeka agreed, but not for the same reasons, observing that she would have
bankrupted
him had the Occupation lasted longer. (
DS9
: "
The Wire
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 324 spelled her name as "Haltla".

")

.

novelization
of

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hendriks
CATEGORIES: Individuals

======================================================================

Hendriks
was a member of
Starfleet
in the
2370s
. He was stationed on
Ajilon Prime
in
2373
.
In that year, during the
Klingon
invasion, Hendriks was terrified by the attacks and ran. (
DS9
: "
Nor the Battle to the Strong
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================

Doctor
Henri Roget
was a
physician
who worked at the
Central Hospital of Altair IV
in the
2370s
.
In
2371
, he, along with
April Wade
,
Ghee P'Trell
,
Senva
, and
Julian Bashir
, were nominated for the
Carrington Award
. Upon hearing that Roget had won,
Miles O'Brien
and
Benjamin Sisko
both expressed their surprise that Bashir wasn't chosen. (
DS9
: "
Prophet Motive
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Herman Zimmerman (Starfleet)

Herman Zimmerman
, or
H. Zimmerman
, was a
Starfleet
officer
from the late
23rd
to late
24th century
.
In
2293
,
Captain
Zimmerman was assigned to the
Office of Science Ops
, according to the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-B
. (
Star Trek Generations
,
dedication plaque
)
According to the unseen fourth page of the
Operation Retrieve
chart, Zimmerman was the
captain
of the
USS
Republic
(NCC-1371), which was at the time located in
Sector 22956
.
He was named as a
design engineer
on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Stargazer
, which was launched in
2326
. (
TNG
: "
The Battle
")
This was referenced listed as "H. Zimmerman".
In
2364
, he was the
commander
of the
USS
Nightwing
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
In
2368
, he was listed as being part of the
Engineering Group
on the second
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2370
, he was the commander of the
USS
Yorktown
on a
Deep Space 9
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
okudagram
)
This was referenced also listed as "H. Zimmerman".
He was listed as being the part of the
Science Ops
team on the dedication plaques of
Defiant
-class
starships
USS
Defiant
(
2370
),
USS
Valiant
(
2372
), and
USS
Sao Paulo
(
2375
). (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
USS Valiant dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2372
, he was listed as belong to the
Spaceframe Development
team on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
,
dedication plaque
)
In
2401
, he was listed as a member of the
Admiralty Board
on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS
Stargazer
,
USS
Titan
-A
, and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after scenery designer
Herman Zimmerman
, who worked on many of the
Star Trek
series.
He was listed as a member of
Fleet Ops
at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

PAGE: Hickman
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hickman
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Astrophysics laboratory
Status:
Active
(
2384
)
For the USS
Bellerophon
crewmember, please see
Hickam
. For the USS
Victory
crewmember, please see
Paul Hickman
.
Ensign
Hickman
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
who served in
astrophysics
aboard the
USS
Voyager
in
2371
. In that year, along with the rest of the crew, Hickman was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
by the entity known as "the
Caretaker
."
In
2373
, upon entering
Borg space
,
Captain
Janeway
turned to the
personal logs
of all of the Starfleet captains who had encountered the
Borg
. Reading selected passages out loud to
Commander
Chakotay
, Janeway was informed that she had been using the
inflections
of the
individuals
whom she was quoting, and that Ensign Hickman did a "passable" impression of Janeway, although Janeway speculated that there was probably a
regulation
against
imitating
the captain. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
The next year
, as speculation began to run rampant aboard
Voyager
regarding Captain Janeway's actions subsequent to the detection of the
Omega molecule
, Hickman guessed that they were attempting to seal an opening to
fluidic space
. (
VOY
: "
The Omega Directive
")
In
2384
, Hickman served aboard the
USS
Dauntless
as an
astrophysics
officer. Hickman was listed with other crew members on an information panel in the ship's
transporter room
. He was listed as a crewmember on leave. (
PRO
: "
Supernova, Part 1
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
A clear view of the information panels were provided by Dominique Rossier of Wardenlight Studio.
[1]
,
[2]

PAGE: Hider
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hider
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Krall
Status:
Unknown
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Fraser Aitcheson
Hider
was an
alien
male
who was stranded on the
planet
Altamid
by
Krall
.
Along with
Fi'Ja
and
Zavanko
, he approached a
crashed
Kelvin pod
and tried to attack
Montgomery Scott
, though they were stopped by
Jaylah
; after a brief fight, they eventually left her and Scotty alone. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Hider was portrayed by
Fraser Aitcheson
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
In concept artwork, this character was referred to as "Quills", whereas the name "Hider" was applied to Fi'Ja.
[1]
[2]
In
Prosthetics
magazine (issue 5, pp. 12, 15), this character was again called "Quills", though the same publication referred to Zavanko as "Hider Zavanko".
[3]
[4]
A picture on Fraser Aitcheson's Instagram account identifies this character as the one he played in
Star Trek Beyond
.
[5]
In the film's credits sequence, the character played by Aitcheson is referred to as "Hider".

PAGE: Hilary Groner (24th century)

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hilary_Groner_(24th_century)
======================================================================

Hilary Groner
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried her from
Denkir Orbital City
to
Janus VI
. Her special accommodations were a
null grav suite
and
class N
atmosphere
suite. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Groner was named after
CBS Digital Technical Operation Department
employee
Hilary Groner
.

======================================================================

Hildebrandt
was a
scientist
whose observations of
tachyon
propagation appeared to be fairly consistent with a Hucklesby field density. This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Marvin Hildebrandt
, a colorist at
CBS Digital
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hill (Doctor)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hill_(Doctor)
======================================================================

Doctor
Hill
was a
physician
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in early
2367
.
After Doctor
Beverly Crusher
became trapped in a
warp bubble
, her thoughts created an entirely separate
universe
where people and objects began to disappear. In this alternate universe, Crusher tried to call Hill and
Selar
to
sickbay
in order to assist in an examination of
Miles O'Brien
. However, both had disappeared and their families and colleagues had no recollection of them. (
TNG
: "
Remember Me
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
cut
scene 15A from "Remember Me" established Hill's first name was Richard and that he was Crusher's staff
osteopath
. He came on board six
months
prior to the episode
with his
wife
,
Cara
, who was an
exobiologist
.
[1]

24th century
.
When
Kira Nerys
was investigating the murder of
Shakaar resistance
members in
2373
, Hoffmeister's name appeared on a suspect list. His identity number, name, and location were listed on this list. He was located in the
Grewler sector
. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Holden
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Holden
======================================================================

Species:
Unnamed
Affiliation:
Federation
Rank:
Doctor
Occupation:
Outpost scientist
,
Outpost 76
Status:
Arrested
(
2381
)
Played by
:
Paul F. Tompkins
Doctor
Holden
was an
alien
male
Federation
outpost scientist
assigned to
Outpost 76
in
2381
.
Holden colluded with one of the native
Scrubble
on
Jengus IV
to manufacture a crisis to lure a
Starfleet
vessel to Jengus IV in order to plant a
listening device
in the
Captain's ready room
and acquire Starfleet secrets they could sell on the
black market
. Though they successfully lured the
USS
Carlsbad
and
USS
Cerritos
to Jengus IV, in the guise of peace talks, the plot was uncovered by
D'Vana Tendi
who noted that the gift the Scrubble was presenting the captains contained a listening device.
When their duplicity was revealed, both Holden and the
Scrubble emissary
were taken into custody to face Federation
tribunal
. (
LD
: "
Mining The Mind's Mines
")
Holden was voiced by
Paul F. Tompkins
His name was not stated on screen, but rather, was shown on an onscreen display.

Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Active
(
24th century
)
Holt
was a
scientist
.
This individual, along with
Abrahamian
, described conditions where causality cannot be established for
tachyons
, on the
BD Wray manifold
, possessing conformal symmetry in the past and future.
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
James Holt
, a digital compositor and flame artist at
CBS Digital
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Homn
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Homn
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Affiliation:
Fifth House of Betazed
Occupation:
Valet
to
Ambassador
Lwaxana Troi
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Carel Struycken
"
Mr. Homn is my valet. He doesn't say much.
"
–
Lwaxana Troi
,
2367
("
Half a Life
")
Mr.
Homn
was the
valet
of
Betazoid
Ambassador
Lwaxana Troi
. Wherever she went, he usually followed.
Quick Answers
How did Mr. Homn preserve Kestra Troi's memory?
Mr. Homn preserved Kestra Troi's memory by secretly saving a photograph of her. Although Lwaxana Troi attempted to erase all evidence of Kestra following her death, Homn kept the photograph hidden for forty years.
Provided by: Fandom
What role did Mr. Homn play in Lwaxana Troi's life?
Mr. Homn was Lwaxana Troi's manservant, hired before 2364 to replace Mr. Xelo. He traveled with Lwaxana to the USS Enterprise-D for her daughter Deanna's almost-wedding in 2364. Homn supported Lwaxana during her ambassadorial duties in 2365 and helped prepare a picnic on Betazed in 2366. Although he did not accompany her to Deep Space 9, Homn was a consistent presence, assisting Lwaxana on various occasions.
Provided by: Fandom
When did Mr. Homn become Lwaxana Troi's valet?
Mr. Homn became Lwaxana Troi's valet before 2364, succeeding Mr. Xelo. He traveled with Lwaxana to the USS Enterprise-D in 2364 for Deanna Troi's almost-wedding to Wyatt Miller.
Provided by: Fandom
What is Mr. Homn's connection to the Troi family?
Mr. Homn had connections to the Troi family since the 2330s. He preserved a photograph of Kestra Troi, Lwaxana's deceased daughter. By 2364, he became Lwaxana Troi's manservant, succeeding Mr. Xelo. Homn traveled with Lwaxana to the USS Enterprise-D for Deanna Troi's almost-wedding and supported her during a conference on Pacifica in 2365. He also helped organize a picnic on Betazed in 2366.
Provided by: Fandom
How is Mr. Homn involved in 'Star Trek: The Next Generation'?
Mr. Homn, played by Carel Struycken, is featured in 'Star Trek: The Next Generation' as Lwaxana Troi's assistant. Debuting in the episode 'Haven' in November 1987, Homn appeared in five episodes. Known for his silence, he spoke only once, thanking Captain Picard for drinks. He traveled with Lwaxana to the USS Enterprise-D for events like Deanna Troi's near-wedding in 2364.
Provided by: Fandom
Biography
Homn had ties to the Troi family as early as the
2330s
. When Lwaxana Troi began destroying all evidence of
Kestra
, a daughter that died in childhood, he secretly saved a photograph of Kestra, which he kept hidden for the next forty years. (
TNG
: "
Dark Page
")
It's unclear if he was employed by the Troi family during the incident, and if so, in what capacity, since he was described as Lwaxana's new manservant when he appeared in "
Haven
".
Homn was hired as Lwaxana's manservant prior to
2364
as a replacement for Mr.
Xelo
. He traveled with Lwaxana to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in 2364 for the almost-wedding between Lwaxana's daughter,
Deanna
, and
Wyatt Miller
. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Although Homn was known to the Troi family prior to the wedding, Deanna didn't appear to recognize him.
Homn returned with Lwaxana to the
Enterprise
-D in
2365
when Lwaxana was sent as an
ambassador
to the conference on
Pacifica
. He assisted Lwaxana during the trip on her ill-fated attempt to find a husband. (
TNG
: "
Manhunt
")
In
2366
, Homn helped prepare a
picnic
on
Betazed
for Lwaxana, Deanna, and guest
William T. Riker
, although all three were subsequently captured by
DaiMon
Tog
. (
TNG
: "
Ménage à Troi
")
In
2367
, Homn and Lwaxana were given passage from a conference back to Betazed aboard the
Enterprise
-D. (
TNG
: "
Half a Life
")
Homn assisted Lwaxana in her almost-wedding to
Campio
, the minister of
Kostolain
, aboard the
Enterprise
-D in
2368
. He helped alter Lwaxana's wedding dress, which had previously been
Campio
's mother's, when she was to
marry
Campio. (
TNG
: "
Cost Of Living
")
When Lwaxana fell into a coma aboard the
Enterprise
-D in
2370
, Homn was one of the people Deanna spoke to in an effort to find out what was causing the coma. After it was discovered that the coma was part of Lwaxana's efforts to suppress the memory of her dead daughter Kestra and Lwaxana had come to terms with the accident, Homn returned the photo of Kestra he had saved to the Troi family. (
TNG
: "
Dark Page
")
Homn did not travel with Lwaxana for any of her visits to
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
The Forsaken
", "
Fascination
", "
The Muse
")
Personal life
Homn often joined Lwaxana on her voyages and was reported to frequently have "outrageously lustful thoughts." Given her penchant for claiming this of many people,
Jean-Luc Picard
included, the veracity of this assertion is questionable at best. (
TNG
: "
Manhunt
")
He had an indulgent temperament in more than one sense of the word. It could be seen in the way he patiently catered to the (sometimes exasperating) whims of his employer, as well as his quiet indulgences in the "perks" of his employment. For example, he apparently had no inhibitions in finishing off half empty glasses of wine while cleaning up dishes, and once even snacked on the very berries he was sent to pick for his employer. (
TNG
: "
Ménage à Troi
")
Appearance and habits
Homn was a tall, pale
humanoid
man with two fin-like ears who rarely spoke save in
sign language
, a skill at which he was quite adept. (
TNG
: "
Haven
") Homn possessed strength considerably superior to that of a
Human
, and was frequently seen consuming very large amounts of
alcohol
without experiencing any apparent effects. (
TNG
: "
Manhunt
")
During Lwaxana Troi's visits to the
Enterprise
-D, Homn was known to have spoken only once, to
Captain
Picard
: "
Thank you for the drinks.
" (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Appendices
Appearances
TNG
:
"
Haven
"
"
Manhunt
"
"
Ménage à Troi
"
"
Half a Life
"
"
Cost Of Living
"
"
Dark Page
" (mentioned)
Background information
Homn was portrayed by
Carel Struycken
. In all of his on-screen appearances, Homn has had only one line of dialogue, in the
first season
episode "
Haven
": "
Thank you for the drinks.
"
The script notes from "Haven" described his first appearance, from the transporter pad, as "
a gaunt figure in a flowing black robe materializes. He has faded white-grey skin and beady purple eyes. He moves smoothly and	deliberately. Quite a ghostly figure this Mr. Homn.
"
"hom" was the pronunciation of this character's name from the script pronunciation guide for "Manhunt".
[1]
Neither his species nor his homeworld have been revealed. His position and demeanor (but not his appearance) are very similar to that of a
Hupyrian
, the members of which species are also highly prized in the galaxy as manservants and who only speak to their employers.
Apocrypha
Homn appears in the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
comics "
Lwaxana Troi and the Wedding of Doom
" and "
Four Funerals and a Wedding
", where he accompanies Lwaxana to
Deep Space 9
so Lwaxana can marry a
Bolian
called Var Ulos.
In the non-
canon
story
Triangle: Imzadi II
it is revealed that, though his appearance and normal traits would insist the contrary, Mr. Homn is revealed to be rather fast, easily dodging the
disruptor
fire of Romulan troops.
In the non-canon story "The Ceremony of Innocence is Drowned" in the anthology
Tales of the Dominion War
, Mr. Homn is killed during the
Battle of Betazed
, shielding Lwaxana and her half-
Tavnian
son when the Troi household collapses under
Dominion
fire. Shortly before his death, Lwaxana confides to two of her Human friends that she exaggerated Homn's lustful thoughts about her.
External link
Homn
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Hope
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hope
======================================================================

For the emotion, please see
hope (feeling)
.
Hope
was the
sister
of
Angie Harkins
, and sister-in-law of
Commander
Pete Harkins
, who lived during the late
24th century
. Hope lived in
Boston
and loved
cats
.
In
2376
, Hope traveled to
San Francisco
to visit the Harkins. Commander Harkins suggested to
Reginald Barclay
to drop by their place and meet Hope, as he thought she might like Barclay. He initially refused, but later changed his mind.
Barclay later told
Deanna Troi
about Hope. (
VOY
: "
Pathfinder
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Evidently, things with Hope didn't work out, as Reg would later become involved with
Leosa
.

Star Trek: Starfleet Academy

spoilers
.

How'el Et-Mon
was an individual who served as a
Starfleet
officer
.
In
3195
, they were listed with other luminaries of
Starfleet Academy
on a
commemoration wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
"
set decoration
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hugh
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hugh
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Borg Artifact Research Institute
,
Federation
(
citizen
status)
Occupation:
Executive director
of the
Borg Reclamation Project
Status:
Deceased
(
2399
)
Died:
2399,
The
Artifact
Played by
:
Jonathan Del Arco
Third of Five
redirects here; for a Borg drone with a similar designation, please see
Three of Five
.
For the USS
Discovery
physician, please see
Hugh Culber
.
"
I will not.
"
–
Hugh
, displaying individuality
,
2368
("
I Borg
")
Hugh
(pronounced "Hue"), formerly
Third of Five
, was a former
Borg drone
who was rescued by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2368
. Once again joining the
Enterprise
to fight against rogue
Borg
led by
Lore
in
2370
, by
2399
he had come to serve a prominent position as a part of the
Borg Artifact Research Institute
, identifying as an
xB
like those aboard the
Artifact
.
Quick Answers
What is the significance of Hugh's name 'Third of Five'?
The name 'Third of Five' denotes Hugh's rank within a Borg group. This ordinal naming is distinct from other Borg, who typically use cardinal numbers. Before becoming a Borg, Hugh was a Ktarian male, identified as a crucial drone by Magnus and Erin Hansen.
Provided by: Fandom
How did Hugh come to serve at the Borg Artifact Research Institute?
In 2368, Hugh was found critically injured in the Argolis Cluster and was treated on the Enterprise-D. Post-recovery, he transitioned into an 'xB', a former Borg drone, and became a Federation citizen. By 2399, he was appointed as the executive director of the Borg Reclamation Project at 'the Artifact', a derelict Borg cube under the control of the Romulan Free State.
Provided by: Fandom
What led to Hugh's critical injury found at the Argolis Cluster?
In the Argolis Cluster, Hugh was critically injured at a Borg scout ship crash site. Dr. Beverly Crusher transported him to the Enterprise-D for medical care, despite Captain Picard's objections. La Forge subsequently repaired Hugh, establishing a friendship and naming him.
Provided by: Fandom
Who is Lore and what is his connection to Hugh?
Lore is a figure who failed to deliver on his leadership promises to a drone crew, including Hugh. Hugh, disillusioned with Lore, led a faction of drones and became a leader. He and his followers aided the Enterprise-D crew in rescuing Picard, La Forge, Troi, and recapturing Data from Lore. Hugh eventually transitioned into an 'xB', a designation for ex-Borg.
Provided by: Fandom
What role does Hugh play in 'Star Trek: The Next Generation' episode 'I, Borg'?
In 'Star Trek: The Next Generation' episode 'I, Borg', Hugh is a Borg drone guarded in the Enterprise's science lab and Captain Picard's ready room. Recognizing Picard as Locutus, Hugh is influenced by the crew, learning resistance isn't futile. Offered asylum on the Enterprise, he returns to the Borg Collective, causing a catastrophic separation of his ship from the Collective due to his individuality.
Provided by: Fandom
Capture
The Borg scout ship, where Hugh was found in 2368
Hugh was found in the
Argolis Cluster
at the
crash
site of a
Borg scout ship
.
Hugh himself was critically injured, and was brought back to the
Enterprise
-D for medical attention by
Dr.
Beverly Crusher
under protest from
Captain
Picard
. Upon arriving on the
Enterprise
-D, Hugh was placed in the
brig
and kept under guard at all times. He had trouble adapting to his new environment, having been cut off from the resources of the
Collective
for the first time in his life. Where there had been millions of voices in his head, there was now only his own. Dr. Crusher eventually nursed him back to health, and
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
found a way to nourish the drone similar to the
regeneration alcoves
on a Borg ship.
Captain Picard saw this as an opportunity to destroy the Borg once and for all. He ordered La Forge and
Lieutenant Commander
Data
to devise a method to destroy the Collective through an implanted program or file in Hugh, much as Data had done to a
Borg cube
through Captain Picard (as
Locutus
) two
years
earlier. Dr. Crusher did not agree with this course of action, because they were talking about
exterminating an entire race
– even if it was the Borg.
Newfound individuality
Hugh examines
Livingston
in Captain Picard's
ready room
As Hugh spent more time with the
Enterprise
-D
crew
, he learned many things. La Forge taught him that
Humans
did not want to be
assimilated
into the Collective, which contradicted what Hugh had been taught by the Collective. La Forge gave Hugh his name, through a mispronunciation of the word "you", and became Hugh's first ever friend.
Guinan
, who originally wanted nothing to do with Hugh, taught him that resistance was
not
futile.
Guinan's homeworld
had been destroyed long ago by the Borg, but the fact that Guinan was still alive was proof of her lessons to Hugh.
Picard eventually confronted Hugh. Hugh immediately recognized Picard as Locutus, and Picard took the role of Locutus while talking to Hugh, to simulate the authority that Hugh was used to. However, La Forge, Guinan, Dr. Crusher, and other members of the
Enterprise
-D crew had had a profound effect on Hugh. When Picard reminded him that resistance was futile, Hugh replied that resistance was
not
futile. When Picard told Hugh that La Forge would be assimilated, Hugh stated that La Forge did not wish to be assimilated. When Picard said that this was irrelevant, Hugh specifically said that he (as Hugh used the word "
I
" rather than "we"; his second use of a singular pronoun) would not assist in the assimilation of La Forge. Picard was stunned that a Borg drone would say such things.
Picard decided that he could not send Hugh back with the file that would destroy the Borg. He offered Hugh
asylum
on board the
Enterprise
-D, but Hugh said that the Collective would not stop looking for him until they found him. Hugh agreed to go back to the crash site, and to be taken back into the Collective, to protect the
Enterprise
-D from harm. (
TNG
: "
I Borg
")
After the
Enterprise
-D
Hugh's newfound sense of individuality proved to be almost as deadly to the Collective as any file stored in his memory could be. His experiences on board the
Enterprise
-D were spread to other drones, and eventually Hugh's cube fell into a complete state of disarray. The Borg's sense of shared identity had been disrupted, and they were no longer able to function. The Collective disconnected Hugh's ship from the hive mind and left the cube drifting in space until
2370
, when it was discovered by
Lore
, Data's
brother
. Lore took advantage of the confused drones and assumed leadership of the group. He brought order to chaos, and allowed the drones to maintain their individuality but was able to control them. Lore "promised" to create the Borg crew in his image, running tests on various drones, leaving them with awful injuries.
Hugh disapproved of Lore's leadership, finally realizing that Lore had no clue how to keep his promise to the drone crew, and while Lore had acquired a ship and had built a base on a remote planet, Hugh took a group of drones underground and became a leader himself. Hugh and the drones loyal to him assisted the
Enterprise
-D crew in rescuing Picard, La Forge, and
Troi
and recapturing Data from Lore. (
TNG
: "
Descent
", "
Descent, Part II
")
Working with the Romulans
Hugh eventually became an "
xB
", as former Borg drones were called, with most of his implants removed; he also became a
Federation
citizen
.
By
2399
, he had been named
executive director
of the
Borg Reclamation Project
based at "the
Artifact
", a derelict Borg cube controlled by the
Romulan Free State
. While serving the project, Hugh became disturbed by the dismissive treatment of his fellow xBs after they were severed from the Collective. He clarified to
Soji Asha
that xBs had by now become the most despised people in the
galaxy
, seen either as property to be exploited or hazards to be warehoused – or both, in the case of the Romulans. However, it was also Soji who impressed him with her empathy toward a recently severed Romulan xB, when she spoke to him in
Romulan
even though he was still unconscious. Soji stated that she was merely putting Hugh's own ideas on the treatment of xBs into practice. Soji's act of respect netted her Hugh's permission to interview the xB
Ramdha
, which he had previously denied her because of Ramdha's fragile state of mind. Although initially pleased with Soji's progress, Hugh's concerns were ultimately validated when the mentally disordered Ramdha became hysterical and attempted
suicide
, which was only narrowly averted thanks to Soji. (
PIC
: "
The End is the Beginning
", "
The Impossible Box
")
Jean-Luc Picard arrived at the Artifact sometime later looking for Soji, meeting Hugh again for the first time in nearly thirty years. Hugh showed Picard the facilities in which he helped the xBs attempt to reclaim their lives after the removal of their implants, believing that as an xB himself, Picard would be an advocate for better treatment for former Borg.
Hugh knew that a Romulan spy,
Narek
, had arrived at the Artifact two weeks before Picard, trying to find out about Soji, and offered Picard his help without hesitation. When Soji's
android
abilities were activated and she fled from Narek and the
Zhat Vash
, Hugh took Picard and Soji into the old cube's
queencell
and used its
spatial trajector
to teleport them to
Nepenthe
, while Picard's sworn protector, a young Romulan named
Elnor
, remained behind with Hugh to cover their escape. (
PIC
: "
The Impossible Box
")
Hugh was
interrogated
by Narek's sister,
Narissa
, who coldly had the xBs
executed
when he refused to give up Picard's location; she only refrained from killing Hugh herself because of the treaty with the Federation. Hugh felt that he had failed the xBs, and ultimately decided to deny the Romulans the cube by activating the queencell. However, as this was a treaty violation as well as an act of insurrection, Narissa now felt justified in killing him, and mortally wounded him with a knife thrown into his neck.
Hugh's final words
Before he died, Hugh told Elnor that he needed an xB to activate the queencell, and thanked him for letting him briefly be a "hopeful fool" again. (
PIC
: "
Nepenthe
")
Appendices
Appearances
TNG
:
"
I Borg
"
"
Descent
"

Descent, Part II
"
