"
"
Nepenthe
"
Background information
Hugh was played by actor
Jonathan Del Arco
.
Hugh's designation used the ordinal "Third," while the
Star Trek: Voyager
character
Seven of Nine
used the cardinal "Seven." The concept behind Hugh was used by the creative staff of
Voyager
in the form of
One
. Del Arco stated on Twitter that his species was Human.
[1]
The name "Hugh" is a pun, as it sounds like the English word "you," conveying the message of individuality. In the original German TV frame, this pun was only partially transferred, as he was consistently called "Du" ("You" in English). In repeated airings, as well as on the
DVD
, the name was changed to "Hugh." In the Italian version the pun was transferred: the Borg was called Thug, as it sounds similar to the Italian word "tu" (you). In the Japanese version, the you/Hugh pun did not make sense and he is instead given the name "Blue." In the Czech version, the pun was transferred by calling him "Tim" which is very similar in pronunciation to the Czech word "ty" meaning "you". In the French version, Hugh is called "Lou".
As Hugh's character was written before the expanded information about the Borg revealed in
Star Trek: Voyager
, which states that all Borg are assimilated and do not reproduce as suggested in the episode
TNG
: "
Q Who
", there is no canon mention of who Hugh was before he was assimilated, nor any attempts to reassert this identity after he was freed from the collective as is seen in all Borg characters who are liberated from the hive mind. In keeping with this, he continues to be known simply as Hugh even as long after his liberation from the Collective as of 2399.
Del Arco was invited to return early in pre-production on
Star Trek: Picard
by his friend
James Duff
, before
Patrick Stewart
, when the show was only a Borg-focused spin-off. Little backstory was provided to him for what Hugh had been doing since his appearances on
The Next Generation
due to the hurried production on
Picard
, beyond being told the character was "broken." Del Arco personally believed Hugh had dedicated himself to bettering the lives of the Borg who had broken from the Collective in "
Descent, Part II
" and accepted the assignment on the Artifact to protect the xBs. He did not learn Hugh would be killed off until reading it in the script. He later lamented the missed opportunity to tell stories with both Hugh and Seven of Nine, as well as not having Hugh see or inquire about Geordi.
[2]
Apocrypha
Hugh appears in the
Shatnerverse
novel
Avenger
, where his group of Borg are able to save the resurrected Captain
James T. Kirk
by removing the Borg nanites that were damaging his system after his rebirth.
Hugh meets the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
in the novel
Greater than the Sum
, where he sacrifices himself to help the
Enterprise
stop a new group of Borg created by the evolution of a Borg cube and the assimilation of
Admiral
Kathryn Janeway
.
Hugh appears in the
Star Trek Online
(STO) expansion "Delta Rising" as a leader of the
Cooperative
. The Cooperative is a faction of liberated Borg drones whose mission is primarily to free more drones from the Collective. While the Cooperative shares the Collective's goal of achieving perfection, they do not force it upon anyone and any Borg they liberate is free to choose what they want to do afterwards, including returning to the Collective. As
Icheb
was removed from the game following his death in
Star Trek: Picard
, Hugh was removed as well. This was eventually reversed in the mission "Axiom", released in February 2025, with Jonathan Del Arco voicing him; in STO's timeline, Hugh appears to be alive and well, and still running the Borg Reclamation Project in 2412. Hugh and the Cooperative are summoned by the Aetherians, a race battling the Borg in a multiversal conflict, to aid them with an outbreak of Borg fused with
Control
nanites; however, he discovers the Aetherians are not what they seem.
In
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine - Millennium
, it is mentioned in an alternate timeline that Hugh and Seven negotiated a treaty between the Borg and the Federation in
2390
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Hyde (alien)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Hyde_(alien)
======================================================================

Species:
Unnamed
Occupation:
Worker at
Kynk's Mugatoland
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
Played by
:
Paul F. Tompkins
Hyde
was an unscrupulous
humanoid
who lived during the late
24th century
.
In
2381
, Hyde landed on
Frylon IV
in his
shuttle
, the
Ladyjane
, to purchase a dozen
mugato
horns
from
Ferengi
poacher
Kynk
. Their meeting was interrupted by an
away team
from the
USS
Cerritos
, which led to a fight in which the caged mugatos in Kynk's camp were accidentally released. Chased by a mugato, Hyde took off in the
Ladyjane
without his merchandise.
While leaving
orbit
, the
Ladyjane
was
hailed
by the
Cerritos
, as
Captain
Carol Freeman
wished to know if Hyde had encountered her team. Hyde brushed her off, arousing her suspicions and causing her to order a
tractor beam
on the
Ladyjane
. Sensing an opportunity, Hyde
self-destructed
his shuttle and, upon being
beamed
to the
Cerritos
, made a spectacle of mourning the loss. Freeman offered him the
Joshua Tree
in recompense, and Hyde was able to further play on her guilt to acquire numerous other items, including all the decorations in Freeman's
ready room
.
Freeman was later alerted to the
scam
by
her husband
, and caught up with Hyde. In lieu of
arrest
and incarceration in a
Federation
penal colony
, she gave him the option of "do[ing] some good for a change" at the newly established
Kynk's Mugatoland
. Hyde accepted, and was put to work shoveling mugato
dung
. (
LD
: "
Mugato, Gumato
")

I. Feinberg
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
who served under
Vice Admiral
Leyton
.
In
2372
, Feinberg was assigned to
Strategic Operations
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49295. Feinberg was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character appears to have been named after
Star Trek
's original property master
Irving Feinberg
.

======================================================================
PAGE: I. McWatt
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/I._McWatt
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
FSO
officer
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
I. McWatt
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In the
2360s
, this individual served under
Captain
Leyton
aboard the
USS
Okinawa
when
Benjamin Sisko
was the
ship
's
executive officer
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Fleet Supply Office
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49293. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in Sisko's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
The unauthorized reference book
The Nitpicker's Guide for Deep Space Nine Trekkers
(p. 339) notes that the crew of the
Okinawa
(apart from Sisko) were named after characters in
Joseph Heller
's novel
Catch-22
.
McWatt
was a pilot in the novel.

")

technician

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ijarna
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ijarna
======================================================================

Ijarna
was an inhabitant of
Deep Space 9
.
In
2372
, he was
assaulted
by his
wife
following an incident of
public lewdness
on the
Promenade
. The night of the reported assault,
Odo
had caught the two "
misbehaving
" behind the
information kiosk
on the second level.
Odo briefed the incident the following Tuesday with
Kira Nerys
during their weekly review of the
criminal activity report
. Kira wondered why Ijarna still stayed with his wife, as that was not the first time she had assaulted him – a question she recanted upon learning of the kiosk incident. (
DS9
: "
Crossfire
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Ijarna was pronounced as "ih-ZHAR-nuh".

PAGE: Immamura
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Immamura
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2267
)
Lieutenant
Immamura
was a
male
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
. He served aboard the
USS
Enterprise
under
Captain
James T. Kirk
, during a
historic
five-year mission
that
starship
undertook.
In
2267
, Immamura was assigned to
landing party
number 2, under the command of
Lieutenant
Kelowitz
. This party was searching for the missing
shuttlecraft
Galileo
, which had
crashed
on the
planet
Taurus II
. When the party was ambushed by
the planet's inhabitants
, Immamura suffered a
dislocated
shoulder
and severe
lacerations
, which were later treated in the
Enterprise
's
sickbay
. (
TOS
: "
The Galileo Seven
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

)

,

India Shigihara
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. Her
serial number
was NN-087-1876 LDP.
In
2371
, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
Her name was listed on the
crew roster
display read by
Harry Kim
in early
2372
. (
VOY
: "
Non Sequitur
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

individual
who lived in the late

.

. When

, upon which Baby's name appeared. (

",

)

======================================================================
PAGE: Ira Steven Behr (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ira_Steven_Behr_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet Command
officer
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Ira Steven Behr
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
During the
2370s
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Command
officer on the
dedication plaques
of the
Defiant
-class
starships
. He worked at the
Antares Ship Yards
in the
Bajor sector
and at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
in the
Sol sector
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
(
2370
)
USS
Defiant
(
2372
)
USS
Valiant
(
2375
)
USS
Sao Paulo
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after television producer and screenwriter
Ira Steven Behr
.
He was listed as a member of Starfleet Command at the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. He held the
rank
of
admiral
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of Ira Steven Behr.
[1]

PAGE: Isam Helewa
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Isam_Helewa
======================================================================

Isam Helewa
was a master of
rhythmic breathing
techniques who took
Julian Bashir
under his wing while he was in
college
.
IN
2370
, Bashir demonstrated one of these techniques (
Bellows Breath
) for
Major
Kira
in an attempt to find common ground with her. (
DS9
: "
Crossover
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Isam Helewa was pronounced as "EE-sahm HEHL-uh-wah".

======================================================================
PAGE: Isis
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Isis
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown;
shapeshifter
Affiliation:
Gary Seven
Status:
Active
(
1968
)
Played by
:
Sambo
and
two other cats
(
cat
form; uncredited)
Barbara Babcock
(voice of cat; uncredited)
April Tatro
(
Human
form; uncredited)
For the planet, please see
Isis III
.
Isis
was a
shapeshifter
who accompanied
Supervisor
Gary Seven
from
his secret base
to
Earth
on a
20th century
mission.
Isis was involved in the
1968
sabotage
and detonation of a
nuclear missile
in the
atmosphere
just 104
miles
above the surface of central
Eurasia
. This near-
calamity
made the rival governments cease, at least for a time, the deployment of nuclear weapons in
orbit
, and rethink their old strategy of "
balance of power
".
Though she was seemingly Seven's
pet
cat
, Isis was more than she appeared to be: she communicated with him
telepathically
, and understood his spoken
words
. She accompanied Seven wherever he went, and watched out for him. Isis was also able to take
Human
form. In both forms, she wore the same
collar
around her
neck
. (
TOS
: "
Assignment: Earth
")
Appendices
Background information
Isis in her cat form was portrayed by three cats, one of whom was named
Sambo
.
[1]
Her purrs were provided by
Barbara Babcock
, who received no credit for her voice performance.
[2]
Isis in her Human form was played by
April Tatro
, who was also uncredited.
[3]
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 215, "
Isis was apparently intended as a continuing character in the proposed series
Assignment: Earth
.
"
Apocrypha
In the
Eugenics Wars
novel
The Rise and Fall of Khan Noonien Singh, Volume Two
, Isis was sent by Gary Seven to spy on
Khan Noonien Singh
's "Great Khanate", based out of the city of Chandigarh in northern India. She assumed the name "Ament" (the name means "one who is hidden", or "hidden goddess" as Seven put it) and became one of Khan's most trusted advisors, while at the same time providing intelligence of Khan's operations to Seven and
Roberta Lincoln
. After Khan was defeated and brought to the
Botany Bay
, Isis was killed when she stepped into the path of a knife (thrown by Khan's bodyguard,
Joaquin
) intended for Lincoln.
External links
Isis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
AssignmentEarth.ca
- a complete reference to the episode

Itzak Arrat
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
,
commanding officer
of the
USS
Ticonderoga
in the
2360s
.
In
2364
, Arrat received an order about the testing of the
Mark 25 warp sensor
from
Commodore
Toki
of the
Advanced Tech Division
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Arrat is among several people in the orders apparently named after characters from the
Albedo Anthropomorphics
comic book series. The original Arrat was male.

.

.

3195
, he was listed with other luminaries of

on a
commemoration wall
in the

: "

.

======================================================================
PAGE: J'onn
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/J'onn
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Affiliation:
Galactic Army of Light
Status:
Active
(
2287
)
Played by
:
Rex Holman
"
Your pain runs deep.
"
–
Sybok
,
2287
(
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
J'onn
was an inhabitant of the
planet
Nimbus III
.
In
2287
, J'onn was recruited by
Sybok
and served in the
Galactic Army of Light
. He, along with Sybok and his numerous followers, stormed
Paradise City
on Nimbus III. He warned
St. John Talbot
, the
Federation
's representative, to get away from the city's
transmitter
when he tried to call for assistance.
Afterwards, he accompanied Sybok on his voyage to the center of
the galaxy
on the
USS
Enterprise
-A
, the vessel dispatched by
Starfleet
to rescue the hostages on Nimbus III. After the
shuttlecraft
Galileo
crashed in the
Enterprise
's
shuttlebay
, he escorted
Captain
James T. Kirk
, Captain
Spock
, and
Dr.
Leonard McCoy
to the ship's
brig
on Sybok's orders. Later, he was injured on the
bridge
, when
Klaa
attacked the
Enterprise
. He later served beverages to both the
Enterprise
-A crew and the
Klingon
contingent of
Klaa's ship
, in the
Enterprise
's
observation lounge
. (
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
)
Appendices
Background information
In the revised final draft of the script for
Star Trek V
, J'onn is introduced as "
a ragged and malnourished homesteader of some alien race.
"
[1]
This character was only named in the source's end credits.
J'onn was played by actor
Rex Holman
, who won the role after auditioning for the part.
William Shatner
commented, "
His face was so wonderfully lean, so gaunt, that it lent itself perfectly to the image we had of J'onn.
" (
Captain's Log: William Shatner's Personal Account of the Making of Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
, p. 78)
It was Art Director
Nilo Rodis
who designed J'onn's costume. In fact, Rodis drew several sketches of the character, dated
April 1988
. (
The Art of Star Trek
, p. 252)
Recalling the prosthetics for the part,
Kenny Myers
– who designed
Star Trek V
's special make-up effects – stated, "
Jim Cale
made a brow piece to cover Holman's eyebrows, and I made him a set of really stunted teeth that almost looked like they'd been gnawed down to the gum, which made him look really pathetic. I designed the character but it was [makeup artists]
Wes
and
Jeff Dawn
who applied the make-up on the set each day.
" (
The Making of the Trek Films
, 3rd ed., p. 87)
External link
J'Onn
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: J. Bartlett

Affiliation:

Bartlett was killed in the line of duty sometime before
2377
. Bartlett's name was read out on a
casualty report
of all who died during that vessel's journey through the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Imperfection
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
As with many names on the "
Imperfection
" list, J. Bartlett is an in-joke (albeit misspelled) referring to
Martin Sheen
's character on
The West Wing
. In that respect, he would indeed be a commander, as well as a chief.

PAGE: J. Byard
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/J._Byard
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Strategic operations officer
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
J. Byard
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. This individual had served under
Leyton
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Strategic Operations
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49253. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: J. Longo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/J._Longo
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Occupation:
Instructor
of
Engineering
,
Starfleet Academy
Status:
Active
(
2285
)
J. Longo
was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
.
In
2285
, they were a
lieutenant commander
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
. They were an
Engineering
instructor
. Their office, located on the second level of a building, was in room #210. Their rank, name, position, and office were listed on a directory placard. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
, set artwork)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This officer was named after property master
Joe Longo
. The placard mentioning this lieutenant commander was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$31.99.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: J. Lowry Johnson
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/J._Lowry_Johnson
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Born:
Alpha Cygnus IX
J. Lowry Johnson
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. Their
planet
of origin was
Alpha Cygnus IX
. They were a
lieutenant
in
2369
.
In 2369, Johnson was a
crew
member aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. They were one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. Their
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a four billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

J. McNamara
was an
Imperial
Starfleet
officer
who served during the early to mid-
23rd century
.
McNamara was named as one of the
yard engineers
at
Tranquility Base
, listed on the
dedication plaque
the
ISS
Discovery
. (
DIS
: "
Despite Yourself
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ja'Dar
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ja'Dar
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Bilana III
Science Institute
Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Active
(
2368
)
Played by
:
Richard McGonagle
Doctor
Ja'Dar
was a
humanoid
scientist
from the
Science Institute
on
Bilana III
who lived during the mid-
24th century
. He was responsible for the first test of a new method of propulsion, the
soliton wave
.
In
2368
Ja'Dar initiated the first test of the soliton wave from Bilana III with cooperation from the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. Unfortunately the wave did not behave as predicted, becoming dangerously unstable and eventually proving a failed experiment. (
TNG
: "
New Ground
")
Ja'Dar was played by actor
Richard McGonagle
.
He was described in the script as "
a middle-aged, humanoid alien who appears totally consumed by his work.
"
His costume was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Jack Litchfeld
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jack_Litchfeld
======================================================================

Jack Litchfeld
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Gamma Trianguli VI
to
Tarsus IV
. His special accommodations were a
low gravity suite
and
Class N
environment. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

PAGE: Jackson (Crewman)

Affiliation:

(formerly)

Occupation:
USS
Voyager
provisional officer
Status:
Active
(

)
Jackson
was a provisional
Starfleet
officer aboard the
USS
Voyager
.
In
2371
, Jackson was a
Maquis
freedom fighter serving underneath
Chakotay
on the
Maquis raider
Val Jean
. When the ship was pulled into the
Delta Quadrant
by the
Caretaker
and eventually destroyed, Jackson was then forced to join the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, who had also been stranded there. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
As a provisional member of
Voyager'
s crew, Jackson was involved in a 'Maquis operation' with
Seska
in order to
steal
mushrooms
from
Neelix
to make
mushroom soup
for
Chakotay
. Due to this, Jackson's
replicator
privileges were revoked for two
days
. (
VOY
: "
State of Flux
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jadzia Dax (alien)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jadzia_Dax_(alien)
======================================================================

Species:
Unidentified non-corporeal species
Occupation:
Explorer
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Terry Farrell
"
She really is submissive, isn't she… Is that how you'd like me to be, Julian? So… submissive?
"
–
Jadzia Dax
regarding her doppelgänger
,
2369
("
If Wishes Were Horses
")
Jadzia Dax
was the persona adopted by a
non-corporeal entity
who traveled from the
Gamma Quadrant
through the
Bajoran wormhole
and had taken a position in the
plasma fields
near the
Denorios belt
, where they read as elevated
thoron emissions
in
2369
.
That year, this Jadzia Dax persona created by the
imagination
of
Julian Bashir
. As she hung all over Julian, he nervously tried to play off the awkwardness of the situation. When Dax requested they go to his
quarters
, he told her he didn't have time for
this
, and she disappeared.
When the real Dax questioned Bashir about the submissive
personality
of her
doppelgänger
, the Dax entity returned and protested the implication, responding, "
I'm just not the
cold fish
you are,
" adding "
If you'd get down off your high horse, you'd start to appreciate Julian.
" As she attempted to entice Bashir to "
Stop
denying
all those yearnings you feel [or] you'll never know what you missed.
"
She, along with two other's in the imagined personas of
Buck Bokai
and
Rumpelstiltskin
, later converged in the
replimat
to discuss their findings, only to be confused by the irony of their results. Dax claiming, "
Why would mine create a woman and then reject her?
"
Following the crew's realization that their own imaginations were responsible for the events that transpired on the station, the crew was finally able to return things back to normal. After returning to his
office
, Bokai revealed himself to
Benjamin Sisko
, explaining their actions, stating that they were "
on an
extended mission
exploring
the galaxy
. Awhile back, we followed one of your ships through that "hole in space" out there. We've been watching you, trying to figure out the rules of your game.
" (
DS9
: "
If Wishes Were Horses
")
This alien portrayal of Jadzia Dax was played by
Terry Farrell
.
She was identified as "Dax" in the script, until the "real" Dax appeared, then the script notes stated, "
The fake Dax hereafter known as "Dax #2".
"

was an
alien
male
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
operations division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)

. This character was only named in the source's end credits.

Joseph Gatt
's character
0718
in
Star Trek Into Darkness
. The makeup application process initially took six hours but was eventually whittled down to five hours.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Jago
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jago
======================================================================

Species:
Unnamed
Occupation:
Geneticist
Status:
Active
(
2384
)
Played by
:
Amy Hill
Doctor
Jago
was a
geneticist
who operated on
Noble Isle
in the
24th century
. She offered
genetic
augmentation
services that she claimed would "bring out the best" in her customers.
In
2384
, Jago hired
Thadiun Okona
to deliver a shipment of
gelatinous putrescine
to her, but he came to her empty-handed, as his
cargo
of
contraband
had been confiscated by the
Xindi-Reptilian
authorities at
Denaxi Depot
. Jago accepted as recompense the presence of Okona's companion:
Dal R'El
, who she identified as a creation of
Arik Soong
's protégés. Though she called their work "sloppy", she was intrigued and offered to fit Dal with an
epigenetic dermal implant
that would activate his
dormant genes
and grant him enhanced abilities.
Dal, who had been feeling insecure next to Okona, secretly accepted Jago's offer. However, when he later maximized the implant to deal with a team of
Tal Shiar
commandos
, he inadvertently activated all of his dormant genes at once and destabilized his physiology. (
PRO
: "
Masquerade
")
Jago was voiced by
Amy Hill
.

PAGE: James Mees (Lieutenant)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/James_Mees_(Lieutenant)

For the Starfleet Captain, please see
James Mees
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant

USS

,

Status:
Active
(
2374
)
Born:
Selcundi Drema system
James "Jim" Mees
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was in the
Selcundi Drema
system
.
In
2367
, while serving as a
lieutenant commander
, Mees was the
shift
supervisor
for the
Third Shift Antimatter Operations Unit
on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was listed as being on duty in the
engineering division
duty roster
. This roster was on display via the
desktop monitor
in the
ready room
. (
TNG
: "
Suddenly Human
",
okudagram
)
In
2369
, his
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a four billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
In
2371
, Mees transferred to the
USS
Voyager
, and was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
")
In
2372
, still as a lieutenant commander, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
In
2374
, he was listed as an
lieutenant
on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after set director
James Mees
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jarlath
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jarlath
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Played by
:
Mark L. Taylor
Jarlath
was a
prisoner
held captive aboard the
Nyrian biosphere vessel
.
His people
were being held captive in a
desert
environment which was similar to the habitat on their
homeworld
. He was able to get in contact with the crew of
USS
Voyager
through one of the portals between different habitats. He wished to trade with the crew and explained that the previous inhabitants were not much for bartering. He told them that the former inhabitants had died from a plague.
His visit gave the crew an idea of using the portals to escape, although he explained that it had taken him almost nine years to find just one portal. After
Kathryn Janeway
traded him a couple baskets of fruit, Jarlath helped
B'Elanna Torres
locate a transmitter that was used to allow The Doctor's optical sensors to detect the microwave signature of the portals. As the crew transversed the portals, Jarlath went with them, although he was captured by the Nyrian guards and sent back to his habitat.
Jarlath and his people were freed after the crew of
Voyager
took control of the
translocator
device and threatened the Nyrians with transportation to an icy cold habitat which was detrimental to the Nyrians' physiology. (
VOY
: "
Displaced
")
Jarlath was played by
Mark L. Taylor
.

Jaron Summers
was a
23rd century
doctor
.
In
2294
and '
95
, he was a
research associate
for the primary investigator, Dr.
Susan Nuress
, on the northern
continent
of

. As a member of the medical research team, Dr. Summers assisted in the development of a cure for the
plasma plague
on the
planet
. His name was listed on an
etiological
report. (

: "
The Child
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The name was a homage to the episode's writer
Jaron Summers
.

Jaron Summers
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

This officer was named after assistant art director Jasmine Graham.
[1]

PAGE: Jay Chattaway (Starfleet)

Jay Chattaway
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was
Regulus V
.
In
2328
, Chattaway was a passenger aboard the
civilian
transport
SS
Wisconsin
, which ferried him from
New Paris Colony Starbase
to
Alpha Braga VII
. His special accommodations were a
Class N
environment suite. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "

",
okudagram
)
In
2369
, he was a
lieutenant
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a four billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
In
2371
, he was a crew member aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Jaylah
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jaylah
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Father:
A
father
Marital status:
Single
Played by
:
Sofia Boutella
"
You help me and I help you.
"
–
Jaylah
to Montgomery Scott
,
2263
(
Star Trek Beyond
)
Jaylah
was a
female
survivor of an attack by
Krall
. For years, she lived in the
crashed
hulk of the
starship
USS
Franklin
on the
planet
Altamid
. In
2263
, Jaylah helped the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
, after it was destroyed, to fight and defeat Krall, leaving Altamid in the process. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Quick Answers
How did Jaylah survive Krall's attack on Altamid?
Jaylah survived Krall's attack on Altamid by escaping from his imprisonment. Her family was captured by Krall, who regularly executed prisoners. During their escape, her father faced Manas, a subordinate of Krall, enabling Jaylah to escape. She later aided the USS Enterprise crew in defeating Krall.
Provided by: Fandom
Who plays Jaylah in Star Trek Beyond?
Sofia Boutella plays Jaylah in Star Trek Beyond. She prepared by watching all Star Trek films and collaborated with director Justin Lin. The makeup process was demanding, requiring four hours daily.
Provided by: Fandom
What role did Jaylah play in defeating Krall?
In 2263, Jaylah helped defeat Krall on Altamid by aiding the USS Enterprise crew. She used her knowledge of Krall's base and set up defensive traps, which were key to their victory and escape.
Provided by: Fandom
How did Jaylah assist the USS Enterprise crew?
In 2263, Jaylah helped the USS Enterprise crew on Altamid after their ship was destroyed. She shared a map of Krall's base and her escape method, aiding in a rescue plan. Jaylah collaborated with Captain Kirk to rescue the crew and defeat Krall, leaving the planet together.
Provided by: Fandom
Biography
Escaping from Krall
Years prior to her encounter with the USS
Enterprise
crew, Jaylah and
her family
, like many before them, were attacked by Krall and imprisoned on Altamid. Jaylah and her family quickly realized that people were routinely taken from Krall's
holding cells
and killed, so they decided to attempt escape. During the attempt, Jaylah and her father were confronted by Krall's subordinate
Manas
. Jaylah's father stayed behind to hold off Manas, buying Jaylah time to flee at the cost of his own life.
Jaylah on the
Franklin
Jaylah discovered the derelict hulk of the
Freedom
-class
USS
Franklin
and made her home in it. She hid it using
holographic technology
and set up multiple defensive
traps
in the surrounding area. Inside the vessel, she discovered a music player, and found she particularly liked
music
which featured "beats and shouting". She also learned how to speak
English
from the ship's records. In time, Jaylah became a formidable warrior, skilled in
martial arts
and in wielding
a quarterstaff
that doubled as a
rifle
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
According to Jaylah actress
Sofia Boutella
, Jaylah herself made her own weapons. (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49)
Alliance with the
Enterprise
crew
Jaylah holds Montgomery Scott at knife point
Jaylah became a scavenger to obtain parts with which to repair the
Franklin
and leave Altamid. In
2263
, she came across
Montgomery Scott
, who had recently escaped the
Enterprise
inside an
advanced long-range torpedo
, and saved him from three rival scavengers. Realizing Scott was an
engineer
, Jaylah agreed to help him find his crew if he helped her finish her repairs.
One of Jaylah's traps was subsequently tripped by
Captain
James T. Kirk
and
Ensign
Pavel Chekov
. After Scott assured Jaylah the newcomers were his friends, she set them free. Using a
transporter
aboard the
Franklin
, Jaylah and her new allies then managed to bring
Spock
and
Leonard McCoy
to them. The group formulated a plan to rescue the rest of the crew and stop Krall from launching an attack on
Starbase
Yorktown
. Jaylah initially refused to participate, revealing her traumatic history with Krall, but Scott convinced her they had a chance if they worked together.
Jaylah accompanies Spock and McCoy to rescue the
Enterprise
crew
Jaylah showed the
Enterprise
crew a makeshift
map
of
Krall's base
and explained her own escape method, allowing them to devise a rescue plan. As part of the plan, Jaylah and Kirk, riding a
PX70 motorcycle
,
beamed
in near the tunnel Jaylah had used to escape and she dismounted once near the inside of the base. While Kirk used the motorcycle, enhanced with Jaylah's traps and holographic technology to create a distraction, Jaylah used a
pulse beacon
to enable McCoy and Spock to beam directly to the base. She directed the two to the crew's holding pen, then laid down covering fire for Kirk, before being attacked by
Manas
. The two fought, but neither was able to overpower the other, and an aggravated Jaylah screamed at Manas during the fight. The battle ended with Jaylah on a roof with Manas, and her pulse beacon on the ground below. At a signal from Kirk, Jaylah threw herself and Manas off of the roof, moments before Kirk caught her, resulting in them beaming back to the
Franklin
together.
Jaylah and Scott monitor Kirk at Yorktown Headquarters
After
witnessing
Krall launching his
Swarm ships
, Jaylah aided the
Enterprise
crew in repairing the
Franklin
for takeoff. She joined the crew in returning to Yorktown, where they found Krall
attacking
. Jaylah referred to the Swarm ships as "
bees
", which inspired Spock to realize that the craft must have a cyberpathic connection, and he beamed over with McCoy to find it. After having studied the connection, the crew came up with the idea to use Jaylah's music player to broadcast a disruptive signal into the Swarm ships in order to destroy them. Jaylah aided Scott in setting up the signal and chose the song "
Sabotage
" for them to use. After the Swarm ships were destroyed, Kirk sent Jaylah and Scott to
Yorktown Headquarters
to shut off the
atmospheric processor
and stop Krall from using the
Abronath
to
murder
everyone on the
starbase
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
An invitation to join Starfleet
Kirk and Scott offer Jaylah entrance into Starfleet Academy
Jaylah joined the
Enterprise
crew in celebrating Kirk's thirtieth
birthday
with a
party
on Yorktown, where she consumed a large number of
alcoholic
beverages to no apparent effect. She was delighted to hear from Scott that, on his recommendation and with Kirk having "pulled a few strings", she had been accepted into
Starfleet Academy
, should she wish to attend. She asked if that meant she would have to wear
a uniform
if she did, which Kirk confirmed. He also advised her that while there were many rules at the Academy, she shouldn't listen to them all. (
Star Trek Beyond
)

Appendices
Background information
Identity
Jaylah was portrayed by
Sofia Boutella
.
The character of Jaylah was created in an effort to increase the female quotient in the main cast of
Star Trek Beyond
. When
Simon Pegg
and
Doug Jung
were writing the
film
, they took inspiration for depicting Jaylah from Ree Dolly,
Jennifer Lawrence
's character in the film
Winter's Bone
. Pegg later explained, "
Doug and I and [Director]
Justin [Lin]
, in the writing room, wanted to create this very independent female, very resourceful character on the Altamid surface.
" However, thinking up a name for the new
Star Trek
character was a struggle. The writing team began calling her "Jennifer Lawrence in
Winter's Bone
" but that understandably became too unwieldy, due to the length of the name. "
It started to get tiring, always saying, 'Jennifer Lawrence in
Winter's Bone
,'
" said Pegg. "
It's a long name! So then we started calling her 'J-Law'.
"
[1]
That shortened form was suggested as a joke but caught on, before the name ultimately changed to "Jaylah". Despite the inspiration, Doug Jung, with a laugh, described the connection between Jennifer Lawrence and Jaylah as "so esoteric." Of the latter, he added, "
Phonetically, it sounded good – 'Jaylah.' It sounded a little foreign.
"
[2]
Regarding how Jaylah's persona was invented, Doug Jung stated, "
We wanted her character to be someone who was outside of the understanding of the Federation and what it means, and really, to really be a blank slate in the sense that she has no real understanding even of her own people, in a way [....] We thought it would be nice to have a character who can feel the full effect of what it means to be a part of this Federation and this group of people. To adopt their sort of unifying way in which they look at who they are.
"
[3]
Justin Lin agreed, "
I knew if we did it right, it would be great to have a new character who could potentially join the family.
" (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76)
Design
A sculpture of Jaylah that served as a basis for her make-up design
Jaylah's make-up was designed by make-up designer and make-up effects supervisor
Joel Harlow
in collaboration with a team led by
Neville Page
. The group aimed to make the character's make-up "instantly iconic," which Harlow felt was most important, as well as "instantly recognizable." (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 81) There were, though, multiple goals that drove the process of designing Jaylah's make-up and costumes. Costume Designer
Sanja Hays
offered, "
She's an alien woman who needed to look interesting, and strange, but also heroic.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 41) Sofia Boutella asked Hays to add heels to Jaylah's boots, so her height difference to the rest of the cast would not be a problem.
[4]
As it turned out, the design direction for Jaylah's make-up was suggested by neither Joel Harlow nor Neville Page. "
That pale skin, black strip look came from
Joe Pepe
, who is one of Neville's designers,
" Harlow divulged. "
It was something Justin [Lin] gravitated towards during one of the meetings.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 81) A character design by Neville Page and a clay sculpture by
Richard Alonzo
served as the basis for Jaylah's prosthetic make-up, which was created by Joel Harlow. (
Cinefex
, No. 148, pp. 85 & 88)
Adapting the selected chromatic facial design into a make-up scheme, however, involved many hurdles, such as making the black lines look the same every time they were applied, on each day of the film's considerably long production period. (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 81) "
The black lines were done sculpturally [on a pretty thin forehead appliance],
" Joel Harlow remembered. "
It would have been a nightmare to maintain those lines every day, even using a template, so I had the idea of making them slightly raised – that way, we knew exactly where they were.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 148, p. 88) Jaylah's make-up was labor-intensive not only due to the effort required to apply the paint, but also because a particular shade of white first had to be selected for the skin color, which called for some trial and error. Ironically, the color that was eventually picked wasn't even truly white. Using a pure white color would have made Jaylah look "clown-like." Instead, a multitude of pale colors was used. This combination gave an overall impression of white because the adjacent black lines were colorized with an absolute shade of black. Applying the various pale shades in different orders would produce slightly different appearances. Harlow said about the pale mix, "
In order to make it look like skin, we [had] to break it up as you would a traditional prosthetic. There was a lot of experimentation for what that color combination would be and in what order those colors would be applied.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 81)
Jaylah's make-up incorporated a pair of contact lenses and some extremely subtle prosthetics, too. Representing ridges near Jaylah's eyes, the prosthetics stretched from the actress' nose to her forehead. However, the entire bottom part of the face was the actress' own skin, not covered at all by prosthetics. (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 70)
The white-and-black motif was designed to extend into Jaylah's hair. Thus, there needed to be a seamless transition between the actress' smooth skin, the prosthetic piece, and a custom wig tied by Wigmaker
Khanh Trance
. "
It was important that her skin tone blended into her hair color,
" said Joel Harlow. "
Sonya Hayes, the costume designer, had the idea of raising the ridges between the black lines, which made it all work. I [subtly] airbrushed the black into the recessed areas, continuing the lines and fading it out.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 148, p. 89)
The make-up of Jaylah boosted the production staff's confidence in the character. "
As soon as we saw the makeup effects they were going to use, we knew it was going to be great,
" Doug Jung reminisced.
[5]
Multiple concept sketches of Jaylah can be seen at TrekCore.
[6]
(X)
[7]
(X)
Casting
When she first auditioned for the role of Jaylah, Sofia Boutella had no idea what character she would be playing, nor in what film. "
It wasn't until the callback that they told me it was
Star Trek
, and what character I was playing,
" she recalled. "
Then they took me to the make-up trailer and said, 'This is what you're going to look like.' And I was like, 'Holy shit.'
" (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76) Boutella, who had never done any prosthetics work before, elaborated, "
At first, when I had the make-up put on me, I thought, 'what remarkable work.' I've never seen anything like it.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 70)
The prospect of casting Boutella as Jaylah immediately seemed right to Justin Lin. "
I knew as soon as I saw her,
" he recollected. (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76)
Boutella approved of the role, not only because she thought being in
Star Trek
would be "a great opportunity" for her but also thanks to Jaylah's newness. "
I was attracted to this character, because she's an alien, and she's a completely original character in the
Star Trek
universe, so I don't have to compare myself with anyone who came before me. I am Jaylah,
" the actress attested. (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49)
Performance
Jaylah actress Sofia Boutella receiving direction from Justin Lin
The casting choice of Sofia Boutella influenced the depiction of Jaylah to an extraordinary extent. "
Usually when you're writing you try to find someone to fit [what you've written], but I started crafting the character to Sofia's strengths,
" stated Justin Lin. "
I wanted someone who when you meet them has this strength, but you realize it might be a mask.
" (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76)
Though she had never seen a
Star Trek
film before she was cast as Jaylah, Sofia Boutella watched all the movies once she was given the part and, during production on
Star Trek Beyond
, she relied on the advice of Justin Lin, a longtime
Star Trek
fan
. "
This is why it's so much fun to be creating an original character like Jaylah,
" she enthused. (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49)
Sofia Boutella found the most difficult aspect of playing Jaylah was the character's make-up. "
This is not a
CG
-created character; I spent four hours in make-up every morning,
" she noted. (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49) Boutella additionally related, "
There were days where I'd wake up at 12:30am to get make-up before a morning shoot. It's a long but fun process.
" The actress found the make-up and the lengthy durations it took to be applied helped her get into character.
[8]
"
When I put on the prosthetic, and saw myself in the mirror,
" she recalled, "
it instantly put me into the character, this alien warrior, along with holding her main weapon, which looks like a stick and which she is very skilled with.
" (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49) Boutella also got used to the make-up. "
The more I had the prosthetic on, it was harder to imagine Jaylah
without
it on. There's weight to it, even if it wasn't that much, and the ponytail has a certain weight [and feel] [....] It would be difficult to play Jaylah without all that going on.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 70)
Jaylah's fighting style is dance-like because Sofia Boutella trained as a dancer. (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76) "
So, she was very up for, you know, the physicality of it,
" Simon Pegg added.
[9]
Indeed, Boutella found her experience as a physical artist was of full benefit to her portrayal of Jaylah. "
It was very helpful to use my dancing ability, and what I've learned as a dancer, to execute everything that was required of me,
" she explained. "
I definitely used some of my old tricks, my body language, or treating things as a choreography, or looking at things with a rhythm so I could learn them.
" Boutella certainly found her dance background helped her with her fight training for
Star Trek Beyond
. She also used it to try to make Jaylah's presence seem particularly alien, for example being conscious of how she moved, sat and walked while playing the character. To participate specifically in the film's fight scenes as Jaylah, Boutella trained in
parkour
and
bōjutsu
. "
It was a lot of fun to learn [parkour], to be honest,
" she admitted, "
though at first I said 'I'm never going to be able to do this,' because it's quite hard.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, pp. 70 & 72)
Watched by Justin Lin, Sofia Boutella records Jaylah's scream
Simon Pegg deliberately wrote Jaylah's dialogue in a broken English that was inspired by Sofia Boutella's French accent. (
Empire
, issue 326, p. 76) Jaylah's way of speaking also referenced
Star Trek: The Original Series
, Doug Jung recalling, "
Simon found a voice for her that was a bit of a wink and a nod to, 'Why is it, whenever they find aliens in the original series, they all speak English? What does that really say?'
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 27) Boutella herself said, "
I definitely tried
not
to speak with my French accent. It would be weird for an alien, wouldn't it? Finding [her] language and speech pattern, it was very interesting to discover.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 72)
It was Sofia Boutella who thought up the moment where Jaylah assumes the command chair onboard the
Franklin
, which wasn't scripted but was proposed by the actress when she improvised it on set, during rehearsal. "
She came in and sat down, and she said, 'This is
my
house,'
" Justin Lin remembered. (
SFX
, issue 276, p. 49)
Jaylah's friendship with Scott had a real-life parallel. Sofia Boutella observed, "
Jaylah and Scotty develop a sort of brother and sister dynamic that Simon and I have off-set.
"
[10]
As Boutella had expected, the process of inventing Jaylah turned out to be delightful for her. (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 72)
Reception
In Simon Pegg's opinion, Sofia Boutella was successful as Jaylah; he called the actress "a golden addition to this group," referring to the film's principal cast.
[11]
Pegg also remarked, "
Sofia charmed the socks off of all of us. She inhabits the character in such a way that made it a real joy to be her foil in the film. She's super-resourceful and tough. I love that she has this strange, syntactical way of speaking, which makes her feel very alien, and yet she's unbelievably sympathetic.
"
[12]
Doug Jung described Jaylah as "one of the best characters to write" and went on to say, "
She ended up being this fun, wild card character, and great for us, because we got to add a new alien race to the
canon
.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 27) Jung also remarked that not only was Jaylah "kind of great," but also that Boutella "was amazing."
[13]
Producer
J.J. Abrams
was likewise impressed with how the role of Jaylah turned out, enthusing, "
I think Jaylah is my favorite new character. She is this unbelievably spirited, sweet, funny, and tough as hell character that becomes a very important ally to the crew, and Sofia did an incredible job of bringing her to life.
" Joel Harlow was proud of Jaylah's appearance, remarking, "
I think the white and black lines of her make-up, combined with the ribbing on her hair gives her a completely unforgettable look.
"
[14]
Glad that Justin Lin had chosen the black-and-white appearance for the character, Harlow therefore deemed the objective of having Jaylah's make-up design "instantly recognizable and instantly iconic" as having been achieved. (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 81) Sanja Hays concurred about the character's design, "
I think that's probably one of the most successful we've done, Joel Harlow and myself.
" (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 41)
Uhura
actress
Zoë Saldana
was very impressed by Sofia Boutella's performance as Jaylah. (
Star Trek Magazine Movie Special 2016
, p. 47)
Boutella has expressed an interest in reprising the role of Jaylah again, saying, "
I would love to see her evolve with all of the guys and with everybody. I would love to go back to her. I love her a lot. I enjoyed that experience so much. I would do it in a heartbeat.
"
[15]
In the
Women at Warp: A Star Trek Podcast
episode "
Jaylah for President
", host
Andi VanderKolk
described Jaylah as "
a perfect example of a pass of the
Mako Mori test
, in which you have a female character that has an individual arc that's not reliant on a man... She was a character who could fight, but she wasn't just your stereotypical like, 'Oh, we need a strong female character, so she better be able to throw a punch' kind of character. She had strong motivations, which I liked.
"
Jarrah Hodge
also noted, "
Kirk does have to come to her aid, but it also doesn't feel a damsel in distress situation at all. She's definitely fighting with all she's got.
"
On TrekMovie.com,
Aaron Harvey
described Jaylah in her interactions with Montgomery Scott as being "approachable but undeniably kick-ass."
[16]
Pegg and Jung considered bringing Jaylah back for a
film
centered around her character. Pegg described the premise of the film at STLV in
August 2025
: "
[
Beyond
co-writer] Doug [Jung] and I, we were going to write a spin-off about the character of Jaylah called '
Jaylah and the Wolf
.' Sophia [Boutella], she did such a great job with her, and she was such a great character. We had this idea that Jaylah was going to go Starfleet Academy and she was going to be put into a group of cadets who were like problem cadets, who were called 'The Wolf Pack' and they were sent off to a real planet called Wolf 359 and would get into an adventure on this planet. Yeah, for a while we were working on that, but in the end, it just didn't happen.
"
[17]
Apocrypha
Jaylah as a Starfleet Academy cadet
In the novel
Fortune of War
, a Tyrakhean (
β
) woman is described as having a pale complexion accentuated by her prominent and symmetrical black facial tattoos. Author
David Mack
has confirmed that she is intended to be the same species as Jaylah.
[18]
The
first issue
of
Star Trek: Boldly Go
established that Jaylah did accept the offer to join Starfleet Academy. She was shown on
Earth
at the Academy, listening to a lecture that Montgomery Scott was giving.
Baby Jaylah
The
fifth issue
centered on Jaylah's backstory. She was born in space, from a family of space traders, losing her mother during childhood. An older sister, Keelah (
β
), died at the hands of Krall the same night Jaylah and her father ran away from the base. The story also established that the black markings on her face had a meaning - "fierce", from which they derived her name.
Jaylah as the Borg drone Five of Ten
In the mobile game
Star Trek: Fleet Command
, Jaylah was one of the cadets serving as officers on the independent starbase Home Base and its assigned vessels. Soon after, Jaylah was captured and
assimilated
by the
Borg
. When Jaylah succumbed to the
Collective
, she shed a single tear for the friends she would be forced to hunt. Jaylah became the
Borg drone
Five of Ten, and was assigned to
Unimatrix
12 in the Alpha Quadrant. Eventually, she was liberated from the Collective.
External link
Jaylah
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
This is a
featured article
. At the time it was  featured (November 2025), it was considered one of the best examples of the
Memory Alpha community
's work. If you see a way this page can be improved further though, we invite you to contribute. You can use the links below to see how the article has changed since it was last featured.

PAGE: Jayme Wing (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jayme_Wing_(comedian)
======================================================================

Jayme Wing (comedian)
Species:
Hologram
Holoprogram:
YT-43584
Occupation:
Comedian
Jayme Wing
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Wing was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
YT-43584
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named for digital compositor
Jayme Wing
, a member of the
TNG remastering
team.
For the remastered edition of the episode, this character replaced
Robert Metoyer
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jeanine
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jeanine
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Andrea Yu
Jeanine
was an
alien
female
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
operations division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
.
Jeanine was later held captive in
Krall's base
. Outside but on the grounds of the facility, she witnessed
Manas
remove the
Abronath
from
Ensign
Syl
's
head
, the
bioweapon
having been hidden from their captors there. Freed by
Spock
and
McCoy
, Jeanine was in the last group of
Starfleet
officers
which escaped from the base. They were protected by
Captain
James T. Kirk
using
a motorcycle
and
holographic technology
, moments before the group was
beamed
to safety aboard the
USS
Franklin
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Jin on the cover of
Prosthetics
magazine
Jeanine was played by actress
Andrea Yu
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
Her face was featured on the cover of
Prosthetics
magazine issue 4, which referred to this character as "J-Nine" (p. 3).
Star Trek Beyond - The Makeup Artistry of Joel Harlow
(pp.103) clarifies that J-9 is short for "Jeanine".

Jeff Hadjikhani
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Icarus IV Spaceport
to
Ingraham B Transit Station
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
CBS Television Distribution
client representative
Jeff Hadjikhani
.

PAGE: Jeffrey Fratis

)

Jeffrey Fratis
was a
Starfleet
crewmember
in the
molecular biology
department aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2369
.
Fratis's
personnel record
was one of the first examined by
Commander
William T. Riker
and
Counselor
Deanna Troi
during
crew evaluations
that year, but they were interrupted by the
death
of
Sev Maylor
. (
TNG
: "

")

The script for "Man of the People" has this individual in the molecular biology department, then in the
physical sciences
department, and, back again, in the molecular biology department.
[1]
The reference to the physical sciences department was cut from the final episode.

PAGE: Jenzo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jenzo
======================================================================

Jenzo
was an
Earth
-based
counselor
and a colleague of
Deanna Troi
.
In
2377
, as the
USS
Enterprise
-E
was seven
light years
away from
Jupiter Station
, Troi suggested to
Reginald Barclay
that she could
refer
him to Jenzo instead of attempting to counsel
Lewis Zimmerman
long distance. Barclay, however, wanted "the best", and declined Troi's referral offer, preferring her advice to anyone else's. (
VOY
: "
Life Line
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

in

.

heard

and
Wheeler
talking about how Jepella had discovered

slumped over his desk when she entered his
ready room
to deliver a
report
. (

directory

dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was listed as a
Tactical Systems
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
",
dedication plaque
)
Background information
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Fleck was named after first assistant director
Jerry Fleck
.
Retcon
: In
2328
, Fleck boarded the
SS
Manila
at
Theta Gluckstern III
bound to
Deep Space 7
. This information is listed in a
passenger manifest
archived in the
commercial transport database
and reviewed by Lieutenant Commander
Data
in 2370. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
This character might be the same as the Jerry Fleck mentioned in
Joe Carey
's Starfleet Academy degree.
[2]
According to the text commentary for "
These Are The Voyages...
" by
Michael
and
Denise Okuda
, there was a
Lieutenant Commander
Jerry Fleck aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
, and he was assigned living quarters across the hall from
Counselor
Deanna Troi
. The character's name was placed on the door across the hall from Troi's quarters on the rebuilt
Enterprise
sets. However the label was never visible on screen in the episode.

Jerry Goldsmith
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was
Aaamazzara
. He was a
commander
in
2369
.
In 2369, he was a
crew
member aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after composer
Jerry Goldsmith
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jin
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Anthony Shim
Jin
was an
alien
male
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
command division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
.
Jin was later held captive in
Krall's base
. Outside but on the grounds of the facility, he witnessed
Manas
remove the
Abronath
from
Ensign
Syl
's
head
, the
bioweapon
having been hidden from their captors there.
Freed by
Spock
and
McCoy
, Jin was in the last group of
Starfleet
officers
which escaped from the base. They were protected by
Captain
James T. Kirk
using
a motorcycle
and
holographic technology
, moments before the group was
beamed
to safety aboard the
USS
Franklin
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Jin was played by actor
Anthony Shim
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
This character's make-up was sculpted by
Norman Cabrera
and applied by
Joel Harlow
. (
Prosthetics
, issue 4, p. 3)

======================================================================
PAGE: Jiri
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jiri
======================================================================

Jiri
was an individual from
Ubaya
who was referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
. This individual's name was found in
metaphorical
phrases.
In
2368
,
Captain
Dathon
opened communications with the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
with this greeting, "
Rai
and Jiri at
Lungha
. Rai of
Lowani
. Lowani under two
moons
. Jiri of Ubaya. Ubaya of crossed roads. At Lungha. Lungha. Her sky grey.
" The captain expected a greeting response in return from the
Enterprise
-D, which did not happen as the crew were clueless about the Tamarian language and society. It became evident to the Tamarians that there was a failure in communication. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Jiri was pronounced as "JEE-ree".

[1]

PAGE: Joe Chess (Lieutenant)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Joe_Chess_(Lieutenant)
======================================================================

For the R&D officer, please see
Joe Chess
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Joe Chess
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
",
crew manifest
)
In
2372
, as a
lieutenant
, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after camera operator
Joe Chess
.

in the
24th century
.
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard in
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was located in room #75274. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)

dedication plaques
of various
Federation
starships
, Longo was listed as a
yard engineer
during the construction of the
USS
Defiant
at
Antares Ship Yards
in
2370
, the
USS
Valiant
at the same
shipyards
in
2372
, and the
USS
Sao Paulo
at
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
in
2375
. (

: "

",
dedication plaque
;

: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
property master
Joe Longo
.

======================================================================
PAGE: John
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John
======================================================================

John
was a
deceased
individual who was once
friends
with
Saul Ramon
.
In
2259
, Ramon reported in his
personal log
that "
I swear I saw
John
standing in the
mess hall
, like he never… Like he was still alive. And I tried to say something to him, and
everyone
just
stared
at me.
" In reality, the vision of John was created by the
extradimensional lifeforms
living in the
Bannon's Nebula
, as they attempted to communicate with Ramon. (
SNW
: "
Lost in Translation
")

======================================================================
PAGE: John A. Fredrickson
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John_A._Fredrickson
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Deceased
(
KIA
)
(
2374
)
Died:
2374
Ensign
John A. Fredrickson
was a
Starfleet
officer during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, files from
USS
Cochrane
,
Starbase 129
and
Starbase 153
all reported Fredrickson
killed in action
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Fredrickson's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode. It is unclear which was his home station, if any.

======================================================================
PAGE: John Bowler
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John_Bowler
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Equinox
crewmember
Status:
Deceased
(
2375
)
Died:
2375
Lieutenant
John Bowler
was a
Starfleet
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Equinox
under the command of
Captain
Rudolph Ransom
in
2371
. In that year, the
Equinox
was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
by the alien entity known as the
Caretaker
.
Bowler was killed in
2375
as the result of retaliatory attacks on the
Equinox
crew by a series of
nucleogenic lifeforms
, whom the crew had been
murdering
for the purpose of converting the remains into a source of fuel. After the surviving
Equinox
crewmembers were rescued by the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, Bowler's name was mentioned during a memorial service aboard that ship. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: John Crunsfeld
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John_Crunsfeld
======================================================================

John Crunsfeld
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Kennedy Space Center
to the
Hubble Space Telescope
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

PAGE: John Eaves (24th century)

.

Rank:

(

)

Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2372
, he was listed as a
Spaceframe Development
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
,
dedication plaque
)
Later that year, he was listed as a
Science Ops
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2375
, he was listed as a
Fleet Ops
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Sao Paulo
. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2401
, he was listed as a member of the
Admiralty Board
on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS

,

-A
, and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (

: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "

")

, the designer of several
Star Trek
ships including the
Enterprise
-E.

======================================================================
PAGE: John Foster
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John_Foster
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Crewmember,
USS
Titan
-A
Status:
Active
(
2401
)
Ensign
John Foster
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the early
25th century
. In
2401
, Foster served on the
USS
Titan
-A
's
gamma shift
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
")
When
Commander
Hansen
was searching for the
Changeling
that had come aboard the
Titan
in order to sabotage it, Hansen searched Foster's quarters and found the
corpse
of Ensign
Eli Foster
. (
PIC
: "
No Win Scenario
")
This character was only mentioned in writing. Foster was listed on an illegible duty roster in "
The Next Generation
" which was later shared by
Bill Krause
on Twitter.
[1]
(X)
The sign on Foster's quarters reads "Ensign John Foster" but the duty roster refers to Foster as "Lieutenant".
External link
John Foster
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: John Mason (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/John_Mason_(comedian)
======================================================================

Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's database
John Mason
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to create a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
humor
". Mason was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
ZB-45234
. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named after producer
John Mason
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, his name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
",
crew manifest
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after regular background actor and
stand-in
John Tampoya
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jon Daily
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jon_Daily
======================================================================

Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commander
,
Astral Queen
Status:
Active
(
2266
)
Played by
:
Frank Vince
(voice)
Jon Daily
was
captain
of the
transport
Astral Queen
in
2266
.
He owed
Captain
Kirk
a dozen favors, so when Kirk asked him to
strand
the
Karidian Company of Players
, he did it without question. This forced the Karidian players to seek alternate transport, which Kirk was happy to provide – so that he could keep them close to hand while he investigated the possibility that their lead
performer
,
Anton Karidian
, was a wanted criminal. (
TOS
: "
The Conscience of the King
")
Appendices
Background information
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 158, the voiceover for Jon Daily was provided by
Frank Vince
.
In the final revised draft script of "The Conscience of the King", Daily had an extra line of dialogue which established that, prior to Kirk's request that he not take aboard the Karidian Company of Players, Daily hadn't had contact with Kirk for a long time.
His name was spelled "John Daly" in the novelization.
External link
Jon Daily
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

humanoid

okudagram
)

Jon Koslowsky
. (

, 2nd ed., p. 70)

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jon_Povill_(Doctor)

Jon Povill
was a
23rd century
doctor
.
In
2294
and '
95
, he was a research associate for the primary investigator, Dr.
Susan Nuress
, on the northern
continent
of
Oby VI
. As a member of the medical research team, Dr. Povill assisted in the development of a cure for the
plasma plague
on the
planet
. His name was listed on an
etiological
report. (
TNG
: "
The Child
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Jov Povill was a homage to the writer
Jon Povill
, who wrote the episode "The Child".

at

.

aboard the

-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
During the
2370s
, he was named as a member of the
Research and Development
team listed on the
dedication plaques
of the

. (

: "

",
dedication plaque

: "

",

;

: "

",

)
(
2370
)
USS
Defiant
(
2372
)
USS
Valiant
(
2375
)
USS
Sao Paulo
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was listed as a
Tactical Systems
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Jorkemo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Jorkemo
======================================================================

Jorkemo
was a
poet
who, according to
Data
, belonged to a tradition started by
John Keats
of poets composing
odes
to individuals who had a profound impact on their life. (
TNG
: "
Schisms
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Judging by the way Data listed Jorkemo as the last of this lineage, it is reasonable to assume Jorkemo was a recent writer as of
2369
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Joseph M. Carey
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Joseph_M._Carey
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
Assistant chief engineer
,
USS
Dauntless
Status:
Active
(
2384
)
For a similarly named individual, please see
Joe Carey
.
Joseph M. Carey
served aboard the
USS
Dauntless
as an
assistant chief engineer
. In
2384
, his
name
was
listed
with other
crew
members
on an
information
panel in the
ship
's
transporter room
. (
PRO
: "
Supernova, Part 1
")
Dominique Rossier of Wardenlight Studio provided a clear view of the information panel.
[1]
While he is not directly identified as being the same person as
Joe Carey
, the crew members mentioned on this list include other people who were identified as having died in
Star Trek: Voyager
. They are
Kurt Bendera
,
Ahni Jetal
,
Timothy Lang
, and
Li-Paz
. No explanation is given for how they are alive in 2384.

Juan Antonio Farrer Melian
.

PAGE: June Haymore (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2368
)
June Haymore
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. She was a
lieutenant
in
2368
.
In 2368, she was a
crew
member aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. In the week of
stardate
45852.1, on
Wednesday
, she had an appointment for an annual
career counseling
session with
Counselor
Deanna Troi
at 11:30. This information was displayed on a
personal appointment log
in Troi's
quarters
. (
TNG
: "
Imaginary Friend
", "
The Next Phase
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character is a reference to makeup artist
June Westmore
who was sometimes credited as June Haymore.

in the

.

, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the

, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, her name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after casting director
Junie Lowry-Johnson
.

during the encounter with the

and
3
of

.
