======================================================================
PAGE: K. Nakonse
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/K._Nakonse
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Administrative
officer
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
K. Nakonse
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. This individual had served under
Leyton
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned to
Administrative Operations
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49318. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

of the

Discovery

Kemp Powers
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kal Dano
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kal_Dano
======================================================================

Kal Dano
was a
scientist
from the mid-
27th century
who invented the
Tox Uthat
, a
quantum phase inhibitor
that could halt all
nuclear fusion
within a
star
.
When two
Vorgon
criminals,
Ajur
and
Boratus
, attempted to steal the Uthat, Dano
traveled back in time
to the
22nd century
and hid it on the planet
Risa
, where it would remain undiscovered until the year
2366
. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
")
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the script, Kal Dano's name was pronounced as "CAL DAY-no".
[1]
Apocrypha
The corpse of the time traveler
According to the novel
Watching the Clock
, Kal Dano was a
Shirna
scientist who invented the Tox Uthat to destroy the Vorgon home system in the past, before the Vorgons developed the warp drive. The Vorgons intercepted him and he was killed evading pursuit.
According to the
computer game
Star Trek Online
, the
pilot
of the
Earth vessel
found by the crew of
Enterprise
NX-01
in "
Future Tense
" is Kal Dano, who is voiced in the game by Jason Charles Miller. Kal Dano is revealed to have
Human
,
Vulcan
, and Lukari ancestry, and travels back in time to prevent the destruction of the Lukari homeworld by using the Tox Uthat to destabilize the Lukari sun. His travel pod was given to him by
Daniels
, a
temporal agent
, in order to ensure his proper place in history. Kal Dano later joins with the player to unsuccessfully prevent the
Tholians
from destabilizing the
Na'kuhl
sun, and became lost in time after his ship was damaged, resulting in his death and discovery by
Enterprise
.
External links
Kal Dano
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Kal Dano
at
the
Star Trek Online
Wiki

PAGE: Kashkooli
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kashkooli
======================================================================

Kashkooli
was a
23rd century
female
doctor
.
Once while
hiking
on the
cliffs
of
Cabo Rojo
on
Earth
, Kashkooli came across an injured
Hugh Culber
, who had fallen about fifteen
meters
when a path had given way beneath his feet. Risking her life, Kashkooli climbed down the cliff to render aid to Culber. Finding an open
puncture wound
on Culber's left
shoulder
, Kashkooli stitched it closed with
fishing line
. The stitches later left what Culber's
husband
Paul Stamets
called a "
very sexy
scar
.
"
By rendering aid, Kashkooli influenced Culber to go to
medical school
and become a doctor. (
DIS
: "
The Sound of Thunder
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kathryn Janeway (superorganism)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kathryn_Janeway_(superorganism)
======================================================================

Species:
Cilia-like vegetation
(
superorganism
)
Status:
Active
(
2383
)
Played by
:
Kate Mulgrew
This "
Kathryn Janeway
" was a creation of the
cilia-based
superorganism
that lived on a
planet
located in the
Hirogen star system
of the
Delta Quadrant
that appeared to in the image of
Hologram Janeway
.
It was encountered by
Dal R'El
after he began approaching two figures he believed to be
his parents
while exploring the planet. When this "Janeway" attempted to convince Dal to stay on the planet, he told the creature that the real Janeway couldn't leave the
USS
Protostar
. This "Janeway" then told Dal that he was too smart for his own good, and stated to "
[c]onsider me a
representative
,
" when asked to identify itself. Dal's
scanned
the creature with his
tricorder
, but detected nothing, informing "Janeway" that he was told that there was no
intelligent life
on the planet. The creature explained that "
life
is everywhere,
" before divulging that her species "
[has] the
ability
to
see what you
] want and give it to you,
" and "
[w]e also sense your
fear
,
" further adding, "
[w]e can protect you, but you have to stay,
" all part of its attempt to trick the crew of the
USS
Protostar
to stay so that they could be
eaten
by the organism.
As Dal walked away, "Janeway", who was now fully revealed to be engulfed in her own
roots
and
branches
, chased after him, diving into the
soil
and emerging in front of him. Dal immediately reacted and fired the
type 1 phaser
he was carrying, destroying the figure. He ran back to the
Runaway
and left as the roots continued to regroup. (
PRO
: "
Dream Catcher
")
This version of Janeway was also voiced by
Kate Mulgrew
.

PAGE: Kazanga

was a
theoretical scientist
, comparable in genius to

,

of
Vulcan
and
Dr.
Richard Daystrom
.
In
2268
,

listed Kazanga and these others when explaining to
Leonard McCoy
that great scientists didn't "
produce new and revolutionary theories on a regular schedule
". (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")

In the non-
canon

, Kazanga is listed on a timeline of medical accomplishments as a famous early
21st century

from

, known for researching respiratory
diseases
in the

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Keenser
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Keenser
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Height:
Approx. 4
ft.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Engineer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Deep Roy
"
And what did you do anyway? You just stood there like an
oyster
looking at me!
"
–
Montgomery Scott
,
2259
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
Keenser
was a
Starfleet
engineering division
crewmember
serving in the
23rd century
. (
Star Trek
;
Star Trek Into Darkness
;
Star Trek Beyond
)
History
2258
In
2258
, in the
alternate reality
, Keenser worked with
Montgomery Scott
at an
automated
Federation
outpost
on
Delta Vega
. He was Scott's only company for
months
before
Spock
and
James T. Kirk
arrived.
Scott often belittled and berated Keenser, particularly regarding Keenser's tendency to climb up on things and the fact that his species required very little food. It was clear that they were fond of each other, however; Keenser, in particular, was saddened when Scott
transported
with Kirk to the
USS
Enterprise
.
After the
Enterprise
successfully defeated
Nero
and the
Romulan
mining vessel
Narada
, Keenser joined the
Enterprise
crew as an
engineer
under Scott. (
Star Trek
)
2259
Keenser in his uniform
A year later
, Keenser left the
Enterprise
with Scott when Scott submitted his resignation; Scott had objected to not being allowed to examine a group of
top secret
photon torpedoes
, which had been brought aboard the ship and were to be used for the targeted killing of
traitor
and mass
murderer
John Harrison
.
While Keenser was dining with Scott in
San Francisco
's
Port of San Francisco bar
, they received a call from Kirk, who had opted to
arrest
Harrison instead, and received
coordinates
from him. Keenser glared at Scott, silently insisting that he investigate the coordinates. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
2260
Keenser resumed his duties on the
Enterprise
as it embarked on its first
five-year mission
. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
2263
Keenser was still serving on the
Enterprise
when it was destroyed during the
Battle of Altamid
. Though suffering from a bad cold at the time, one which Scott feared might lead him to
sneeze
on the
warp core
and cause a
warp core breach
, Keenser helped Scott hook the core up to the
impulse engines
and was captured by
Krall
's forces during the escape from the ship.
Keenser's sneeze dissolving metal
He later used his acidic sneezes to briefly break
Uhura
and
Sulu
out of confinement, but they were later recaptured. Along with the rest of the crew, Keenser was rescued by Kirk,
McCoy
, Spock, and
Jaylah
.
Keenser was beamed to the
USS
Franklin
with the last batch of crewmembers where Scott excitedly greeted him. Scott nearly hugged Keenser, but ultimately settled for a
handshake
. Keenser then helped Scott repair the
Franklin
and worked in engineering during the
attack on Yorktown
.
Keenser later attended Kirk's
birthday party
and presumably joined the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-A
along with everyone else. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Key dates
Prior to
2258
: Is assigned to the Starfleet outpost on
Delta Vega
2258
–
2263
: Engineer aboard the USS
Enterprise
2259
: Temporarily resigns from being a member of the
Enterprise
2260
Attends the re-
christening
ceremony of the
Enterprise
Sets off on first
five-year mission
Appendices
Appearances
Star Trek
films
Star Trek
Star Trek Into Darkness
Star Trek Beyond
Background information
Keenser was played by actor
Deep Roy
.
In the script of the
film
Star Trek
, Keenser was described as "a small, dark, oddly alien creature."
[1]
Much of the work that went into designing Keenser was done with sculptures of his head and shoulders. (
Star Trek - The Art of the Film
, p. 130) One initial decision that Director
J.J. Abrams
made was that Keenser's eyes shouldn't look totally Human. When Lead Creature Designer
Neville Page
was showing Abrams one of the early Keenser concept sculptures but was unsure precisely where the eyes should be on Keenser's face, Abrams told him that these features would stay in the same place but that, instead of having normal Human eyes covered by contact lenses, the idea was for Keenser to have a pair of strange, little, silver nubs that pop out of each eye socket. ("Aliens" special feature,
Star Trek
(Special Edition)
and
(Three disc Blu-ray)
) In retrospect,
Industrial Light & Magic
Visual Effects Art Director
Alex Jaeger
commented, "
Seeing Human eyes, it didn't look alien enough. To take it out of that realm [...] J.J. had described what he wanted as like the metal tip end of a pencil.
" (
Star Trek - The Art of the Film
, p. 131)
A total of at least four concept sculptures were crafted for Keenser. Additionally, a series of at least five concept sketches were done in pen, once the facial design was selected, to illustrate possible costume configurations. (
Star Trek - The Art of the Film
, pp. 130 & 131)
Proteus FX
created Keenser as a full-head silicone makeup design. (
Cinefex
, No. 118, pp. 62 & 68) "
We created all our original Keenser molds in BJB Enterprises' TC-1630 UltraCast polyurethane casting resin,
" recollected Prosthetic Makeup Designer
Barney Burman
. "
They were very strong, and held up well.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 82) The makeup was designed with the intention of featuring subtle
CGI
enhancement. (
Cinefex
, No. 118, pp. 62 & 68) Representing the eyes digitally was a very costly option, but it was thought that adding the metallic probes to Keenser would appropriately give him a more convincingly alien appearance. ("Aliens"featurette,
Star Trek
(Special Edition)
and
(Three disc Blu-ray)
)
Deep Roy was specifically sought to play this character. "
I was introduced to J.J. Abrams by the art director. They were looking for me,
" Roy stated. "
My good friend,
Tommy Harper
, who is the co-producer/production manager, was the one who recommended me. I was introduced to J.J. and he hired me, and that’s how it started.
" The role required two makeup artists to apply the makeup for approximately two hours each day. Despite Roy having been provided with his scripted dialogue, the filming crew chose to eliminate these lines during production. "
They said, 'It doesn't look right for this guy to talk because, after all, he's from another planet. He's an alien. He doesn't speak the language.' So that's what happened [....] J.J. told me why and I said, 'Sure, whatever it is, it is.'
"
[2]
Nevertheless, Keenser does have one line, when replying to Scott's rhetorical question of whether any other starved and freezing Starfleet officer was in the room: "
Me!
"
Originally, Keenser was not going to join the
Enterprise
crew. However, Scott actor
Simon Pegg
talked to J.J. Abrams about how sad it was that Keenser was left on Delta Vega. Abrams then asked Costume Designer
Michael Kaplan
for a small
Starfleet uniform
.
[3]
Recalled Deep Roy, "
They were debating whether to leave me or put me on the
Enterprise
. J.J. created a special scene for me. I was finished with the movie, but I got a callback and they said, 'They want you for one more day.' They didn't tell me for what. They said, 'You'll find out when you come on the set.' When I went on to the set, J.J. said, 'We're going to put you on the
Enterprise
' [....] They actually made a
Star Trek
uniform for me, practically on the spot.
" Roy was delighted and grateful that the character was granted the opportunity to go aboard the
Enterprise
. "
I said, 'Well, here comes the sequel. Maybe I'm a permanent fixture now.'
"
[4]
Following production on the film
Star Trek
,
Digital Domain
edited out the actor's eyes, created a dark cavity which was blended into the eye sockets, then added antenna-like rods that could dart in various directions. The animation of these rods required tracking of the actor's head and eyes to root the rods to the back of each eye socket and gauge their movements. Digital Domain Visual Effects Supervisor
Kelly Port
explained, "
We tracked the actor's head and eyes separately, and then created a rig to align the tips of the rods with the actor's pupils. That gave us our first pass on the eye-line. Then we played with eye-stalk motion, adding little twitchy moments that resembled blinks.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 118, pp. 62 & 68)
Roger Guyett
, the film's visual effects supervisor and second unit director, remarked that, despite the resultant probes being a subtle detail, it made "a tremendous difference to the believability" of Keenser's alien nature. ("Aliens" featurette,
Star Trek
(Special Edition)
and
(Three disc Blu-ray)
)
For
Star Trek Into Darkness
,
B2FX
– the same makeup studio which had originally produced the Keenser makeup under the name Proteus FX – reprised the character's makeup. "
Jamie Kelman
and I applied the makeups with some subtle color changes,
" said Barney Burman, "
but we kept as close as possible to the original.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 82) Having the character not talk in the sequel "made sense," observed Deep Roy.
[5]
Nonetheless, off-camera alterations were made to the makeup to enable more flexibility of expression in dialogue. B2FX did this by creating facial appliances with three densities of silicone. "
We first applied one very firm outer layer of skin inside the mold. We then added a thickened layer and a soft 180-percent deadened layer. That created rigid, shell-like outer structures, while the under-skin could flex whenever Deep emoted.
" (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 82) On a daily basis, the time it took to apply Keenser's makeup was now reduced to one hour and forty-five minutes, and only required one artist.
[6]
This time, the makeup was digitally enhanced by
Pixomondo
, adding the characteristic CGI animated eye-stalks to Keenser. (
Cinefex
, No. 134, p. 82)
When asked how he felt about the character following his participation in
Star Trek Into Darkness
, Deep Roy commented, "
Keenser is a smart alien. I try to bring a little bit of comedy into it, but he's a great thinker. He is a [quiet] guy… silence is golden, as they say, but he's well aware of what's going on around him. He doesn't hold grudges against anyone. Even though Scotty gives him a hard time and always blames Keenser – whatever happens, it's Keenser's fault – Keenser just takes it with a grain of salt and moves on.
" Roy also expressed happiness that his work as Keenser was appreciated by
fans
and speculated, "
Maybe Keenser can get his own spin-off,
The New Adventures of Keenser
. That could be a new franchise.
"
[7]
A set of three concept sketches of Keenser can be found at
TrekCore
.
Simon Pegg once characterized Keenser as "an
oyster
-faced alien." ("Aliens" special feature,
Star Trek
(Special Edition)
and
(Three disc Blu-ray)
) The magazine
Cinefex
(No. 134, p. 82) has similarly likened Keenser's distinctive eye-stalks to those of a
crab
.
Keenser is not identified by name in dialog until
Star Trek Beyond
, only being identified in the end credits of
Star Trek
and
Star Trek Into Darkness
.
Apocrypha
The name of Keenser's species was not stated in either of the movies.
Keenser was the protagonist of the
fourteenth issue
of
IDW Publishing
's
Star Trek: Ongoing
, which identifies Keenser as a Roylan from the planet of Royla, where he lived with his parents. He was often ridiculed by his so-called "friends" because he was taller than all the others of his kind. In
2230
, after
first contact
with his people was made by the Federation Starfleet ship
USS
Kelvin
, Keenser demonstrated his engineering skills to the
Kelvin
's
first officer
, Lt. Cmdr.
George Kirk
, and security officer Lt. K'Bentayr by giving them a complete diagnostic and pinpointing the problem. With his parents' best wishes, Keenser was allowed to leave with the
Kelvin
's
away team
. He experienced
space sickness
on the way to the ship. He had graduated from
Starfleet Academy
in
2233
, becoming the first Roylan to have left Royla and joined Starfleet, dedicating his service to the fallen
George Kirk
. The issue also identifies Keenser as having the rank of
Lieutenant
. His service on Delta Vega preceded Scott's, having outlasted all the other personnel formerly employed there.
As 2230 is also the year Spock was born, this indicates that Keenser may have existed in the prime reality as well.
Keensera
In a
parallel universe
depicted in "
Parallel Lives, Part 1
", a female equivalent of Keenser, named "Keensera", is shown serving as a lieutenant aboard the
Enterprise
of that reality. Although Keensera herself does not talk in the
comic book
, Marjorie "Scotty" Scott, the ship's chief engineer, mentions she initially suspected Keensera of causing, by messing about with the
Enterprise
's functions, entanglement in the ship's systems, an opinion Scotty implies she has since revised.
In "
The Tholian Webs, Part 2
", Keenser was rendered unconscious. After Scott went mad and tried to take over the ship, though, Keenser ended up saving the vessel. Captain Kirk subsequently noted, in a captain's log entry, that he wished to commend Keenser. They thereafter had a drink together in the ship's lounge and, although Kirk tried to make conversation, Keenser was extremely quiet, as usual.
Keenser appeared in the
Esurance
automobile insurance company flash game "Delta Vega: Meltdown on the Ice Planet (
β
)", where he was rescued by Esurance advertising mascot Erin Esurance.
He also featured in the
2013
Star Trek
video game, in which he was established to be
color blind
. He plays an important role in the second half of the game; Kirk and Spock check out the source of a sound nearby, which turns out to be Keenser, who is cowering behind some storage crates in an attempt to hide from the Gorn. When they escort him safely to engineering, he is met with joy by Scott, who hadn't been sure the Roylan was even still alive. After performing a diagnostic on the
Enterprise
, he informs the player (through Scott) that the warp cores are offline. Kirk and Spock clear engineering of Gorn so Keenser can effect repairs. When Kirk shouts at Keenser to hurry up, Scott tells Kirk he doesn't have to be condescending, seeming to indicate that Scott has a low tolerance for anyone
else
insulting his friend.
Keenser also appeared on card #52, Engineer Keenser, of the
virtual collectible card battle game
Star Trek: Rivals
.
External links
Keenser (alternate reality)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Keenser (prime reality)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Keeper
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Keeper
======================================================================

Species:
Keeper's species
Occupation:
Caretaker
of the
Shore Leave Planet
Status:
Deceased
(
2269
)
Died:
Shore Leave Planet
Played by
:
Oliver McGowan
For additional meanings of "Keeper", please see
Keeper
.
The
Keeper
or
caretaker
of the
Shore Leave Planet
was a
male
from an advanced
alien
race. He was the last individual of this race who resided on the Shore Leave Planet, where he was responsible for overseeing the proper operation of the
planet
.
In
2267
,
crew
from the
USS
Enterprise
visited this planet, and after a series of unexplained occurrences, they actually met this caretaker. He offered apologies for their confusion, explaining that he had not initially realized the planet's nature was unknown to the visitors. He described the planet as a place his people had constructed so they might play. He then offered the use of the planet for
shore leave
, as long as appropriate precautions were taken. But the caretaker refused to comment on his people, saying enigmatically that the
Federation
visitors were not yet ready to understand them – a statement with which
Spock
concurred. (
TOS
: "
Shore Leave
")
In
2269
, the
Enterprise
returned to this world and discovered that the Caretaker had died in the interim. The
landing party
was directed to his simple
grave
. His
epitaph
read: "
Last of his race
. Ceased to function fifth
day
of the twelfth
moon
,
year
7009
of this planet.
" (
TAS
: "
Once Upon a Planet
")
The Caretaker was portrayed by
Oliver McGowan
.
His character was described in the script of "Shore Leave" as "slight, erect, a tremendously dignified robed figure – an aspect of infinite kindness and wisdom." Furthermore, the character was identified as "The Caretaker"/"Caretaker" in the script, the latter of which was how he was credited.
Further references to the character made in "Once Upon a Planet", as described throughout the dialogue, refer to him as "the Keeper of the planet", or more specifically "the Keeper". Indeed, as intended in the script, the top of his headstone was to have read "The Keeper".
External link

.

Rick
was responsible for including them in the treaty. (

: "

")

and

were references to the Japanese
anime
(Japanese animation) series
Dirty Pair
.

what is funny?

.

PAGE: Ketteract
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ketteract
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Occupation:
Physicist
Status:
Deceased
(
23rd century
)
Ketteract
was a
23rd century
Federation
physicist
who synthesized a single
Omega molecule
.
Intending to create a new
energy
source, Ketteract was assigned a
classified
research station
in the
Lantaru sector
where he successfully synthesized a single Omega molecule particle. Unfortunately, it only existed for a fraction of a
second
before destabilizing, killing Ketteract and
126 of his research colleagues
. As a secondary effect, it also ripped open the fabric of
subspace
and made the sector impassable to standard
warp drive
travel. (
VOY
: "
The Omega Directive
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
Pocket TOS
novel
Cloak
, his full name was Bendes Kettaract and he was Human.
Ketteract was also mentioned in
Star Trek: Legacy
and in
Star Trek: Year Four - The Enterprise Experiment
, where
Spock
described him as "
quite renowned for his experimentation with the time waves associated with the
Guardian of Forever
.
"
External link
Bendes Kettaract
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Kiazi
CATEGORIES: Individuals

Kiazi
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Kiazi’s
children
. Their faces wet
".
In
2368
, on the planet
El-Adrel IV
, after fighting a native
lifeform
,
Captain
Dathon
experienced spasms of pain, which distressed him considerably. In an attempt at hiding his condition, the Tamarian laughed. Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
could see that something was amiss with Dathon and asked him if he was alright, for which in response he uttered this phrase. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kid Chaos
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kid_Chaos
======================================================================

Visions and illusions
(covers information from a
vision
or
illusion
)
Species:
Chaos entity
Occupation:
Boxer
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Tarik Ergin
Kid Chaos
was the
boxer
personae who appeared on behalf of the
Chaos entities
residing in
chaotic space
who were trying to contact
Chakotay
in
2375
.
Kid Chaos appeared to Chakotay during a series of
vision quests
after the aliens affected his
DNA
to reawaken a
genetic marker
for
sensory tremens
, a cognitive disorder whose symptoms include auditory and visual
hallucinations
. Via those hallucinations, Chakotay was able to perceive the aliens, who existed on a perceptual wavelength undetectable to most
humanoid
senses, enabling them to instruct him on how to escape chaotic space. (
VOY
: "
The Fight
")
Kid Chaos was played by regular background actor
Tarik Ergin
who received no credit for this appearance.
He filmed his scenes on Thursday
29 October
1998
on
Paramount Stage 16
.

PAGE: Kipp
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kipp
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Merchant
Status:
Active
(
2377
)
Played by
:
John Franklin
Kipp
was a
humanoid
merchant
with purple skin and a ridge running down his forehead who resided on the
planetoid
Velos
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2377
, he agreed to sell
Gar
3,000
induction units
on consignment, not realizing that Gar had no intention of returning to him with the payment. Gar sold the induction units to a
Telsian
mining
operation on an
asteroid
, during which he also
stole
some
iridium
ore
which he traded to the
USS
Voyager
.
Two days later, the
Voyager
crew tracked Gar's activities to Velos after he stole the
EMH
. Kipp informed them that he had mistakenly trusted Gar because of a recommendation from one of his buyers. He directed
Voyager
to the buyer, who had given the recommendation because his wife and Gar had been "friends". (
VOY
: "
Critical Care
")
Kipp was played by
John Franklin
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Klarc-Tarn-Droth
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Klarc-Tarn-Droth
======================================================================

Federation Archaeology Council, 2367
Klarc-Tarn-Droth
was a mid-
24th century
Federation
archaeologist
. According to
Jean-Luc Picard
, he was considered to be one of the "greatest scientific minds" in the Federation and a "giant in the field of
archaeology
." Although an avid archaeologist, as well, Picard described himself a mere "enthusiastic amateur" compared to Klarc-Tarn-Droth.
Klarc-Tarn-Droth attended the annual
Federation Archaeology Council
-held symposium aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2367
. (
TNG
: "
Qpid
")
Klarc-Tarn-Droth was played by an
unknown performer
, primarily because it was not made apparent which character he/she was among the group.
Among the unidentified individuals appearing in this episode, who may have also been Klarc-Tarn-Droth, include an
Algolian archaeologist
, a
Bolian archaeologist
, or a
Vulcan archaeologist
.

2328
, he was ferried aboard the
civilian

SS

from
Spaceport Jonathan (W)
to

. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by

aboard the

-D
in

by reviewing this data in the

. (

",
okudagram

: In 2328, Krosskove boarded the
transport ship
SS
Manila
at
Alpha Roller II
bound to
McCrystal's Planet
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
During the
2370s
, Krossgrove served as a
Starfleet
officer
. He was part of the
Research and Development
team involved in the construction of several
Defiant
-class
starships
. In
2370
, he attached to
Antares Ship Yards
during the construction of the
USS
Defiant
, and the
USS
Valiant
, in
2372
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
) By
2375
he was attached to

during the construction of the
USS
Sao Paulo
.
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)

He was named after camera operator
Kris Krosskove
. The correct spelling, "Kris", only appeared on the dedication plaque for the
Valiant
; the
Defiant
and
Sao Paulo
were both incorrectly spelled "Chris".

======================================================================
PAGE: Krole
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Krole
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Member of the
Orion Syndicate
Status:
Presumably
deceased
(
2374
)
Died:
2374
Played by
:
Carlos Carrasco
Krole
was a
male
humanoid
member of the
Orion Syndicate
based on
Farius
. He was a compatriot of
Flith
, who worked under
Liam Bilby
, who in turn, worked under
Raimus
.
An undercover
Miles O'Brien
used Krole to meet Bilby and infiltrate the Orion Syndicate. Krole was using a
dataport
to interface with a com booth to order free food. O'Brien rigged the booth to shock Krole, fusing his device. O'Brien then offered to fix the device. When he returned, Krole tried out the device, which worked, and O'Brien was welcomed as a part of the Syndicate. Krole later used the dataport to steal
money
from the
Bank of Bolias
. Feedback in his dataport usually made Krole suffer from
headaches
.
Krole was presumably killed in an attempt to kill a
Klingon
ambassador
. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
")
Krole was played by actor
Carlos Carrasco
.
External link

======================================================================
PAGE: Krotus
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Krotus
======================================================================

Krotus
was a noted
historical
despot who, in the opinion of
Fleet Captain
Garth of Izar
, failed to achieve Garth's ideal of
ultimate conquest.
"Lord" Garth boasted to
Captain
James T. Kirk
that as "Master of the Universe," he would go much farther and have more success then other previous rulers such as
Alexander the Great
,
Caesar
,
Napoléon Bonaparte
,
Adolf Hitler
,
Lee Kuan
, and Krotus. (
TOS
: "
Whom Gods Destroy
")
According to the
RPG
manual
Among the Clans
, Krotus was the
Ka'Thelan Conqueror
of
Andoria
, who swept across the planet, forcing the Andorians into a new cultural and technological era. His entire world bowed to him, but his empire ultimately crumbled and he was murdered by his own daughter.
This information is further referenced by
Michael A. Martin
in his
Pocket ENT
novel,
Beneath the Raptor's Wing
.
External link
Krotus
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kukulkan
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Teacher
Status:
Active
(
2270
)
Played by
:
James Doohan
(voice)
For the similarly named teddy bear, please see
Kukalaka
.
Kukulkan
was the last of an ancient
serpent
species
who visited
Earth
's
distant past
. There he was instrumental in the
technological
and
architectural
advances of the
Egyptian
,
Mayan
,
Aztec
and South
Asian
civilizations. He was identified with the lore of
Quetzalcoatl
, as well as the legend of the
Chinese dragon
.
He also taught them new techniques in
art
and
agriculture
. He hoped that they would combine this knowledge to complete
Kukulkan's city
and live in peace; however, they did not. Had it been completed, it would have become an
energy amplification system
to be used as a
signaling device
to summon Kukulkan.
Kukulkan depicted in an ancient mural.
Kukulkan's visit to Earth was manifested in
history
by legends of a winged serpent creature that came from the sky bringing knowledge. These records were available in the
library computer
of the
Constitution
-class
USS
Enterprise
.
In
2270
, he sent
a probe
to Earth, which was investigated by the
Enterprise
. (
TAS
: "
How Sharper Than a Serpent's Tooth
")
An illusory Kukulkan (2381)
In
2381
,
psychic mines
on
Jengus IV
, reading the minds of a
Cerritos
away team, created an
illusion
of Kukulkan that attacked
Lieutenant Commander
Steve Stevens
. (
LD
: "
Mining The Mind's Mines
")
Appendices
Background information
Kukulkan (pronounced: Koo-kul-kon) was voiced by
James Doohan
.
In the
German version
of the episode, Kukulkan was spelled "Kulkulkan."
According to
David Wise
's
audio commentary
for this episode, "'
One other thing you're gonna notice here is that we
[Wise and co-writer
Russell Bates
]
were at the recording sessions, everybody's recording sessions except
McCoy
's and
Kirk
's. Everybody else did their voice work in one session, and, unfortunately, because we were not at
Bill Shatner
's session, we were not there to correct his pronunciation of the lead guest character of this episode, so, undoubtedly you've noticed that everybody is calling him "Kukulkan" (
Listen
file info
), which is the correct pronunciation, except for the captain of the
Enterprise
, who's saying "Kuklakan" (
Listen
file info
), which Russell and I found very amusing. And we would refer to it as Kuklakan and Ollie, because when you see Kukulkan shortly he looks like a hand puppet, and he really looks like the hand puppet of the dragon Ollie from the old '50s TV kids' show
Kukla, Fran and Ollie
. So he is kind of Kuklakan and Ollie.
"
Despite the episode saying that "Quetzalcoatl" is another name for Kukulkan, an entirely different version of Quetzalcoatl appeared in the computer game
Star Trek: 25th Anniversary
.
External links
Kukulkan
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Kukulkan
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Kula
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kula
======================================================================

Kula
was a
male
Starfleet
officer
who served aboard the
USS
Cerritos
in
2380
.
That year, while
Ensigns
Sam Rutherford
and
D'Vana Tendi
anticipated visiting the
USS
Vancouver
, several advanced features of that ship were discussed, having previously mentioned by Kula, including
fluidic processors
that
replicated
their own
silicas
,
tritanium
hull brackets
, and a supply of
T88s
. (
LD
: "
Cupid's Errant Arrow
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kullnark
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kullnark
======================================================================

Kullnark
was a famous
actor
known for his roles in
Shakespeare
's
plays
.
When studying Shakespeare and performing the title role in
Henry V
,
Data
planned to study the performance of Kullnark as well as those of
Olivier
,
Branagh
, and
Shapiro
. (
TNG
: "
The Defector
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kurros
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kurros
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Think Tank
Occupation:
Spokesperson
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Jason Alexander
Kurros
was the
spokesperson
of the "
Think Tank
", a diverse group of
alien
scholars who traveled about the
galaxy
, offering to solve people's problems for a price. The "Think Tank"s primary intermediary to their clients, he was a brilliant
tactician
who delighted in solving problems and being challenged.
Kurros joined the "Think Tank" at a very young age. It saved his
planet
from a cataclysmic problem, and Kurros was traded as payment. His fellow companions were a
jelly fish-like lifeform
, suspended in fluid, the
humanoid
Fennim
, a
bioplasmic lifeform
which was thousands of
years
old, and an
artificial intelligence
. They lived on a vessel made of a
neutronium
alloy
residing in
subspace
.
Kurros and his group had learned about the
USS
Voyager
and, in particular,
Seven of Nine
, in
2375
. As a former
Borg drone
, she was a very intelligent and unique person, making her desired as another colleague in the group. They arranged a plan to force her to join the group by hiring the
Hazari
as
bounty hunters
to capture
Voyager
, while at the same time making a deal with
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
to give them a way out of their predicament, with Seven as part of the payment.
Kurros hired the Hazari as a
Malon
through an
isomorphic projection
. After the Hazari attacked and
Voyager
realized it was cornered, Kurros then appeared as himself to Janeway on her ship for introductions, and told her that they could solve her Hazari problem. He told her that his group of problem-solvers had cured, for example, the
Phage
which had been afflicting the
Vidiians
. He gave her a bit of information on the Hazari locations, just enough so she would take him seriously and board
his ship
. Kurros would request Janeway bring only one crewmember with him and predictably she chose to bring Seven leading to an interview while the latter was communicating with the Think Tank's artificial intelligence. He impressed Janeway, and received data from her on what they could provide as payment. Later, he gave her his response: the
quantum slipstream
drive,
Neelix
's
recipe
of
chadre'kab
,
Chakotay
's
Olmec
figurine, and Seven of Nine. Janeway refused, but Kurros encouraged her to ask Seven and returned to his ship.
Kurros later spoke with Seven, trying to make her see how beneficial it would be to her if she were to join the Think Tank. However, Seven expressed skepticism with how the Think Tank used their ability to extort their clients to get what they wanted and ultimately declined their offer. Subsequently, during another Hazari attack on
Voyager
, Kurros provided free tactical advice to Janeway in the hopes that Seven would change her mind, albeit unsuccessfully.
Voyager
soon remodulated their shields to block Kurros' isomorphic projection, forcing him to report back to the Think Tank, who decided to simply wait, believing that Seven would inevitably join them.
Unbeknownst to Kurros,
Voyager
was able to capture a
Hazari shuttle
and its crew. From its computer, the crew unmasked Kurros' Malon disguise. Furthermore, he was not aware that Janeway enlisted the Hazari by exposing the trick and made a deal with them: they would stop their attacks and she would deliver the "Think Tank"'s ship to them. The Hazari would then contact Kurros directly, demanding triple the bounty he offered in exchange for
Voyager
. Kurros appeared to
Voyager
again to "witness" that Seven had left the ship without authorization to settle the deal. On Kurros' ship, he was delighted to see Seven, who demanded he call the Hazari off. However, he sensed deception when the Hazari demanded immediate payment, leading him to conclude that Janeway had trapped the Think Tank in their own paradox; if they decloak, the Hazari would collect the substantial bounty on the group, whereas if they call their bluff, the Hazari would destroy
Voyager
, giving Seven no reason to join the Think Tank. Kurros then attempted to telepathically probe Seven's mind to learn Janeway's plan, only to spring the actual trap, as
Voyager
transmitted a
carrier wave
through Seven's implants, disabling their telepathic device, shortly before the Hazari fired
spatial charges
which pulled Kurros' ship out of subspace and commenced their attack.
Voyager
then
transported
Seven back aboard, forcing Kurros to project himself onto the bridge again to make one last plea, but Janeway made it clear he wasn't welcomed anymore and Seven chided Kurros for failing to let the pursuit of knowledge not enlighten him. Kurros' ship soon experienced technical problems, causing his projection to start to glitch, but Janeway smugly told him that he could solve his own Hazari problem if he gave it some thought. The Think Tank was then forced to battle the Hazari while
Voyager
continued on its way. (
VOY
: "
Think Tank
")
Kurros was played by actor
Jason Alexander
, who filmed his scenes on Thursday
7 January
1999
and between Monday
11 January
1999
and Friday
15 January
1999
on
Paramount Stage 8
and
9
.
Alexander always wanted to perform on a
Star Trek
series but was always offered parts as a Human when what he really wanted was to play an alien. It was only when the
Voyager
role arose that he decided to accept. Of his playing Kurros, he stated in a 2011 interview, "
They gave me everything – an alien guise, great intellect and evil. The trifecta. What more could you ask for?
" The makeup took two and a half hours to apply initially, but that was later reduced down to ninety minutes. With regard to his makeup, Alexander comments, "
My older son was about 10 when I did the show and when I finished getting the makeup on, I called my wife and said to "Bring Gabe over." I thought he'd be either frightened or intrigued. He came up, took one look and nonchalantly said, "Dad, you look like snot.
"
[1]
Alexander's costume was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay, and the trousers were later worn by background actor
Patrick Barnitt
.
[2]
(X)
External links
Kurros
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

com mode to

of the

okudagram

(

Captain
Kurt S. Hansen
was a
Starfleet
officer
during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, files from
starship
USS
Cochrane
,
Starbase 129
, and
Starbase 153
all reported Hansen
missing in action
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Hansen's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode. It is unclear which of the above was his home station, if any, or any relation he shares with listmate Nurse
Teri T. Hansen
.

festschrift

======================================================================
PAGE: Kyoto (Ensign)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kyoto_(Ensign)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2374
)
Ensign
Kyoto
was a
female
officer
on board the
USS
Voyager
, serving under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
in the
2370s
.
Following a
2371
visit to
sickbay
, Kyoto accidentally deactivated
The Doctor
while he was preparing a
culture
to test
Lieutenant
Hargrove
for
Arethian flu
. When he complained about Kyoto's actions to
Captain
Janeway
, she assured The Doctor that Kyoto probably didn't realize that he was busy. (
VOY
: "
Eye of the Needle
")
Kyoto's
quarters
on board the
Voyager
were located on
Deck 6
. When the
ship
was affected by an
inversion field
, Kyoto's quarters suddenly were located on Deck 8 near Hargrove's,
Nicoletti
's, and
Ayala
's quarters, which also normally were on other decks. Kes recognized the changes, as she had somehow memorized the location of everyone's quarters. (
VOY
: "
Twisted
")
In
2374
, Kyoto received a
letter
from home that had been transmitted from the
Alpha Quadrant
over the
Hirogen communications network
, but she was not present when
Neelix
handed them out in the
mess hall
. (
VOY
: "
Hunters
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

,
OCL
, was an individual living in the
29th century
.

Professor
at the
University of Copernicus
.
Burton was also a member of the
Temporal Integrity Commission
. Burton's work with the Commission included the development of the
USS
Relativity
, which was noted on the
dedication plaque
of that ship. (
VOY
: "
Relativity
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

Voyager
director,
LeVar Burton
.

was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
23rd century
.

2285
, they were a
rear admiral
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
. They were the
Supervisor of Curriculum
. Their office, located on the second level of a building, was in room #204. Their rank, name, position, and office were listed on a directory placard. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
, set artwork)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

Lee Cole
. The placard was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$31.99.

======================================================================
PAGE: L. McGarry
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/L._McGarry
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Serial number:
RM 700-4077
Status:
Deceased
(
2370s
)
Died:
2370s
L. McGarry
was a
Starfleet
officer assigned to the
starship
USS
Voyager
, a
lieutenant commander
who was killed in the line of duty sometime before
2377
. McGarry's name was read out on a
casualty report
of all who died during that vessel's journey through the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Imperfection
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Judging by the lieutenant commander rank, it is possible this name refers to one of the senior officers killed en route when
Voyager
was dragged to the Delta Quadrant, perhaps the
chief engineer
or the unnamed
chief medical officer
played by
Jeff McCarthy
.
As with many names on the "
Imperfection
" list, Leo McGarry is an
in-joke
referring to the character on
The West Wing
.

Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. This individual had served under
Leyton
.

2372
, this individual was assigned to
Planetary Defense Systems
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49300. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)

PAGE: L.M. McDonald
CATEGORIES: Individuals

L.M. McDonald
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the

of the

Langford
was a
Federation
archeologist
during the
24th century

excavation
on

: "

")

Langford was named after a woman
Ronald D. Moore
was dating at the time. He remarked, "
She wasn't a fan and we stopped going out soon after, so I don't think she ever saw it!
" (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 224)

======================================================================
PAGE: Lars
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lars
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Drill thrall
Status:
Deceased; impaled by a spear meant for Kirk
(
2268
)
Died:
2268
,
Triskelion
Played by
:
Steve Sandor
Lars
was a
drill thrall
on the
planet
Triskelion
. He was responsible for training the new
thralls
.
In
2268
, he was chosen to train
Lieutenant
Uhura
after she and her crewmates were
kidnapped
from the starship
USS
Enterprise
by the
Providers
. He was eventually "selected" for Uhura and attempted to
rape
her in her thrall
cage
. Uhura was able to fight him off at which time Lars informed her that it was "not allowed to refuse selection".
Lars died in a match with
Captain
Kirk
where victory for him would mean reuniting him with his crew, when the
Andorian thrall
threw a
spear
at Kirk, who ducked under it. The spear impaled Lars, who was standing behind Kirk at the time. (
TOS
: "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
Lars was played by actor
Steve Sandor
.
Lars was likened to a
Viking
in the
de Forest Research
review of the episode's script.
External links
Lars
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Langmatz's info (3rd name from the top)
Laura Langmatz
was an individual living in the
24th century
.
When
Kira Nerys
was investigating the murder of
Shakaar resistance
members in
2373
, Langmatz's name appeared on a suspect list. Her identity number, name, and location were listed on this list. She was located at
Cardassia Prime
. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after visual effects associate
Laura Lang-Matz
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Laura Richarz (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Laura_Richarz_(Starfleet)

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Yard engineer
,
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
Status:
Active
(

)

was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the late
24th century
.
In
2370
, Richarz was named as one of the
yard engineers
at the
Antares Ship Yards
who were listed on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
)
Two years later
, she was listed with the same details on the dedication plaque for the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
Later, in
2375
, she again served as one of the yard engineers during the construction of the
USS

, this time while stationed at the
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)

production staff
associate
Laura Richarz
.

yard engineer
at
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. The plaque can be viewed at the Twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

Lay
, along with
Burnett
, was a
scientist
who expressed the relationship between the negation of internal quantum numbers and spacetime inversion as "applying quantum vertex transformation to antiquark phenomena".
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

aboard the

. In the week of

with

PAGE: Leslie Blitman

was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
24th century
. In
2364
, she was the
commander
of the
USS Copernicus (NCC-55632)
.
In
2364
, while on a
mission
in the
Sigma Antares sector
, this ship sent a request for additional
fuel
allotments. On
stardate
41417.3,
Vice Admiral
Maxwell Headroom
of
Starfleet Mission Operations Division
sent a reply in which she denied the request. The admiral advised the starship to maximize its resources for greater operational efficiency. The captain was to acknowledge this
order
on
subspace com net
security code
645, code 150 alpha. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

Mike Okuda
into joining the
Honolulu
Science Fiction Society.
[1]

PAGE: Leslie Wong

and

of the

during the
Dominion War
. She was one of

'

at

.
In
2374
, Captain Wong was aboard the
Cairo
when it disappeared while on patrol near the
Romulan Neutral Zone
. The

Akagi
reported Wong
missing in action
, while the
USS
Zapata
reported her
killed
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
", display graphic)
The ship was ultimately destroyed with the wreckage being found in the
Delta Quadrant
in
2384
. (
PRO
: "
The Fast and the Curious
")
This character was only mentioned in writing and dialogue. Her name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a
computer
readout in that episode.
Wong appears in
IDW Publishing
's graphic novel
Captain's Log: Jellico
as the first officer of the
Cairo
in the late
2360s
under
Edward Jellico
.

at

, the wiki for licensed

works

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lewis (Voyager)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lewis_(Voyager)
======================================================================

Lewis
was a
crewman
who was stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
on the
USS
Voyager
.
In
2372
, Lewis was one of a number of officers who participated in
Tom Paris
's
gambling operation
out of the
Chez Sandrine
holoprogram
.
Replicator
rations were paid to play and whoever predicted what the
radiogenic particle
count would be at 1200 hours the following day won the rations of everyone who played, minus a small
handling fee
for the
bank
, i.e. Paris. Lewis's name was called out when the numbers were generated. (
VOY
: "
Meld
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Litana
was an
engineer
living on
Deep Space 9
who worked on the
day shift
under
Chief
Miles O'Brien
.
After
Rom
joined the day shift, he noted that everyone on the shift liked him, especially Litana. He noted that she was "always finding excuses" to talk to him. (
DS9
: "
The Assignment
")
According to the script, her name was pronounced as "lee-TAN-uh".

======================================================================
PAGE: Loomis (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Loomis_(24th_century)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Deceased
(
2374
)
Died:
2374
,
AR-558
Captain
Loomis
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
who served in the
Dominion War
.
After the
Federation Alliance
captured the
Chin'toka system
in
2374
, Loomis and his crew were assigned to protect a
Dominion
communication array
on
AR-558
. He was later
killed
by the
Jem'Hadar
, as was his second in command,
Commander
Parker
. (
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p.
?
, the new characters written for "
The Siege of AR-558
" were named after cast and characters from the 1962 war film
Hell Is for Heroes
. In Loomis' case, his name was an homage to Joseph Hoover's character in the movie, who was also a captain (albeit an army
captain
, i.e. the equivalent of a Starfleet
lieutenant
).

)

and her husband,

and

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lorkin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lorkin
======================================================================

Lorkin
was an
employee
of the
Tigan family
in
2370s
. He served as the head
engineer
of the
pergium
mining
business. He was also involved with
Orion Syndicate
.
In
2375
, Lorkin was discretely
sabotaging
equipment in an overall effort for the Syndicate to pressure the Tigan's company to do more business with it. Though not suspected of sabotage, he was blamed for not maintaining the
waveguides
of the
mine
when one malfunctioned and began to cost the
Trill
family a thousand bars of
latinum
a day.
Yanas Tigan
told her
son
Janel
to pay Lorkin and then fire him.
Thadial Bokar
, another Syndicate operative, later visited Janel to press him on Lorkin's firing. Seeing the problems with the
transtator
of the
trans-sonic drill
, he claimed it would not have happened if Lorkin had not been dismissed. Bokar also hoped Yanas would change her mind about him. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Lourenco Marques

The assay office list
Lourenco Marques
was an
individual
who lived in the late
24th century
.
In
2373
, Marques stored valuables in the
assay office
on
Deep Space 9
. When
Arissa
accessed the office's
computer
, she pulled up a list of
names
, upon which Marques' name appeared. (
DS9
: "

",
display graphic
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.
This individual might be named after the
16th century
Portuguese
explorer
Lourenço Marques
or the
capital city
in
Mozambique
, which was named after the explorer and is now known as
Maputo
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Louvier
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Louvier
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Chief engineer
,
USS
Enterprise
Status:
Active
(
2257
)
"
I don't think
Enterprise
will ever have a chief engineer more in love with his ship.
"
–
Christopher Pike
to
Una Chin-Riley
,
2257
("
An Obol for Charon
")
Louvier
was a
23rd century
male
Federation
Starfleet
officer
who served as
chief engineer
of the
USS
Enterprise
in
2257
.
For some time prior, Louvier had warned
Captain
Christopher Pike
about the
holographic comm system
Starfleet was using. Due to the resulting cascading system failures, Pike ordered
Una Chin-Riley
to have Louvier strip the system from the ship, in favor of "good ol' fashioned
viewscreens
." Number One had told Louvier that Pike thought
holographics
looked too much like
ghosts
.
Captain Pike once commented that he didn't think
Enterprise
would have a chief engineer more in love with his ship. (
DIS
: "
An Obol for Charon
")
By
2259
, Louvier was superseded as chief engineer by
Lieutenant
Hemmer
. (
SNW
: "
Strange New Worlds
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the novel "
Desperate Hours
", Caitlin Barry (
β
) was chief engineer of  the
Enterprise
before being reassigned in "
The Enterprise War
" and Avedis Galadjian (
β
) taking the chief engineer position in
2256
, prior to Louvier's assignment.

Enterprise

Enterprise
NX-01
:

•
Kelby

Enterprise
:

•

•

•

-A
:

Enterprise
-D
:
MacDougal
•
Argyle
•
Logan
•
Lynch
•
La Forge
USS
Enterprise
-E

:

ISS
Enterprise

:
Scott

Enterprise
:

•

•

-A
:

at

, the wiki for licensed

. When
Arissa
accessed the office's
computer
, she pulled up a list of
names
, upon which Seat's name appeared. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
",
display graphic
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.

in the

through the

. (

unknown actress
.

PAGE: Lussilla
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lussilla
======================================================================

Lussilla
was a person the
Bajoran
farmer
Mullibok
mentioned while having a bad
dream
. After protesting that someone was hurting her, he called out her
name
, and told someone to take their hands off of him.
When awake, he asked
Major
Kira Nerys
if he said something, but she negated it. (
DS9
: "
Progress
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: M'Pella
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M'Pella
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Quark's
employee
Occupation:
Dabo girl
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Cathy DeBuono
M'Pella
was a
dabo girl
who worked in
Quark's
on
Deep Space 9
for many years.
Together with many non-
Starfleet
residents of Deep Space 9, she listened to
Benjamin Sisko
's final
speech
, shortly before Starfleet left the
station
and
Cardassians
and the
Dominion
took control of Deep Space 9. After the speech she cleaned some tables in Quark's. (
DS9
: "
Call to Arms
")
M'Pella worked on a
dabo table
when a group of Cardassians and
Jem'Hadar
started a brawl during which some of them died. After this, she and
Morn
were
interrogated
by a
Bajoran
security deputy and she seemed shocked when she used a
handkerchief
. (
DS9
: "
Behind the Lines
")
In the time of the occupation she also worked in Quark's and entertained Cardassians and Jem'Hadar. (
DS9
: "
Favor the Bold
")
Along with
Quark
and another dabo girl, she welcomed home the returning
Starfleet
crew
of Deep Space 9 after the joint forces of the Cardassians and the Dominion had left the station. (
DS9
: "
Sacrifice of Angels
")
M'Pella attended the memorial for Morn in Quark's and later comforted a distressed Quark when Morn seemed to have
died
. (
DS9
: "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
When the crew of the
USS
Rubicon
returned to the station after they encountered a
subspace compression anomaly
, it was
Odo
who remarked that
Chief
Miles O'Brien
and
Julian Bashir
somehow seemed just a little smaller than usual. When M'Pella rose up from her barstool and was considerably taller than the two men, they went to the
infirmary
immediately. (
DS9
: "
One Little Ship
")
M'Pella was working at Quark's when
Major
Kira
received
Bajoran lilacs
from Quark, celebrating
her
mother
's
birthday
. (
DS9
: "
Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night
")
During her brief appearance in this episode, M'Pella's tattoo on her right back was clearly visible.
In
2374
, an intoxicated
Grathon Tolar
attempted to
dance
with M'Pella while she was operating the
dabo wheel
. When Tolar became insistent, there was a brief struggle before Quark intervened. Tolar subsequently stabbed the
Ferengi
. The incident was quietly covered up after Sisko offered a
bribe
to Quark. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
When Odo and Sisko arrived in the aftermath of this incident, M'Pella's clothing was noticeably torn at her chest and her left shoulder, suggesting Tolar had more malevolent intentions than just dancing. Unlike Quark, however, she seemed more annoyed than injured from the incident.
She was running the dabo wheel when a grown up
Molly O'Brien
stabbed the
Markalian
Madrat
in the chest with a broken
bottle
. (
DS9
: "
Time's Orphan
")
M'Pella was running a gambling table in Quark's shortly before Quark and
Rom
smuggled a
cloaking device
to the
mirror universe
Terok Nor
. (
DS9
: "
The Emperor's New Cloak
")
M'Pella with Leeta and Rom in 2375
In
2375
, M'Pella and
Leeta
asked Quark to reduce the amount he took from their tips from 20% to 10%; Quark said he would "think about it". Shortly after, when it seemed Quark would be the next
Grand Nagus
, M'Pella offered to keep him company in his "great, big
Nagal Residence
", and Quark was more than happy to oblige her. M'Pella continued working at Quark's after it turned out that Rom was to be the next Nagus. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
")
Appendices
Appearances
DS9
:
"
Empok Nor
"

"

Background information
M'Pella was played by
Terry Farrell
's
stand-in
and
body double
Cathy DeBuono
and (along with
Broik
) was seen in the background at Quark's in several seasons of
Deep Space Nine
, but was never given lines or credit until her final appearance in "The Dogs of War."
She was first named as M'Pella in "
In the Pale Moonlight
". According to the episode's script, her name was pronounced as "meh-PEL-uh".
[1]
Apocrypha
The novel
The 34th Rule
identified M'Pella as being Bajoran, although she never had Bajoran nose ridges in the series. In
Raise the Dawn
she is identified as being "
one of the rare Bajorans born with no rhinal ridges.
"
External links
M'Pella
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: M'Tell
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M'Tell
======================================================================

M'Tell
was a famous
female
archaeologist
.
Among her greatest findings was
Ya'Seem
, an event which
Galen
ranked comparable to the discovery of
Troy
by
Heinrich Schliemann
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, the pronunciation for M'Tell was "muh-TELL".

was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
23rd century
.
In
2285
, they were a
captain
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
. They were a
Communications
instructor. Their office, located on the second level of a building, was in room #287. Their rank, name, position, and office were listed on a directory placard. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
, set artwork)

Martin Becker
. The placard was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$31.99.

was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
who had served under
Admiral
Leyton
.
In
2372
, Carriere was assigned to
Planetary Defense Systems
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49257. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. Carriere's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in

. (

M. Piller
was the
Federation
Secretary of Exploration
in the mid-
24th century
. During his tenure, Piller authorized the annexation of the
Minos Korva
system into Federation territory. The secretary's recommendation for the establishment of an extended range security perimeter for this system was deferred. (
TNG-R
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

of the

Gh'aster

(

was a
starship
captain
in the
24th century
. Prince was the
commander
of the
CGM
Gh'aster
.

2370

Deep Space 9
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)

okudagram
graphic of an
arrival roster
on a
viewscreen
. The graphic was also populated by a number of other
in-jokes
, so it seems this could be a reference to nerdy character
Martin Prince
from
The Simpsons
.

======================================================================
PAGE: M.C. Minor
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/M.C._Minor
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Rear admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer,
Starfleet Academy
Status:
Active
(
2285
)
M.C. Minor
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
23rd century
.
In
2285
, they were a
rear admiral
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
. Their position at the Academy was that of
Commandante Cadets
. Their office, located on the second level of a building, was in room #203. Their rank, name, position, and office were listed on a directory placard. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
, set artwork)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This officer was named after illustrator
Mike Minor
. The placard was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Macormak
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Macormak
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Ensign
Macormak
was a
female
24th century
Starfleet
officer
who served aboard the
USS
Voyager
, under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
, while that ship was lost in the
Delta Quadrant
.
According to
Tuvok
, Macormak would miss Ensign
Bennet
after his
death
in
2372
, after Bennet claimed that he wasn't going to leave anybody behind. Bennet seemed surprised by that, saying that he 'was never really sure how she–", but he died before he could finish his sentence. (
VOY
: "
Innocence
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Macormak was also mentioned in an ultimately
unused line
from the
script
for "
Investigations
". According to that dialog,
Chakotay
recommended Macormak as a possible replacement for the departing
Tom Paris
as the ship's
pilot
. Janeway wondered if she had the temperament for the job, but Chakotay reminded her that "her cool head kept us alive more than once."

======================================================================
PAGE: Madeline (alternate reality)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Madeline_(alternate_reality)
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Officer
Occupation:
Science officer
Status:
Active
(
2259
)
Played by
:
Kimberly Broumand
Madeline
was an
alien
Starfleet
cadet
present at
James T. Kirk
's disciplinary
hearing
at
Starfleet Academy
in
2258
. Following a
distress call
from
Vulcan
, she was transferred to the
USS
Enterprise
where she served as a
science officer
on the
bridge
. (
Star Trek
)
She continued serving on the ship when Kirk was promoted to
commanding officer
, and joined its
five-year mission
two
years
later as one of the
bridge officers
. Madeline informed
Spock
that the ships
auxiliary power
failed when the ship began to lose its
orbit
and fell towards
Earth
. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
Appendices
Background information
Madeline was played by
Kimberly Broumand
who was credited for this role in the end credits of
Star Trek Into Darkness
. For her appearance in
Star Trek
, she received credit as "Cadet Alien" and worked on it under her maiden name,
Kimberly Arland
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
The character design, which was inspired by an image of facial ornamentation seen by
J.J. Abrams
, was done by
Neville Page
. His concept for the design was
tribal scarification
. (
Star Trek - The Art of the Film
, p. 73)
The sculpting of the character's make-up was achieved by
Proteus FX
. "
We used silicone appliances tinted with bronze metallic powders to keep application times down,
" recalled
Barney Burman
. "
Then it was just a matter of doing the detailing work.
" Also involved in the make-up were chrome silver corneal contact lenses, which were created by
Justin Stafford
, and tiny, pointed prosthetic dentures. The make-up was applied by
Margaret Prentice
and
Ned Neidhart
. (
Cinefex
, number 118, pp. 46 & 52)
A behind-the-scenes picture of Cadet Madeline was used as card #8 in the
virtual collectible card battle game
Star Trek: Rivals
, titled Cadet Kar'dla. Another image was used for card #46, titled Science Officer Madeline.
External link
Madeline
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Magen
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Magen
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Elysian Ruling Council
Occupation:
Advisor
to the
Interpreter of Laws
Status:
Active
(
2269
)
Played by
:
Nichelle Nichols
(voice)
Magen
was a female with narrow features and
cat
-like
eyes
, who was trapped in
Elysia
within the
Delta Triangle
. Possessed of
psionic abilities
, Magen served as an
advisor
to
Devna
, the
Orion
Interpreter of Laws
, and
Xerius
, the
Romulan
speaker
for the
Elysian Council
.
When the
USS
Enterprise
and the
Klingon
battle cruiser
Klothos
were also trapped in Elysia, in
2269
, Magen used her abilities to reveal a
bomb
hidden aboard the
Federation
ship before it had a chance to activate. (
TAS
: "
The Time Trap
")
Appendices
Background information
The voice of Magen was provided by
Nichelle Nichols
.
Magan was described in the final draft of the script as follows: "
a female alien from a planet in the
Omega Cygni system
. The planet is one of light gravity; and the alien, MAGEN is elongated with slender limbs and an almost ethereal quality.
"
The novelization,
Star Trek Log 4
by
Alan Dean Foster
, spells her name "Megan" and states that she is a
Cygnian
. Her powers are described as a sympathy shared by her people and intensified by their minds enabling them to see all that others do, concepts that are revisited in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Corps of Engineers
eBook
Where Time Stands Still
by
Dayton Ward
and
Kevin Dilmore
.
External link
Magen
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Magnus
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Magnus
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation

crewmember

2256
)

was a

living in the mid-

.

, Magnus was ordered to report to
engineering
of the

. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Maltuvis
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Maltuvis
======================================================================

Maltuvis
was a
dictator
who became a noted historical figure by the
23rd century
.
In
2266
,
Roger Korby
believed that by transferring
consciousness
into an
android
body, a
Human
would have practical
immortality
.
James T. Kirk
responded that this was programming. He continued further by saying that others had made the same promises, using different words, and that these others were
Genghis Khan
,
Julius Caesar
,
Adolf Hitler
,
Ferris
, and Maltuvis. (
TOS
: "
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Bernard Maltuvis is an
Augmented
Human depicted as ruling over Australasia in the late 20th century in the
Star Trek: Khan
comic series.
In the
Pocket ENT
novels
A Choice of Futures
to
Patterns of Interference
, Maltuvis played a prominent role as a
Saurian
monarch who conquered his new-to-warp homeworld with the help of the
Orion Syndicate
. The
Pocket TOS
novel
Living Memory
reveals that Maltuvis tried to conquer Arcturus IV in order to use the quick-bred
Arcturian
Warborn as shock troops to sweep across the galaxy. Maltuvis was defeated by the Arcturians, which ultimately led to his downfall.
External links
Bernard Maltuvis
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Maltuvis (Saurian)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Manway
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Manway
======================================================================

Doctor
Manway
was the leader of a
Federation
science team
studying the
Malurians
in
2267
. They had a special
transmitter
.
In
2267
,
James T. Kirk
questioned whether Manway's transmitter was broadcasting following the cessation of the Malurian's
distress calls
.
Nyota Uhura
couldn't find anything on any of the
frequencies
.
Unbeknownst to them at the time, the
space
probe
Nomad
had performed a
sterilization procedure
on the
Malurian
system
, resulting in the deaths of
the science team
and more than
four billion Malurians
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Manway
at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Marayna
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Marayna
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Played by
:
Sandra Nelson
Marayna
was an alien of an unknown species responsible for preserving an
inversion nebula
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
She worked alone on a hidden space station within the nebula, generating a
dampening field
to stabilize the nebula so that others from her
homeworld
may enjoy its beauty. Although she took up the assignment by choice, the monotonous routine and separation from her people made her feel lonely. To amuse herself, she tapped into the computers of unknowing passing ships to observe the details of their crews' lives.
When the
USS
Voyager
encountered the nebula in
2373
and studied it in detail, Marayna tapped into
Voyager
's system and discovered the
holodeck
. She created an
uplink
to the holodeck and appeared as a
Human
holo-character
in Neelix's
Paxau Resort
holoprogram. From there, she interacted with
Voyager
's crew members, including giving
hydrosailing
lessons to
Harry Kim
and
Kes
. Harry became infatuated with the Marayna hologram to the point of being distracted while on duty, and asked
Tuvok
for help in suppressing his emotions.
During a
luau
organized by Neelix on the holodeck, Tuvok saw the Marayna hologram playing with
kal-toh
and engaged in a deep conversation with her. Although Tuvok was only intellectually interested in her, Marayna took a romantic
interest
in him.
When
Voyager
finished studying the nebula and attempted to leave, Marayna stopped them from leaving by interfering with
Voyager
's systems. When Harry visited the holodeck on a break from isolating the problem, he was jealous seeing Tuvok playing
kal-toh
with the Marayna hologram, thinking that Tuvok had taken an interest in her. In the ensuing argument, Tuvok deleted the Marayna hologram. However, when Tuvok returned to his quarters, he found the Marayna hologram there (having transferred herself into sickbay and stolen the
mobile emitter
), and demonstrated her control over
Voyager
by silencing the intruder alert alarm.
The crew managed to trace a subspace signal that was interfacing with the ship's computer, and Tuvok
transported
to the source of the signal. He found himself aboard a space station with the real Marayna. She demanded that Tuvok stay with her or she would destroy
Voyager
, but eventually consented to let him and
Voyager
go when Tuvok appealed to her senses by reminding her that he had a
wife
back on his
homeworld
and a ship that depended on him, and whatever reason he remained for her would not be the one she wanted. He suggested that she call for a replacement and return to her homeworld to live with her people where she might find someone more suitable. (
VOY
: "
Alter Ego
")
Marayna was played by actress
Sandra Nelson
. Her species and origin were not identified.
The costume from her appearance as an alien was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 19, Marayna was a holographic character in Neelix's
Polynesian
resort. She was the entertainment director of the resort. This character became a conduit for an unnamed alien woman to communicate with the crew of the
Voyager
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Marc Brooks
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Marc_Brooks
======================================================================

Marc Brooks
was a
male
Human
in the
24th century
. Born in
2329
, he met and
married
Janet Brooks
by
2367
and lived with her aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
Five months prior to
stardate
44356.9, Marc died in an accident. Shortly following the
funeral
, Janet had all of Marc's belongings removed from their
quarters
. Unbeknownst to her,
Deanna Troi
had saved one special item, a
music box
, just in case Janet changed her mind. The grief over his death led Janet to seek counseling from Troi, where Deanna returned the music box to her. (
TNG
: "
The Loss
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
It was never made clear whether or not Marc Brooks was a
Starfleet
crewmember, although the fact that Troi was present following the funeral and that
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
seemed familiar with Brooks implied he was aboard the
Enterprise
at the time of his death.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 57 stated Marc Brooks was a
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
who died in late
2366
.

and

Sector 450
. These modifications were for diplomatic reasons relating to negotiations with the
New Zealand
government. She requested that the
captains
send acknowledgement of receipt of the order through
subspace com net
security mode
045, code 100 alpha. (

: "
Conspiracy
"
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
