ferried her and her traveling companion
Michael Westmore
from
Earth Colony II
to
Tellun system
. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander

aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (

: "

",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Marion Westmore is also the name of TNG make-up supervisor
Michael Westmore
's wife, and mother of
Michael, Jr.
and
McKenzie Westmore
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Marla Aster (Koinonian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Marla_Aster_(Koinonian)
======================================================================

Species:
Koinonian
in
Human
form
Status:
Disassociated
(
2366
)
Played by
:
Susan Powell
This
Koinonian
manifestation
of Marla Aster
was created following the accidental
death
of the
Human
Marla Aster
on the
Koinonian homeworld
in
2366
, as a means to comfort her now
orphan
son
Jeremy Aster
.
Unknown to the
Enterprise
crew, the planet was inhabited by
non-corporeal
Koinonians. They felt Jeremy's pain and created a manifestation of Marla Aster with the intention of raising him on their planet in a recreation of
his old house
on
Earth
. The
Enterprise
crew intervened and made it clear that Jeremy would not get over the death of his
mother
. Jeremy asked the replica to leave. (
TNG
: "
The Bonding
")
The Marla Aster was portrayed by
Susan Powell
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Masada
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Masada
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Science officer
,
USS
Constellation
Status:
Deceased
(
2267
)
Died:
2267
Masada
was a
Starfleet
officer
, assigned to the
USS
Constellation
as
science officer
in
2267
.
Upon the arrival of the
Constellation
to
system L-374
, Masada reported the breakup of
L-374 IV
.
Later, when the
Constellation
encountered an extragalactic "
planet killer
," Masada was
beamed
down to
L-374 III
with the rest of the
Constellation
's complement by
Commodore
Matt Decker
, and was killed when the device destroyed that planet. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 25, Masada was probably "
named for the
hilltop fortress
where Jews defied Roman legions, eventually committing suicide en masse rather than surrender.
"
Apocrypha
In several
Pocket Books
novels, Masada was named Guillermo Masada, and was a Starfleet
lieutenant
who was also Decker's
second officer
.
The USS
Masada
was a hijacked
Ptolemy
-class
tug
in
Interplay
's
Star Trek: 25th Anniversary
game
.
External links
Guillermo Masada
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

and

", "

and a

======================================================================
PAGE: Matt Franklin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Matt_Franklin
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Jenolan
crewmember
Status:
Deceased
(
24th century
)
Died:
24th century
,
USS
Jenolan
"
Come on, Franklin… I know you're still in there…
"
–
Montgomery Scott
,
2369
("
Relics
")
Ensign
Matt Franklin
was a
Starfleet
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Jenolan
in
2294
.
In that year, the
Jenolan
crashed
on the surface of a previously-undiscovered
Dyson sphere
. Along with former Starfleet
officer
Montgomery Scott
, who was a passenger on board the
Jenolan
bound for the
Norpin colony
, Franklin was the only other survivor.
Realizing that they did not have enough supplies to wait for a rescue, Scott conceived the idea of locking the
transporter
pattern buffer
into a
diagnostic
cycle, and then cross-connecting the buffer to the
phase inducers
to provide a regenerative power source. This effectively allowed Scott and Franklin to survive in a dematerialized state of
suspended animation
until they could be rescued.
This innovation proved successful for Scott, who was rescued by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
75 years later
, but Franklin's pattern had degraded 53
percent
due to the failure of one of the inducers, effectively killing him.
Scott later remarked that "he was a good lad". He also dismissed
Geordi La Forge
's admiration of the method used to preserve them, which La Forge had referred to as "absolutely brilliant", to only have been "50% brilliant", adding, "Franklin deserved better". (
TNG
: "
Relics
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Franklin's status as an ensign was mentioned in the episode's script, but not spoken on-screen.
[1]
Franklin's first meeting with Scott is depicted in the
Pocket TNG
novel
Engines of Destiny
.
External link
Matt Franklin
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Maturin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Maturin

Species:
Maturin's species
Rank:
Governor
Occupation:
Leader of the
Caldos colony
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Michael Keenan
Maturin
was the
governor
of the
Caldos colony
in
2370
. He was a member of a
humanoid
species that had an upturned
nose
which transitioned into a ridge extending to the forehead.
As a child, he and his family visited
Glamis Castle
in
Scotland
on
Earth
. When he looked out across the
Scottish Highlands
, he immediately felt "as if he'd come home". Therefore he later moved to the
colony
, modeled after Scotland and later on even became its governor.
He was a good friend of
Felisa Howard
and participated in her
funeral
. (
TNG
: "
Sub Rosa
")
Maturin was played by actor
Michael Keenan
.
He was described in the script as "
an ALIEN man in his late Fifties --
".

PAGE: Maxwell Headroom

Affiliation:

Maxwell Headroom
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. He was a
flag officer
with the rank of
vice admiral
in
2364
.
Between
stardates
41417 and 41607, he was an officer at the Starfleet
Mission Operations Division
.
He sent an order about the information compiled from the testing of the
Mark 25 warp sensor
to
Captain
Mary van de Ven
of the
USS
Elmer Fudd
.
He sent an order in which he denied a fuel allotment request from the captain of the
USS
Copernicus
. The admiral requested the captain to send acknowledgement of the receipt of this order through
subspace com net
security mode 045, code 100 alpha.
After 41607, he was an officer at the Starfleet
Planetary Geosciences Division
.
He sent an order about a cultural evolution survey of the intelligent lifeforms on
Gamma Argus II
to Captain
Seth Goldberg
of the
USS
Callisto
. The admiral requested the captain to send acknowledgement of the receipt of this order through
subspace com net
security mode 045, code 100 alpha. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
mission order #1
;
mission order #2
;
mission order #3
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Headroom was named after the 1980s fictional artificial intelligence character
Max Headroom
. This character was portrayed by
Matt Frewer
, who later guest-starred in "
A Matter Of Time
".

at

, the wiki for licensed

PAGE: Maxwell Soroyan

Maxwell Soroyan
was a friend of
Harry
and
Dennis Kim
.
A few years prior to
2377
, Soroyan was killed by the
Maquis
. When the
USS
Voyager
reestablished communications with the
Alpha Quadrant
, Dennis wrote a
letter
to Harry notifying him of Soroyan's death.
While investigating the assaults against several ex-Maquis members of
Voyager
's crew,
Tuvok
, having read the letter, questioned whether Harry was responsible for placing them in
comas
. Kim
sarcastically
responded to the accusation, saying he fired his
Captain Proton
Comatizer
at them after hearing of Soroyan's death. Afterwards, he assured Tuvok that he did not blame the Maquis for the incident. (
VOY
: "
Repression
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: May Ahearn (jahSepp)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/May_Ahearn_(jahSepp)
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
Species:
jahSepp
Played by
:
Bahia Watson
(illusory
Human
form)
"
May Ahearn
" was a
jahSepp
who appeared in the form of a particular
Human
female
– the real
May Ahearn
– to
Sylvia Tilly
in
2257
. (
DIS
: "
New Eden
", "
Point of Light
")
That year, after being injured excavating a sample of a
dark matter
asteroid
, Tilly began to see a
hallucination
of Ahearn as a
Starfleet
engineering officer
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. Thinking she was an actual member of the crew, Tilly used her as a sounding board to work out how to save the planet of
Terralysium
from radioactive debris. The hallucination of Ahearn encouraged her to use the asteroid itself to collect the debris, and later appeared on the
bridge
to congratulate her.
Tilly was able to identify her by the use of Tilly's old
nickname
of
Stilly
, and attempted to locate her on the starship. When advised by the ship's computer that Ahearn was not on board, Tilly called up Ahearn's file, and learned of her
death
. (
DIS
: "
New Eden
")
As time went on, "May" became more and more insistent, making references to a "plan" that she needed Tilly to enact. After an outburst at "May" on the
Discovery
's bridge, in full view of
Captain
Christopher Pike
and the
senior staff
, Tilly admitted to her friend
Michael Burnham
that she had started seeing a "
ghost
" after the incident with the asteroid, and suspected she was going insane. However, when Tilly mentioned that "May" seemed confused at the concept of
tears
, Burnham realized that "May" was much more than a hallucination, and deduced that the dark matter had reacted to exposure to the
mycelium spores
around which Tilly regularly worked.
Astromycologist
Paul Stamets
, whom "May" believed was in fact the captain due to his being the interface that powered
Discovery
's
spore drive
, confirmed that a
multidimensional fungal parasite
had grown inside Tilly's body from a spore native to the
mirror universe
, which had attached itself to Tilly during
their escape
from that dimension.
First Officer
Saru
speculated that it had taken the form of May through brain manipulation. Stamets used a piece of the asteroid Tilly had obtained using a
laser core sampler
to remove "May" from Tilly's body. (
DIS
: "
Point of Light
")
Quick Answers
How did May Ahearn appear to Sylvia Tilly on Discovery?
In 2257, Sylvia Tilly encountered a hallucination of May Ahearn on the USS Discovery after an injury involving a dark matter asteroid. Tilly, thinking Ahearn was a real crew member, used her advice to address a crisis with radioactive debris threatening Terralysium. Ahearn's hallucination encouraged Tilly to use the asteroid for debris collection. Tilly later found out Ahearn was deceased, raising concerns about her mental health.
Provided by: Fandom
What role did May Ahearn play in Tilly's journey on Discovery?
In 2257, May Ahearn appeared as a hallucination to Sylvia Tilly on the USS Discovery. After Tilly was injured while excavating a dark matter asteroid, she began seeing Ahearn, who appeared as a Starfleet engineering officer. Tilly initially believed Ahearn was a real crew member and used her as a sounding board to devise a plan to save the planet Terralysium from radioactive debris. Ahearn encouraged Tilly to use the asteroid to collect the debris. Tilly later discovered that Ahearn was not on board and had died previously. Despite this, Ahearn's presence helped Tilly gain confidence and solve critical problems on Discovery.
Provided by: Fandom
Why did Tilly see May Ahearn as a hallucination on Discovery?
In 2257, aboard the USS Discovery, Sylvia Tilly saw a hallucination of May Ahearn after an injury involving a dark matter asteroid. Initially, Tilly thought Ahearn was a Starfleet officer and consulted her to save Terralysium from radioactive debris. Tilly later learned that the real May Ahearn had died in 2252, and the hallucination was a being from the mycelial network.
Provided by: Fandom
How did May Ahearn help Tilly save Terralysium in Discovery?
May Ahearn, appearing as a hallucination to Ensign Tilly on the USS Discovery in 2257, helped save Terralysium. Tilly, believing Ahearn was a crew member, used her as a sounding board to devise a plan to remove radioactive debris from the planet. Ahearn suggested using an asteroid to collect the debris, leading to a successful mission. Tilly later learned that Ahearn had passed away before these events.
Provided by: Fandom
What is the connection between May Ahearn and the dark matter asteroid?
May Ahearn is linked to the dark matter asteroid through Sylvia Tilly's experiences on the USS Discovery. In 2257, Tilly hallucinated Ahearn after an injury involving the asteroid. This vision was actually a being from the mycelial network. The dark matter asteroid, rich in metreon particles, played a key role in the Discovery's mission.
Provided by: Fandom

was a
Starfleet
captain known for having written the revised
first contact procedures
for
Starfleet
.
In
2369
, after trying to remember his name for six
hours
,
Benjamin Sisko
finally recalled it while at
Quark's
. He wished aloud that McCoullough was on
Deep Space 9
instead of him, to supervise
first contact
with the incessantly
gambling
Wadi
.
Exhausted (
"With all due respect to the procedures and to McCoullough, I have had it."
), he finally decided to leave the
bar
and let
Quark
handle them.  (
DS9
: "
Move Along Home
")

======================================================================
PAGE: McFly
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/McFly
======================================================================

McFly's gravestone
McFly
was the name of a resident of
Caldos colony
who had passed away some time prior to
2370
.
His
gravestone
was in the same
cemetery
where
Felisa Howard
was laid to rest. In that
year
, the area around McFly's grave was the scene of a battle between Felisa's
granddaughter
Beverly Crusher
and an
anaphasic lifeform
named
Ronin
. (
TNG
: "
Sub Rosa
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The gravestones bore
in-joke
references to various science fiction characters. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 280) This one was a nod to the McFly family of the
Back to the Future
series
of films.

of the

.

Vulcan
was

by the

======================================================================
PAGE: McGreevey
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/McGreevey
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Science
officer
Status:
Deceased
(
2375
)
McGreevey
was a
male
officer
in the
science division
of the
24th century
Starfleet
.
McGreevey served during the
Dominion War
in
2375
. He was assigned to
AR-558
, a
planet
that the
Federation Alliance
had captured from the
Dominion
, under the command of
Captain
Loomis
. They were assigned to protect the planet's
communication array
. Other members of the 150-person unit included
Commander
Parker
,
Reese
,
Crewman
Kellin
,
Vargas
and
Lieutenant
Nadia Larkin
.
McGreevey was extremely talkative, and was intensely disliked by his fellow soldier,
Vargas
, who thought he was a "
jerk
" he "couldn't stand", complained that McGreevey "
wouldn't shut up.
Yap
, yap, yap, yap. He thought he was the world's greatest authority on everything.
"
During a battle, McGreevey had been tending Vargas's wound at the time and, as usual, "
talking his head off
, and the next minute he's lying flat on his back with a hole in his
chest
. And I just sat there and I looked at him. That was so great. He was so quiet. One time in his life he's quiet.
"
McGreevey's death by the
Jem'Hadar
and the wide-eyed expression he wore, stunned Vargas, deeply affecting him, and he would not allow anyone to change his wound's dressing following the incident as it reminded him of his dead compatriot, stating, "
he ripped up
his own uniform
to make it.
" (
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
His division is indicated by the blue bandage fashioned out of his uniform.

PAGE: McKenna

"
"Lieutenant, Where's Helmsman McKenna?"
"He has lungworm, sir. He couldn't report to his post."
"
–
Christopher Pike
and Hikaru Sulu
,
2258
(
Star Trek
)

McKenna
was the
conn officer
assigned to the

for its launch in

Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Since lungworm is a real species that does not infect
Humans
, McKenna might not be (completely) Human. Alternately, McKenna might have contracted an alien species known by this name.
Apocrypha
McKenna's first name was revealed to be Kyle in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
novel
The Gemini Agent
. The
IDW
Star Trek: Ongoing
comic book
Red Level Down
revealed that he had a sister at
Starfleet Academy
named Amy (
β
), a member of
Red Squad
, who had also come down with the same condition.
External link
Kyle McKenna
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

was a well-known
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.

died
under mysterious circumstances in
2364
, possibly a victim of the
parasitic beings
that had infiltrated

. (
TNG
: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: McMinn
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/McMinn
======================================================================

McMinn
was a
crewmember
serving on
USS
Voyager
during its time in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2376
, McMinn was injured by a
power
surge caused by an
electromagnetic lifeform
occupying
Voyager
, after he and crewman
Gibson
attempted to vent
toxic
gas
from
Deck 7
. (
VOY
: "
The Haunting of Deck Twelve
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the
24th century
.
In
2353
, she was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on the directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. Her office was located in room #35951. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2371
, she was one of the Starfleet Command officers at
Earth Station McKinley
during the construction of the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2373
, she was one of the Starfleet Command officers at
Beta Antares Ship Yards
during the construction of the
USS
Prometheus
. (
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, she was listed, with the rank of
admiral
, as a Starfleet Command officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after producer and assistant director
Merri Howard
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Merri Howard (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Merri_Howard_(comedian)
======================================================================

For other individuals of the same name, please see
Merri Howard
.
Species:
Humanoid
Occupation:
Comedian
Merri Howard
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Howard was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was "NT-45323". (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Merri Howard was mentioned in the biofilter analysis in "
Shades of Gray
". The analysis was a copy of a list of comedians first seen in "The Outrageous Okona".
This character was named after producer
Merri Howard
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

requiring
Gabl representation
to be replaced with
Bruno matrices

cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the

in

")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

on the

2363

Exploratory Division
in the Yoyodyne Division at 40 Eridani A Starfleet Construction Yards during the construction of the
USS
Phoenix
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
",
dedication plaque
) Later the same year, he was one of the
Starfleet Command
officers
at the
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
whose name later appeared on the
2368
updated
dedication plaque
for the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2367
, he was again with Fleet Operations with
San Francisco Fleet Yards
'
Yoyodyne Propulsion Systems
at the time of the construction of the
USS
Sutherland
. (
TNG
: "
Redemption II
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2370
, he was one of the Starfleet Command officers at
Antares Ship Yards
during the construction of the
USS
Defiant
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2371
, he was one of the Starfleet Command officers at
Earth Station McKinley
during the construction of the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Equinox, Part II
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2372
, he was one of the Starfleet Command officers at Antares Ship Yards during the construction of the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2375
, he was one of the Starfleet Command officers at Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards during the construction of the
USS
Sao Paulo
. (
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Command
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after television producer and writer
Michael Piller
.
He was listed as a member of Starfleet Command at the Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Michael Westmore (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Michael_Westmore_(24th_century)
======================================================================

For the 23rd century officer, please see
Mike Westmore
.
For Michael Westmore, Jr., please see
Michael Westmore, Jr.
.
Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Yard engineer
,
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
Status:
Active
(

)
Marital status:
Married
Spouse(s):

Michael "Mike" Westmore
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
24th century
.
In
2328
, Westmore was listed as a commercial transport passenger aboard the
SS
Kogin
, where he was ferried with his traveling companion,
Marion Westmore
, from
Earth Colony II
to the
Tellun system
.
His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
According to the
dedication plaques
of various
Federation
starships
, Westmore was listed as a
yard engineer
during the construction of the
USS
Defiant
at
Antares Ship Yards
in 2370,
USS
Voyager
at
Earth Station McKinley
in
2371
,
USS
Valiant
, again, at Antares Ship Yards in
2372
,
USS
Prometheus
at
Beta Antares Ship Yards
in
2374
, and
USS
Sao Paulo
at
Utopia Planitia Ship Yards
in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
",
dedication plaque
;
VOY
: "
Caretaker
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
;
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
",
dedication plaque
;
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
During this time, as well in 2372, he was named as member of the
Propulsion Systems
team at
San Francisco Yards
, listed on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
,
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was named as a member of the
Fleet Ops
team at the
Marin County Starfleet Yards
,
Skywalker Division
, listed on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
makeup artist
Michael Westmore
.

PAGE: Midia

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Midia

Quark's
(employee)
Occupation:
Dabo girl
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Played by
:
Unknown actress
Midia
was a
dabo girl
at
Quark's
in the year
2372
.
She was among the employees of Quark's who went into strike against pay cut. Along with
Leeta
,
Rom
,
Broik
, and several other staff members, she tried to pay the customers on the
Promenade
not to visit Quark's. (
DS9
: "
Bar Association
")
When
Quark
was questioned by
Ch'Pok
, a
Klingon
prosecutor during the trial of
Worf
on
Deep Space 9
, about what the
Ferengi
overheard about the attack conducted by Worf on a Klingon ship, Quark said that it was when
Dr.
Julian Bashir
was talking to Midia. However, Quark then believed it was another dabo girl. (
DS9
: "
Rules of Engagement
")
Midia was played by an
unknown actress
.

PAGE: Mike Gray (comedian)

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mike_Gray_(comedian)

Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's database
Mike Gray
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Gray was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
YC-34852
. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The list of comedians was taken from the production office phone directory, with this character named for co-producer
Mike Gray
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

======================================================================
PAGE: Milan (scientist)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Milan_(scientist)
======================================================================

Milan
was a
scientist
who worked with
radiation sensitivity
. This scientist's work suggested it was possible for
Humans
to adapt to high
radiation
environments.
In
2366
, when considering how Humans would survive on
Tau Cygna V
, a
planet
bathed in
hyperonic radiation
,
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
, who was familiar with Milan, presented the results of this scientist's research as an explanation. She, then, suggested the possiblity of the Tau Cygna Humans achieving this feat with extensive
viral therapy
. (
TNG
: "
The Ensigns of Command
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Miller
was a
scientist
at the
Daystrom Institute
. This individual worked with
Payne
on
stardate
41039. Their search for spontaneous transformation under a set condition resulted in a finding that "
tachyon
propagation can be described by hybrid-classical interpretations of sub-quantum mechanics."
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")

Steven Miller
, a flame artist at
CBS Digital
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mira
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mira
======================================================================

Star
Mira
was the the primary star of
Omicron Ceti
(and the designation used for the system by the International Astronomical Union). It appeared as the name of the system in some source material, such as
Star Trek Maps
.
Character
Mira
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Karno
, in the
forest
with Mira,
" which referred to
overeating
or
weight
gain.
In
2381
,
Ensign
Brad Boimler
said this to
Lt.
Kayshon
, inadvertently offending the Tamarian. (
LD
: "
wej Duj
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Mira
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Mirab
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mirab
======================================================================

Mirab
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases, "
Mirab. His sails unfurled.
" These phrases were used as a metaphor for departure or retreat, a calm tone meaning the former and an urgent tone meaning the latter. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
";
LD
: "
Old Friends, New Planets
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Mirab was pronounced as "mee-RAHB".

was a
Starfleet

.

Green's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode. It is unclear whether he served on this ship or was simply reported missing by it.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mith bin Tong
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mith_bin_Tong
======================================================================

Mith bin Tong
was a
Federation
judge
who resided on
Earth
in the
24th century
.
In
2381
, bin Tong was assigned to the case of
Captain
Carol Freeman
's alleged destruction of
Pakled Planet
with a
Varuvian bomb
.
Admiral
Les Buenamigo
relayed this news to Admiral
Alonzo Freeman
and
Ensign
Beckett Mariner
, who protested that bin Tong was a "planets' rights
lunatic
" who would be inclined to rule against her
mother
. Nevertheless,
Starfleet
was able to gather enough evidence to exonerate Freeman by the time of the
trial
. (
LD
: "
Grounded
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Mitsuya

24th century
Starfleet
flag officer
. Mitsuya was one of the best
poker
players in the
fleet
, next to
Jadzia Dax
.
In mid-
2370
, Mitsuya diverted the
USS
Crockett
to
starbase
Deep Space 9
to talk about
Cardassian
foreign policy
. Dax claimed that that was just an excuse to get
Commander
Sisko
in a
game
of poker because Mitsuya cleaned him out
the year before
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Mobar
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mobar
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Con artist
Status:
Arrested
(
2376
)
Played by
:
Gregg Daniel
"
Your logic is flawed.
"
–
Tuvok
,
2376
("
Live Fast and Prosper
")
Mobar
was an
alien
con artist
who
impersonated
Starfleet
Lieutenant Commander
Tuvok as part of a scam performed in conjunction with his partners
Dala
and
Zar
in
2376
.
During this deception, Mobar and his partners were able to move from
system
to system offering services for different goods from various
alien
species, but not delivering what they promised. Eventually, the
USS
Voyager
caught up with the three and captured them, returning everything they had
stolen
.
Mobar took quite a liking to Tuvok and his
Vulcan
mannerisms – going so far as to remain in character even when just among his fellow con artists – and felt privileged when he met the real Tuvok. When faced with a stand-off of drawn phasers with Tuvok, Mobar commented that "
logic
would indicate" that neither of them had an advantage over the other. Tuvok, unamused by Mobar's sendup of Vulcan behavior, promptly
blinded
him with the beam of a
SIMs beacon
and
phasered
him. (
VOY
: "
Live Fast and Prosper
")
Mobar was played by
Gregg Daniel
. The costume worn by Daniel as Mobar was auctioned off in the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
.
[1]
(X)
External link

, Admiral Moore was given the first name of "James", this reference, however, would not be made in the finished episode.

PAGE: Moran
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Moran

Affiliation:

Occupation:
Chief medical officer
,
USS
Intrepid
Status:
Active
(
2346
)
For the unidentified actor, please see
Moran (actor)
.
Doctor
Moran
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
. In
2346
, Moran was the
chief medical officer
of the
USS
Intrepid
.
After the
attack
on
Khitomer
, a
Klingon
survivor named
Kahlest
was rescued by the
starship
. An evaluation of Kahlest revealed she had severe injuries. CMO Moran stabilized Kahlest's condition, but recommended that she be transferred to a
starbase
medical facility. She was later transferred to
Starbase 24
. (
TNG
: "
Sins of The Father
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Chief Medical Officer Moran may be named after the episode's co-writer
W. Reed Moran
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Morgo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Morgo
======================================================================

Species:
Sentient volcano
Rank:
Volcanic Lord
Occupation:
Co-leader of
Dulaine
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
Played by
:
Fred Tatasciore
Morgo
was an ancient
sentient volcano
on
Dulaine
. He served as one of the three co-leaders of the
planet
in the late
24th century
, alongside
Mendrick
and
355
.
Known as "
Volcanic Lord
" and "
Navel
of the
Planet
", Morgo was worshiped as a
god
by the
Dulainians
and formed the center of their
religion
. Those who
sinned
against the Dulainian faith were
sacrificed
by being thrown into the molten
lava
within Morgo's
crater
.
In
2381
,
Lieutenant Commander
Andy Billups
and
Ensign
Sam Rutherford
from the
USS
Cerritos
were to be sacrificed to Morgo for the offense of entering a sacred hall with covered
belly buttons
. During the proceedings, Morgo confirmed to
Commander
Jack Ransom
that
health
was the Dulainians' highest calling, to which Ransom responded by revealing his
muscled
physique. Impressed by Ransom's perfect "
lava tubes
", he and his co-leaders agreed to listen to his parley. (
LD
: "
The Least Dangerous Game
")
Morgo was voiced by
Fred Tatasciore
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Morianna Taal

======================================================================

Morianna Taal
was a
telepathically
sensitive woman who was
Tarquin
's first companion during his
exile
.
She stayed with him for the rest of her life and eventually
died
. She was buried, along with Tarquin's three later companions, in a small plot on
Tarquin's planet
. (
ENT
: "
Exile
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Moto
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Moto
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Security officer
Status:
Active
(
2259
)
Played by
:
Heather Langenkamp
Moto
was a
Starfleet
security
officer
who was stationed in the
brig
of the
USS
Enterprise
in
2259
. Moto had dark brown, mottled skin, a large, bulbous head, a hump on the back and
webbed
hands.
When
Khan Noonien Singh
beamed
Captain
James T. Kirk
,
Chief Engineer
Montgomery Scott
and
Weapons Officer
Carol Marcus
into one of the cells of the
Enterprise
's brig from aboard the
USS
Vengeance
, Moto turned around to view the new arrivals and was told by a frustrated Scott to immediately let them out of the cell. The trapped officers were later released. (
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
Background information
Moto was played by
Heather Langenkamp
, a makeup artist on
Star Trek Into Darkness
who had acted in the
Elm Street
film franchise and whose husband,
David LeRoy Anderson
, was involved in helping design the make-up for Moto. "
I wanted to be in
Star Trek
really desperately because, after having experienced all the
Elm Street
fan love, having been a part of the conventions and having been a part of such an iconic franchise, I knew it was something similar with
Star Trek
[....] So, when my husband got the job, when our studio got the job to do the makeups, I begged my husband, like, 'Please, can we make one that I can wear?' I tried to be cool about it, but I probably wasn't very cool. But I was persuasive enough and he caved.
"
[1]
The design of Moto was originally conceived by influential make-up effects sculptor
Moto Hata
. "
There was no movie that it was for, originally,
" explained Heather Langenkamp. "
A sculptor had come up with these designs. It was kind of a spec project and it ended up sitting on the shelf for many years. The sculptor actually passed away from a tragic bout with cancer. So we were even more determined to get this makeup on screen and we said, 'If it ever gets on screen we’ll call the character Moto,' because that was the name of the sculptor.
"
[2]
Heather Langenkamp, in particular, was very eager to persuade Director
J.J. Abrams
into including the character in the
film
as an homage to Hata. (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 94) She described the makeup as "a very special design that we’d developed in our studio."
[3]
Langenkamp also recalled, "
We begged J.J. to let us put it in the movie and he was gracious enough to let us.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 94) The director, in fact, was highly interested in using the makeup in the film. "
J.J. was really open-minded when David told him the scenario and the story, and he loved the design. So we found a place in the film where this character could fit, where it could logically be, which is in the brig.
"
[4]
Interested in discovering what it was like to wear such an extreme makeup design for a
Star Trek
film, Langenkamp was chosen to play the role of Moto. "
I wanted to see what it felt like to wear that kind of make-up and act [....] Even though I'm a background character, I wanted to take it seriously.
"
The makeup Heather Langenkamp wore to portray Moto in
Star Trek Into Darkness
was a six-part, time-consuming arrangement of prosthetics. "
That was a five-hour application,
" noted Langenkamp. "
We arrived at 3 a.m.
" After her hair was tied back (so it wouldn't become accidentally glued into the makeup) and skin protectant was applied to her (so the glue wouldn't harm her skin), the makeup application process began. Langenkamp's father-in-law, Lance Anderson, was one of the persons who applied the makeup. "
We [...] put on a cowl, which is basically a headpiece,
" stated Langenkamp. The main weight of all the prosthetics was in the headpiece, which was glued onto the shoulders. Also included in the makeup were a chin-piece and face-piece. "
There was a lip and throat-piece that went over that [face-piece]. Then there was another lip they put on at the end. The next piece had an internal bladder that could puff up real big.
" The bladder, an inflatable bag that would be obscured from being shown on-screen but was designed to emulate breathing, could not only blow up but also light up.
Following a short break in the application process, Heather Langenkamp donned the portion of the costume used to represent the hump on Moto's back. Still to be applied was the texture of the alien's skin. "
The painting begins and the painting was designed in advance, so we have the bust of the design in front of the artists. Most of the pieces have already been pre-painted to save time. You had layers and layers of spots and streaks, to make it look like real skin. I glossed it up with some kind of shiny substance, so it looks like the sheen of a frog's skin. Then I put on the rest of the costume [....] [Costume Designer]
Michael Kaplan
and his crew were so patient because they had to make a special shirt for this character and the hump.
" (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 94)
Heather Langenkamp played Moto over a period of three or four days. "
The first day was in the brig, when the
Enterprise
characters are imprisoning Khan,
" Langenkamp reflected. "
The second day was also in the brig, when
Alice Eve
and
Simon Pegg
get transported back to the
Enterprise
after their adventure.
" She found things to occupy her attention during this time. "
I imagined that Moto is a climate-control officer, keeping the humidity at a proper level. I sat at a desk and had all these dials to play with.
" Moto was also filmed as part of a memorial scene near the end of the movie, though the character's involvement in the scene was ultimately
deleted
.
Although Heather Langenkamp is fairly well known from the
Elm Street
franchise, the prosthetics for Moto were so extensive that many people are unaware she was in
Star Trek Into Darkness
. "
I actually have to tell them I'm in the movie because nobody in the world is going to know that on their own, unless they like to read the credits.
" In summation of her experience playing Moto, she said, "
It was exciting. I hope I didn't jump the shark, but I'm really happy that I got to do it.
"
[5]
Owing especially to the family connections in designing and applying the Moto makeup, Langenkamp noted, "
It was a fun make-up for me to do, and to wear.
" She referred to putting on the character's Starfleet uniform as "probably the most exciting part of the whole thing for me." (
Star Trek Magazine
issue 172
, p. 94)
A behind the scenes picture of Moto was used for card #17, Security Officer Moto, of the
virtual collectible card battle game
Star Trek: Rivals
in
2013
.

. (

======================================================================
PAGE: N'Duwo Skra
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/N'Duwo_Skra
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Species:
Furie
Affiliation:
Venari Ral
Occupation:
Pirate
Status:
Deceased
(
3195
)
Died:
3195,
USS
Miyazaki
Played by
:
Stephen Adekolu
N'Duwo Skra
was a
Furie
who led a band of
pirates
in the late
32nd century
. He was once imprisoned by the
Federation
, and killed nineteen
guards
during his escape. Operating in the
disputed territories
, he was known to pay his
mercenaries
handsomely because their survival was never guaranteed.
In
3195
, Skra captured a team of
Starfleet Academy
and
War College
cadets
amid a training
mission
on the
USS
Miyazaki
. Claiming the ship as
salvage
, he demanded thirty thousand bars of
latinum
for his
hostages
' release. He planned to throw one of the hostages out the
airlock
to send a message to the
USS
Athena
, but
Lieutenant Commander
Tomov
fought him and his men to give his cadets the chance to flee. Skra had Tomov's partially-
cannibalized
corpse ejected into
space
, while the cadets barricaded themselves on the
bridge
.
The Furies eventually managed to break into the bridge. Skra advanced on
Caleb Mir
, but shot
B'Avi
instead when he jumped in the way of Skra's
weapon
. Skra and the remaining Furies then succumbed to a
telepathic
attack from
Tarima Sadal
on the
Athena
. It emerged that Skra had in fact been working with
Nus Braka
of the
Venari Ral
. (
SA
: "
Come, Let's Away
")
Skra was played by
Stephen Adekolu
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nagilum
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nagilum
======================================================================

Species:
Extra-dimensional
non-humanoid lifeform
Status:
Active
(
2365
)
Played by
:
Earl Boen
(voice)
"
Why are you so alarmed when I've gone to such trouble to look just like you?
"
"
Captain, sensors show nothing out there. Absolutely nothing.
"
"
Sure is a damned ugly nothing.
"
–
Nagilum
,
Data
and
Geordi La Forge
,
2365
("
Where Silence Has Lease
")
Nagilum
was a
non-corporeal
immortal
being that lived outside the
universe
and could not be detected by
Federation
sensors
, insomuch as being called by
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, "
something that both is and is not there.
" Its
intelligence
was described as "vast", such as that at first
Deanna Troi
could not discern it. It had no concept of
gender
or
death
, and demonstrated
telepathic
and
telekinetic
abilities; it was also able to
kill
by a mere
thought
.
In
2365
, Nagilum encountered the
USS
Enterprise
-D
en route
to the
Morgana Quadrant
. It trapped the
Enterprise
within a "
hole in space
" and proceeded to test the
Enterprise
crew
's reactions to various stimuli. Once
Doctor
Katherine Pulaski
surmised that they were being experimented upon, Nagilum manifested a
humanoid
face to communicate with the crew.
As an immortal, Nagilum professed interest about the concept of a "limited existence", and killed the
Enterprise
conn
officer
Ensign
Haskell
to observe it. Further intrigued, it decided to thoroughly study this phenomenon, which would have cost the lives of one-third to one-half of the
Enterprise
crew. Rather than submit to the experiment, Captain Jean-Luc Picard activated the
Enterprise
's
auto-destruct sequence
.
As Picard prepared for his death in his quarters, he was confronted by Nagilum's manifestations of Troi and Data, both attempting to convince Picard that his actions were not right. When Picard realized that his officers were acting unusually, he told Nagilum that its attempts to dissuade him would not work, causing Nagilum to release the ship from the void, and Picard and Riker to end the auto-destruct sequence with
seconds
to spare.
In a final conversation with Picard, Nagilum revealed that it had learned everything it needed to know. Nagilum judged Humanity as being aggressive and militant, obsessed with defying the inevitable and concluded that "
as species, we have no common ground
"; though it did agree with Picard that they perhaps shared the trait of curiosity. (
TNG
: "
Where Silence Has Lease
")
According to the script for "Where Silence Has Lease", Nagilum was pronounced "nah-GHEE-lum."
[1]
Nagilum was portrayed by actor
Earl Boen
. Nagilum's name was inspired by
Richard Mulligan
, who was originally sought to play the role. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 68)
External links
Nagilum
(X)
at
StarTrek.com
Nagilum
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Najwa Sisko

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Najwa_Sisko
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Najwa Sisko
was the
wife
of
Rashon Sisko
and
daughter-in-law
to
Kaira Sisko
.

32nd century
,
Emissary of the Prophets
Benjamin Sisko
's extended family was listed on a
family tree
exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")

PAGE: Nara
CATEGORIES: Individuals

======================================================================

Species:

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant junior grade
Occupation:
Nacelle control room
supervisor
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Nancy Harewood
Lieutenant junior grade
Nara
was a
nacelle control room
supervisor
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
. Nara was of a species whose distinguishing characteristics included enhanced facial ridges on their forehead and nostrils extenuated over their nose.
Lieutenant j.g.
Daniel Kwan
was serving under her when Kwan committed
suicide
. Prior to his
death
, she feared he was interested in her job, something the half-
Napean
empath
sensed.
She was interviewed during the investigation of the suicide, and stated that Kwan was having no trouble with his duties and seemed happy at his work. He did not confide in her that anything was troubling him. (
TNG
: "
Eye of the Beholder
")
Appendices
Background information
Nara was played by actress
Nancy Harewood
. In the first draft script, this character was called Lieutenant Harper. In the final draft she was referred to as a "humanoid, mid-thirties", which suggests she was born in the mid-
2330s
.
She was a member of an unidentified species, which was identified in her
Star Trek: Starship Creator
biography as Canopian from the
Canopus Planet
. Her father is named Phacops and her mother is named Rana. She has two "pod sisters" named Aran and Anra.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 67 mistakenly indicates this character made an appearance in
Star Trek: Insurrection
, referencing a different character named Nara ("Human male, 35,...") in the script.
[1]
Lieutenant
Daniels
replaced Nara's role in the film.
External link
Nara
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Nathaniel Teros
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nathaniel_Teros
======================================================================

Nathaniel Teros
was a
scientist
who in
2340
developed the theory of
neuromuscular adaptation
for
low-gravity species
. The principles of his theory was sound, even though at the time it had no practical success.
In
2370
, advancements in
neurochemistry
allowed
Dr.
Julian Bashir
to adapt Teros' theory for
Melora Pazlar
. (
DS9
: "
Melora
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================

Neffie Beumont
was a female resident of
New Orleans
in the mid-
24th century
.
The teenage
Benjamin Sisko
had a crush on her for several
weeks
, not having the courage to ask her for a
date
. After some advice from
his father
, he finally went over to her house and was turned down by her. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ngomo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ngomo
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Ngomo
was a
Starfleet
admiral
who was in Starfleet in the
2370s
.
In
2371
, she was on
Earth
. Following a
transporter accident
aboard the
USS
Defiant
, which displaced
Benjamin Sisko
,
Julian Bashir
, and
Jadzia Dax
to
2024
, Admiral Ngomo wanted to come aboard the
starship
and supervise the investigation.
Kira Nerys
expressed her appreciation for the admiral's offer; however, until it was determined what caused the accident and what happened to the missing
senior staff
members, Kira did not want anyone beaming to the ship. If the admiral came by
shuttle
, that was fine. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nichelle Sisko
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nichelle_Sisko
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
For Joseph Sisko's mother of the same name, please see
Nichelle Sisko (Joseph's mother)
.
Sisko family lineage
Nichelle Sisko
was a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
offspring
of
Tyrell Sisko
.
In the
32nd century
, Nichelle's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
A dotted line off of Nichelle on the tree suggests they had an offspring.
This character's name is likely a reference to
Star Trek: The Original Series
's
Nichelle Nichols
.

Sisko family lineage
Nichelle Sisko
was a
Human
female
in the late
23rd
/early
24th centuries
, the
spouse
of
Raimy Sisko
,
mother
to
Joseph Sisko
, and
grandmother
to famous
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
Nichelle gave birth to Joseph in
2309
. (
DS9
: "
Homefront
")
In the
32nd century
, Nichelle's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
.)
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character's name is likely a reference to
Star Trek: The Original Series
's
Nichelle Nichols
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nilo Saharan
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nilo_Saharan
======================================================================

The assay office list
Nilo Saharan
was an
individual
who lived in the late
24th century
.
In
2373
, Saharan stored valuables in the
assay office
on
Deep Space 9
. When
Arissa
accessed the office's
computer
, she pulled up a list of
names
, upon which Saharan's name appeared. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
",
display graphic
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.
Nilo is an
Italian
,
Spanish
, and
Portuguese
name
for a male child.
As well, this name might be a reference to the proposed
Nilo-Saharan languages
of
Africa
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nils Diaz
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nils_Diaz
======================================================================

Dr.
Nils Diaz
was a
scientist
working on different
warp field
techniques at the
Tanaline Propulsion Laboratory
in the
24th century
.
In
2370
,
Ensign
Taurik
ran a
computer simulation
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
wherein he tested a new warp field configuration. In the simulation, Taurik deliberately altered the
plasma flow
to the
warp nacelles
, so that it was out of sync, and increased the
warp field integrity
by seven percent. He was simulating a technique first experimented on by Dr. Diaz.
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
was familiar with his work, but not with this new technique on warp field, which had not yet been released. Taurik was able to read a preliminary review of his work while at
Starfleet Academy
. (
TNG
: "
Lower Decks
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Nils Diaz was named after "Lower Decks" writer
René Echevarria
's godfather.
(
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 282)

======================================================================
PAGE: Nima
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nima
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Ennis
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Patricia Tallman
Nima
was an
Ennis
woman fighting her rivals, the
Nol-Ennis
, on a small
moon
in the
Gamma Quadrant
.
She was always standing
guard
at the side of the Ennis' leader,
Golin Shel-la
, and was
killed
several
times
while the
crew
of the
runabout
USS
Yangtzee Kiang
was stranded on the moon. She was revived and her wounds were healed by tiny artificial
microbes
every time she was killed. (
DS9
: "
Battle Lines
")
Nima was portrayed by stuntwoman
Patricia Tallman
who received no credit for her appearance.
Her name was not mentioned in the episode but in the script for the episode. She was described in the script as a lieutenant of Shel-la.
[1]
Nima appears in the
Star Trek Online
expansion
Victory Is Life
.
External link
Nima
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Nimembeh
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nimembeh
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:

Starfleet Academy
instructor
Status:
Active
(
2368
)
Admiral
Nimembeh
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
serving at
Starfleet Command
during the mid-
24th century
. During his career, Nimembeh had also taught the course called
Tactical Analysis
at
Starfleet Academy
.
Chakotay
gave his
resignation
to Admiral Nimembeh on
March 3
,
2368
before joining the
Maquis
.
Seven years later
, Chakotay said he could still remember "
the look on the Admiral's face.
" (
VOY
: "
In the Flesh
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
VOY Season 5 DVD
Region 1 closed captioning, his name was spelled "Namimby".

in the

Athena
. (

set decoration

======================================================================
PAGE: Nocona
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nocona
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Miner
Status:
Active
(
2378
)
Played by
:
John Kenton Shull
Nocona
was an
alien
commander
for a
mining
company in the
Delta Quadrant
, near the
Beta Quadrant
border. He owned an
asteroid
field that contained a large
Talaxian
colony
, which he wanted to
evict
.
In
2378
,
his ship
launched
thermolyte explosives
into the field to shatter open the
asteroids
. At the same time, the
Delta Flyer
was passing through, and got knocked to the asteroid the Talaxians lived in. He was later hailed by the
USS
Voyager
, and told them that he wasn't aware their
away team
had entered the field, and that they should have asked permission. After
Voyager
assured him they didn't want the resources from the field, he let their crewmen return.
Aboard the asteroid, he gave a final deadline for the Talaxians to leave, as he'd been warning them for years, as their asteroid held more than 30% of the
ore
. After a confrontation on the asteroid, ending in
Neelix
sending them away at
phaser
-point, they negotiated for more time aboard
Voyager
. Later when the Talaxians tried to install
shield generators
into their asteroid, to protect from Commander Nocona's ship, he attacked the
Baxial
, and the
Talaxian fighter
. With help from the
Delta Flyer
, they were successful in placing the field, so Nocona went off to mine other asteroids, leaving the Talaxians alone. (
VOY
: "
Homestead
")
Commander Nocona was played by actor
John Kenton Shull
.
His costume was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Nogura
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nogura
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Starfleet Headquarters
flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2270s
)
Admiral
Nogura
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
who served during the late
23rd century
.
Nogura temporarily transferred command of the
USS
Enterprise
to
Rear Admiral
James T. Kirk
, and
demoted
Captain
Will Decker
. The
transfer
was done at Kirk's
request
in reference to the
V'ger
crisis. When hearing that Admiral Kirk had talked Nogura into giving him command of the
Enterprise
, now-
Commander
Montgomery Scott
commented, "
I doubt it was that easy with Nogura!
" Kirk responded, imitating the engineer's Scottish burr, "
Yer right.
"
Nogura was also responsible for "
drafting
"
Doctor
McCoy
by invoking the little-known, seldom-used
reserve activation clause
, much to the doctor's initial frustration. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
Appendices
Background information
This Nogura was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
shooting script
, after the
Enterprise
locked onto the Starfleet
transceivers
, the ship received notification that Nogura had received their reports. This information was cut from the theatrical film.
The character's first name was never revealed on screen, but it was given as "Heihachiro" by
Gene Roddenberry
in the
novelization
of
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
which Roddenberry wrote himself.
Apocrypha
Nogura was also referred to several times, but never actually shown, in the
Star Trek
newspaper comic strip
as well as in the novel
The Lost Years
, where he was said to have been a decades-long friend to the Kirk family and the man responsible for getting Kirk his first assignment as captain of the
Enterprise
. Nogura also appeared in the
second DC Comics series
and the
Star Trek: Vanguard
novel series.
The character was intended for Japanese actor
James Shigeta
, who, unfortunately, never got to act out the role.
External link
Heihachiro Nogura
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

passenger in the

.

ferried her from

to the

passenger manifest

aboard the

-D
in

by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (

: "

",
okudagram
)

Leonhardt was named after regular TNG background actress and stand-in
Nora Leonhardt
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Noss
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Noss
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Lori Petty
Noss
was a
female
humanoid
who lived during the mid-
24th century
.
Noss was encountered by
Tuvok
and
Tom Paris
after they
crashed
on an
uncharted planet
in
2375
. She found their
shuttle
, and pulled a
weapon
and stole their
medical kit
. Noss was later attacked by
reptilian-looking aliens
, but Tuvok saved her. Noss led them to
her ship
, which had a working
force field
but no working
engines
. Noss told them that she lived there for fourteen
seasons
, and had never seen a ship escape the
gravity well
. She helped them survive on the hostile planet by teaching them how to
hunt
and providing them with shelter.
During their time together, Noss became romantic towards Tuvok. After he was hurt, she nursed him back to health and kissed him, only to be rebuffed by him. She attempted to leave them, but Paris stopped her.
After
USS
Voyager
rescued Tuvok and Paris, Noss was returned to her
home planet
, but not before Tuvok performed a
mind meld
on Noss that helped her gain a better understanding of him. (
VOY
: "
Gravity
")
Noss was played by actress
Lori Petty
.

PAGE: Okaro
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Okaro
======================================================================

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
Director
Okaro
was a high-ranking member of
Starfleet Intelligence
in
2404
in an
alternate timeline
.
In that year,
The Doctor
inquired with Director Okaro as to the whereabouts of
Admiral
Janeway
, under the impression that she was on a classified
mission
, but was assured that Janeway hadn't been involved with any classified work since she started teaching at
Starfleet Academy
. When The Doctor told
Reginald Barclay
this, Barclay suggested that the director might have been trying to throw him off the scent.
This timeline was later negated when Janeway
traveled back in time
to
2378
and assisted the crew of the
USS
Voyager
with returning to the
Alpha Quadrant
sixteen years earlier than they would have without the admiral's assistance. (
VOY
: "
Endgame
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

remembered the day when he realized, as a

in Doctor Olafson's class on Transporter Theory, listening to the doctor speak about the body being converted into billions of

of

and zipping through

atom
out of place and poof! You never come back..."

PAGE: Onafuwa

Onafuwa
was an
instructor
at
Starfleet Academy
during the mid-
23rd century
. They taught
Fundamentals of Quantum Stochastics
, as well as advanced one and two, and
seminar
.
Cadet
Spock
took all of these and received
top marks
.

characterized Onafuwa as a brilliant
monster
, because of the brutality of their courses. (
ST
: "
Q&A
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Onafuwa model
was likely named for them.

======================================================================
PAGE: Onaya
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Onaya
======================================================================

Species:
Energy being
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Played by
:
Meg Foster
"
It's not what I am that matters, it's what I do. You don't know the minds I've touched.
Catullus
,
Tarbolde
,
Keats
, a hundred others. I unlocked their potential.
"
–
Onaya
,
2372
("
The Muse
")
Onaya
was an
energy being
that could assume
corporeal
form.
It both increased exponentially and fed off the creative
energy
generated by
the stimulated
brain
of another being. The process usually proved damaging to the subject being stimulated, as the strain of increased
capillary
dilation,
neurotransmitter
production, and general
neural
activity would cause
synaptic collapse
and, if left untreated,
death
. Onaya justified its activities by saying that victims would receive
immortality
for the
creative ventures
that were a byproduct of its activities.
It had traveled for thousands of years seeking compatible creative beings, and
found hundreds
such as the
Earth
poets
Catullus
and
John Keats
, the
Cardassian
architect
Tavor Kell
, and
Tarbolde
from
Canopus Planet
. Onaya could be detected by
tricorders
set to
scan
for
psionic energy
residue.
Onaya arrived aboard a
Bajoran transport
to
Deep Space 9
in
2372
, where she met
Jake Sisko
, and eventually fed off his creative energies as he was working on his first
novel
,
Anslem
. Jake's life was saved when his father,
Benjamin
, intervened. Onaya, however, was able to escape from the
station
, vanishing into the unknown. (
DS9
: "
The Muse
")
Onaya was played by actress
Meg Foster
.
She was described in the script as "
a striking Alien Woman [...] Her features are vaguely
feline
, though you might have to look twice to see she wasn't Human. Her skin is pale, her hair long and dark, like her dress, which is flowing and diaphanous. It's hard to guess her age, mid-thirties, maybe forties.
" (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion - A Series Guide and Script Library
)
In
The Ashgate Encyclopedia of Literary and Cinematic Monsters
, Bradley Skene compares Onaya to
Lamia
of Greek mythology and the
Leanan sídhe
of Irish mythology.
External link
Onaya
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Onna Karapleedeez
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Onna_Karapleedeez
======================================================================

Onna Karapleedeez
was a well-known
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
Karapleedeez
died
under mysterious circumstances in
2364
, possibly a victim of the
parasitic beings
that had infiltrated
Starfleet Command
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The name is an exotic rendering of the Greek surname "Karaplides".
In "
The Lost Era
" novel
The Buried Age
, Karapleedeez is identified as a female
Kreetassan
, commanding the USS
Mary Kingsley
circa
2359
. Her crew at the time included
second officer
Kathryn Janeway
.
External link
Onna Karapleedeez
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Orak (Deep Space 9)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Orak_(Deep_Space_9)
======================================================================

Orak
was a mutual friend of
Jake Sisko
and
Leanne
, the latter of whom he dated for a time until mid-
2371
.
After Leanne's return to
Deep Space 9
from
Bajor
, she bumped into Jake and humorously recalled the time that she, Jake, and Orak had dinner at the
Klingon restaurant
. Jake, who hadn't seen Orak in a while, asked Leanne how he was doing, to which she replied that they had stopped seeing each other a few weeks earlier. (
DS9
: "
Life Support
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Oros
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Oros
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Emerald Chain
Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Unknown
(
3180s
)
Played by
:
Osric Chau
Oros
was a
scientist
in captivity by the
Emerald Chain
in the late
32nd century
. He was imprisoned in a
work camp
on an unknown
planet
, in a cell alongside
Ruon Tarka
.  Oros eventually revealed to Tarka that he was devising a method of escaping the Emerald Chain by building a special
transporter
capable of transporting him to an
alternate universe
. More specifically, a place called
Kayalise
, a paradise from Oros'
people's
legends and
mythology
, which he believed to have located somewhere in the
multiverse
.
After quite some time, the two were able to build a
prototype
transporter. They also grew close to one another. However, the facility they were being held in did not have sufficient power for the transporter to function. Emerald Chain enforcers tracked the power drain to Oros' quarters and threatened him. Tarka shot the enforcer with his own
weapon
, and removed Oros'
slave controller
, as well as his own. Tarka escaped the Emerald Chain facility, whilst Oros, too injured to join Tarka, promised to finish the transporter.
Tarka hid in a nearby cave for approximately a week, at which point he witnessed a major power surge from the Emerald Chain facility, along with ships leaving. Upon investigating, Tarka found the facility completely abandoned. A symbol representing Kayalise was carved into the wall of his and Oros' old quarters, leading him to believe Oros made it out alive, having successfully completed the
interdimensional transporter
.  (
DIS
: "
The Galactic Barrier
")
The designing of Oros was discussed in the "Creating Aliens With Star Trek: Discovery's
Glenn Hetrick
" segment of
TRR
: "
The Galactic Barrier
".

will be less enjoyable
" than Ortiz's. (

: "

")

was a
male
Starfleet
command division
junior officer
who lived during the late
24th century
.
In
2380
, he served on the
USS
Cerritos
.
That year, he was mentioned in Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
's second incorrect answer to
Brad Boimler
's
rhetorical question
about who had just "
scored the best assignment on the ship.
" Tendi swooned over P'jok, exclaiming "
that guy can do anything, and what a snack.
" (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
In
2381
, P'jok, who was a member of the
Redshirts
aboard the
Cerritos
, was promoted to the
USS
Ventura
. P'jok's departure left an opening in the
club
on the
Cerritos
that was temporarily filled by
Brad Boimler
. (
LD
: "
The Spy Humongous
")
This ensign was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Pablo Baytart
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pablo_Baytart
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Helmsman
,
USS Voyager
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Played by
:
Unknown performer
(voice)
Ensign
Pablo Baytart
was a
Starfleet
officer
aboard the
USS
Voyager
during its voyage through the
Delta Quadrant
in the
2370s
.
History
Baytart was serving under
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
aboard the USS
Voyager
, when the ship and its
crew
were pulled into the Delta Quadrant in
2371
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
A skilled
pilot
, Baytart briefed Neelix on the basics of Starfleet
shuttle
operations, and was even considered as a replacement
conn officer
when it appeared as if
Lieutenant
Tom Paris
had decided to disembark from
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
", "
Investigations
")
In
2372
, when
Captain
Janeway
assigned
Lieutenant
Paris and Neelix to a mission to collect
food
supplies from a
planet
, Paris, who was experiencing personal problems with Neelix at the time, suggested that Baytart would be a better choice to pilot the shuttle. Janeway insisted that Paris take the mission, however, due to
electromagnetic
anomalies in the planet's
atmosphere
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
Baytart was also a skilled
juggler
, and was set to showcase his talents on Neelix's news show,
A Briefing with Neelix
. According to Neelix, Baytart would keep
PADDs
,
hyposprays
, even
phasers
, in the air "
for minutes at a time.
" (
VOY
: "
Investigations
")
Baytart was assigned
quarters
next to Ensign
Harry Kim
,
Voyager
's
operations officer
. The noise produced by Kim's
clarinet
would often be conducted by the
fluid conduits
in the wall between their quarters, a fact which annoyed Baytart. In 2372, when Baytart banged on the wall in response to Kim's playing, Kim and Paris joked that they should have Baytart transferred to the
night shift
. (
VOY
: "
The Thaw
")
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
He was heard off screen (voiced by an
unknown actor
) saying "
All set, Neelix
" in "Investigations".

searched
enrollment records
for all Trill music academies while seeking information on Belar. (

: "
Equilibrium
",
display graphic
)

PAGE: Parker (Commander)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Parker_(Commander)

Affiliation:

of an unit
Status:
Deceased
(

)
(
2374
)
Died:
2374
,
AR-558
Commander
Parker
was a member of
Starfleet
in the
2370s
.
He was the
second-in-command
of the unit that was assigned to protect the
Dominion
communication array
on
AR-558
after the
Federation Alliance
captured the
Chin'toka system
in late
2374
. He was later
killed
, and command of the depleted unit passed to
Lieutenant
Nadia Larkin
. (
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 628, Parker's name was an homage to
Fess Parker
, who played Sergeant Pike in the movie
Hell Is for Heroes
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Patient 8923/3
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Patient_8923/3
======================================================================

Species:
Nameless
Serial number:
Patient 8923/3
Status:
Active
(
2399
)
Played by
:
Zachary James Rukavina
Patient 8923/3
was a
Nameless
who had been previously
assimilated
by the
Borg
. After
his cube
was disconnected from the
Borg Collective
in
2385
, he went into
regenestasis
, where he remained for
fourteen years
.
That year, he was "
reclaimed
" by the
Borg Reclamation Project
, where he had his
Borg implants
removed by
Romulan
Doctor
Kabath
. The procedure was assisted by Dr.
Soji Asha
, who was in turn, observed by
Narek
. Following the procedure, Dr. Asha addressed the drone in his
mother tongue
, telling him that he was finally free. (
PIC
: "
Maps and Legends
")
The following day, the Reclamation Project's
executive director
,
Hugh
, observed Dr. Asha's tribute to Patient 8923/3 during his review of the procedure. For her actions, Hugh finally granted her access to the Romulan xB
Ramdha
for an interview. (
PIC
: "
The End is the Beginning
")
He was later brought out of regenestasis and survived the Romulan assault on the remaining xBs and drones. He later recognized and called out the name "
Locutus
" as he observed
Jean-Luc Picard
and the crew of
La Sirena
searching through the Artifact after it had crashed on
Coppelius
. (
PIC
: "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 1
")
He was portrayed by actor
Zachary James Rukavina
. He was credited as "XB/Nameless" in "Maps and Legends" and "Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 1"; he was uncredited in the archived footage that Hugh reviewed in "The End is the Beginning".

officer

24th century

in
2368

In 2368, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. In the week of
stardate
45852.1, on
Tuesday
, she had an appointment for a follow up session with
Counselor
Deanna Troi
at 11:30. This information was displayed on a
personal appointment log
in Troi's
quarters
. (

: "
Imaginary Friend
", "
The Next Phase
"
okudagram
)
In
2371
, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
. In that year, her name was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
Projections
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named for production staffer
Patricia Miller
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Paul Bogrow
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Paul_Bogrow
======================================================================

Paul Bogrow
was an old acquaintance of former
USS
Victory
crew members
Geordi La Forge
and
Susanna Leijten
, distinguished by his thin
mustache
.
Susanna nearly
married
Bogrow, believing she had unfairly judged him, but later changed her mind, deciding she'd been right the first time. He used to drive her crazy as she believed him to be "so full of himself". La Forge and Leijten discussed him while reminiscing over old times. (
TNG
: "
Identity Crisis
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

aboard the

PAGE: Pauline Sisko

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Pauline Sisko
was a
Starfleet
officer
and a distant
relative
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
offspring
of
David Sisko
and
Dikembe Sisko
, sibling to
Shevon Sisko
, and parent to
Ulan Sisko
. Pauline also served in Starfleet.
In the
32nd century
, Pauline's extended family linked to Benjamin Sisko was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

PAGE: Payne
