======================================================================

Occupation:
Scientist
Status:
Active
(
Stardate 41039
)
Payne
was a
scientist
at the
Daystrom Institute
. This individual worked with
Miller
on
stardate
41039. Their search for spontaneous transformation under a set condition resulted in a finding that "
tachyon
propagation can be described by hybrid-classical interpretations of sub-quantum mechanics."
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Chris Payne
, a flame artist at
CBS Digital
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Pell Underhill
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pell_Underhill
======================================================================

Pell Underhill
was a
22nd century
physicist
who, according to
Data
, "
conjectured that a major disruption in
time
-continuity could be compensated for by trillions of counter reactions.
"
In an attempt to deceive the crew, Data formulated a hypothesis, using Underhill as a reference, to explain why
Doctor
Crusher
's
mosses
displayed an unusual amount of growth after a trip through a
Paxan
wormhole
. While it was pointed out that Underhill's theories were with regards of
energy
, Data attempted to further explain his hypothesis that "
it was possible that the phenomenon could occur in matter at much higher levels of organization. Given the proper conditions.
"
After Data was excused from the meeting,
Geordi La Forge
described Data's hypothesis as completely implausible, based on Underhill's theories, further explaining that he was "
honestly amazed that [Data] even proposed it.
" (
TNG
: "
Clues
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Underhill figures in the
Star Trek: Enterprise - The Romulan War
novels
.
External link
Pell Underhill
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Penny M. Juday
CATEGORIES: Individuals

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Uncertain
(
2374
)
Penny M. Juday
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2372
, she was named as a
Spaceframe Development
officer on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
. (
Star Trek: First Contact
,
dedication plaque
)
Ensign
Juday later served in the
Dominion War
. In
2374
, a file from the
USS
Repulse
reported Ensign Juday
wounded in action
. A file from the
USS
Sarajevo
reported her missing. A file from the
USS
Cochrane
reported her killed. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Juday's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a computer readout in that episode. It is unclear which of the above was her home station, if any.
This character is most likely an in-joke referencing
Penny Juday
, a member of the
Star Trek
production staff.

======================================================================
PAGE: Penny Muroc
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Penny_Muroc
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2327
)
Played by
:
Rae Norman
Penny Muroc
was an older acquaintance of
Ensign
Jean-Luc Picard
's whom he met on
Starbase Earhart
in
2327
.
Penny was from
Rigel
and liked men in
uniform
. She met Picard twice in a
bar
in the
Bonestell Recreation Facility
; he impressed her with his smooth talk about her
eyes
the first night. She was much older than Picard and when Picard called her a "handsome woman" the second night, she threw her
drink
into the ensign's face, very much to the amusement of a watching
Q
. (
TNG
: "
Tapestry
")
Penny was played by actress
Rae Norman
. According to the final draft script, Penny was "
well into her forties
" by 2327 (putting her birthyear around the early
2280s
), while the actress was only 34 when the episode aired.
According to the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 236, Penny was named for an older woman that writer
Ronald D. Moore
had once dated.

during the construction of the

updated

for the

during the construction of the

,

during the construction of the

. (
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2375
, he was one of the Starfleet Command officers at Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards during the construction of the
USS
Sao Paulo
(
DS9
: "
The Dogs of War
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, the admiral was listed as a
Starfleet Command
representative on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after producer and director
Peter Lauritson
.
He was listed on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Odyssey
. This plaque was made for the
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
episode "
The Jem'Hadar
", but did not make an appearance in the episode. This plaque can be viewed at the twitter account of DS9's writer and producer
Ira Steven Behr
.
[1]

to intercept

which had taken position near

and which was attempting to open a
geodesic fold
to the
Delta Quadrant
. However, as the
Carolina
was 0.7
light years
away, they were unable to reach the
Ferengi
vessel in time, forcing
Lieutenant
Reginald Barclay
to improvise a way to manipulate the Ferengi into closing the fold before the crew of the
USS
Voyager
was killed by the
geodesic radiation
. (

")

crewman

Defiant
in the early

.

bridge
of the

of a

Boyce

fighters
attacked the

. (

these
three
bridge officers
.

Peterson family
who resided on DS9.

PAGE: Pomet
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pomet
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Mercenary
Status:
Deceased
, killed during the
baryon sweep
(
2369
)
Died:
2369
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
Played by
:
Alan Altshuld
Pomet
was a member of a group of
mercenaries
that boarded the
USS
Enterprise
-D
during preparations for a
baryon sweep
in
2369
at
Arkaria Base
with the intent of
stealing
trilithium resin
.
Pomet first worked in with the team in Main Engineering, preparing the trilithium for extraction. After their transport module for the resin was sabotaged by Captain Jean-Luc Picard, Pomet was reassigned to capture him. However, Picard, armed with a
crossbow
, shot Pomet in the leg with an arrow dipped in a sort of liquid, which knocked him out. He was
killed
by the baryon sweep. (
TNG
: "
Starship Mine
")
Pomet was played by
Alan Altshuld
.
His species
was not named.
In his article at
StarTrek.com
, the image used is that of
Satler
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Portal 63
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Portal_63
======================================================================

Species:
Tkon
Affiliation:
Tkon Empire
Occupation:
Guardian
of
Delphi Ardu IV
Status:
Active
(
2364
)
Played by
:
Darryl Henriques
Portal 63
was a
guardian
on a
planet
in the
Delphi Ardu
star system
that was active over
six hundred thousand years ago
, during the
Age of Bastu
as time was marked by the
Tkon Empire
. He was  invested with great powers, including the ability to immobilize
starships
and could appear in various corporeal forms according to the appearance of the beings he encountered. Portal 63 also had some form of
telepathy
. He remained inactive until
2364
when the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
encountered his
outpost
along with a
Ferengi
starship. At that time, the Portal learned that the Tkon Empire had fallen shortly after the
Age of Makto
and that he had been inactive all that time.
Both ships were caught in an
energy field
around the unexplored planet and sent
away teams
to try to disable the energy field. After the Ferengi attacked the
Enterprise
away team, the Portal appeared and threatened both teams. He asked "
Who will meet the challenge?
" After the Ferengi tried to lay blame on the
Humans
, the Portal became disgusted with the Ferengi's profiteer responses to his questions. When
Riker
refused to attack the Ferengi, the Portal was impressed and took an interest in Riker's wisdom. Once Riker passed several of the Portal's intellectual "tests," Riker convinced the Portal to free the two vessels in orbit and they departed. After releasing the ships, the Portal returned to his post until he was needed again. (
TNG
: "
The Last Outpost
")
Appendices
Background information
"Portal Six-Three", as he called himself, was played by
Darryl Henriques
.
In
Richard Krzemien
's original story, Portal 63 was named "Dilo" and was kind of a caretaker of the planet who slept when the Tkon Empire was destroyed. (
Creating the Next Generation
, pp. 39-40) The revised final draft of the script described him as "A tall, thin, aged (make-up effect) humanoid figure (Portal) in a fine long robe." Before making his "strike" towards Riker with his staff, the script notes described: "as the Figure seems a bit younger, better muscled, the robe shorter and more practical, with a large, lethal appearing scimitar-like weapon in hand," however his age appeared unchanged in the aired episode.
Apocrypha
In the video game
Star Trek: Resurgence
set in the year
2380
, Portal 63 is recruited to help the crew of the USS
Resolute
in finding and stopping a rogue faction of Tkon known as "the Scions of the Flame" from assimilating individuals en masse and reviving the Tkon Empire. During the battle aboard the Tkon
bioforming
warship
Aphelion
, Petty Officer Carter Diaz, with assistance of Alydian
major
Sarlit Arminta and Petty Officer Nili Edsilar, either kills Portal 63 after he betrays the away team, or he stays loyal to them and holds the line against the Scions of the Flame and dies as a result of his wounds.
Portal 63 is voiced by
Mark Rolston
in the game.
External link
Portal 63
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Prah Mantoos
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Prah_Mantoos
======================================================================

Species:
Prah Mantoos' species
Occupation:
Pilgrim
leader
Status:
Active
(
2151
)
Played by
:
Joseph Hindy
Prah Mantoos
was leader of a group of
pilgrims
of
his species
and several
Borothans
who, in
2151
, were on their way to witness a
protostar
that emits an
energy
burst every eleven
years
, which the pilgrims called the
Great Plume of Agosoria
.
Jonathan Archer
invited the group aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
for a tour and
dinner
and to witness the Plume aboard his
ship
. Unknown to anyone else at the time,
Suliban
agent
Silik
was among Mantoos' group. (
ENT
: "
Cold Front
")
Prah Mantoos was played by actor
Joseph Hindy
.
In the script of "Cold Front", Prah Mantoos was described thus; "
He's a gaunt, austere man.
" The script also referred to him as "wearing a ceremonial robe."
His costume was later sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Price Jones
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Price_Jones
======================================================================

Price Jones
was a
boxer
during the
24th century
.
Prior to
2371
, Jones fought against a
Cardassian
gul
named
Tulet
. Jones won the
match
after twenty-three rounds in an
event
which became known as "
The Knockout in the Neutral Zone
". One of those who witnessed the event was
Chakotay
, who believed it to be "
the best match
" he ever saw.
The Doctor
, who had a disdain for recreational fisticuffs, asked whether Jones had driven Tulet's
nasal bone
into his
brain
. (
VOY
: "
The Fight
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 186 spelled this boxer's name as "Pryce-Jones".

This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Probert
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Probert
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commodore
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2270s
)
Commodore
Probert
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
23rd century
.
In the mid-
2270s
, in a transmission intercepted by
Epsilon IX station
, this commodore had ordered a rendezvous for the
USS
Columbia
with the
USS
Revere
. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Commodore Probert's name was an inside reference to
Andrew Probert
, one of the illustrators who worked on the film and, later, the designer of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 181)
Andrew Probert himself recalled, "
The communications station "
Epsilon-9
" was briefly looked at but the design and building went to
John Dykstra
's
Apogee
group. I had a second brush with that station however, when
Trumbull
asked me to write some appropriate dialogue to run over scenes of the station as background chatter. This introduced the station without a big sign hanging on it. I wrote several levels of dialogue and even stuck my own name in a lower level, but when Trumbull saw it, he had that level moved to the top, and you can hear a message from Commodore Probert if you listen closely.
" (
Starlog photo guidebook Special Effects
, vol. 5, p. 96)
External link
Probert
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

notified the

of the doctor's demise. Spock then informed McCoy that he had just inherited Puri's duties as CMO, to which the doctor replied,

======================================================================
PAGE: Pusan Korea
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pusan_Korea
======================================================================

The assay office list
Pusan Korea
was an
individual
who lived in the late
24th century
.
In
2373
, Korea stored his valuables in the
assay office
on
Deep Space 9
. When
Arissa
accessed the office's
computer
, she pulled up a list of
names
, upon which Korea's name appeared. (
DS9
: "
A Simple Investigation
",
display graphic
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.
For followers of Hinduism, Pusan was a male name and it meant "sage".
As well, this name might be a reference to the city in
South Korea
known as
Busan
. Pusan was the Romanized form of this name.

======================================================================
PAGE: Qatai
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Qatai
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Pilot
Local
monster expert
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
W. Morgan Sheppard
"
And who might you be, the local
monster expert
?
"
"
As a matter of fact I am.
"
–
The Doctor
and
Qatai
,
2375
("
Bliss
")
Qatai
was a
pilot
of a
run-down ship
who was
obsessed
with killing a ship-devouring
protoplasmic beast
, which lured in ships to consume by generating
telepathic
illusions
to make them believe the creature was whatever the crew most wanted to see.
Qatai had been hunting the beast for
thirty-nine years
since it consumed
a ship
carrying his family, and had become mostly immune to the beast's
telepathy
, though he could not capture or destroy it. He had been swallowed by the creature when his latest attempt to kill it resulted in it tricking him with one of its illusions. When the
USS
Voyager
began to approach the creature, he was able to transmit a weak message to warn them away, but the crew of
Voyager,
believing that they were entering a
wormhole
that would take them
home
, ignored his warnings as random fluctuations.
After
Voyager
was swallowed by the beast, Qatai
beamed
over to
Voyager
. Only
Seven of Nine
and
Naomi Wildman
were conscious, as neither of them had been especially bothered about getting back to Earth (
The Doctor
was also available, but he had been deactivated prior to entering the "wormhole", and, as an
artificial lifeform
, was immune anyway). Qatai explained the situation to them, and the four were able to come up with a means of tricking the beast into expelling them by making
Voyager
indigestible.
After
Voyager
had left, Qatai retired to make repairs on his ship before resuming his quest, asking his new friends to get their crew home shortly before he vanished from their lives.
He was last seen finishing repairs on his engines and engaging the creature for yet another attempt at its destruction. (
VOY
: "
Bliss
")
Personality
Although he came across as a simple hunter, Qatai apparently had some degree of technical skill, saying that he once attempted to help someone subjected to the creature's influence throw off the illusion using a method similar to an attempt to lower the subject's
neurotransmitter
signals with a
cortical inhibitor
, only to be met with failure. While focused on his self-imposed mission to destroy the creature, he was not without compassion, treating Naomi Wildman with simple affection and expressing amusement at her description of the creature as a "pitcher plant" based on her biology classes, as well as exchanging jocular comments with The Doctor.
Qatai was played by
W. Morgan Sheppard
.

PAGE: Quentin Swofford

Affiliation:

,

a wife

was a

officer
and a friend of

. Sisko had introduced Swofford to his
wife
.

2374

, was destroyed while on patrol near the
Cardassian border
, likely by the
Jem'Hadar
. After
Joseph Sisko
heard of Swofford's loss and that Benjamin Sisko was considering resigning from Starfleet, Joseph commented that Swofford would have something to say about Sisko's decision. (
DS9
: "
Far Beyond the Stars
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Admiral
Leyton
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned as an
ombudsman
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49352. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Rai
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rai
======================================================================

Rai
was an individual from
Lowani
who was referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
. This individual's name was found in
metaphorical
phrases.
In
2368
,
Captain
Dathon
opened communications with the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
with this greeting, "
Rai and
Jiri
at
Lungha
. Rai of Lowani. Lowani under two moons. Jiri of Ubaya. Ubaya of crossed roads. At Lungha. Lungha. Her sky grey.
" The captain expected a greeting response in return from the
Enterprise
-D, which did not happen as the crew were clueless about the Tamarian language and society. It became evident to the Tamarians that there was a failure in communication. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Rai was pronounced as "RIE".

ancestor
on

Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
offspring
of
Kendis Sisko
and
spouse
to
Joseph Sisko
's
mother
Nichelle
.
In the
32nd century
, Raimy's extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Rajiin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rajiin
======================================================================

Species:
Oran'taku
Occupation:
Recruited by the
Xindi-Reptilians
to gather information on the
Humans
Status:
Active
(
2153
)
Played by
:
Nikita Ager
For the
ENT
episode of the same name, please see "
Rajiin
".
Rajiin
was a
female
Oran'taku
, native to the
planet
Oran'taku
. She spoke several
languages
.
Biography
Rajiin claimed that, when she was
very young
, she was
kidnapped
from her
homeworld
and sold into
slavery
, adding that "
There's a reason I was taken from my home. I have certain gifts.
" She also claimed that she didn't remember her
homeworld
or her
family
, but had had many
owners
in her lifetime. The last ship she lived on, she never left the
cargo hold
, "
except for a
few visits
to the
Captain's quarters
.
"
Rajiin evokes Archer's compassion
In
2153
, she was recruited by the
Xindi-Reptilians
to gather information on the
Humans
. She was placed in
Zjod
's sex slave
shop
in a
floating bazaar
on the
Xanthan planet
, where she conducted a staged escape.
Captain
Jonathan Archer
, who was on the planet to obtain
trellium-D
, saved her and took her aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
. When
T'Pol
discovered the location of her homeworld, Rajiin seemed apprehensive about returning, stating that "
I don't remember much about it, and I doubt anyone there would remember me.
"
Aboard
Enterprise
, Rajiin used her
telepathic
powers to seduce members of the crew into a trance-like state, including Captain Archer, and presumably
Hoshi Sato
. During these encounters, she would illuminate the
anatomy
inside the other person's body by placing her hands near or on parts of the body she wanted to highlight, in order to acquire
biometric data
on them. After further attacking
T'Pol
, who resisted and suffered disruptions to her
neocortex
, Rajiin contacted her Reptilian handlers,
stole
a
phase-pistol
, but was eventually thrown in the
brig
.
When Archer noted the convenience of the timing of Rajiin's arrival and escape, he figured she was a plant of some sort. Although she apologized that she could not give him the information he requested of her, for fear of life, she did warn him that they were in danger. During this interrogation, two
Xindi-Reptilian warships
approached
Enterprise
, knocking the
Earth ship
out of
warp
before attempting to dock. During the fray, Rajiin finally explained to Archer that the Reptilians wanted information to build a
bioweapon
, but they required data about
Humans
before they could begin. After successfully boarding
Enterprise
and securing her release, they departed without further issues.
Afterward, she reported to the
Xindi Council
, who viewed the data she had acquired for use in developing a
bio-weapon
. She showed the
councilors
the
scans
she had taken by placing her hands atop the
table
in the
Xindi Council chamber
, which displayed the scans
holographically
above the table. Although the Reptilians were satisfied, Rajiin warned there was more to Humans than they knew, but
Commander
Dolim
ordered her out of the Council chamber. (
ENT
: "
Rajiin
")
Later, when Archer and T'Pol
time travelled
to
2004
Detroit
and discovered the Xindi-Reptilian
bio-reactor
, Archer recalled how Rajiin had warned them about a bio-weapon, before presuming what they had just found might be it. (
ENT
: "
Carpenter Street
")
Appendices
Background information
Rajiin was portrayed by actress
Nikita Ager
. In some footage from the episode, the character was played by Ager's
stunt double
for the role,
Stacey Elizabeth Carino
.
Scripted depiction
In the final draft script of "Rajiin", the character of Rajiin was initially described as "a striking, humanoid female". The teleplay later stated, "
There
is
something undeniably charming about Rajiin [....] She's very aware, and unashamed, of who she is.
" A scene description concerning Rajiin and Archer remarked, "
There is something about her that he can't resist.
"
During the course of the episode "Rajiin", the character is shown in multiple different outfits and with slightly different hairstyles. A scene description in the script described the second look which Rajiin is shown with by stating, "
[She] has cleaned up very nicely in fresh clothes.
" The third look Rajiin is portrayed in was described in the script as "a seductive outfit – her sleeping clothes" and a "stunning appearance."
Most of Rajiin's back-story is questionable; she may have been making at least part of it up to earn sympathy from Archer. The script provided an insight into her backstory by suggesting that, prior to her time aboard
Enterprise
, she might not have smiled in a long time. Regarding her powers, the script revealed, "
Part of Rajiin's seductive power is the ability to block any memory of what she's done.
" Concerning her agility, the teleplay commented, "
She's a highly-trained operative with extraordinary physical skill.
" In ultimately unused dialogue from the script, T'Pol clarified that Rajiin didn't read thoughts, despite using invasive telepathy, and that it was a more "physically invasive" form of telepathy than a
mind meld
.
Portraying the character
To adopt the role of Rajiin, Nikita Ager had to depict both the character's sensuality toward Captain Archer and members of his crew as well as Rajiin's duplicity as a spy for the Xindi-Reptilians. "
I liked that the character, at least from what I gathered from it, had been on her own for so long that she had nerve,
" Ager recalled. "
I was a little nervous about the sensuality, but I had to trust that I was in good hands there. I had to go with their knowing what they were doing.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 151
, p. 46)
Nikita Ager was completely surprised but also very glad that, judging by the responses from
fans
of
Star Trek: Enterprise
, Rajiin turned out to be a character which energized the show's fan base by proving controversial. "
From the fan mail I'm getting – and oh my goodness, you would not believe the fan mail that the show has sparked – I didn't know that they hadn't had an alien seductress like her before on
Enterprise
,
" Ager remarked, with a laugh. "
Half the mail is, 'Wow!' People were so excited about a seductress who is an 'equal opportunity' seductress for the men and the women. And then I get letters from people saying, 'I can't believe
Star Trek
has gone there.'
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 151
, p. 46)
In addition to being pleased with the episode "Rajiin", Nikita Ager was hopeful that she might return as Rajiin. "
They left it so maybe Rajiin can come back,
" she observed. "
I'm not sure that the captain and crew can embrace Rajiin after she wreaked the havoc she did. Maybe she's seen the light, and the
Enterprise
can embrace her. I don't know, but in any direction, I am excited and hopeful that Rajiin can come back.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 151
, p. 46)
External link

PAGE: Ralidia
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ralidia
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Quark's
(employee)
Guild of Restaurant and Casino Employees
Occupation:
Dabo girl
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
Played by
:
Unknown actress
Ralidia
was a
Dabo girl
at
Quark's
in
2372
.
She was among the employees of Quark's who went on
strike
to protest a pay cut. Alongside
Leeta
,
Rom
,
Etheria
,
Broik
, and several others that formed the
Guild of Restaurant and Casino Employees
, she tried to pay customers not to visit Quark's any longer. She was later among the group of
union
members that were present when
Liquidator
Brunt
from the
Ferengi Commerce Authority
attempted to shut down their strike with threats and showed visible disgust with
Frool
for his immediate willingness to turn on the other strikers. Along with the remaining members (bar Frool), Ralidia was rallied to continue their strike by Rom, answering positively when he asked them if they were with him, then resuming her previous efforts to support the strike. Ultimately, Rom agreed to dissolve the Guild if
Quark
acquiesced with their demands. (
DS9
: "
Bar Association
")
When
Quark
was questioned by
Ch'Pok
, a
Klingon
prosecutor
during the trial of
Worf
on
Deep Space 9
, about what the
Ferengi
overheard about the attack conducted by Worf on a Klingon ship, Quark said that it was when
Dr.
Julian Bashir
was talking to Ralidia. (
DS9
: "
Rules of Engagement
")
Ralidia was played by an
unknown actress
.

,

: "

",
directory
)

2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the

. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
In

, as a

duty roster
. (

: "

")

electrician

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ramirez (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ramirez_(24th_century)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commanding officer
,
USS
Valiant
Status:
Deceased
(
2373
)
Died:
2373
,
USS
Valiant
Captain
Ramirez
was the
commanding officer
of the
starship
USS
Valiant
.
In the early
2370s
, he took several members of
Red Squad
on an three-month
training cruise
mission to circumnavigate the entire
Federation
.  The plan was for the cadets to run the ship while the
officers
aboard observed and critiqued their performance.
In
2373
, Ramirez met
Lieutenant Commanders
Jadzia Dax
and
Worf
on
Starbase 63
. After Jadzia boasted about her
Tongo
skills, Ramirez managed to persuade her into playing a no-limit game. Ramirez, however, was a three-time Tongo champion, so Dax lost two
bars
of
latinum
to him. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
In late 2373,
Starfleet
sent orders to Ramirez to gain information on a new
Dominion battleship
, that was believed to be near the
Valiant
's position.
The
Valiant
was transiting the
Kepla sector
when the
Dominion War
began. Consequently, Ramirez ordered the
Valiant
back to
Federation space
. On their way home, the
Valiant
encountered a
Cardassian battle cruiser
near
El-Gatark
. The two ships attacked each other, both vessels losing main power in the ensuing battle. Ramirez himself was critically wounded during the attack with a
punctured
lung
and massive internal injuries, but he refused to go to
sickbay
. Instead, he stayed on the
bridge
to direct the entire damage control effort and was able to get the
Valiant
's systems back online in three hours and destroy the
Cardassian
ship.
Just before he died the next day, Ramirez gave
Cadet
Tim Watters
a
battlefield commission
and ordered him to take command of the
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
authors
of the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 203 believed that there were two Captain Ramirezes, with a main entry for each.
External links
Ramirez (Captain)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ramsey (Crewman)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ramsey_(Crewman)
======================================================================

Ramsey
was a
Starfleet
crewman
aboard
Deep Space 9
who served
under
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
in the
2370s
.
In
2372
, Ramsey was aboard the
USS
Defiant
during its battle with the
USS
Lakota
. Both Ramsey and
Bartlett
were
killed
in the unfortunate attack. Seven others were injured. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================

Doctor
Ramsey
was an
archaeologist
who, in
2365
, was in charge of an
archaeological dig
on the
planet
Denius III
.
That year, the
USS
Yamato
visited the planet and Ramsey allowed
Captain
Donald Varley
,
commanding officer
of the
Yamato
, to carry an
artifact
back with him to his
starship
. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Randall Pflugg
was a male individual assigned to
Deep Space 9
.
By
September
2373
, he was a
junior engineering chief
. On one day in that month, he was scheduled to begin his shift at midnight. He was mentioned in the "DS-9 Routines and Daily Roster". (
DS9
: "
The Assignment
")
He was named after
stand-in
and occasional background actor,
Randy Pflug
.

Admiral
Raner
was a
24th century
Starfleet
flag officer
.
In
2370
, she was serving as the
Chief
of
Starfleet Security
.
Raner was responsible for ordering Admiral
Erik Pressman
to recover the
USS
Pegasus
and further experimentation on the
prototype
for a
Federation
phasing cloaking device
, which had been lost in
2358
. Due to the sensitive political nature of the experiment, she ordered Pressman to keep the effort a secret, and gave written orders to
Commander
William T. Riker
to assist him without informing his
captain
,
Jean-Luc Picard
. (
TNG
: "
The Pegasus
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Depending on the circumstances or outcome of her involvement, she may or may not be the same
female Chief of Starfleet Security
who was later mentioned in
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
and
Star Trek: Voyager
.

Rapunki
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrase, "
Rapunki, when he joined
the Seven
", which was a greeting indicating honor upon joining a new group.
Lt.
Kayshon
said this upon first arriving on the
bridge
of the
USS
Cerritos
. Immediately afterwards, he apologized for the failure of the
universal translator
, and provided the translation himself: "
It is my honor, captain.
" (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Ravis
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ravis
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Status:
Active
(
2152
)
Played by
:
Rudolf Martin
Ravis
was an
alien
who
Hoshi Sato
met on
Risa
in
2152
.
She became intent on learning
his language
, which was very difficult to master. He attempted to teach his language to her, without much success. Ravis then invited Sato to join him in the exotic
steampools
of Risa, which she did. In the end, they wound up spending the night
together
.
Hoshi and Ravis spend the night together on Risa
In the morning after, Ravis said he hoped that Sato doesn't feel that he took advantage of her, but she assured him "not for a
minute
" did she feel this way, and that although Risa has a lot to offer, she could "spend the entire
day
" right there with him. (
ENT
: "
Two Days and Two Nights
")
In the final draft script of "Two Days and Two Nights", Ravis was initially referred to as "a handsome young alien man," a note pointed out, "
He's not Risan,
" and a subsequent description stated, "
He's very charming and pleasant.
"
Ravis was played by actor
Rudolf Martin
.

PAGE: Rawlins

Rawlins
was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
. They served in the
sciences division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
under
Captain
James T. Kirk
, during Kirk's
historic
five-year mission
. In
2268
, Rawlins was assigned as the
ship's
chief geologist
.
That year, Rawlins was recommended to be among the
landing party
by Captain Kirk to
planet
Alpha Carinae II
. However, the
M-5 multitronic unit
recommended
Ensign
Carstairs
over Rawlins as the former had visited the planet on a
geology
survey for a
mining
company while serving in the
Merchant Marine
. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 207 spelled this character's name as Rawlens.
The novel
A Choice of Catastrophes
gives his first name as "Niall". In the episode's novelization, the mentioned geologist was named Rawls.

======================================================================
PAGE: Redjac
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Redjac
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Cloud creature
Status:
Beamed
into
deep space
(
2267
)
Played by
:
John Fiedler
(voice)
For the Klingon warrior, please see
Rejac
, or for the objet d'art, please see
Rejac Crystal
.
"
It has a name! Beratis, Kesla... Redjac!
"
–
Sybo
,
2267
("
Wolf in the Fold
")
Redjac
(also known as
Red Jack
,
Jack the Ripper
,
Beratis
, and
Kesla
) was a
non-humanoid
lifeform
that existed for centuries by journeying from
planet
to planet and feeding on the
pain and fear
he caused by
committing
mass murders
. (
TOS
: "
Wolf in the Fold
")
Quick Answers
How does Redjac relate to the Jack the Ripper legend in Star Trek?
In Star Trek, Redjac is connected to the Jack the Ripper legend in the episode "Wolf in the Fold." The USS Enterprise computer equates "Jack the Ripper" with Redjac. The entity takes over the Enterprise-D's holodeck, creating a Victorian London program, the time of Jack the Ripper. Redjac feeds on fear, leading to chaos. Captain Picard and his crew confront it by becoming classic Terran monsters. This link to Jack the Ripper is key to the plot.
Provided by: Fandom
What are Redjac's other known aliases in Star Trek?
In Star Trek, Redjac is known by aliases such as Red Jack, Jack the Ripper, Beratis, and Kesla. This entity has survived for centuries by feeding on fear and pain.
Provided by: Fandom
What did Mr. Scott describe Redjac's presence as feeling like?
Mr. Scott described Redjac's presence as cold, like a stinking draft.
Provided by: Fandom
How does Redjac's form differ from typical Star Trek villains?
Redjac, unlike typical Star Trek villains, is a non-humanoid entity known as 'star trek jack the ripper.' It lacks a permanent form, often appearing as a cloud-like being. Redjac feeds on fear and pain, surviving by inciting chaos, such as a nuclear war on a Federation planet. It can possess technology, as demonstrated when it took control of the USS Enterprise-D's holodeck. Redjac can manifest a skeletal figure with glowing eyes, differing from villains with consistent physical forms.
Provided by: Fandom
Physiology
The Redjac-possessed
Hengist
takes Yeoman
Tankris
hostage, threatening to kill her with a knife.
As a
being without form
, which was likened to a
cloud creature
, its physical appearance unknown, but it was described by Mr. Scott after a brief encounter in the dark as, "
cold... like a stinking draft out of a
slaughterhouse
, but it wasn't really there.
"
Spock
believed that
female
victims were chosen more often than male ones because "
women are more easily and more deeply terrified, generating more sheer horror than the
male
of the species.
"
It was similar to a
parasitic
organism, and required the
possession
of a
humanoid
host to commit crimes. It also had the ability to possess objects, like the
USS
Enterprise
. (
TOS
: "
Wolf in the Fold
")
Redjac was voiced by
John Fiedler
(
Hengist
), but was uncredited on-screen despite having dialogue.
History
Prefect
Jaris
possessed by Redjac attempting to kill
James T. Kirk
Crimes committed under various pseudonyms:
On
Earth
and
Mars
:
19th century
:
several women
as Jack the Ripper
1932
:
Seven women
in
Shanghai
,
China
1974
:
Five women
in the
city
of
Kiev
,
USSR
2105
:
Eight women
in the
Martian
colonies
Outside Sol system:
2156
:
Ten women
in
Heliopolis
on
Alpha Eridani II
Interim:
several women
(as "Kesla") on
Deneb II
.
2266
:
several women
(as "Beratis") on
Rigel IV
2267
:
several women
(in the form of
Hengist
) on
Argelius II
After Redjac committed a murder on the
pleasure planet
Argelius II
in 2267 as "Hengist",
Lieutenant Commander
Montgomery Scott
from the
Federation
starship
Enterprise
was accused of the
crime
. However, Hengist's true identity was later revealed. In the process, Redjac briefly possessed
Prefect
Jaris
, who was quickly liberated by a nerve pinch from
Spock
. Subsequently, the Redjac entity was
beamed
, on a dispersal pattern, into
deep space
by Spock, where it was hoped that it would die from lack of nourishment. (
TOS
: "
Wolf in the Fold
")
Appendices
Apocrypha
In
James Blish
's
novelization
of the original script, Redjac manifested itself on the
viewscreen
in the form of a
Dantesque
scene, with fire and brimstone, naked women riding on the backs of satyrs, horned
toads
, etc.
Kirk
declared that it was a vision of
Hell
, whereby the "filthy thing" was showing the
Enterprise
crew where it was born.
In a
comic book
story released by
WildStorm Comics
titled "
Embrace the Wolf
", it was revealed that Redjac survived by latching on to a nearby starship. After an absence of over a century, Redjac returned to confront the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, where, having provoked a
nuclear war
on a
Federation
planet, he transferred himself into the
holodeck
and abducted several crewmembers, challenging
Data
in his
Sherlock Holmes
persona to save them, while Redjac manifested a body for himself in the form of tall, gray-skinned alien with a tentacle for his left hand. Data was able to save
Counselor
Troi
from Redjac, and was also able to contact
Captain
Picard
,
Dr.
Crusher
, and
Lt.
Worf
. Tricking Redjac into a confrontation with Worf, the crew was able to have Redjac focus all his
energy
on one spot, before trapping him in a
photon torpedo
. The torpedo was subsequently abandoned on a nearby barren
moon
.
Redjac also returned in two issues of the
first
Star Trek
series
from
DC Comics
, in a two-part comic with the titles "
Wolf on the Prowl
" and "
Wolf at the Door
". In this story, the entity possessed
Enterprise
crewperson Nancy Bryce and forced her to kill a number of ship personnel. Redjac had been revealed to have spent the twenty years between the TV episode and the comics on a primitive planet where it set itself up as their "God" and presented an image of Kirk in an opposite role as a "Devil" in the context to this "God". Redjac was defeated by following the Excelsior into a wormhole, which was then collapsed as the ship exited, trapping Redjac inside.
External links
Redjac
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Jack the Ripper
at
Wikipedia

Revalus' pen
Revalus
was an
author
who wrote
The Wait
.
Jake Sisko
knew of him.
A believer in
visceral writing
, Revalus often said that
"a writer should put
pen
to
paper
as if he were a
painter
putting
brush
to
canvas
"
. He was one of several talented people to have met
Onaya
prior to Jake's
encounter
with her. (
DS9
: "
The Muse
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

Rew P'Cott Claudia-Jean
was a
Starfleet
cadet
in the early
25th century
.
In
2401
, they were assigned to the
USS
Titan
-A
. (
PIC
: "

")
Prop featuring name
This character was only mentioned in writing.

containing cadets and their assignments used by a Starfleet Academy officer, with the prop appearing in the

episode "

".

.

Dr.
Rich Thorne
was a
physician
in the
24th century
. He had been awarded an
MD
and a
FASFS
.
In
2368
, he worked at the
Taylor Hospital
(
Gray Municipality
) on
Melina II
. On
stardate
44041.7, he was the attending physician for patient
G. Hutzel
. He diagnosed his patient with
Iresine Syndrome
. This information was archived in the
Melina II Planetary Medical Database
. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
okudagram
)
Thorne was named after
Digital Magic
creative director
Rich Thorne
who worked on
Star Trek: The Next Generation
,
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
, and
Star Trek: Voyager
.

. (

Richard James
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was
Eminiar VII
.
In
2369
, he was a
crew
member aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In
2374
, as a
lieutenant commander
, he was listed on a
crew manifest
. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named for production designer
Richard James
.

, the wiki for licensed

======================================================================
PAGE: Rick
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rick
======================================================================

Rick in the text of the Treaty of Armen
Rick
was a person involved in drafting the
Treaty of Armens
.
He was responsible for including
Kei
and
Yuri
in the treaty. (
TNG
: "
The Ensigns of Command
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Rick seems to refer to
Rick Sternbach
. The text also claimed that many involved in drafting the treaty ended up in
hospital
.

)

,
Skywalker Division

.
(

dedication plaque

======================================================================
PAGE: Riska
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Riska
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Other relative(s):
Riska's uncle
Played by
:
Eva Loseth
Riska
was a teenaged female resident of
Deep Space 9
during the
2370s
. She had an
uncle
who ran a
bar
on
Osinar VI
.
In
2371
, Riska joined
Jake Sisko
and her friend
Leanne
, who was dating Jake, on a double
date
with
Nog
. The date did not go well, beginning with Nog insulting Riska by telling her she talked too much, only to inform her that she should not speak unless spoken to.
The date ended when Nog asked Riska to cut up his
food
for him (Nog thought that he was being sensitive by not asking the young lady to chew his food for him). Riska and her friend Leanne walked out on Jake and Nog. (
DS9
: "
Life Support
")
Riska was played by actress
Eva Loseth
.
The
10 November
1994
final draft of the episode's script described Riska as a "Human girl", rather than the alien she appeared as.

Rob Bowman
was a
23rd century
doctor
. In
2294
and '
95
, he was a research associate for the primary investigator, Dr.
Susan Nuress
, on the northern
continent
of
Oby VI
. As a member of the medical research team, Dr. Bowman assisted in the development of a cure for the
plasma plague
on the
planet
. His name was listed on an
etiological
report. (
TNG
: "
The Child
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

Rob Bowman
.

was a
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the mid-
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was
Rigel VI
.
In
2328
, R. Blackman was a passenger aboard the
civilian
transport
SS
Kogin
, which ferried him from
Sherman's Planet Spaceport
to
Starbase 11
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
Blackman served as a
lieutenant
in
2369
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after costume designer
Robert Blackman
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Robert L. Comsol
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Robert_L._Comsol
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Flag officer
,
Starfleet Command
Status:
Active
(
2267
)
Robert L. Comsol
was a male
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
23rd century
. He was a
commanding officer
at
Starfleet Command
.
Following the
2254
visit of the
USS
Enterprise
to the
planet
Talos IV
, under
Captain
Christopher Pike
, he was the primary officer involved in issuing
Starfleet
General Order 7
, as evidenced by his
signing
off on the document.
Comsol later sent an message to the
Enterprise
in
2267
that was relayed by
Lieutenant
Uhura
, and was read as follows:
"
Commodore
Mendez
. Urgent.
Subspace monitors
show
Enterprise
receiving transmissions from Planet Talos IV, in violation of
Starfleet General Orders
. Captain
Kirk
is hereby relieved. You are ordered to assume command of the
Enterprise
. Disable vessel if necessary to prevent further contact." (Signed Comsol, Starfleet Command)
Spock
argued with Mendez that Comsol's order to relieve Kirk, despite Kirk knowing nothing of the Talos connect, Mendez reminded Spock that as long as Kirk commanded the
Enterprise
, he was responsible for everything that happens aboard his ship. (
TOS
: "
The Menagerie, Part I
")
Comsol's name is the sole signature on the copy of General Order 7, read by Captain Kirk.
The
Star Trek Concordance
(
page number?
•
edit
)
assumed that "Comsol" meant "Command, Solar Forces", apparently not aware that the aforementioned General Order was signed by the same individual.
External link
Robert Comsol
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Robert Legato (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Robert_Legato_(Starfleet)

Affiliation:

Rank:

,

(

)
Robert "Rob" Legato
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
As
vice admiral
, on
stardate
22519.5, he was head of the
Mission Ops
group at
40 Eridani A Starfleet Construction Yards
during the construction of the
USS
Brattain
. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
",
dedication plaque
)
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory for
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was located in room #35882. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
Legato, was a
captain
in
2363
, when he was listed as a member of the
Warp Technologies Development Group
on the 2368 version of the
dedication plaque
for the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
",
dedication plaque
)
By
2364
, Legato became the
commanding officer
of the
USS
Matte Fringe
. After
stardate
41547.8, he received an order about
security clearances
to
classified material
for a project from
Admiral
Daniel Curry
of  the
Starfleet Mission Operations Division
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
In early
2367
, on stardate 44034.3, he was treated in the
Wiemer Medical Facility
on
Melina II
for the
Iresine Syndrome
. It was later discovered that in fact he had been subjected to a
telepathic memory invasion
by the
Ullian
Jev
. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
Star Trek: The Next Generation
director and visual effects coordinator
Robert Legato
.

PAGE: Rogers

For additional meanings of "Rogers", please see
Rogers
.
Rogers
was a
crewman
on the
USS
Voyager
during the
ship
's journey across the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2372
, Rogers was one of a number of
officers
who participated in the
Paris Radiogenic Sweepstakes
, which
Tom Paris
ran out of the
Chez Sandrine
's
holoprogram
.
Replicator
rations
were paid to play and whoever predicted what the
radiogenic particle
count would be at 1200 hours the following day would win the
rations
of everyone who played, minus a small
handling fee
for the
bank
(in other words, Paris). Rogers' name was called out when the numbers were generated. (
VOY
: "
Meld
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Based on a number of unidentified crewmembers present during the sweepstakes, Rogers may have been
him
,
him
, or
her
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Rondus
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rondus
======================================================================

Species:
Orb
Status:
Active
(
2382
)
Played by
:
Fred Tatasciore
Rondus
was a member of the
Orbs
, a
photonic
species from the
Veraflex Nebula
.
Biography
In 2382, Rondus visited the
USS
Cerritos
during the peace talks between the Orbs and the
Cubes
. Rondus was spotted by
Beckett Mariner
and
Ensign
Olly
exiting the ship's locker room while it was not glowing, a state comparable to being nude for Orbs. The two found anti-Cube propaganda on Rondus'
PADD
when they searched the lockers.
Quadralon
, the offspring of the lead Cube was thought to have gone missing during the peace talks, and Olly and Mariner suspected Rondus of foul play. Under interrogation, Rondus, bribed with the potential energy from some a
Starfleet tricorder
's
power cells
, claimed to feel blessed to be a part of the historic peace process, despite finding Cubes weird and stupid-looking. Rondus denied its involvement and supported an end to the hostilities, planning to destroy the propaganda in a display of solidarity with the Cubes.
Rondus fled its interrogation and informed
Spheronius
and the other Orbs that they were being accused of
murder
, specifically by Olly. This information significantly escalated tensions between the two groups into open conflict. Rondus joined the other Orbs in the ensuing "shape war" throughout the
Cerritos
, and subsequently forming the multi-Orb construct
Orbiculus
before the situation was diffused. (
LD
: "
Of Gods and Angles
")
Rondus was voiced by
Fred Tatasciore
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ronin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ronin
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
(
anaphasic lifeform
)
Status:
Deceased
(
2370
)
Born:
Before
1647
Died:
2370,
Caldos
Played by
:
Duncan Regehr
Ronin
was an
anaphasic lifeform
, which needed a
corporeal lifeform
to take physical form. He was bonded with
females
of the Howard family until
2370
, when he was destroyed by
Beverly Crusher
. Ronin used a
candle
in order to maintain molecular cohesion. In corporeal form, he took the appearance of a male
Human
who was approximately thirty-four
years
of age. He was able to maintain a strong grip on his hosts, and the result of their bonding was causing them to react and behave unusually, and their
eye
color
to change to
green
; something that was mistaken for a
genetic trait
of the Howard women.
History
He was known to have existed since at least
1647
, when he took up residence with
Jessel Howard
shortly after appearing in the
city
of
Glasgow
on
Earth
. He realized that Jessel Howard had a compatible biochemistry and lived with her until she died, and then took up residence with her daughter. This continued with every subsequent Howard
daughter
for twenty generations. Ronin moved with the Howard family from
Scotland
to
North America
and eventually out to
space
. By the mid
24th century
, he was living with
Felisa Howard
at the
Caldos colony
. Ronin did not reveal his corporeal form to anyone other than his host, leading many (such as
Ned Quint
) to believe he was a genuine
ghost
. Felisa was made very happy with him and wrote detailed
descriptions
of her experiences with Ronin in her journals. Felisa never mentioned Ronin to Beverly or anyone else, however, in order to keep his secret.
When Felisa
died
in 2370, Ronin moved on to her
granddaughter
, Beverly Crusher. He appeared to her at Felisa's
funeral
, throwing a
camellia
on Felisa's
grave
, and later filled her grave and house with camellias. Ronin seduced Beverly, and his hold on her was quite powerful. While he was in the process of merging with Beverly, he maintained cohesion with help from the colony's
weather control system
, causing systems disruptions and killing Quint, who was attempting to stop Ronin.
After Beverly resigned from
Starfleet
in order to move to
Caldos
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
became suspicious. He wished to meet Ronin, who no one else seemed to have noticed. Ronin showed himself, hoping to drive Picard away. The captain then began bombarding him with questions as to how he came to Caldos, and subsequently received a communication from
Data
and
Geordi La Forge
that they had detected
anaphasic energy
on Felisa's body. Ronin threatened to go to the
governor
to stop them from exhuming the body, but Picard pointed out that the governor would have the same questions that he did. Ronin then attacked the captain, went to Felisa's grave, reanimated her body and incapacitated the two
Starfleet
officers
. This was the
breaking point
for Beverly, and she realized Ronin was a malevolent leech who had used her entire family for generations. She destroyed the candle with La Forge's
phaser
and had
Commander
Will Riker
close off all of the
plasma conduits
in the colony's
weather control systems
in order to cut off Ronin's escape route. When Ronin tried to forcefully merge with her, Crusher destroyed him with the phaser. (
TNG
: "
Sub Rosa
")
Ronin was played by actor
Duncan Regehr
, who also played
Shakaar Edon
in several episodes of
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ronnie Knox (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ronnie_Knox_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Ronnie Knox
was a
Starfleet
officer
.
In
2372
, he was named as a
Research and Development
officer on the
dedication plaque
on the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was named as a
Fleet Ops
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
. (
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after chief lighting technician
R.D. Knox
.
Retroactive continuity
: In
2328
, Knox boarded the
transport ship
SS
Manila
at
Coopersworld
bound to
Johnson Station
. This information of the
passenger manifest
was recorded in the
commercial transport database
and reviewed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Rork
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rork
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Species:
Human
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Occupation:
Academic liaison
,
USS
Athena
Status:
Active
(
3195
)
Played by
:
Tricia Black
Lieutenant
Rork
was a
Human
who served as the
academic liaison
aboard the
USS
Athena
during the late
32nd century
. Her duty was to assess the educational value of the ship's activities and potentially incorporate them into the
Starfleet Academy
curriculum.
In
3195
, Rork was on the
bridge
for the
Athena
's
maiden voyage
to
Earth
. En route, the ship detected an unusual
spatial anomaly
near the
Badlands
, which she thought would make for an excellent
practical study module
for the
spatial harmonics
lab
. However, the anomaly turned out to be a
trap
set by
Nus Braka
of the
Venari Ral
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
Rork was played by
Tricia Black
.

was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2367
, Ross was assigned to the
Engineering
Department of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. During a routine
power transfer conduit
service when the
starship
was performing scheduled
impulse
maneuvers, they examined the condition of the conduit linings. He reported that
plasma
erosion was within expected parameters, remaining well within safety limits. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Rossi April
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rossi_April
======================================================================

April's info (10th name from the top)
Rossi April
was an individual who lived during the late
24th century
.
After
Odo
called in a few favors from his sources on
Cardassia
of individuals with "
the
computer skills
, the opportunity and the motive to carry out [recent] attacks
" against former
Shakaar resistance
members in
2373
, Rossi's name appeared on his list as one of twenty-five possible suspects. Her identity number, name, and location were listed on this list. She was located at
Posell VI
. (
DS9
: "
The Darkness and the Light
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Considering both the source and parties involved, this individual was likely a
Bajoran
or
Cardassian
.
This character was named after production associate
April Rossi
.
External link
Rossi April
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Rul the Obscure
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rul_the_Obscure
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Businessman
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Stan Petier
(uncredited)
Rul the Obscure
was a patron of
Quark's
on
Deep Space 9
.
In
2369
, he was one of a select clientele Quark allowed to participate in an
auction
in
Quark's
, whom Quark had described as "
all ridiculously wealthy and not too bright
". He was accompanied by
two
associates
.
Rul purchased a
jeweled
necklace
for 150
bars
of
latinum
that had been obtained from somewhere in the
Gamma Quadrant
by
Vash
. (
DS9
: "
Q-Less
")
Rul the Obscure was portrayed by
Stan Petier
, who received no credit for his appearance. Rul's gloves are different than the gloves of the other two members. His group might be "
Celestine
", which were mentioned in the script's list of nonspeaking characters.
An individual wearing a similar costume was a
Verex III slave
in
ENT
: "
Borderland
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Rumpelstiltskin (alien)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rumpelstiltskin_(alien)
======================================================================

Species:
Unidentified non-corporeal species
Occupation:
Explorer
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Michael John Anderson
Rumpelstiltskin
was the persona adopted by a
non-corporeal species
who traveled from the
Gamma Quadrant
through the
Bajoran wormhole
and had taken a position in the
plasma fields
near the
Denorios belt
, where they read as elevated
thoron emissions
in
2369
.
That year, this Rumpelstiltskin persona was taken from the
imagination
of
Miles O'Brien
.
He, along with two other's in the imagined personas of
Buck Bokai
and
Jadzia Dax
later converged in the
replimat
to discuss their findings, only to be confused by the irony of their results.
Following the crew's realization that their own imaginations were responsible for the events that transpired on the station, the crew was finally able to return things back to normal. After returning to his
office
, Bokai revealed himself to
Benjamin Sisko
, explaining their actions, stating that they were "
on an
extended mission
exploring
the galaxy
. Awhile back, we followed one of your ships through that "hole in space" out there. We've been watching you, trying to figure out the rules of your game.
" (
DS9
: "
If Wishes Were Horses
")
This alien portrayal of Rumpelstiltskin was played by
Michael John Anderson
.

PAGE: Rupert Crandall
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Rupert_Crandall
======================================================================

Occupation:
Starship
captain
Status:
Alive
(
2399
)
Captain
Rupert Crandall
was a
male
individual who lived in
Infinity Lake
on
Nepenthe
during the late
24th century
. He claimed to have "
been everywhere from
Qo'noS
to
Tyken's Rift
", and owned a
starship
called the
Inside Straight
.
Kestra Troi-Riker
was friends with Crandall, though her
parents
–
William T. Riker
and
Deanna Troi
– were less impressed with him. In
2399
, Kestra suggested that he could help
Jean-Luc Picard
and
Soji Asha
. She contacted Crandall with the details of Soji's
homeworld
, which he was able to identify as a
planet
in the
Ghulion system
. (
PIC
: "
Nepenthe
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Ryan Adams (24th century)

.

was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him and his traveling companion
Wynsdey Adams
from
Triskelion Orbital Station
to
Talos IV
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)

He was named after
CBS Television Distribution
Multimedia Director
Ryan Adams
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ryan Adams (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ryan_Adams_(comedian)
======================================================================

For the 24th century individual, please see
Ryan Adams
.
Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's database
Ryan Adams
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to located a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Adams was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program code list for this comedian was
HB-34233
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named for
Ryan Adams
, Director of Multimedia at
CBS Television Distribution
(as of December 2012), and was not listed in the original version of "The Outrageous Okona".
For the remastered edition of this episode, Ryan Adams replaced
Maurice Hurley
.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Samuel Estragon
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Samuel_Estragon
======================================================================

Professor
Samuel Estragon
was a
scientist
that devoted half of his life to locating the
Tox Uthat
, a kind of
quantum phase inhibitor
invented by
Kal Dano
in the
27th century
. It was a palm-sized
crystal
capable of stopping
nuclear fusion
within a
star
.
From
2361
to
2366
, he employed a
personal assistant
,
Vash
, to help him as well as a
Ferengi
,
Sovak
, for less-than-
ethical
archaeological expeditions
.
Shortly before his
death
in 2366, Estragon uncovered new evidence of the artifact's location on
Risa
and encoded his research notes and maps on a
data disk
. After he died, Vash used his notes to locate the artifact, and used Sovak to finance her trip there. Sovak and two
Vorgon
felons from the
27th century
also tried to obtain the device.
Captain
Picard
, vacationing on Risa, became involved and eventually destroyed it to make sure no one could do harm with it. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the script, Samuel Estragon's last name was pronounced as "EST-ra-gone".
[1]
The name
Samuel Estragon
is probably a reference to
Waiting for Godot
, which features a character with the rare name of Estragon and was authored by
Samuel Beckett
.

======================================================================
PAGE: San
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/San
======================================================================

Mirror universe
(
coexisting on another dimensional plane
)
Species:
Terran
Affiliation:
Terran Empire
Status:
Deceased
(
24th century
)
Played by
:
James Hiroyuki Liao
James Huang
Jhaleil Swaby
San
was a
male
Terran
from the
mirror universe
born in the
23rd century
.
Quick Answers
What was San's ultimate plan for the United Federation of Planets?
San planned to invade the United Federation of Planets. His strategy included the destruction of Earth, leading to the annihilation of the Federation Council and Starfleet Command. This would leave the Federation defenseless against a subsequent Romulan Blitzkrieg-style attack. San's ultimate goal was to be recognized as the individual who annihilated the Federation, thus outshining Picard in historical records.
Provided by: Fandom
How did San meet his end in the 24th century?
San, recognized as Sarek in Star Trek, succumbed to an illness. In a dialogue with Captain Picard, he was incapacitated by his ailment and passed away soon after, aged 203. His demise was serene, and he managed to express his genuine affection and respect for his son, Spock, via a mind meld with Picard.
Provided by: Fandom
How did San's relationship with Section 31 evolve over time?
Initially, Section 31 tested San's loyalty by simulating her defection to the Dominion. Once convinced of her allegiance, Sloan offered San a role within the organization. However, San rejected the offer, disturbed by Section 31's lack of accountability and its frequent breaches of the Federation's foundational principles.
Provided by: Fandom
Biography
23rd century
This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
24th century Prime universe
During her time in the
24th century
, Georgiou learned that San was actually still alive and after the
Godsend
weapon, planning to use it to destabilize the
United Federation of Planets
and lead an invasion of the
prime universe
. Georgiou worked with
Section 31
to stop her old friend, but was unable to talk him down. During a fight between the two, San took a fatal hit to the side of the neck from his own sword when Georgiou managed to knock the blade away from herself, accidentally hitting San in the process. In San's final moments, the two reconciled before San died in a heartbroken Georgiou's arms. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
Legacy
Georgiou flashes back to her bloodied hand over San's body
In
3189
,
Philippa Georgiou
began having flashbacks to her earlier life in the
Terran Empire
, including standing over San's
bloody
body. (
DIS
: "
Scavengers
", "
The Sanctuary
")
Before being sent back in time by the
Guardian of Forever
, not knowing that San had survived his apparent death in the 23rd century, Georgiou told
Michael Burnham
that
"I wish I'd learned all this before now. I had a chance, once, long ago. He was called San. I wish I told you about him."
Burnham encouraged Georgiou to tell the people that she was about to meet instead. (
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
Appendices
Appearances
DIS
:
"
Scavengers
"
"
The Sanctuary
"
Star Trek: Section 31
Background information
San was played by
Jhaleil Swaby
in
Discovery
and by
James Hiroyuki Liao
and
James Huang
in
Section 31
.
Apocrypha
San first appeared in the
Gallery DIS
novel
Die Standing
. He later appeared in
IDW
's
Section 31
prequel comic
.
External link
San
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Sanchez (lieutenant)

read off Sanchez as being on duty on the bridge while
Michael Burnham
had to explain to the bridge crew their situation; though no changes to the
timeline
resulted.
(
DIS
: "
Face the Strange
")
This individual was only mentioned in dialogue and writing.

.

in

(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).

,

visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Saowin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Saowin
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Representative of his
species
Status:
Active
(
2375
)
Played by
:
Christopher Shea
Saowin
was a representative of a
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
that was assisted by the "
Think Tank
".
His world was plagued by
seismic
reactions until the "Think Tank" devised a planetary
containment field
to restore the planet's geostability in
2375
. In return, the "Think Tank" demanded a shipment of
bernicium
ore
. Since Saowin's people needed this ore to repair the
replicator
systems that fed their people, they hid the bernicium, claiming it had been buried by the last series of quakes, and tried to offer the "Think Tank" a
rubidium
geode
instead.
Saowin was persuaded to honor the original agreement when
Kurros
threatened to deactivate the planet's containment field. He was ushered back to
his shuttle
. (
VOY
: "
Think Tank
")
Saowin was played by actor
Christopher Shea
.
He filmed his scenes on Friday
15 January
1999
on
Paramount Stage 9
with a makeup call at 7:30 am and a set call at 10:30 am.

======================================================================
PAGE: Satine
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Satine
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Bryce Soderberg
(uncredited)
Satine
was an
alien
male
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
operations division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
.
He was among the
crewmembers
who were held captive by
Krall
on
Altamid
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Background information
Satine was played by actor
Bryce Soderberg
, who received no on-screen credit for his appearance.
The makeup for the character of Satine was based on the design of a
Venus fly trap
and was sculpted by
Matt Rose
.
[1]
[2]
Original test paint for Satine was designed by Special Makeup Effects Artist
Bronwyne Sloley
. Next, Special Makeup Effects Artist
Daemon Cadman
was given the task of slightly changing the makeup scheme, so there were two variations of it. In total, approximately twelve sets of Satine makeup were produced. Makeup Effects Artists
Charles Poirlier
and
Suzie Klimack
handled the application of the makeup sets, and they were all painted by Cadman.
[3]
The task of portraying Satine turned out to be a challenge. "
Bryce was completely covered in makeup and prosthetics. He even was covered with acrylic eyes that had to be removed [between takes] with magnets so he could see. Somehow, one day when we took out those acrylic eyes, a bee managed to fly into one of the tiny holes. I looked over at him, and suddenly his arms were flailing. Fortunately, he didn't get stung,
" laughed Special Makeup Effects Supervisor
Joel Harlow
. "
We got in there and the bee was trapped, but found its way out.
"
[4]
In the background of a
deleted scene
from
Star Trek Beyond
, Satine met
Deep C-Zar
in a busy terminal at
Starbase
Yorktown
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Satler
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Satler
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Mercenary
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
baryon sweep
(
2369
)
Died:
2369
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
Played by
:
Tim deZarn
Satler
was an individual from an
alien
species that was characterized by ridges on the nose and forehead.
In
2369
, he was part of a group of
mercenaries
that boarded the
USS
Enterprise
-D
during preparations for a
baryon sweep
in
2369
at
Arkaria Base
with the intent of
stealing
trilithium resin
.
Satler first assisted the team in the warp core, helping to prepare the trilithium for extraction. When Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
was brought to Main Engineering, after attempting to sabotage the team's plans, Satler was assigned to guard him. However, when Captain Picard used a plasma torch to trigger an emergency seal-off in Main Engineering, he used the distraction to knock Satler down and sabotage the module that the team had prepared to transport the resin in. Satler then chased Picard through several
Jefferies tubes
, but was misdirected by Picard into running into the sweep, killing him.  (
TNG
: "
Starship Mine
")
Satler was played by
Tim deZarn
.
In his article at
StarTrek.com
, the image used is that of
Neil
.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Saul Rubichik
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Saul_Rubichik
======================================================================

Not to be confused with actor
Saul Rubinek
Professor
Saul Rubichik
was an instructor at
Starfleet Academy
in the late
24th century
. He died prior to
stardate
57752.6
.
On one occasion,
Cadets
Beckett Mariner
and
Amina Ramsey
stole
Rubichik's "old, special
car
" and
drove
it into
San Francisco Bay
. Mariner remembered the mischief fondly, including how
angry
it made Rubichik.
Ottessa Warren
noted that Rubichik served as her
mentor
at the academy, and that she had even spoken at his
funeral
. (
LD
: "
Much Ado About Boimler
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Scooter T. Rocketboy

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Scooter_T._Rocketboy
======================================================================

Scooter T. Rocketboy
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Tycho IV Spaceport
to
Vega colony
. His special accommodation was a
low gravity suite
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Scooter T. Rocketboy was named after a
dog
owned by
Michael
and
Denise Okuda
. At least in the case of the dog, the middle initial stands for "the".
[1]

PAGE: Seth Shostak
