Commander
Seth Shostak
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2364
, this individual was the
commander
of
Terrestrial Defense Division
. He received an order about STFL
Cargo
Manifest 045-349374 from
Captain
Jonas Grumby
of the
USS
Minnow
. The cargo was
sensor drones
, which were delivered to
Starbase 74
. There was concern that
Commander
Quinteros
would be unable to acknowledge readiness for deploying sensor drones. In that case, the captain suggested to the commander that a secondary readiness scenario should be prepared in the event that sensor drones would have to be deployed prior to full operation of the data transmission
network
. The commander was then asked to advise the captain of their decision.
Later that year, this
order
was read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
during an investigation into an alleged
conspiracy
in the highest levels of
Starfleet Command
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was presumably named after the real
Seth Shostak
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Shahna
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shahna
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Drill thrall
on the
planet
Triskelion
Status:
Active
(
2268
)
Born:
Triskelion
Mother:
Shahna's mother
Played by
:
Angelique Pettyjohn
Shahna
was a
humanoid
drill thrall
on the
planet
Triskelion
, where she was born in the mid-
23rd century
.
Her mother
, who had also been a
thrall
, had previously been
killed
in a
freestyle match
.
In
2268
, she was encountered by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
, who were
kidnapped
from their ship by the
Providers
. Shahna was responsible for training the new thralls, becoming romantically involved with
Captain
James T. Kirk
in the process.
To free his crew, Kirk wagered with the Providers that he would fight and defeat three thralls at the same time. Two of the three were killed in competition with Kirk, but the third was only injured, and Shahna replaced this thrall. Competition continued and Kirk forced her to submit, but refused to kill her.
Following the defeat of the Providers, she requested to leave with the
Enterprise
, but her request was denied by Kirk, who thought it was better for her to remain on Triskelion and learn a new way of life. (
TOS
: "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
In
2381
, a framed
photograph
of Shahna,
Galt
, and
Tamoon
was among several others displayed on the
walls
of a
bar
on
Starbase 25
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Shahna was played by
Angelique Pettyjohn
.
Ronald D. Moore
once admitted to having had a crush on the character of Shahna, referring to her as "she of the silver bondage get-up and big hair." (
Star Trek: The Magazine
Volume 1, Issue 1
, p. 112)
External link
Shahna
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Shelby
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shelby
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
Commanding officer
,
USS
Sutherland
Status:
Active
(
2374
)
For a similarly-named starfleet captain, please see
Elizabeth Shelby
.
Captain
Shelby
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the mid-
24th century
. In
2374
, Shelby was the
commanding officer
of the
Federation
starship
USS
Sutherland
.
Lieutenant Commander
Jadzia Dax
was a friend of Shelby's. During a resupply visit to
Deep Space 9
that same year, Captain Shelby returned a favor to Dax by granting
Lieutenant
Manuele Atoa
a
day off
so he could perform his
Samoan firedance
at Dax'
wedding party
. (
DS9
: "
You Are Cordially Invited
")
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Regarding the reference to "Captain Shelby" in "You Are Cordially Invited",
Ronald D. Moore
commented:
My intent when I wrote the line was that this was indeed the same
Shelby
from
BO
BW
. I thought it was a cool reference to throw in for the fans, but I had completely forgotten that
John Ordover
and Co. had very specifically and very politely asked us if we had any intention of ever using this character again and we (including me) had said, "No way – do what you want with her." This very salient fact was pointed out to me after the show had aired and I had a rather sheepish conversation with
Paula Block
over in licensing and with John via e-mail explaining what had happened. Since I only used Shelby's last name in the episode, you're free to look at this either way – it's really her or it's someone else with the same last name (it's a big fleet, after all). (
AOL chat
,
1997
)
Peter David
later wrote passages into a
New Frontier
novel explaining that there were two unrelated Shelbys in Starfleet.
External link
Shelby
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Siggi
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Siggi
======================================================================

Species:
Unnamed
Affiliation:
Collector's Guild
Rank:
Chairman
Occupation:
Collector
Status:
Deceased
(
2381
)
Died:
2381,
Hauze's ship
Played by
:
Ben Rodgers
"
I don't got to answer to nobody but me. That's how I got on top; that's how I'm staying on top.
"
–
Siggi
,
2381
("
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
Chairman
Siggi
was a
sarcastic
, self-interested, multi-
tentacled
alien
collector
who represented the
Collector's Guild
. He owned
his own starship
, and owned a sizeable collection. He was heavily prejudiced against
Orions
.
In
2381
, he requested the
Federation
's assistance in cataloguing the objects carried aboard
Kerner Hauze
's
automated ship
, as well as possibly disposing of the hazardous materials, prior to the
auction
of Hauze's possessions. During the cataloguing process, he attempted to
steal
Kahless
'
fornication helmet
, triggering the ship's
automated defenses
. He attempted to divert the blame towards
Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
, an Orion, until his theft of the helmet was exposed.
As Siggi and the
away team
assisting from the
USS
Cerritos
attempted to escape the attacks, they entered an
ossuary room
, where Siggi was killed by the falling
skeleton
of
Spock Two
. (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
Siggi was voiced by
Ben Rodgers
.

that he
commandeered
a

and flew "halfway to the

Professor
Somak
was a
female
instructor
at
Starfleet Academy
during the mid-
24th century
who taught a course entitled
Moral and Ethical Issues of Command
. Among the students she taught this course to during her career were
Benjamin Sisko
and
Jadzia Dax
.
One of her favorite
speeches
, according to Sisko, was "
Always maintain
emotional
distance between yourself and those under your command.
" Although Dax considered it good advice and as much as Sisko tried to follow it, he noted that it was a lot more complicated in practice than in theory. (
DS9
: "
The Ship
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sord
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sord
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Reptilian
Occupation:
Specialist
Status:
Active
(
2269
)
Played by
:
James Doohan
(voice)
Sord
was a being from a
spacefaring
reptilian
race
who lived during the mid-
23rd century
. He was one of six
specialists
chosen by the
Vedala
in
2269
to recover the
stolen
Soul of the Skorr
, along with
Captain
Kirk
,
Commander
Spock
,
Tchar
,
Em/3/Green
, and
Lara
.
Sord's ship among the lot, in orbit of the Vedala asteroid
Sord's species was dark green,
scaly
skin
, with a large crest running back from the front of their heads at the mid-line of the skull. These digitigrades possessed a tail and clawed hands (with three fingers and a thumb) and feet (with three toes) and were exceptionally strong, physically. They were not built for vertical climbing.
Upon their arrival to the "
mad planet
", his initial observations were that "
I like this place, it's got variety.
" When their trip was threatened by a
lava flow
, he assisted Kirk and Lara in creating a
barricade
by maneuvering large
rocks
into place to divert the flow.
Sord assisting Kirk in pushing a boulder off a cliff
Later, after completing the trek across the
ice
plains, the tired Em wanted to give up, but was instead carried by Sord, over the
sand
hills and through the heavy
brush
. As Sord led the way, he stopped suddenly, as he believed he saw something moving in the brush. After Spock explained that there was no
life
on that planet, Sord acquiesced, stating that that particular planet "
just gets on your
nerves
.
"
Sord with his handheld weapon
When the group reached the
Skorr temple
and came under attack by a number of
mechanized sentinels
, he protected Em while he picked the lock to the temple's door. Sord even managed to knock one of the mechanicals out of the sky and destroyed it. Spock later believed that these mechanicals were what Sord perhaps saw earlier in the brush.
As the group considered resting prior to entering the temple, Sord agreed to press on, stating, "
let's finish it. Or give this
deadfall
a chance to finish us.
" As they entered, he viewed the Soul of the Skorr, he thought that it was "pretty". When the team found a ledge to climb along the wall of the temple, he stayed behind, due to his inability to climb in that manner.
After it was revealed that Tchar was in fact the enemy, where after he activated a
gravity neutralizer
and caused the group to float weightlessly within the temple, so as to "
fly and fight as a Skorr
", Sord told the group, "
no offense, little ones, but let me
tackle
him.
"
Though his attempt failed, Tchar was nonetheless captured, and the group returned to the
Vedala asteroid
with the Soul of Alar. When informed by the Vedala that they would not be rewarded for completing their task, Sord thought just as well, as he "
got nowhere to keep a
medal
, anyway.
" A short time later he was returned to his ship and left to go on his way. (
TAS
: "
The Jihad
")
Appendices
Background information
The voice of Sord was provided by
James Doohan
.
In a deleted line from the
script
, he was referred to as a "great scaly lump" by Em, after he nearly flipped the cart over climbing into it.
Unlike the other individuals chosen for the mission, Sord's specialty was never disclosed.
Apocrypha
Author
Michael Jan Friedman
has agreed with fan speculation that Sord is probably Gnalish (
β
), a race first featured in his
Pocket TNG
novel
,
Reunion
, in particular of the relatively smaller Fejjimaera sub-species introduced in
Progenitor
.
[1]
[2]
In the
Star Trek: Stargazer
novel series
, this species is given the name of "Gnalish" and a member of the species, Phigus Simenon, is the chief engineer of the
USS
Stargazer
under
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. The Gnalish species also contains a number of sub-species including the "Fejjimeara" and the "Mazzereht."
External links
Gnalish
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

dorsal

thought it would be "too esoteric" and wanted something more "generic" — which turned out to be

======================================================================
PAGE: Starfleet cadets (23rd century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Starfleet_cadets_(23rd_century)
======================================================================

For the
alternate reality
counterpart, please see
Starfleet cadets (alternate reality)
.
In the
23rd century
, prospective
Starfleet
officers
enrolled at
Starfleet Academy
on
Earth
.
After being accepted into the Academy, they would be educated and trained to be
Starfleet cadets
. Cadets usually spent four
years
in the Academy, but more or fewer years were possible, depending on circumstance and performance.
Officer cadets were sometimes known as
midshipmen
.
After graduation,
cadets
would be commissioned as
Ensigns
, and in some specific circumstances, potentially even a higher rank.
Enlisted
officers often had at least some amount of their training at Starfleet Academy, but were not considered cadets.
Being a Starfleet cadet was not the only way to become an officer. Other methods included
field commissions
,
Officers candidate
programs, and an education at
Starfleet Medical Academy
. Individuals enrolled at
Starfleet Medical Academy
were typically referred to as students, not cadets.
Due to other methods of recieving an officer's commission, only individuals known to have been cadets are listed below.
Unknown timeframe

(graduated)

(graduated)

(graduated)

(graduated)

(graduated)

Elijah April
was introduced. It is unclear if a real Elijah April existed for the vision to be based off of, or if he was a complete fabrication.

roommate
at Starfleet Academy was a

part-

accident
led to his

: "just relax and let me do-". (
DIS

Brother
")

species
of her other
genetic
parentage
was not revealed.

======================================================================
PAGE: Starfleet captains
CATEGORIES: Starfleet personnel
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Starfleet_captains
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
"
Starfleet captains are like
children
. They want everything right now and they want it their way, but the secret is to give them only what they need, not what they want.
"
–
Montgomery Scott
,
2369
("
Relics
")
In the history of
Starfleet
, many
officers
held the
rank
of
captain
or held the title of
captain
while
commanding
starships
.
According to
Travis Mayweather
,
Matthew Ryan
spent more time in
space
than most Starfleet captains. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")
List of
prime reality
captains
Senior captains
Starfleet
flag officers
sometimes retained or took command of a vessel. Most often, these officers held the rank of
commodore
, but there were instances of
admirals
operating as starship captains.
Commodore
Decker, Matthew
(
USS
Constellation
)
Fleet Captain
Garth
(
Garth's starship
)

(
USS

and
USS

-A
)

(
USS
Enterprise
)
Commodore
Wesley, Bob
(
USS
Lexington
)
Fleet Admiral
Shelby, Elizabeth
(
USS
Enterprise
-F
)
Admiral
Picard, Jean-Luc
(
USS
Enterprise
-D
)
Captains
The substantive rank of captain was typically held by officers captaining starships. Officers of this rank also sometimes served in other capacities, such as in command of starbases or divisions of support personnel (such as
JAG
officers or members of the
Starfleet Corps of Engineers
), or as members of experimental engineering programs.

33rd century
Leto
Junior captains
According to the rules of naval parlance, any officer could be addressed as "captain" while serving as commanding officer of any starship; the term referred to their
role
rather than their
rank
. Captains could also have been awarded provisional (brevet) ranks or field promotions to be accorded the title of captain.

Captains
The substantive rank of captain was typically held by officers captaining starships. Officers of this rank also sometimes served in other capacities, such as members of experimental engineering programs.
Abbott, Frank
(
USS
Bradbury
)

Kelvin
)
Junior captains
According to the rules of naval parlance, a person of any officer rank could be addressed as captain if they were assigned as sole commanding officer of any starship. Captains may have also been awarded field promotions to be accorded the title of captain.

Background information
Numerous officers were often referred to as commanders, or wore commander's rank insignia, but were actually other ranks. In
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds
",
Jean-Luc Picard
remembers meeting
Lieutenant Commander
Riker, but Riker was wearing commander's insignia in the corresponding scene in
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
". This happened in
TOS
also, as
Spock
was referred to as an lieutenant commander in
TOS
: "
Court Martial
" even though he regularly wore commander's stripes up to and including that point. The same went for
Benjamin Finney
in that episode and
Giotto
in
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
", both of whom wore commander's insignia but were clearly referred to as lieutenant commander in dialogue. The reverse happened to
Chakotay
, who wore lieutenant commander's insignia but was
only
referred to as commander for
VOY
's entire run.
See also
Commander, Starfleet
Theater Commander

of

, many

,

flag officers

Footnotes
Background information
Numerous officers were referred to as commanders, or wore commander
rank
insignia
, but were actually other ranks. In
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds
",
Jean-Luc Picard
remembered meeting
Lieutenant Commander
Riker, but Riker was wearing commander's insignia in the corresponding scene in
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
". This happened in
Star Trek: The Original Series
also, as
Spock
was referred to as an lieutenant commander in
TOS
: "
Court Martial
" even though he regularly wore commander's stripes up to and including that point. The same went for
Benjamin Finney
in that episode and
Giotto
in
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
", both of whom wore commander's insignia but were clearly referred to as lieutenant commander in dialogue. The reverse happened to
Chakotay
, who wore lieutenant commander's insignia but was only referred to as commander for
Star Trek: Voyager
's entire run.

)
Background information
Only one officer in
TOS
wore a lieutenant junior grade braid, Tormolen in "
The Naked Time
", even though some officers (for example,
Kyle
,
Leslie
,
Charlene Masters
, and
Marla McGivers
) were referred to as "lieutenant" in dialogue but wore no rank insignia, which otherwise indicated an
ensign
or
crewman
, presenting a number of ambiguities in identifying a character's true rank. Contrarily, the junior engineer encountered aboard the
Enterprise
in "
Trials and Tribble-ations
" was described in the script as a "lieutenant, junior grade", but wore the stripes of a full lieutenant.
According to one early draft of
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
(dated
19 July
1978
), Lieutenant
Ilia
was identified in dialogue as a "lieutenant junior grade".

======================================================================
PAGE: Stefan
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Stefan
======================================================================

Stefan
was a male
soccer
player, born in
2321
.
In
2332
, when
Beverly Howard
was eight, she had a major crush on an eleven-year-old Stefan, even though he did not know she existed. She
fantasized
that they would eventually
marry
and have three
children
:
Andrew
and
Alexander
(
twins
) and
Jennifer
. She also
imagined
that Stefan became a great
artist
, creating breathtaking
metal
sculptures
.
In
2367
, she mentioned her crush on Stefan to
Deanna Troi
when trying to sort through her feelings for
Odan
, then temporarily living in
William T. Riker
's body. (
TNG
: "
The Host
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The spelling comes from the script.
[1]
His name was spelled as Stephen in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 478.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sten
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sten
======================================================================

Sten
was an
artist
from
Marcus II
.
Flint
possessed one of his works among the collections he kept in his
castle
on
Holberg 917G
. (
TOS
: "
Requiem for Methuselah
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Several other individuals whose art Flint possessed in his collection (Brahms, Leonardo) were later revealed to have been Flint himself, who had assumed these identities.

======================================================================
PAGE: Stepan
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Stepan
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Engineer
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
Status:
Active
(
2367
)
Ensign
Stepan
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2367
, Stepan was assigned to the
Engineering
Department of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. When it was discovered that a
preburner
was producing a higher
deuterium
flow,
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
consulted with
Coffin
and Stepan. Coffin suggested that this situation might be related to a
pastore pulse
overpressure, while Stepan thought it was an issue of
thermal conductivity
. The chief engineer disagreed with Stepan. (
TNG-R
: "
Galaxy's Child
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered edition of "Galaxy's Child", the original
okudagram
was replaced by a new graphic. Stepan was mentioned in that graphic.

======================================================================

======================================================================

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
Steph Silvestri
was a
Starfleet
officer
.
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, she was named as a
Fleet Ops
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
. (
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named for production assistant
Steph Silvestri
.
Retroactive continuity
: In
2328
, Silvestri was a passenger aboard the
transport ship
SS
Manila
at
Alpha Zayra II
bound to
Omicron Beta
. This information from the
passenger manifest
was recorded in the
commercial transport database
and reviewed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Steth (impostor)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Steth_(impostor)
======================================================================

..as
Steth
..as
Tom Paris
..as
Kathryn Janeway
..as
Daelen
1/4
Steth
was the name an unknown
DNA
thief
lifeform
was using when it was encountered by
USS
Voyager
.
In
2374
, it was rescued by
Voyager
before
its ship's
coaxial warp drive
could explode. Once it was
transported
aboard, it won the friendship of the crew, most notably
Tom Paris
, who helped repair its ship. Once the repairs were completed, the lifeform
switched DNA
with Paris, effectively taking his appearance
without
actually switching bodies or minds, leaving him in its ship which shortly thereafter warped away.
The lifeform remained on
Voyager
to live out Paris' life, but began to exhibit erratic behavior. When it threatened
Seven of Nine
while
intoxicated
on duty,
Kathryn Janeway
called what she thought was Paris to her
ready room
. The lifeform then attacked and switched DNA with her when she became suspicious. When
Tuvok
arrived, having been called, he stunned Janeway, who now had Paris's appearance. Janeway was taken to
sickbay
.
Paris, in the form of Steth, along with the real
Steth
, in the form the lifeform it had
previously inhabited
, returned to
Voyager
, "Janeway" attempted to escape by taking a
Class 2
shuttle
with a modified coaxial warp drive. Paris was able to convince
Chakotay
that the lifeform had stolen his appearance, and Chakotay allowed Paris and Steth to pursue the lifeform and stop it before it could warp away.
Back on
Voyager
, they managed to force the lifeform to return the DNA of its previous victims, returning Janeway, Tom, and Steth to their original appearance. The real Steth planned to return to
Benthos
with the impostor, where he hoped to find its previous victims. (
VOY
: "
Vis à Vis
")
Appendices
Background information
The lifeform was played by
Dan Butler
as Steth,
Robert Duncan McNeill
as Tom Paris,
Kate Mulgrew
as Kathryn Janeway, and
Mary Elizabeth McGlynn
as Daelen.
The known chain of victims:
Daelen
to
Steth
to
Tom Paris
to
Kathryn Janeway
.
In the script he was described initially as "Steth", then, after trading bodies with Paris, the script notes: "
From this point on, the chamelionic alien will be referred to as "Paris/Steth.
"
See also
Coalescent organism
Biomimetic lifeform
External link

Steve Bowen
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. He was a
lieutenant commander
aboard the
USS
Matte Fringe
in
2364
.
Prior to
stardate
41547.8, according to an order sent by
Admiral
Daniel Curry
of
Starfleet Advanced Technologies Division
, he had received
security clearances
to classified material for a project. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after visual effects compositor
Steve Bowen
.

PAGE: Steve Gallacci
CATEGORIES: Individuals

Robert Louis Stevenson

Serial number:
FSN 682-58-1047
Status:
Active
(

)
Steve Gallaci
was a
Starfleet
officer
of the
24th century
. His
serial number
was FSN 682-58-1047.
In
2364
, he was a
professor
aboard the
USS
Robert Louis Stevenson
. On or about
stardate
41044.2, according to an order sent by
Commodore
Bill Marafe
of
Starbase 55
, he was scheduled for a transfer to the
USS
Discovery
. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He might have been named after the comic writer
Steve Gallaci
, who's
Albedo Anthropomorphics
comic book series provided several references within the set of orders.

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Steve Gausche, Jr.
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the late
24th century
.
Retroactive continuity
: In
2328
, Gausche was listed to receive the bill for
Scott McKnight
's travel aboard the
SS
Manila
from
Beta Wiltz III
to
Lehman's Planet
. This information was listed in the
passenger manifest
records in the
commercial transport database
and reviewed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
")
In
2372
, Gausche was a member of the
Tactical Ops
team at
Antares Ship Yards
during the construction of the
USS
Valiant
. (
DS9
: "
Valiant
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, he was named as a
Fleet Ops
officer at the
Marin County Starfleet Yards
,
Skywalker Division
during the construction of the
USS
Pasteur
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
production staff
member,
Steve Gausche, Jr.
.
External link
Steve Gausche
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Steve Tucker (Lieutenant Commander)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Steve_Tucker_(Lieutenant_Commander)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Born:
Triskelion Prime
Steve Tucker
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
. His
planet
of origin was
Triskelion Prime
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Wisconsin
ferried him from
Excalbia Tourist Resort
to
New Fabrina Settlement
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
In
2369
, he was a
lieutenant commander
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. He was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. His
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a 4 billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after editor
Steve Tucker
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Steve Wiener (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Steve_Wiener_(comedian)
======================================================================

Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D's
database
Steve Wiener
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Wiener was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
EN-54395
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
In the remastered version of the episode, this character replaced
Diane Overdiek
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Steven Gerber
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Steven_Gerber
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Commander
Occupation:
First officer
,
USS
Yamato
Status:
Deceased
(
2365
)
Died:
2365
,
USS
Yamato
Commander
Steven Gerber
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2365
, he was assigned to the
USS
Yamato
. He served under
Captain
Donald Varley
as a
first officer
. He recorded a number of
captain's logs
, which were later seen in a list sorted by stardate on Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
's monitor on aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
According to Varley, after he ordered a course to
Iconia
, Garber questioned the wisdom of his captain in giving this order as it violated the
Romulan Neutral Zone
. Later, he was killed when his ship suddenly self-destructed. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
",
okudagram
)
He was named after episode co-writer
Steve Gerber
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 541)
His first name is spelled two different ways in the list; three times with a "v" and once with a "ph".

was a
male
Starfleet
officer
in the
23rd century
. He was the
commanding officer
of the
USS
Yeager
in the
2250s
.
In
2256
, he was
killed
when his
starship
was destroyed during the
Battle of the Binary Stars
. He was described as being one of the finest captains by the
Klingon
Voq
, who was
impersonating
Lieutenant
Ash Tyler
, a
Human
who served under him. (
DIS
: "
Choose Your Pain
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sully
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sully
======================================================================

Sully
was a member of
Starfleet
who was stationed on
Ajilon Prime
in
2373
.
In that year, during the
Battle of Ajilon Prime
, Sully was shot in the
leg
by the invading
Klingons
and taken off the
front line
by the
field medics
. An
ensign
later recounted to
Jake Sisko
about seeing Sully get shot and wishing
he
had been shot instead so he wouldn't have to fight. It prompted the
ensign
to shoot himself in the
foot
instinctively, thereby removing him from the fighting. (
DS9
: "
Nor the Battle to the Strong
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Susan Nuress
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Susan_Nuress
======================================================================

Susan Nuress
was a
23rd century
doctor
and
scientist
.
In
2294
and '
95
, she was the principal investigator for a project in the northern
continent
of
Oby VI
. With her team of research associates,
Doctors
Rob Bowman
,
Maurice Hurley
,
Jaron Summers
, and
Jon Povill
, she was responsible for curing the victims of an outbreak of
plasma plague
.
When the
plasma plague
threatened the
Rachelis system
and the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2365
, her project and the cure developed by her team was still viable. Her name was listed on an
etiological
report for the virus. (
TNG
: "
The Child
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
External link
Susan Nuress
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Susie Crabtree
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Susie_Crabtree
======================================================================

Susie Crabtree
was a
24th century
female
and the first love of
Tom Paris
.
When she dumped him in his first year at the
Starfleet Academy
, Paris felt deeply
depressed
. After breaking out in
hives
, he couldn't get out of
bed
for a
week
. He almost failed
Stellar Cartography
because of his condition; he eventually got over her, but "
walked around in a daze for the rest of that year.
" Even years later, Paris could be reminded of the
day
she left him by a certain
smell
or a few notes of a
song
. (
VOY
: "
Lifesigns
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Suzanne Dumont
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Suzanne_Dumont
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2366
)
Ensign
Suzanne Dumont
was a
female
24th century
Starfleet
officer
assigned to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
under
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
during the
2360s
.
In late
2366
,
Wesley Crusher
scheduled a
date
with Ensign Dumont.
Geordi La Forge
, who described Dumont as "
one attractive lady
", was surprised that Crusher had the nerve to ask her on a date. He congratulated Crusher on his success.
Later, while under the influence of
Ambassador
Sarek
's
emotions
, La Forge told Crusher that she was "
way out of [Crusher's] league
" and guaranteed Crusher would "
get more action out of a good
book
than he would ever see on this date.
"
Crusher had told
his mother
that he was planning to attend the
Mozart
concert
with Dumont, but Dumont opted to go the
arboretum
instead. (
TNG
: "
Sarek
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Suzi Shimizu (Lieutenant)

female

24th century

2328
, the
SS
Manoa
ferried her from
Midos V Satellite City Spaceport
to
Murasaki 312
. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander

aboard the

-D
in

by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (

: "

",

)

2353
, she was a
Starfleet
officer
listed as a member of the

on the directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. Her office was located in room #35559. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2371
, she was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. She was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In
2374
, as a
lieutenant
, she was listed on a
crew manifest
. (

: "

",
crew manifest
)
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

associate

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Sveta
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sveta
======================================================================

Sveta
was a member of the
Maquis
and a friend of
Chakotay
. She recruited him into the Maquis some time around
2370
. She had also known
B'Elanna Torres
. As of
2374
she was in a
prison
, like the few other Maquis to escape
Dominion
's massacre of the Maquis in
2373
.
In
2374
, she sent a
letter
to Chakotay aboard the
USS
Voyager
via the
Hirogen communications network
. (
VOY
: "
Hunters
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Sveta originated in the
Jeri Taylor
novel
Pathways
. Svetlana "Sveta" Korepanova was an Academy friend of Chakotay who later encouraged him to join the Maquis and defend his home planet in the
Demilitarized Zone
from the
Cardassians
.
Chakotay described Sveta as an old friend. However, B'Elanna was surprised that she would write him.
External link
Svetlana Korepanova
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Syl
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Syl
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Syl's species
Affiliation:
United Federation of Planets
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Krall
(
2263
)
Died:
2263
,
Altamid
Played by
:
Melissa Roxburgh
Ensign
Syl
was an
alien
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
in the
2260s
. She had multiple cranial appendages like crab's legs that folded around the back of her head.
When
Krall
attacked
the
Enterprise
in
2263
to acquire a piece of a
weapon
called the
Abronath
, Syl was shocked to see one of her fellow
crewmembers
fall, so she helped him up, unknowingly watched by
Captain
James T. Kirk
. She was assigned by him to hide the artifact, which Syl did by placing it within the clutch of her cranial appendages. Syl was subsequently captured by Krall's
drones
and imprisoned at
his base
alongside other
Enterprise
crewmembers.
Syl opening her cranial appendages
After Krall publicly threatened to
drain
Hikaru Sulu
's life unless he obtained the rest of the Abronath, Syl surrendered it despite Sulu's repeated objections.
Later, Krall unleashed the Abronath on Syl as a demonstration of its power to
Nyota Uhura
. The weapon painfully disintegrated Syl's entire body. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Syl was played by actress
Melissa Roxburgh
.
Syl was named after the daughter of
Star Trek Beyond
co-writer
Doug Jung
.
[1]
According to make-up artist
Joel Harlow
, the design was an homage to the
facehugger
from the
Alien
franchise.
[2]

")

.
T'nag
was an old enemy of the
Klingon
warriors
Kor
,
Koloth
, and
Kang
. The three Klingons fought a glorious battle against T'nag with an army of forty
legions
. They emerged victorious against overwhelming odds, killing T'nag and feasting on his
heart
. (
DS9
: "
The Sword of Kahless
")
Kor once held the
Korma Pass
against T'nag's forces. (
DS9
: "
Once More Unto the Breach
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

of this assignment was 49306. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in
Benjamin Sisko
's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: T. Snowdon
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T._Snowdon
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Dental
Hygienist
Status:
Active
(
2372
)
T. Snowdon
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In the
2360s
, this individual served under
Captain
Leyton
aboard the
USS
Okinawa
when
Benjamin Sisko
was the
ship
's
executive officer
.
In
2372
, this individual was assigned as a
Senior Hygienist
,
Starfleet Dental
. The effective
date
of this assignment was 49267. This officer was one of over four hundred officers reassigned in a three week period by Leyton. This officer's name was listed in the personnel assignment log that was displayed on a screen in Sisko's temporary office in
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
",
okudagram
)
The unauthorized reference book
The Nitpicker's Guide for Deep Space Nine Trekkers
(p. 339) notes that the crew of the
Okinawa
(apart from Sisko) were named after characters in
Joseph Heller
's novel
Catch-22
.
Snowden
was a radio-gunner in the novel.
According to the ebook
The Oppressor's Wrong
, his full name is Ishmael Snowden. By contrast, the novel
Rough Beasts of Empire
depicted Snowden as a woman.

======================================================================
PAGE: T. Ziegler
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/T._Ziegler
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Occupation:
USS
Voyager
crewmember
Serial number:
RM 713-1138
Status:
Deceased
(
2370s
)
Died:
2370s
T. Ziegler
was a
Starfleet
officer assigned to the
starship
USS
Voyager
, a
lieutenant commander
who was killed in the line of duty sometime before
2377
.
Ziegler's name was read out on a
casualty report
of all who died during that vessel's journey through the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Imperfection
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Judging by the lieutenant commander rank, it is possible this name refers to one of the senior officers killed en route when
Voyager
was dragged to the Delta Quadrant, perhaps the
chief engineer
or the unnamed
chief medical officer
played by
Jeff McCarthy
. Then again, it might refer to neither.
As with many names on the "Imperfection" list, Toby Ziegler is an
in-joke
referring to the character on
The West Wing
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Taggert (Ensign)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taggert_(Ensign)
======================================================================

For the captain of the Repulse, please see
Taggert
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2367
)
Played by
:
Unknown performer
(voice)
Ensign
Taggert
was an
officer
serving aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2367
.
That year he prepared the
shuttlecraft
Hawking
to transport
Odan
to the surface of
Peliar Zel II
.
Commander
William T. Riker
piloted the shuttle himself but it came under attack and Odan was severely wounded. (
TNG
: "
The Host
")
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
Taggert was voiced by an
unknown actor
.
Taking place in 2409, the non-canon
computer game
Star Trek Online
features a
Denobulan
Starfleet Captain called "Masc Taggart" who hints at having once served under Cpt. Picard. Possibly this was intended as a reference to Ensign Taggert.

======================================================================
PAGE: Taglonen
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taglonen
======================================================================

Species:
Abronian
Status:
Deceased
(
3180s
)
Taglonen
was an
Abronian
, one of the last surviving members of the
species
who evacuated their doomed
homeworld
on a ship built from their
planet
's
moon
.
In the
3180s
, Taglonen awoke from
cryostasis
when
grave robbers
attacked the Abronian ship, and managed to send a
telepathic
distress call
that drew the attention of
J'Vini
, a
sister
of the
Qowat Milat
. Taglonen succumbed to his wounds as J'Vini dispatched the last of the grave robbers. To honor him, J'Vini covered his body with her
cloak
and took on his cause as her own: to protect the Abronians and ensure that they would wake to settle their new home. (
DIS
: "
Choose to Live
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Takashi
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Takashi
======================================================================

Takashi
was a
24th century
sumo
wrestler.
Kathryn Janeway
once watched Takashi compete during the
77th Emperor's Cup
, where she recalled that Takashi forced his opponent,
Kar-pek
, out of the circle in less than three
seconds
.
Chakotay
and
Tuvok
disagreed, stating that Takashi had actually lost the match because his
knee
broke the
sand
. (
VOY
: "
Latent Image
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Talmadge
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Talmadge
======================================================================

Rank:
Captain
Occupation:
CO
,
Dorian
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Terrence Beasor
(voice)
Captain
Talmadge
was the
commanding officer
of the
24th century
transport ship
Dorian
in the late
2360s
.
He sent out a
distress call
when his ship was attacked by two
battle cruisers
from
Rekag-Seronia
while he transported
Federation
mediator
Ambassador
Ves Alkar
and his "
mother
"
Sev Maylor
to peace talks on the
planet
.
The
USS
Enterprise
-D
under command of Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
answered the call and met the transport vessel. Captain Talmadge asked Picard to
beam
the two
Lumerians
aboard to make sure they were safe and the
Enterprise
-D continued the mission. (
TNG
: "
Man Of The People
")
This character appeared as an off-screen voiceover.
He was voiced by
Terrence Beasor
who received no credit for this work.
External links
Talmadge
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Talon W'Xaria

Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain

.

Violacean

Bartender
,
The Academy
Status:
Active
(
3195
)
Played by
:
Darius "Jackie Cox" Rose
Talon W'Xaria
was a
Violacean
who worked as a
bartender
at
The Academy
nightclub
in
San Francisco
during the late
32nd century
.
In
3195
, W'Xaria spotted a brewing altercation between two groups of
cadets
from
Starfleet Academy
and the
War College
, and intervened with a round of
shots
and an admonishment to "
play nice
". (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
W'Xaria was played by
Darius "Jackie Cox" Rose
.
Rose revealed this character's name and species on Instagram.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Tamarius Sisko
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tamarius_Sisko
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Sisko family lineage
Tamarius Sisko
was an
ancestor
on
Starfleet
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
's
family tree
, the
spouse
of
Sonequa Sisko
, and
parent
to
Kendis
and
Sophia
.
In the
32nd century
, Tamarius' extended family linked to Benjamin was listed on a family tree exhibit at the
Benjamin Sisko Museum
in
New Orleans
(and accessible via
virtual access portal
).
In
3195
,
SAM
visited the Sisko Museum virtually and viewed the exhibit while attempting to solve the mystery of Captain Sisko's disappearance. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

.

or

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tamoon
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tamoon
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Drill thrall
on the
planet
Triskelion
Status:
Active
(
2268
)
Played by
:
Jane Ross
Tamoon
was a deep-voiced, yellow-
skinned
humanoid
, with orange hair and large bushy eyebrows, who was
held captive
on the
planet
Triskelion
, where she served as a
thrall
for
Provider 2
.
In
2268
she was chosen to be the "
drill thrall
" for
Ensign
Chekov
when he,
Kirk
and
Uhura
were transported to Triskelion from the
USS
Enterprise
by the
Providers
. As Chekov's drill thrall, she was responsible for his training, and providing him with nourishment.
During their first encounter, she commented on his name, which she first interpreted as "Chee-koo" and later "Chee-koof," describing it a "a very nice name." Surprised by her deep voice, the nervous Chekov, who was unsure if she was even
female
, remained polite towards her advances on him. She proceeded to grab his arms near the shoulders and gave him a squeeze, telling him that he was "a fine specimen" and that she liked him "better than the others."
Tamoon had plans of instructing Chekov well, so that her Provider would take him, and if the Provider was pleased with the result, he might "select" Chekov and Tamoon for each other.
During a later visit, Chekov tied Tamoon up with his
training harness
, and when released from his
quarters
by Captain Kirk, Chekov regretted that "
this is going to kill our romance.
"
Following the defeat of the
Providers
, she stayed on Triskelion to learn a new way of life. (
TOS
: "
The Gamesters of Triskelion
")
In
2381
, a framed
photograph
of Tamoon,
Galt
, and
Shahna
was among several others displayed on the
walls
of a
bar
on
Starbase 25
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Tamoon was played by actress
Jane Ross
.
The script lists her as "Taky".
External link
Tamoon
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarbolde
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarbolde
======================================================================

Tarbolde
was an
author
from the
Canopus Planet
who, in
1996
, wrote "
Nightingale Woman
," which was widely regarded as one of the most passionate
love
sonnets
for many centuries. While under the influence of exposure to the
Galactic barrier
,
Gary Mitchell
recited an excerpt of Tarbolde's famous sonnet. (
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")
His brilliance was encouraged by his encounter with
Onaya
who helped him in his career like she did with
Catullus
,
John Keats
and
Tavor Kell
. He
died
young, having his mental energy drained by Onaya. (
DS9
: "
The Muse
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
Star Trek Concordance
, Tarbolde's first name was "Phineas". This was later adopted by the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 371. The name was also referenced in "
Far Beyond the Stars
".
Tarbolde's work was mentioned in the novel
Q-Space
, as being among the personal collection of
Jean-Luc Picard
.
See also
Phineas Tarbolde and the Nightingale Women
External link
Phineas Tarbolde
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarquin
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarquin
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Status:
Active
(
2153
)
Played by
:
Maury Sterling
Tarquin
was a
humanoid alien
with
telepathic
abilities and some undefined power or
technology
allowing him to shut down the power supplies of
starships
. He was encountered by
Enterprise
NX-01
in
October
2153
.
Born sometime prior to the
1750s
, Tarquin was discovered to have telepathic abilities. As such abilities were considered threatening on
his homeworld
, he was
exiled
to
his own planet
to live out the rest of his days in isolation.
Tarquin's telepathic ability had a number of different aspects. According to him, every artifact has, imprinted on it, traces of the people who made and used it. Tarquin could read those imprints. He could also connect with certain minds on a very deep level, being able to read their every thought. Very few people have minds this sensitive; those who did included
Morianna Taal
and
Hoshi Sato
. His telepathy was limited in range; as a
going-away present
, his family presented him with an
egg-shaped object
he could use to extend it.
Lonely, Tarquin began telepathically searching for someone he could share his exile with. Morianna Taal was the first, but as his species was very long-lived, she died while he continued to live. He searched for another companion; he had had four in total by the time the last one died before the
2050s
. He said that sometimes he did not know what was worse: living alone or burying his companions.
In 2153, Tarquin detected Hoshi Sato aboard
Enterprise
as it traveled through the
Delphic Expanse
. Her mind was of the right type for him to connect to, and he began to monitor her thoughts for several days. Eventually, he made full telepathic contact with her, inviting her to join him on his
planet
in exchange for giving the
Enterprise
crew information on the
Xindi
.
Sato stayed with Tarquin for several days while
Enterprise
investigated a
Delphic Expanse sphere
. During that time, she learned of his succession of companions and that he wanted her to become one. Sato refused, and foiled his attempts to telepathically trick her into staying. Once she saw through his ruse, he tried and failed to coerce her by threatening
Enterprise
, shutting down all power and systems on the vessel.
As a parting gift, Tarquin gave Sato the coordinates of
a colony
where part of the
Xindi weapon
was being constructed. (
ENT
: "
Exile
")
Background information
Tarquin was played by
Maury Sterling
. He was also played by
Scott Bakula
and
John Billingsley
when he projected himself in the image of
Jonathan Archer
and
Phlox
respectively.
Tarquin disguised as a human
In the final draft script of "Exile", Tarquin's
Human
appearance was described as "an attractive Human man." A note in the script also pointed out, "
The same actor will play Tarquin as a Human and an alien.
" Tarquin in his true appearance was scripted to be "a hulking, grotesque creature."
Also in the final draft script of "Exile", Tarquin was described as having had five companions prior to meeting Hoshi Sato, with the graves of all five at his sanctuary. However, this quantity was clearly reduced to four companions and graves.
Star Trek: Enterprise
co-creator and Executive Producer
Brannon Braga
was pleased with how Tarquin is portrayed in the episode "Exile". "
There's just something interesting about a lonely alien who's captivated by Hoshi's ability to communicate,
" Braga remarked. "
The alien's interest in her helped elucidate what makes Hoshi interesting as a character [....] It was also interesting to use the telepathic alien to help us get some crucial information about the Xindi.
" (
Star Trek: Communicator
issue 151
, p. 29)
Tarquin's costume was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
Unused special effects make-up face and head applications for him were also sold off.
[2]
(X)

music

, when given the choice between the
android
performing in the style of
Heifetz
,
Menuhin
,

over 300
he was capable of imitating,

chose Tataglia. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Tau (pirate)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tau_(pirate)
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Thief
/
Weapons dealer
Status:
Active
(
2374
)
Played by
:
John Vargas
Tau
was the a
thief
who sold
weapons
and
technology
that
his ships
confiscated
from other passing
starships
. His business made him a very rich man, and put him in control of "
the seventh
province
in the
north
" on a
planet
located in the
Delta Quadrant
. He dealt mainly in weapons, and if a buyer balked at his price, he would threaten to sell the weapons to the buyer's enemies.
In
2374
, he stole
USS
Voyager
's main
computer processor
, a
holographic
mobile emitter
, and other items right through
Voyager
's
shielding
using a stolen
translocator device
. In addition to these physical items, he acquired from
Voyager
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
's
Leonardo da Vinci
hologram
. Tau became Leonardo's "
patron
" and sold his inventions to the highest bidder.
Janeway eventually discovered Tau's location, rescued Leonardo, and recovered
Voyager
's stolen property. (
VOY
: "
Concerning Flight
")
Tau was played by actor
John Vargas
.
His costume was later sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)

Borg

. During a following battle with an

======================================================================
PAGE: Taylor (Admiral)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taylor_(Admiral)
======================================================================

It has been suggested that this article be
merged
with
Jeri Taylor (Vice Admiral)
(
discuss
).
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Admiral
Taylor
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2369
,
Geordi La Forge
, when investigating the
murder
of
Lieutenant
Keith Rocha
, requested a display of the personal records of the lieutenant from the
Relay Station 47
computer
for
stardates
46455 to 46460. One of these records mentioned this individual.
In the log entry, Rocha wrote,"
Heavy load today.
Starfleet Command
requested 20 new lines between
Starbase 515
and
Sector 32834
. Apparently in support of expanded exploratory ops. The only problem is that we've already promised those lines to
Admiral
Taylor.
"  (
TNG
: "
Aquiel
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
For the remastered episode, the log entry stardate was changed from 46457 to 45457. The log entry, with the admiral's name, was new to the episode, having replaced the pre-remastered entry.

======================================================================
PAGE: Taymon
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Taymon
======================================================================

Occupation:
Merchant
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Taresians
(
2373
)
Died:
2373
,
Taresia
Marital status:
Married
Spouse(s):
Three
Taresian
wives
Played by
:
Patrick Fabian
Taymon
was a
humanoid
male
, who claimed to have joined his
civilization
's
merchant fleet
because he wanted to explore every quarter of the
galaxy
.
At some point before
2373
he was infected with a
Taresian
retrovirus
that transformed him into a male Taresian. He "returned" to the Taresian
homeworld
, and was among the group to welcome
Harry Kim
's "return" to
Taresia
.
He was eventually
married
to three of its women, including
Malia
, who on their honeymoon
killed
him for his
genetic
material. This was the way the Taresians propagated their
species
. (
VOY
: "
Favorite Son
")
Taymon was played by actor
Patrick Fabian
.
In Taymon's original telling of his origins, he stated that "my
foster parents
on
Archellia
didn't want me to join the merchant fleet. They thought it was too dangerous." Those lines were trimmed from the final episode to what is stated above.

was

's

, whom she described as her "rock". Detmer credited them with saving her life in the aftermath of her

during the

engaging in battle with a

. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Telnorri
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Telnorri
======================================================================

Telnorri
was a
counselor
stationed on board
Deep Space 9
.
In
2372
, he was assigned to counsel
Chief
Miles O'Brien
three times a week, following his experience in an
Argrathi
prison
.
O'Brien disliked his sessions with Telnorri, stating that all he ever did was "
sit there and listen to me tell stories about what it was like to be in an Argrathi prison. How often did they
beat
me? Was I distressed by the lack of
toilet facilities
? If you ask me, he's the one with the problem.
" With these
feelings
in mind, O'Brien skipped all of his
appointments
with Telnorri over a period of ten
days
. Telnorri, who grew very frustrated with this, contacted
Doctor
Julian Bashir
about O'Brien's lack of commitment.
After an unsuccessful intervention by Doctor Bashir,
Captain
Benjamin Sisko
ordered O'Brien to report to Counselor Telnorri and begin attending daily counseling sessions for as long as Telnorri thought it was advisable.
Following O'Brien's recounting of his actions while incarcerated, he remarked to Bashir, "Now's the part where you tell me I have to start seeing Counsellor Telnorri again." The doctor offered to talk to him instead, but O'Brien assured him that (continuing to speak to) Telnorri would be fine. (
DS9
: "
Hard Time
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
O'Brien later admitted their sessions helped in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Corps of Engineers
book
Aftermath
.
External link
Telnorri
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Telsia Murphy

======================================================================
PAGE: Temba
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Temba
======================================================================

Temba
was an individual referenced in
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases.
In the phrase "
Temba. His arms wide
", it was a metaphor for the giving of a
gift
; in the phrase "
Temba at rest
", it was a metaphor for the declining of a gift. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
"Temba!" could also be used as "Take this!" when attacking an opponent. (
LD
: "
Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the script, Temba was pronounced as "TEM-buh".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Tennyson
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tennyson
======================================================================

For the 19th century poet, please see
Alfred Tennyson
.
Part of the
Cerritos
medical roster
Tennyson
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served aboard the
USS
Cerritos
in the
medical division
in

.

, Ensign

======================================================================
PAGE: The Albino
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/The_Albino
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Criminal
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Kang
(
2370
)
Born:
23rd century
Died:
2370,
Secarus IV
Played by
:
Bill Bolender
For the medical condition, please see
albino
.
"
No
vengeance
today,
Kang
.
"
–
The Albino
,
2370
("
Blood Oath
")
"
The Albino
" was an infamous
criminal
who launched numerous
raids
against
Klingon colonies
in the late
23rd century
.
Quick Answers
What motivated the infamous Albino's raids on Klingon colonies?
The infamous Albino sought revenge against the Klingon Empire. In 2290, after his raids on Klingon colonies were stopped by captains Kor, Koloth, and Kang, he promised retribution. He targeted their firstborn sons as part of his vengeful plan.
Provided by: Fandom
What was the Albino's promise to the Klingon captains?
The Albino vowed to take revenge on the firstborn sons of the Klingon captains Kor, Koloth, and Kang after they tried to capture him in 2290. He kept this promise by infecting their sons with a genetic virus that resulted in their deaths.
Provided by: Fandom
How did the Albino evade capture by three Klingon warships?
In 2290, the infamous Albino evaded capture by three Klingon warships led by Kor, Koloth, and Kang. Despite disrupting his operations, the Klingons could not apprehend him. The Albino later sought revenge by targeting the firstborn sons of the commanders.
Provided by: Fandom
What impact did the Albino have on Klingon history?
The Albino, an infamous criminal, impacted Klingon history with his raids on Klingon colonies in the late 23rd century. In 2290, captains Kor, Koloth, and Kang led a mission to capture him. Although they disrupted his power, they failed to catch him. The Albino vowed revenge on their firstborn sons, which he later achieved by infecting them. This vendetta became a significant event in Klingon history.
Provided by: Fandom
How did the Albino seek revenge on the Klingon captains?
In 2290, the infamous Albino sought revenge on Klingon captains Kor, Koloth, and Kang after they disrupted his operations but failed to capture him. He promised to kill their firstborn sons and later succeeded by infecting them, resulting in their deaths.
Provided by: Fandom
History
In
2290
, three
Klingon
warships
were dispatched to apprehend the Albino:
Kor's
,
Koloth's
, and
Kang's
. The raid was successful in breaking up most of the Albino's power base, but they failed to capture the Albino himself. In a last message to the Klingons, he promised revenge upon the
firstborn
sons of each of the captains.
A few years later, the Albino infected
each of the
three captains
'
firstborn sons
with a
genetic virus
that slowly killed the children. The three fathers, plus
Curzon Dax
, the godfather and namesake of
Kang
's son, swore a
blood oath
to track down and kill the Albino in revenge. Kang nearly tracked him down on
Galdonterre
, but the Albino was warned that they were coming and escaped in time. As a result, the Albino went into hiding, finally establishing a compound on
Secarus IV
in
2345
.
The Albino's compound on Secarus IV
The Albino was finally tracked down again in
2370
, when Kang learned of his location from
one of his discarded wives
. When Kang went to scout out the location, the Albino contacted him and offered a glorious battle – forty of the Albino's best warriors against his four pursuers. Kang accepted, however, the Albino instead booby-trapped the threshold of the
compound
with a
gravitic mine
. Confident in his security, he stayed in the compound and waited.
Instead,
Kang
,
Kor
,
Koloth
, and
Jadzia Dax
(the then-current host of the
Dax symbiont
) launched their own sneak attack against the Albino's compound. Dax bombarded the Albino's compound with
tetryon
particles, rendering the energy weapons of the Albino's soldiers useless. This allowed the four comrades to fight their way, hand-to-hand, into the Albino's headquarters. Kang himself landed the killing blow on the Albino, although both he and Koloth were mortally wounded in the battle. (
DS9
: "
Blood Oath
")
Memorable quotes
"The Trill. Of course. You're far too young and lovely to sport such a deadly weapon. All right, then, use it if that's what you came to do. Kill me in cold blood. Run me through the belly and cut out my heart… isn't that what your blood oath promised? Or have you lost your appetite, my dear?"
- Upon being confronted by Jadzia Dax
Appendices
Background information
Jim Martin
sketch of Albino's courtyard
The Albino was played by
Bill Bolender
.
StarTrek.com
describes The Albino as a "ruthless Klingon criminal," and while he had a slightly Klingon-look to his appearance, he regarded other Klingons as "filth" and "scum".
[1]
(X)
According to the
script
, however, he was described simply as "an
all-white
humanoid
in good physical shape for a man of
a hundred
."
[2]
Apocrypha
Forged in Fire
gives his name as Qagh (
β
).
The comic "
Remembrance
" features the Albino's granddaughter seeking revenge for his death. Jadzia is on Qo'noS. She is there to attend, with Kor, the
quv van je tay
ritual to honor the recently deceased Kang and Koloth. However, the Albino's granddaughter tries to use the opportunity to seek revenge for his death and has allied with
Toral
of the
House of Duras
.
The Albino appears in
Michael A. Martin
and
Andy Mangels
' novel
Forged in Fire
, which tells the story of his piracy and later his revenge on Kang, Kor, and Koloth. The book confirms that he is indeed Klingon – a cousin of Kor – who was born an albino after his mother attempted to genetically alter him in the womb to cure him of the
augment virus
. The procedure is only partially successful, and the infant is born with brow ridges (unlike his mother), but entirely white in coloring.  His revolted mother sends the infant away to be killed, but he is found by a pair of
Orion
pirates, who raise him in their trade. One of the pirates with a passing knowledge of the
Klingon language
whimsically names the infant
Qagh
, "mistake". This history explains his dislike of fellow Klingons. The virus – which he actually gave to the Klingons themselves and they passed to the children – was also given to
Sulu
, who had assisted the Klingons in the hunt for the Albino, in hopes that it would kill his
daughter
. Because a key component came from
Omega IV
, a planet Sulu had visited and become resistant to its adverse effects, the virus did not infect him. The virus also afflicted Curzon Dax, but he expressed no real concern about it, explaining to Sulu that he would retain a form of immortality thanks to his symbiont, clarifying that
Dax
had never contracted the virus in the first place as it was incompatible with the symbiont and would thus not pass the virus on to future hosts. Sulu notably rejected the offer to aid in the Klingon blood oath as he felt that it would be a mistake to focus so much of their lives on revenge against one man, although he nevertheless agreed to keep silent about the events that had taken place and allow the other four to proceed as they saw fit.
External link
Albino
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: The Cleaner
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/The_Cleaner
======================================================================

The Cleaner
was a
Starfleet
covert
agent
in the late
24th century
.
In
2380
, the Cleaner was to support a
black ops team
including
Commander
Jack Ransom
in a clandestine mission to
Romulus
, but the team mistook
Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
(who had been assigned to clean the
USS
Cerritos
conference room
) for the Cleaner, and took her on the mission instead. As the Cleaner, Tendi was expected to
transport
the team back to the ship once a package had been secured, though Tendi misunderstood her role, and physically fought a group of
Romulan
guards instead. During a
Prime
history stone
etching
ceremony, Tendi related her experience to
Imperium Magistrate
Clar
and others present. (
LD
: "
Veritas
")
It was not explained what became of the actual "Cleaner".

(
superorganism
)

:

This version of "
The Diviner
" was a creation of the
cilium-like vegetation
superorganism
that lived on a
planet
located in the
Hirogen star system
of the
Delta Quadrant
that appeared to in the image of
Gwyn
's
father
,
The Diviner
.
It was encountered by Gwyn after she had cut away the
root
growth that had ensnared the
USS
Protostar
's
landing strut
and began to board the ship. When this "The Diviner" attempted to convince Gwyn to stay on the planet, and that among other things, how proud he was of her followed by a gesture for a
hug
, Gwyn saw through the uncharacteristic feedback, snapped out of her momentary trance, and told the creature that it was not her father. "The Diviner" was then encompassed in its own roots and told her to "stay", and as she ran up the
shuttlebay
hatch
bunches of roots bounded after her. (
PRO
: "
Dream Catcher
")
