John Noble
.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Traveler
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/The_Traveler
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation Starfleet
(formerly)
Occupation:
Traveler
Status:
Departed for another reality
(
2384
)
Played by
:
Eric Menyuk
Tom Jackson
(as
Lakanta
)
For the unrelated photonovel of the same name, please see
The Traveler
.
"
There is no specific 'place' I wish to go.
"
"
Then what is the purpose of your journey?
"
"
Curiosity.
"
–
The Traveler
being  questioned by
Jean-Luc Picard
,
2364
("
Where No One Has Gone Before
")
A being who described himself as a
Traveler
, but whose real name he claimed was unpronounceable by
Humans
, was a
humanoid
from a
mysterious species
, encountered by
Starfleet
during the
2360s
. Reported to be from
Tau Alpha C
, he himself claimed to be a
traveler
on a journey to experience "your
reality
". He also claimed that while by his understanding he was not from another time, he might be according to Starfleet's understanding of
time
. (
TNG
: "
Where No One Has Gone Before
", "
Journey's End
")
The Traveler had certain abilities, like the ability to act like "a
lens
which focuses
thought
", which allowed him to alter
space
,
time
, and
warp fields
with the power of his mind. He could phase out of time and
dimension
and move between
planets
and
starships
. These abilities were based on his ability to focus the energy of thoughts and in his advanced understanding of the nature of reality. According to The Traveler, thought was the basis of all reality. In addition,
Deanna Troi
noted that his presence could not be detected with the empathic abilities of
Betazoids
. He could also
shapeshift
to appear as humanoids from other species with different clothing. (
TNG
: "
Where No One Has Gone Before
", "
Journey's End
")
Around
2364
, The Traveler had been trading his understanding of propulsion for passage on Starfleet vessels. This was the first time his species visited this culture. He claimed to be motivated by curiosity, his stated goal being experiencing "your reality." During these travels, he became an assistant to Starfleet
propulsion expert
Kosinski
, who claimed to have discovered a revolutionary new method of
warp drive
. Although some of his theories did have merit, Kosinski's formulas turned out to be bogus, as
Wesley Crusher
discovered that it was actually Kosinski's
assistant
, The Traveler, who was the driving force behind the propulsion method. Things went awry, however, and the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was catapulted millions of
light years
to the galaxy known as
M-33
. Later, while attempting to return back to the
Milky Way Galaxy
, the
Enterprise
instead traveled to the
end of the universe
, a billion light years away. The Traveler collapsed in
exhaustion
and was taken to
sickbay
, where he revealed the secret of his abilities. He eventually returned to
engineering
, where he helped return the ship and crew home to the Milky Way Galaxy.
The Traveler noticed something special about the young
Wesley Crusher
, comparing him to a young
Mozart
. Inspired in part by this,
Captain Picard
promoted Wesley to an
acting ensign
in the traditions of Starfleet. The Traveler played a significant role in the destiny of Wesley Crusher. (
TNG
: "
Where No One Has Gone Before
")
In
2365
, when the
Enterprise
encountered a duplicate version of Captain Picard, apparently transported six hours back in time, the contemporary Captain Picard briefly suspected The Traveler as a cause of this anomaly - noting that The Traveler was capable of movement through time using the power of his mind.
Commander Riker
, perhaps recalling The Traveler's largely benign nature, did not share this suspicion. (
TNG
: "
Time Squared
")
The Traveler was next encountered by the
Enterprise
crew in
2367
, after a "
warp bubble
" experiment by Wesley Crusher went awry and trapped his mother
Beverly Crusher
in an alternate reality. Due to Wesley's increasing realization of his powers, the Traveler was drawn to him and sensed the predicament. He appeared and directed the crew on her rescue. He needed Wesley's help in opening a doorway to the other reality and had to convince him he had the ability. They succeeded in rescuing the elder Crusher, while the younger Crusher continued his maturation. (
TNG
: "
Remember Me
")
In
2370
, The Traveler appeared incognito as
Lakanta
, a villager on
Dorvan V
, to Wesley Crusher. Lakanta, who seemed to have intimate knowledge of Wesley, directed him to seek the answers to his troubled destiny. Wesley was, at the time, quite uncertain about his future in Starfleet. The Traveler guided Wes to the "
habak
" where Wesley had a
vision
of his
deceased father
telling him that his destiny lay somewhere
other
than with Starfleet. The
cadet
heeded the advice and resigned from Starfleet, after which in the middle of a riot he managed to dissociate himself from time; it is then that The Traveler revealed his true identity and promised to mentor the young man on his new journey. (
TNG
: "
Journey's End
")
In
2024
, Wesley revealed to
Kore Soong
that he and the Traveler were part of a larger group who had created the
Watchers
to help ensure the survival of various species throughout time and space. Much like the Traveler had done to Wesley, Wesley extended an invitation for Kore to join them, which she accepted. (
PIC
: "
Farewell
")
In
2384
, after a
temporal paradox
threatened to destroy the
prime universe
, the Traveler and the rest of his kind decided that the paradox was too complicated for them to resolve, particularly as their numbers had been depleted by the
Temporal Wars
, and they left for other realities. The sole exception was Wesley who refused to abandon his home universe to destruction and insisted upon remaining behind to fix the paradox. When the
USS
Protostar
crew arrived at the Travelers'
time ziggurat
, Wesley and the Traveler were arguing on the matter and were audible to the crew in the outer areas of the temple. The Traveler insisted to Wesley that there were other realities, and this plane of existence was doomed, but Wesley wouldn't give up. The Traveler was forced to leave without Wesley (
PRO
: "
The Devourer of All Things, Part I
") who was ultimately proven to be right, repairing the broken timeline and saving the prime universe with the help of the crews of the
Protostar
and the
USS
Voyager
-A
. (
PRO
: "
Ouroboros, Part I
", "
Ouroboros, Part II
")
The Traveler as part of course material
By
3195
, the existence of The Travler was common knowledge in Starfleet, being taught as a subject in the
Starfleet Academy
seminar
Confronting the Unexplainable
. (
SA
: "
Series Acclimation Mil
")
Quick Answers
What is the true name of the character known as 'The Traveler'?
The Traveler, a character from the 2360s Starfleet encounters, is a mysterious humanoid from Tau Alpha C. His true name is unpronounceable by Humans. He identifies himself as a traveler on a journey to experience 'our reality'.
Provided by: Fandom
What is the origin of 'The Traveler' in Star Trek?
Originating from Tau Alpha C, The Traveler first emerged in Star Trek circa 2364. He exchanged his propulsion expertise for travel on Starfleet vessels, aiming to experience different realities. He assisted Starfleet's Kosinski, a propulsion expert, and was known for his significant standing within the Q Continuum. Starfleet encountered The Traveler thrice between the mid to late 2360s.
Provided by: Fandom
What is the purpose of 'The Traveler's' journey?
The Traveler embarks on his journey driven by curiosity, aiming to experience 'your reality.' He exchanges his knowledge of propulsion for travel on Starfleet vessels, marking his species' first interaction with this culture. He collaborates with Starfleet propulsion expert Kosinski, who purports to have found a groundbreaking warp drive method.
Provided by: Fandom
How does 'The Traveler' perceive time differently from Starfleet?
The Traveler's perception of time differs from Starfleet's due to his unique capabilities. He can traverse time using his mind and can phase out of time and dimension. His interpretation of time is distinct from Starfleet's, as he asserts he is not from another era, but might be according to Starfleet's conception. He can act as a lens focusing thought, enabling him to modify space, time, and warp fields. These capabilities stem from his ability to concentrate energy.
Provided by: Fandom
How did Wesley Crusher become a traveler like 'The Traveler'?
Wesley Crusher became a traveler akin to 'The Traveler' due to his discontent with his Starfleet career. Recognizing Wesley's potential, The Traveler urged him to explore realms where thought and energy intersect in unimaginable ways. Consequently, Wesley departed Starfleet to traverse new planes of existence under The Traveler's guidance. However, his appearance at Riker and Troi's wedding in a Starfleet uniform implies he may have served as a Starfleet officer eventually.
Provided by: Fandom
Appendices
Appearances
TNG
:
"
Where No One Has Gone Before
"
"
Remember Me
"
"
Journey's End
"
PRO
: "
The Devourer of All Things, Part I
"
(voice)
Background information
The Traveler was played by actor
Eric Menyuk
.
Menyuk was a
finalist
for the role of
Data
, and was given the recurring role of The Traveler as a consolation prize. (
Starlog
,
December 1988
, Issue 137, p. 54;
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 36;
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 520)
An early draft of "
Journey's End
" included the revelation that
Boothby
was, in fact, The Traveler. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 290)
Apocrypha
According to the
Q Continuum
series, The Traveler even has a significant reputation among the
Q Continuum
; when the
female Q
learned that Wesley had been chosen by The Traveler, she was almost impressed by this fact.

Theodore Wallace
was a
scientist
in the mid-
23rd century
, a
biologist
who specialized in
endocrinology
.
Sometime between
2261
and
2267
, he was stationed at a lonely
outpost
at

, where he met his future
wife
,
Janet
. Though he was 26 years older than her, he fell in
love
with her and they
married
.
Later, they worked together on trying various
carbohydrate
compounds to slow down the degeneration of
plant
life. Wallace
died
sometime prior to 2267; he was described by his wife as a 'brilliant' and 'great' man, whom she admired. At the time of her marriage to Theodore, she believed herself to be over
James T. Kirk
, but she was wrong. (
TOS
: "
The Deadly Years
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Tiron
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tiron
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Businessman
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Played by
:
Jeffrey Combs
Tiron
was a wealthy
business associate
of
Quark
's on
Deep Space 9
and a member of a
humanoid
species whose faces were segmented, with the largest divisions between the forehead from the rest of the face; green coloration was present on the forehead of a generally pale face. Tiron, himself, was well-endowed with
money
, as he could afford his own
holosuite
as a gift to himself.
In
2371
, Tiron was attracted to
Kira Nerys
, but she drove off his advances by claiming that
Odo
was her
lover
. Still obsessed with her, Tiron offered to pay Quark for a
holosuite program
that featured Kira. When Kira discovered Quark's scheme she made significant changes to the program, including placing Quark's head on her body, much to Tiron's displeasure. (
DS9
: "
Meridian
")
Appendices
Background information
Tiron was the first of nine
Star Trek
roles for actor
Jeffrey Combs
.
Combs filmed his scenes on Thursday
22 September
1994
and Friday
23 September
1994
on
Paramount Stage 17
. His makeup artist was
Dean Gates
. His costume was later sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
According to
StarTrek.com
, he is a member of the
Serilian
species, which is also mentioned in the episode as having an ambassador on the station.
[2]
(X)
Complicating this is a note under the Serilian entry in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
: "
We never saw the ambassador
".
The
mirror universe
Tiron (
β
) appears in the
Shards and Shadows
short story "
A Terrible Beauty
".
External links
Tiron
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Tom Norris
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tom_Norris
======================================================================

Tom Norris
was an acquaintance of
Beverly Crusher
during her youth. She went on one
date
with him, which abruptly ended with the question "
Is that a
beard
or is your face dirty?
" Beverly later realized that she had really hurt him, and from that point on she was more conscious of others' feelings.
Jean-Luc Picard
referenced the incident, while he and Crusher temporarily had the ability to read each other's thoughts in
2370
. (
TNG
: "
Attached
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Status:
Uncertain
(
2374
)
Ensign
Tom P. Mahoney
was a
Starfleet
officer
during the
Dominion War
.
In
2374
, a file from the
starship
USS
Akagi
reported Mahoney
missing in action
. A file from the
USS
Zapata
reported him
killed in action
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Mahoney's name appeared on the
casualty list
display seen on a
computer
readout in that episode. It is unclear which of the above was his home station, if any.
Mahoney may have been named after visual effects artist
Thomas Mahoney
, though there is currently no source to suggest he worked on
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
.

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek

PAGE: Tosin

:

Tosin
was an
alien
Kolaati
trader
who dealt in
illegal
substances near the
Nekrit Expanse
. In
2373
, after a prior deal with
Wixiban
and
Neelix
had failed, Tosin agreed to spare their lives only if they delivered three grams of
warp plasma
. Several people were injured and one
killed
when one of Tosin's men fired his
weapon
, igniting the warp plasma. One of Tosin's party was killed, the others taken into custody by
Bahrat
soon after. (
VOY
: "
Fair Trade
")
Tosin was played by Trek regular
James Horan
.
One of the group of three in which Tosin was said to be killed, but it was not specified who.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tosk
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tosk
======================================================================

Species:
Tosk
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Played by
:
Scott MacDonald
"
I am Tosk.
"
–
Tosk
,
2369
("
Captive Pursuit
")
Tosk
was a
Tosk
who lived during the
24th century
.
In
2369
, Tosk became the
first visitor
to the
Alpha Quadrant
through the
Bajoran wormhole
. He was convinced to dock at
Starbase
Deep Space 9
for repairs.
Tosk's ship
was a one-person vessel, with standard systems, which enabled
Chief
O'Brien
to repair his ship easily. O'Brien later helped the Tosk evade the
Hunters
, to retain his
honor
, rather than be captured alive. (
DS9
: "
Captive Pursuit
")
Quick Answers
What was Tosk's mission in the Alpha Quadrant?
Tosk's mission in the Alpha Quadrant was to engage in a hunt. As the first from the Gamma Quadrant to enter the Alpha Quadrant via the Bajoran wormhole, he docked at Deep Space 9 for repairs. Tosk was specifically bred for the hunt by the Hunters, dedicating his life to this pursuit. Once repairs were completed, the Hunters continued their pursuit, taking Tosk back to the Gamma Quadrant.
Provided by: Fandom
How did Tosk maintain his honor on Deep Space 9?
Tosk maintained his honor on Deep Space 9 by adhering to his cultural values, which emphasized dying with honor. Despite being unable to disclose his predicament, Tosk sought to preserve his dignity by requesting O'Brien to allow him to die honorably. This request was rooted in Tosk's belief system, which prevented him from breaking his oath of silence or seeking asylum. The bond between Tosk and O'Brien strengthened as O'Brien respected Tosk's wishes, ultimately allowing Tosk to continue his journey with the Hunter ship pursuing him.
Provided by: Fandom
Why did Chief O'Brien help Tosk evade the Hunters?
Chief O'Brien assisted Tosk in evading the Hunters to help Tosk maintain his honor. O'Brien's decision was influenced by their bond and the aim to restore the hunt to its original state. Although O'Brien faced a reprimand in his Starfleet record, Captain Sisko privately understood his reasons.
Provided by: Fandom
What role did the Bajoran wormhole play in Tosk's journey?
The Bajoran wormhole was key in Tosk's journey, linking the Gamma Quadrant to the Alpha Quadrant. Tosk, from a reptilian species, was the first to pass through it, arriving at Deep Space 9. He was pursued by Hunters, as he was bred for this ritual hunt. The wormhole enabled the Hunters to continue their pursuit and return Tosk to the Gamma Quadrant, following events involving Chief O'Brien.
Provided by: Fandom
What challenges did Tosk face on Starbase Deep Space 9?
On Starbase Deep Space 9, Tosk needed repairs for his ship, which brought him into contact with Chief O'Brien. While on the station, he observed his surroundings closely, including a diagram of Deep Space 9. The emissions from his ship risked revealing his presence to the Hunters pursuing him, adding to his challenges.
Provided by: Fandom

"

That's your name, or your species?
"

Quark
Appendices
Background information
Filming Tosk
The Tosk seen in "Captive Pursuit" was played by
Scott MacDonald
, with stunt work performed by
Tom Morga
.
The character was the only Tosk ever to appear in a
canon
production. Regarding MacDonald's portrayal of Tosk, O'Brien actor
Colm Meaney
remarked, "
He gave a great performance.
" (
The Official Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Magazine
issue 5
, pp. 8-9)
MacDonald commented:
There was so much to overcome to get the character across. Those contact lenses were murder. I don't wear them in real life so it was rough to get used to. I had makeup on my hands so they hired an eye tech to handle them. Plus they were cats-eye, straight up and down, so occasionally they would just roll and ruin a take. But try getting a stranger's finger in your eye right before you do a take - it's an experience I won't ever forget! But to have Tosk turn out really well despite all the hurdles makes him my favourite I think. In the audition I remember they were having me read him meaner than I saw him when I looked at the words. I was lucky, it is a very well written piece. I think I felt if he was too scary then the audience might not root for Tosk and thus, not root for O'Brien either. Much of the humour came out of Colm's great responses - it is inherent in the word play and the scenes. Structurally, I remember thinking it was such a solid episode from so many areas. Colm is a fine actor and I really liked working with him, and
Corey
was an active director taking an interest in actor's choices, and the script was excellent. In the end I was proud of how Tosk turned out - a good tragic hero, and likeable enough to root for”.
[1]
The writers considered bringing back Tosk and the Hunters.
Ron Moore
commented: "
This comes up from time to time in our story meetings. We don't have
anything on the board for Tosk or the Hunters at the moment, but you never
know
".
External link
Tosk
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Townof Bedrock's name in the enrollment records (left column, fourth from the top)
Townof Bedrock
was a
student
enrolled at a
Trill
music academy
sometime in
Joran Belar
's lifetime.
In
2371
, Bedrock's name was among those that came up when
Benjamin Sisko
and
Julian Bashir
searched
enrollment records
for all Trill music academies while seeking information on Belar. (
DS9
: "
Equilibrium
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This name was an
in-joke
reference to the town of
Bedrock
from
The Flintstones
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Trajok
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trajok
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Wrestler
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Played by
:
Chris Nelson Norris
Trajok
was a
male
humanoid
wrestler in the
24th century
, specializing in the
Galeo-Manada
style. Trajok's species had three sets of thorned ridges, two on each side of their foreheads and one in the middle.
In
2370
, he coached
Jadzia Dax
in the technique while visiting
Deep Space 9
. When
Arjin
came to meet with her, Trajok answered Jadzia's door, initially taking Arjin off guard and making him think he had the wrong
quarters
. Trajok informed the
initiate
that she was in the
shower
, then asked if she was expecting him.
Afterward, he departed for
Calondia IV
, but promised Dax a rematch when he returned the following
week
. (
DS9
: "
Playing God
")
Background information
Trajok was played by
Chris Nelson Norris
.
According to the script his name was pronounced TRAY-jahk, and he was described in the script notes as "
a very
alien
man [...] with an athletic build.
"
The costume worn by Norris as Trajok was sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]
(X)
Given Trajok's appearance, he might be a full-blooded
Ktarian
, as evidenced by the same (subtler) features seen on the half-Ktarian
Naomi Wildman
of
USS
Voyager
.

PAGE: Travers
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Travers
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Rank:
Commodore
Occupation:
Commander
,
Earth
observation outpost
on
Cestus III
Status:
Deceased
(
KIA
)
(
2267
)
Died:
2267, Cestus III
Played by
:
Unknown actor
(voice)
For similarly named couple, please see
Alicia Travers
or
her husband
.
Commodore
Travers
was a male
Starfleet
flag officer
in the
23rd century
, who was the
commander
of the
observation outpost
on
Cestus III
in
2267
.
Travers was famous for his
hospitality
, thanks in part to
his personal chef
, whom
Dr.
Leonard McCoy
wondered had been brought along to the planet.
James T. Kirk
guessed he probably had, as his
rank
would have given him the right to do so.
That same year, the
Gorn
, who regarded Cestus III as part of their
space
, attacked and destroyed this outpost. Travers presumably died in this attack. After destroying the outpost, the Gorn faked several messages from the commodore to lure the
USS
Enterprise
into a trap. Because of these messages,
Captain
Kirk and his
landing party
beamed
down and were attacked immediately by the Gorn. (
TOS
: "
Arena
")
Travers only appears as an off-screen voice-over provided by an
unknown actor
.
He appears in the
Pocket TNG
novel
Requiem
, in which
Jean-Luc Picard
is thrown back in time to just before Kirk and the Gorn commander's fight. He is suspicious of "Dixon Hill", the
alias
Picard uses while there.
The novel
Open Secrets
gives him the first name Howard.
External links
Howard Travers
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Trellit
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trellit
======================================================================

Species:
Zobral's species
Affiliation:
Torothan
caste
Rank:
Chancellor
Occupation:
Leader
of
Toroth
Status:
Active
(
2152
)
Played by
:
Charles Dennis
Chancellor
Trellit
was a
male
member of the
Torothan
clan and a
government
official on
Toroth
.
In
2152
,
T'Pol
contacted Trellit for assistance when
Captain
Jonathan Archer
and
Commander
Tucker
vanished while visiting the officially outlawed
Zobral
. Trellit proved to be of little help, as Zobral was the leader of a faction opposing the ruling
caste
of Toroth. (
ENT
: "
Desert Crossing
")
Trellit was played by actor
Charles Dennis
.
In the final draft script of "Desert Crossing", Trellit was described as "a terse and unceremonious City Official [...] wearing a distinctive uniform." The character was thereafter referred to in the script as "City Official", even after giving his name.

PAGE: Trelos Vren
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trelos_Vren
======================================================================

Trelos Vren
was an inhabitant of
Deep Space 9
.
He had once been
arrested
for attempting to break into the station's
assay office
. Later, in
2372
, he was arrested outside the
Klingon restaurant
and
Odo
held him on four counts of
petty theft
.
Odo reviewed the case with
Kira Nerys
,
joking
that one might "
have to admire his persistence
" despite his lack of success. (
DS9
: "
Indiscretion
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

.

.
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Fleet Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Fleet Admiral
Trevor Roth
was a
Starfleet
flag officer
in the late
24th century
and early
25th century
.
Roth was listed as a
chief of staff
on the
dedication plaque
of the
USS
Stargazer
,
USS
Titan
-A
, and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
In
3195
, he was listed with other luminaries of
Starfleet Academy
on a
commemoration wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
"
set decoration
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
It can be presumed that this individual was named after executive producer
Trevor Roth
.

.

was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from
Sigma Draconis IV Tourist Center
to
Sol IV
(
Marsport
). His special accommodation was a
class K
environment suite. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named for producer
Trevor Roth
.

alien

who served during the late
23rd century
. In
2286
, she served aboard the
USS

as a

.
That year, the

was among the
starships
disabled by the
Whale Probe
when Trillya sent a
distress call
to
Starfleet Headquarters
. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
Set still
Trillya was played by
Jane Wiedlin
, rhythm guitarist and songwriter for the rock band
The Go-Go's
.
The name Trillya originated from the source's
script
.
The fact that she is an "alien" is taken from the film's credits, where she was as "Alien Communications Officer".

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Trodo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Trodo
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
The Unwanted
Occupation:
Miner
Status:
Active
(
2383
)
Played by
:
Ben Thomas
Trodo
was an
alien
Unwanted
who was forced to work as a
chimerium
miner
on
Tars Lamora
for
the Diviner
for several
years
. Their species appeared to only have a single eye.
Trodo and their work companion,
Grjördslat
, were both discovered by
Dal
, hanging onto a
pipe
after the
planetoid
had lost
gravity
. Dal, who was equipped with his
Starfleet
combadge
, a type of
short wave translator
, approached the two and instructed them to get to the
Rev-12
, because the
atmospheric shields
would soon fail.
The two miners, however, where more interested in their new found ability to understand and communicate with each other, and proceeded to introduce themselves. Trodo proceeded to tell Grjördslat that "
after so many years, it's nice to finally
talk
!
" As the two miners joined their hands together in an embrace, Trodo further confessed, "
I've been holding in these
thoughts
and
feelings
for so long, Grjördslat–
" but they were cut off by Dal, who apologized for interrupting "this beautiful moment", who then pushed them along towards the
Rev-12
. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
")
Trodo was voiced by
Ben Thomas
, and credited as "Miner #2".
External link
Trodo
at
StarTrek.com

PAGE: Tyvanna
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tyvanna

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Bridge officer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Anita Brown
Tyvanna
was an
alien
female
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
command division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
.
Tyvanna was at her station on the
bridge
of the
Enterprise
, which was the room's foremost
starboard
station, when the ship arrived at
Starbase
Yorktown
. She returned to the bridge just as the ship was entering the
Necro Cloud
nebula
.
During the ensuing
Battle of Altamid
, she was among the first to leave the bridge, when the
order
was given to
evacuate
the ship. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
A behind the scenes shot of Tyvanna
Tyvanna was played by actress
Anita Brown
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
The
reference book
Star Trek Beyond - The Makeup Artistry of Joel Harlow
spells this character's name "Ty Vana", although the end credits of
Star Trek Beyond
spelled it "Tyvanna".
The character's make-up was applied to her by
Leonard MacDonald
and
Werner Pretorius
. (
Prosthetics
, issue 4, p. 15)

======================================================================
PAGE: Umali
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Umali
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Owner of a
bar
Status:
Active
(
2377
)
Played by
:
Iona Morris
Umali
was a
humanoid
who lived on the
planet
Quarra
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2377
, Umali owned a
bar
that mainly served workers from the nearby
Central Power Facility
.
Tom Paris
, whose memories of his past life had been wiped, approached her for a job after being terminated from the plant. She was surprised they fired him due to the planet's labor shortage, but he managed to convince her that his natural charm and personality would be an asset to her. On his first shift, Paris tried to increase "customer loyalty" from two women by giving away free
drinks
. Umali did not approve and told him to attend to the other tables. (
VOY
: "
Workforce
")
Umali was played by actress
Iona Morris
.
Umali was described in the script as a "
exotic, middle-aged alien proprietress,
" adding that "
she may or may not be a
telepath
, but we get the sense she has a talent for reading people.
"

PAGE: Uttan Narsu
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Uttan_Narsu

Admiral
Uttan Narsu
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
22nd century
. Stationed at
Starbase 12
, Narsu commanded all of the
Federation
starships
in the
sector
, including the
USS
Essex
.
In
2368
, after having taken control of the bodies of three crewmembers of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, a group of
non-corporeal
entities attempted to convince
Captain
Picard
that they were the disembodied
spirits
of members of the
Essex
crew. Attempting to test them, Captain Picard asked whose command they had been under, a question to which the entity which had taken over
Counselor
Deanna Troi
responded correctly. Picard remained unconvinced, however, and was eventually successful in foiling the entities' plans. (
TNG
: "
Power Play
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Uzani
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Uzani
======================================================================

Uzani
was a military leader referenced in
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases. He commanded an army at
Lashmir
.
In the phrases "
Uzani. His army. With fists open.
", it was a metaphor for luring the enemy towards you by spreading your forces.
In the phrases "
Uzani. His army. With fists closed.
", it was a metaphor for closing rank and attack after luring the enemy. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
In the phrase "
Uzani, when the light came over the mountain.
", it was a metaphor for revealing information. (
LD
: "
The New Next Generation
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Uzani was pronounced as "oo-ZAH-nee".

======================================================================
PAGE: Vader
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vader
======================================================================

Vader's gravestone
For the Starfleet cadet with a similar sounding name, please see
Rader
.
Vader
was the name of a resident of
Caldos colony
who had passed away on the 23rd of
September
in the
year
2242
.
Vader's
gravestone
was in the same
cemetery
where
Felisa Howard
was laid to rest in
2370
. In that year, the area around Vader's grave was the scene of a battle between Felisa's
granddaughter
Beverly Crusher
and an
anaphasic lifeform
named
Ronin
. (
TNG
: "
Sub Rosa
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
The gravestones bore in-joke references to science fiction characters. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 280) This one was a reference to
Darth Vader
of the
Star Wars
saga.
External link
Vader
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Van Doren
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Van_Doren
======================================================================

Van Doren
was an individual who was largely responsible for establishing a successful
cardiac replacement
procedure. By
2365
, this technique had been perfected to a very low 2.4
%
mortality rate
.
That year, while en route to have the procedure performed on him,
Jean-Luc Picard
mentioned the low mortality rate of Van Doren's technique to
Wesley Crusher
. (
TNG
: "
Samaritan Snare
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Varn
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Varn
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Starship
captain
Status:
Active
(
2376
)
Played by
:
Ted Rooney
Varn
was an
alien
starship
captain
in the
Delta Quadrant
. He lived on
his ship
with
his crew
, and had many enemies.
In
2376
, he was contacted by three people claiming to be
Starfleet
officers
, a Captain
Janeway
,
Commander
Chakotay
, and
Lieutenant
Tuvok
. He wasn't aware that these were actually the
thieves
,
Dala
,
Zar
, and
Mobar
. After they gave him a tour of their ship, the
Delta Flyer
, they offered him membership into a scam
Federation
. They showed him the impressive
weaponry
the mother ship,
USS
Voyager
, had and told him that the Federation was a
family
, and that they'd gladly help him fight his enemies. They claimed they had to leave for a six-
month
mission, so he paid an admission fee to join quickly.
However, he learned he had been scammed when he found that the
photon torpedoes
they sold him were just empty casings, and didn't affect his enemies, the
Polonians
who had
also
bought a Federation membership.
As reimbursement, he decided to take the false
Delta Flyer
for himself. However, at just that time the real
USS
Voyager
dropped out of
warp
nearby. He threatened to fire on them, but before they could tell him he'd been misled, he fired at their
tractor beam
emitters.
Voyager
quickly disabled Varn's ship, and went after the impostor
Delta Flyer
. (
VOY
: "
Live Fast and Prosper
")
Varn was played by actor
Ted Rooney
.
His costume was auctioned off in the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
.
[1]
(X)
[2]
(X)
According to the script notes, Varn was described as "a rugged-looking alien who's more brawn than brains."

======================================================================
PAGE: Varria
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Varria
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Aide
to
Kivas Fajo
Status:
Deceased
,
murdered
by
Kivas Fajo
(
2366
)
Died:
2366,
Jovis
Played by
:
Jane Daly
Varria
was a
female
humanoid
living in the
24th century
.
From young adulthood in
2352
to
2366
, she was an
aide
to
Kivas Fajo
, a
Zibalian
trader
and collector, and served him on his ship, the
Jovis
. It was also hinted that she and Kivas may have been romantically involved. She helped him acquire unique items and entertain his guests. She knew him to be immoral and dangerous, but stayed in his company out of fear and manipulation; Fajo lavishly rewarded his crew when they were obedient, but also cruelly punished anyone who defied him or attempted to escape. Varria's face was visibly scarred, which she bitterly hinted was one of Fajo's "equally lavish" rewards for disobedience.
In
2366
, Varria helped to
capture
the
android
Starfleet
Lieutenant Commander
Data
to add him to Fajo's collection of rare items. She helped to stage his
death
by determining his construction and putting the same material on his shuttle which exploded soon after returning to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. Varria was loyal to Fajo until he threatened to kill her with a
Varon-T disruptor
if Data did not cooperate with him.
As a result, Varria chose to escape from Fajo, assisting Data in exchange for her own safe passage. In doing so, she tripped an alarm, alerting Fajo. He caught them and
murdered
her with the disruptor, just before Data was rescued by the
Enterprise
. (
TNG
: "
The Most Toys
")
Varria was played by
Jane Daly
. She was described in the script notes simply as "a female humanoid".

======================================================================
PAGE: Vassbinder
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vassbinder
======================================================================

Doctor
Vassbinder
was a
professor
at
Starfleet Academy
in the
24th century
.
During his time at Starfleet Academy,
Chakotay
failed Professor Vassbinder's course in
temporal mechanics
. (
VOY
: "
Year of Hell, Part II
")
Vassbinder also lectured at the
physiognomy
workshop
during a
psychological conference
on the effects on
deep space assignments
in
2369
. He mistakenly gave an hour-long
dissertation
on the ionization effect of
warp nacelles
where the topic in question was supposed to be
psychology
. His presentation style both bored the attendees and prevented them from alerting him to his mistake;
Captain
Picard
stated, "
He just kept talking in one long incredibly unbroken sentence moving from topic to topic so that no-one had a chance to interrupt; it was really quite
hypnotic
." (
TNG
: "
Timescape
")
In
2370
, Vassbinder published some new theories on
warp propulsion inter-relays
, which
Wesley Crusher
used to discredit
Geordi La Forge
's work on a new
plasmadyne relay
for the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's
warp core
. (
TNG
: "
Journey's End
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Vassbinder was named after a high-school teacher of
Brannon Braga
's. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 251)
According to the
Star Trek Adventures - The Sciences Division
sourcebook, his full name is Ernst Vassbinder.
Appendices
External link
Vassbinder
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Vekor
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vekor
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
;
Humanoid
Occupation:
Mercenary
Status:
Deceased
, killed by
Tallera
(
2370
)
Died:
2370
,
Vulcan
Played by
:
Caitlin Brown
Vekor
was a female
mercenary
who lived during the late
24th century
.
She was from a
humanoid
species
with enlarged temples, which connect to the helix of the
ears
. They had no earlobes; their ear instead sloped directly into their
jaws
. They also had short, narrow eyebrows. Her species was very similar in appearance to the
Klaestrons
, with the exception the top of the ear ridge ends at the hairline rather than the browline.
In
2370
, she worked with
Arctus Baran
and a group of mercenaries to find the
Stone of Gol
and was present when
Commander
William T. Riker
was abducted from
Barradas III
. When
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, posing as
Galen
,
mutinied
against Baran, Vekor joined with him and was part of the mercenary
landing party
that landed on
Vulcan
.
She was later killed by
Vulcan
isolationist
Tallera
using the stone, which was actually a
psionic resonator
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
Vekor was played by actress
Caitlin Brown
.
She was described in the script as "a large, intimidating alien female."

in the
2370s
.

originally assigned her
quarters
similar to Admiral
Colti
's, but
Worf
insisted that Colti get better quarters since she outranked Veta. When Odo then proposed new quarters, Worf also complained that

must get quarters equal to Veta's since he commanded a
starship
. (

: "

")

five admirals
were present at the signing ceremony, visual evidence would indicate that Veta may be any one of those admirals.

language

======================================================================
PAGE: Vigo
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vigo
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Officer
Occupation:
Weapons officer
,
USS
Stargazer
Status:
Active
(
2355
)
For other similarly named individuals, please see
Miranda Vigo
or
Jason Vigo
.
Vigo
was a
24th century
Starfleet
officer
, who served aboard the
USS
Stargazer
under the
command
of
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
.
Vigo served as the ship's
weapons officer
in
2355
during the so-called
Battle of Maxia
.
During Picard's
2364
reliving of the incident, he asked Vigo if he could identify their assailants. He later ordered Vigo to get a
fire control party
to the bridge. Following that, he briefly mistook
William T. Riker
for Vigo. (
TNG
: "
The Battle
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
novel
Reunion
, Vigo was a member of the Pandrilite race. Vigo was also mentioned and heard in the
2006
video game
Star Trek: Legacy
.
Additionally, in the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 545, it was mused by the authors that Vigo shared their name with
Miranda Vigo
, a woman with whom Picard had a brief affair and may have sired a son,
Jason
around the time of his
Stargazer
captaincy. It was speculated therein that the two Vigos may have been connected at an earlier draft of the
episode
in which Jason appeared, and that Picard may have slept with this Vigo's wife, but this was omitted from the final teleplay. The name, however, was not changed.
External link
Vigo
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Vilix'pran
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Vilix'pran
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant
Status:
Deep Space 9
crewmember
(
2373
)
Children:
Vilix'pran's hatchlings
Lieutenant
Vilix'pran
was a
Starfleet
crew member assigned to
Deep Space 9
in the
2370s
.
In
2371
, he was an
ensign
assigned to
cargo inspection
, but
Julian Bashir
made a request for the
ensign
's transfer because he was
budding
with
twins
. Bashir and
Miles O'Brien
were throwing him a
baby shower
; O'Brien was also building him a hatchling
pond
, while Bashir had ordered baby
clothes
from
Elim Garak
. A prior budding had produced four offspring. (
DS9
: "
Heart of Stone
")
By
2373
, Vilix'pran had been promoted to
lieutenant
, and was in the process of budding for a third time. Doctor Bashir indicated this would bring the total number of children between eight and eighteen, indicating the nature of his species' reproductive system produces
litters
of two to twelve (as Vilix'pran already had 6 children). Bashir indicated that
Major Kira
should expect a request soon for larger
quarters
. (
DS9
: "
Apocalypse Rising
")
Jake Sisko
babysat
his hatchlings on several occasions, a task which involved preventing their
wings
from getting tangled. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
The name of
Vilix'pran's species
was not revealed. It seems that his species shorten their names when used in general conversation, as he was sometimes just called Pran. In "Business as Usual", Jake referred to Vilix'pran as Ensign Pran. Presumably, this was just a slip of the tongue, and Vilix'pran was still a Lieutenant.
According to the script for "Apocalypse Rising", Vilix'pran's name was pronounced as "vih-LIX pran".
[1]
External link
Vilix'pran
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Villus Thed
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Villus_Thed
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Occupation:
Criminal
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
Villus Thed
was a member of a
telepathic
species
.
In
2370
,
Odo
received a
subspace
transmission that indicated that Thed was on his way to
Deep Space 9
and told two of his
Bajoran
deputies
to notify him when Thed arrived and to keep him under constant
surveillance
. Odo also reminded them that since Thed was a short-range
telepath
, they were to stay "at least" five
meters
from Thed as he would be able to read their thoughts if they came any closer. (
DS9
: "
Second Sight
")
A
wanted poster
of Thed could be regularly seen in Odo's
security office
. (
DS9
: "
Rocks and Shoals
")

PAGE: W.F. Beyer
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/W.F._Beyer
======================================================================

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Admiral
W.F. Beyer
was an individual who served as a
Starfleet
flag officer
.
In
3195
, they were listed with other luminaries of
Starfleet Academy
on a
commemoration wall
in the
USS
Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
"
set decoration
)
This individual was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wade Felker (24th century)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wade_Felker_(24th_century)
======================================================================

Wade Felker
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Kogin
ferried him from the
Excalbia Tourist Resort
to
New Fabrina Settlement
. His
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
when reviewing data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Felker was named after
CBS Digital
film transfer coordinator
Wade Felker
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wadjet
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wadjet
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Starfleet
officer
Status:
Active
(
2263
)
Played by
:
Dan Payne
Wadjet
was an
alien
who, in the
alternate reality
, was serving in the
operations division
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
shortly before that
starship
was destroyed in
2263
.
Wadjet was later held captive in
Krall's base
. He witnessed
Manas
remove the
Abronath
from
Ensign
Syl
's
head
, the
bioweapon
having been hidden from their captors there. Freed by
Spock
and
McCoy
, he was in the last group of
Starfleet
officers
which escaped from the base. They were protected by
Captain
James T. Kirk
's distraction tactic using a
a motorcycle
and
holographic technology
that made him appear to be in several places at once, moments before the group was
beamed
to safety aboard the
USS
Franklin
. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Wadjet was played by
Dan Payne
. This character was only named in the source's end credits.
The makeup for this character was sculpted by Makeup Effects Sculptor
Joey Orosco
. The makeup was applied by Special Makeup Effects supervisor
Joel Harlow
, makeup artist
Kevin Haney
, and Makeup Effects Artist
Holland Miller
. (
Prosthetics
, issue 4, pp. 8 & 9)
In a
deleted scene
from
Star Trek Beyond
, Wadjet can be viewed walking behind Kirk and
Montgomery Scott
in a busy terminal at
Starbase
Yorktown
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wagner
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wagner
======================================================================

For "real world" composer, please see
Richard Wagner
.
"
He brought two hundred scientists from all over the Federation, and all he did was put us to sleep!
"
–
Deanna Troi
,
2369
("
Timescape
")
Professor
Wagner
was one of the
lecturers
at a
psychological conference
attended by several
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmembers
in
2369
.
Deanna Troi
and
Data
visited his
phylobiology
seminar
with about two hundred other
Federation
scientists
. Troi thought the idea of the seminar was that everyone would participate; however, the seminar was dominated by Wagner's
lecture
, which bored the
scientists
to
sleep
. Data, however, kept a full record of Wagner's lecture in his
memory record
. (
TNG
: "
Timescape
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Walker (Ensign)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Walker_(Ensign)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Ensign
Occupation:
Deep Space 9
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2374
)
Ensign
Walker
was a
female
officer
who served on
Deep Space 9
during the
2370s
.
In
2374
, she turned down three
dinner
invitations from
Julian Bashir
, but after Bashir got "pointers" from
Vic Fontaine
, the two began
dating
. Bashir related this to
Miles O'Brien
, who was incredulous at the thought of the doctor discussing such matters with a
hologram
. (
DS9
: "
His Way
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
External link
Walker
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Walter Horne
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Walter_Horne
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Professor
of
Creative Writing
,
Starfleet Academy
Status:
Active
(
24th century
)
Walter Horne
was a
professor
of
Creative Writing
at
Starfleet Academy
for a large part of the
24th century
.
Both
Jean-Luc Picard
in the
2320s
and
Wesley Crusher
in the
2360s
took his course during their Academy days. When Picard expressed his surprise that Horne was still teaching by
2368
, Wesley confirmed it and added that he was a good teacher. (
TNG
: "
The Game
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Walter M. Jefferies (Starfleet)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Walter_M._Jefferies_(Starfleet)
======================================================================

Walter M. Jeffries
was a
Starfleet
officer
who was listed as a member of the
Admiralty Board
on the
dedication plaques
of the
USS
Stargazer
and
USS
Titan
-A
and
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Star Gazer
", "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
It can be presumed that this individual was named after art director
Matt Jefferies
.

Starfleet
Rank:

USS

science officer
Status:

)
Waverly Smothers
was a
Starfleet
officer
in the
24th century
.
In
2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard in
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was in room #65114. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
directory
)
In
2371
, he was a
crewmember
aboard the
USS
Voyager
. He was among the crew stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, when
Voyager
was forcefully brought there by the
Caretaker
.
In
2372
, as a
lieutenant
, he was listed on the
Planetary Geosciences Division
duty roster
. (
VOY
: "
Parturition
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
He was named after
second grip
Waverly Smothers
.

on the

while the ship was in the

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Weiss (physicist)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Weiss_(physicist)
======================================================================

Doctor
Weiss
was a
theoretical researcher
at the
Daystrom Institute
.
This individual's name was mentioned in journals read by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
as he was searching for clues on how to detect
cloaked
Romulan
warbirds
during the
Klingon Civil War
in
2368
. (
TNG-R
: "
Redemption II
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This scientist was named after
Craig Weiss
, a director of visual effects at
CBS Digital
.

PAGE: Wendy Drapanas (Starfleet)

For the Voyager crewmember, please see
Wendy Drapanas
.
Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
Science Ops
,
Beta Antares Ship Yards
Status:
Active
(
2373
)
Wendy Drapanas
was a
Starfleet
officer
who served during the late
24th century
.
During the
2370s
, she was among the members of the
Science Ops
team listed on the
dedication plaques
of
starships
USS
Voyager
in
2370
, and the
USS
Prometheus
in
2373
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
",
dedication plaque
;
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
",
dedication plaque
)
In an
unknown, alternate future timeframe
, she was named as a
Fleet Ops
officer on the dedication plaque of the
USS
Pasteur
. (
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
"
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Wendy Drapanas was named for scenic artist
Wendy Drapanas
.

Wendy Knoller
was a commercial
transport
passenger in the
24th century
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Wisconsin
ferried her from
Camus II
to
Deneb II City
. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wendy Neuss (Lieutenant Commander)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wendy_Neuss_(Lieutenant_Commander)
======================================================================

For other people with the same name, please see
Wendy Neuss
.
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Lieutenant Commander
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
-D
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
Born:
Gamma Trianguli IV
Wendy Neuss
was a
female
Starfleet
officer
who lived during the
24th century
. Her
planet
of origin was
Gamma Trianguli IV
.
In
2328
, the
SS
Manoa
ferried her from
New Paris Colony Starbase
to
Alpha Braga VII
. Her special accommodation was a
class N
environment suite. Her
passenger manifest
was accessed by
Lieutenant Commander
Data
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2370
by reviewing this data in the
commercial transport database
. (
TNG
: "
Inheritance
",
okudagram
)
In
2368
, she resided in
Epsilon Municipality
on
Nel III
. On
stardate
45323.7, she was treated at the
Farrell Hospital
(
Ryan Center
) for a
coma
of unknown cause. It was later discovered that Neuss had been the victim of a
telepathic memory invasion
by the
Ullian
Jev
. (
TNG-R
: "
Violations
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Retroactive continuity
: She was treated at the
Wiemer Medical Facility
. (
TNG
: "
Violations
",
display graphic
)
In
2369
, she was a lieutenant commander aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. She was one of seventeen individuals who were from worlds outside the
Federation
. Her
proteins
were tested by
Doctor
Beverly Crusher
for a possible configuration match with the fragments of a four billion-year old
genetic
program. The results came back negative. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
",
display graphic
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
She was named after the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
producer of the
same name
.

2371

aboard the

======================================================================
PAGE: Wendy Neuss (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wendy_Neuss_(comedian)
======================================================================

For other people with the same name, please see
Wendy Neuss
.
Listed in the
USS
Enterprise
-D
's database
Wendy Neuss
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to located a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Neuss was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program list code for this comedian was
DP-34284
. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
Neuss was named after the
Star Trek: The Next Generation
producer
of the same name
. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 2nd ed., p. 70)

PAGE: Wendy Ruiz (comedian)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wendy_Ruiz_(comedian)
======================================================================

For the 24th century individual, please see
Wendy Ruiz
.
Species:
Humanoid
Occupation:
Comedian
Wendy Ruiz
was a notable
humanoid
comedian
.
In
2365
,
Data
attempted to locate a comedian to help him better understand the concept of "
what is funny?
". Ruiz was among the
holodeck program
files that were contained aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. The program code list for this comedian was
EP-45629
. (
TNG-R
: "
The Outrageous Okona
",
okudagram
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.
This character was named for
CBS Digital
head of mastering
Wendy Ruiz
.
For the remastered version of this episode, this character replaced
Terri Martinez
.

officer

24th century
.

2353
, he was listed as a
Starfleet Operational Support Services
officer on a directory placard at
Starbase 32
,
Maxia sector
. His office was located in room #53964. (

: "

",
directory
)
In
2365
, he held the rank of
captain
, and served as the
commanding officer
of the

Lexington
. His name was listed on the

chart that was on display in the

of

. (
TNG
: "

",
okudagram
)
In

, he was a member of the

of the

. (

: "
Message in a Bottle
",
dedication plaque
)
This character was only mentioned in writing.

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Will Kayden
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Will_Kayden
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Occupation:
USS
Rutledge
crewmember
Status:
Deceased
Will Kayden
was a
Starfleet
officer
serving in the
24th century
.
During the
Federation-Cardassian War
, Kayden served aboard the
USS
Rutledge
under
Captain
Benjamin Maxwell
. Kayden was good friends with fellow
Rutledge
crewman
Miles O'Brien
. He was affectionately
nicknamed
"Stompie" by his shipmates and was well known for his fondness of the old
Earth
song "
The Minstrel Boy
".
In combat, Maxwell remembered Kayden as being as cool under fire as a
mountain
lake
. He died fighting the
Cardassians
on
Setlik III
during the
Setlik III massacre
. (
TNG
: "
The Wounded
")

novelization
of

.

Enterprise
-B came to be best known for (mapping 142 systems, and seventeen first contacts). Captain George was replaced circa 2298 by Captain Harriman's second assignment to the position.

======================================================================
PAGE: Worene
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Worene
======================================================================

Species:
Unknown
Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Enlisted
Occupation:
USS
Enterprise
crewmember
Status:
Active
(
2270s
)
Played by
:
Paula Crist
Worene
was a
female
alien
humanoid
crewman
with an upturned
nose
who lived during the latter half of the
23rd century
. She served as an
enlisted
Starfleet
crewman
aboard of the
USS
Enterprise
under
Captain
James T. Kirk
during the mid-
2270s
.
During that time, she attended the
V'ger
briefing on the
recreation deck
. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
)
Appendices
Background information
Worene was played by stunt actress
Paula Crist
. The alien, including the name, was her own creation.
"Worene" came about when
Susan Sackett
,
Gene Roddenberry
's personal assistant, saw Paula in her "Thorwor" [a male alien entirely from her own imagination] costume and mentioned Paula to GR when they needed "bridge aliens" for the first ST film. He didn't feel that "Thorwor" was a good alien to use in the movie because that character has a very strong personality not suitable for background parts. In discussion with GR, Paula conceived "Worene," a female relative to "Thorwor" who would be a lot less weird and slightly more
Human
-looking. Paula worked on "Worene" for some time before she was ready to show the result to the studio.
By the time
Paramount
casting wanted some "bridge aliens," Paula was ready to show off "Worene" and made some tests. Gene and Paramount loved the tests, but director
Robert Wise
felt the makeup was too good and would detract from the principal actors.
That is a puzzle, since Mr. Wise evidently did not feel that
large, purple
,
globby-headed
or
lizard-faced
aliens would be distracting, but "Worene" would be. For one thing, "Worene" was short, while the other aliens were almost always tall males, which is a tad more noticeable. We all felt that the main actors could handle having aliens on the bridge, that their acting abilities would have prevented any extra attention being paid to the non-Humans, but Mr. Wise's decision was final. "Worene" could appear, with the rest of us, on the rec deck and nowhere else.
"Worene" was put into a
Starfleet uniform
and given a small "cameo" walk-on in the scene. Even that got
badly cut
, and much of Paula's hoped-for Big Moment ended up on the cutting room floor. She was philosophical about this, commenting "Even though I got cut badly, it was an honor to be chosen by Gene Roddenberry to appear in ST:TMP. He also made sure I got screen credit. It wasn't my biggest break, but how many actresses get the chance to do their own creation in a large film?" (
On the Good Ship Enterprise
, pp. 274-275)
The part that Crist was originally supposed to have as a bridge alien went to the
Rhaandarite ensign
played by
Billy Van Zandt
. On the day of filming, actress Crist told fellow background actor
Dennis Fischer
that her species was "Czintii" (which he assumed was not intended to be related to the
Kzinti
). He thought she resembled a female
Tellarite
.
[1]
Apocrypha
In
Christopher L. Bennett
's novel
Ex Machina
, Crewman Worene plays a small role. She is said to be a member of the Aulacri species, a feral humanoid species with tails.
Forgotten History
has her getting in a fight started by
Betelgeusian
crewman Hrrii'ush Uuvu'it (
β
), only to give her the mistaken impression that he was flirting with her.
External link
Worene
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Wright (Admiral)
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Wright_(Admiral)
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Federation
Starfleet
Rank:
Admiral
Occupation:
Flag officer
Status:
Active
(
2371
)
Wright
was a
Human
male
who served as a
Starfleet
officer
during the late-
24th century
.
In
2371
, Wright held the rank of Admiral. He served at
Starfleet Headquarters
on
Earth
.
That year, the crew of the
USS
Defiant
visited the
planet
to attend a
symposium
on the
Gamma Quadrant
, however during
transport
to the surface,
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
,
Lieutenant
Jadzia Dax
and
Doctor
Julian Bashir
inadvertently materialized in
21st century
San Francisco
, just days before the
Bell Riots
were due to take place.
During his investigation into the
incident
,
Chief
Miles O'Brien
developed a method of duplicating the original transporter accident which would allow an
away team
to travel back in time to search for the stranded crew, however Wright ordered him not to do so for fear they may contaminate the timeline further.
Major
Kira
objected to his decision but when she attempted to contact Wright to discuss it further, Starfleet and the
Federation
had already disappeared from the timeline due to the presence of Sisko, Dax and Bashir. (
DS9
: "
Past Tense, Part I
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

.

captain
at
Starfleet Academy
's

. Their office, located on the second level of a building, was in room #213. The rank, name, and office of this individual was listed on a directory placard. (

, set artwork)
In
2380
,
Beckett Mariner
described
Brad Boimler
's would-be role as "kind of a Xon" when he refused to participate in her "
movie
"
holodeck
program,

: "

")
The original Xon
This individual was named after the
Star Trek: Phase II
character
Xon
. The placard was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$31.99.
[1]
The mention of Xon in
LD
: "
Crisis Point
" was a joke based on the fact that Xon was a character who was supposed to appear but was ultimately cut from the final version of
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
. Absent this context, it can simply be taken as an
Appeal to Obscurity
.

PAGE: Yint

Species:

Yint
was a weapons supplier for
Liam Bilby
of the
Orion Syndicate
. Members of his species bore ridges along the top of their heads, and in the middle of their faces.
In
2374
, Yint sold Bilby three defective
Klingon
disruptors
, claiming them to be in perfect working order. He was later killed by Bilby using the very same disruptors, after they had been repaired by
Connelly
, to prove a point about lying to the Syndicate. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
")
Yint was played by actor
Brad Blaisdell
.
This character was only named in the source's end credits.
The prosthetics for
Yint's species
somewhat resemble the
Tak Tak
face, with the top and back of a
Rotciv
covering the rest of the head.

,

, and

after their

======================================================================
PAGE: Yuen
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Yuen
======================================================================

Yuen
was the name of a member of the
Maquis
called out by
Michael Eddington
during his last stand against the
Jem'Hadar
on the
planet
Athos IV
in
2373
.
Eddington gave orders to the names
Crenshaw
, Yuen,
Stiles
, and
Dimitris
in an attempt to make it appear that more than he was fighting against the Jem'Hadar. Specifically, Eddington ordered Yuen and Crenshaw to circle around to the left of the Jem'Hadar. (
DS9
: "
Blaze of Glory
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Given the nature of the situation, this name may have been either made up or an actual Maquis he knew.
According to the script, the pronunciation for Yuen was " YEW-EN (equal emphasis on both syllables)"
[1]

PAGE: Yuri (person)

.

.

was responsible for including them in the treaty. (

and

were references to the Japanese

.

PAGE: Z. Orton

Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Z. Orton
was a
Starfleet
officer
who was listed as a member of the
Engineering Division
on the

of the

Athena
. (
SA
: "
Kids These Days
")
This character was only mentioned in writing.

on his first mission by assuring him she knew some things that Old Sneezy hadn't taught her. Confused by the nickname, Kim asked who it was, and Torres identified Zakarian, asking if Kim remembered him and his apparent allergies to everything. Kim noted that he never cared for Zakarian. (

in

.

Dala
and

were able to move from
star system
to
star
system offering services for different goods from the various
races
, but not delivering what they promised. Eventually,

caught up with the three and captured them, returning everything they had
stolen
. (

: "

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zareh
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zareh
======================================================================

Affiliation:
Emerald Chain
Occupation:
Courier
Status:
Deceased
(
3189
)
Died:
3189
,
USS
Discovery
Played by
:
Jake Weber
Zareh
was a
male
courier
who lived during the
3180s
. He had little regard for the
United Federation of Planets
(or "
V'draysh
", as he called it) and felt that nothing mattered after
the Burn
but hoarding
dilithium
.
History
Prior to
3189
, he assumed the role of courier for "
the Colony
" by
beheading
the planet's previous courier. He supplied the planet's
mining
settlement with whatever it could not produce for itself, and held immense power over its
Coridanite
miners as a result. He also had experience with the planet's
parasitic ice
, and claimed to have seen it go down a person's throat on one occasion.
In 3189,
his ship
detected high-energy
gamma rays
and
gravitational waves
in the vicinity of the Colony, and subsequently found the time-displaced
USS
Discovery
wrecked on the surface. He landed at the settlement to investigate and found
Commander
Saru
and
Ensign
Sylvia Tilly
trading
dilithium
to miners
Os'ir
and
Kal
in exchange for repairs to
Discovery
's
transtator
. Correctly deducing that
Discovery
had arrived from
the past
, and that Os'ir and Kal were planning to turn against him, Zareh took the group hostage and
murdered
Kal in retaliation.
Zareh threatens Tilly
Zareh planned to break down the
Starfleet
officers' equipment and sell their parts on the
Tellarite Exchange
, and demanded dilithium from
Discovery
's stores. He knew that the starship had come down on a
glacier
of the parasitic ice, and that
Discovery
would be crushed after sunset as the ice spread faster in the shade. He planned to send Tilly to collect the dilithium, reasoning that she stood a decent chance of making it there and back alive.
One of Zareh's henchmen caught
Philippa Georgiou
sneaking around outside the settlement, and brought her inside. Zareh tried to torture her into submission, but she brushed off his attempts. When he tried to have her killed, she and Saru fought back and killed Zareh's men, leaving him battered but alive. Zareh made a last-ditch attempt to sway Os'ir, threatening to destroy the settlement's
life support
and leave them to
asphyxiate
, but Os'ir refused and Tilly smashed a
bottle
on his head, leaving him dazed.
Georgiou attempted to kill Zareh with his own weapon, but Saru refused to allow this, as summary execution was against Starfleet's principles. Saru instead left the courier's fate to Os'ir, who opted to claim Zareh's ship and leave him exiled on the planet's inhospitable surface with only Tilly's pack to sustain him. (
DIS
: "
Far From Home
")
While stranded on "the Colony" Zareh lost some of his fingers on his left hand and the surrounding flesh was blackened due to the parasitic ice.  He blamed Tilly for his mutilation.
Having reemerged as a member of the
Emerald Chain
who served on the
Viridian
, Zareh was later part of
Osyraa
's boarding party when she hijacked
Discovery
.  He was put as commander of the captured ship while Osyraa was speaking with
Charles Vance
.  (
DIS
: "
There Is A Tide...
") After capturing
Cleveland Booker
, Zareh tortured him in
Discovery
's sick bay hoping to get the location of the
dilithium nursery
within the
Verubin Nebula
.  However, he never got the information as both Book and
Michael Burnham
escaped his custody.  Zareh confronted Book in an open
turbolift
and a fight ensued.  Book came out victorious after Zareh insulted Book's cat
Grudge
; Zareh fell out of the turbolift to his death as Book shouted "[Grudge]'s a queen!" at him. (
DIS
: "
That Hope Is You, Part 2
")
Zareh was played by
Jake Weber
.

murder
of

: "

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Zima
CATEGORIES: Individuals
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Zima
======================================================================

Zima
was an individual referenced in the
Children of Tama
language
metaphorical
phrases, "
Bakor
at
Anzo
.
" and
"Bakor and Zima at Anzo.
"
In
2368
, after a failed greeting with the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
Captain
Dathon
spoke of
Darmok
. This hunter allied with
Jalad
when they fought the beast of
Tanagra
. This mutual pairing brought the two individuals closer together. Dathon's
first officer
responded to this by saying, "
Bakor at Anzo. Bakor and Zima at Anzo.
" The captain remained firm in his conviction that success in communication with the Federation would be best achieved by following Darmok's lead, and rejected the first officer's suggestion. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
According to the
script
, Zima was pronounced as "ZEE-mah".

PAGE: Zimbata

Affiliation:

,

Captain
Zimbata
was a
24th century
male
Starfleet
officer
. By
2365
, he was in command of the
Constellation
-class
starship
USS
Victory
.
Lt. Commander
Geordi La Forge
served under Zimbata as an
ensign
on the USS
Victory
and gave him the gift of a model of his command's namesake, the ancient
Earth
sailing vessel
HMS
Victory
, in
2365
. (
TNG
: "
Elementary, Dear Data
")
Appendices
Background information
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.
Though La Forge stated that he served as an ensign under Zimbata in "Elementary, Dear Data", it was established in
TNG
: "
Identity Crisis
" that he was a
lieutenant (junior grade)
on the
Victory
in
2362
for the away mission to
Tarchannen III
. This partially dates Zimbata's command of the
Victory
, even though Zimbata was not mentioned in "Identity Crisis."
According to a
deleted scene
from
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part I
", Zimbata attended
Starfleet Academy
with Captain
Edward Jellico
; they played on the Academy's
rugby
team together, where Jellico was even worse than Zimbata.
It was not stated if Zimbata was still in command of the
Victory
when it fought in the
Dominion War
, as referenced in
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
".
Apocrypha
The novel
The Buried Age
gives him the first name Marien.

at

Star Trek
